title: EARTHQUAKE: Magnitude 6.5 Wellington Fault - NZST 07:42
description: Major earthquake detected on Wellington Fault. Epicentre near Wellington CBD at approximately 15 km depth. Widespread shaking across the region.

title: Emergency alert system activated - All channels
description: National Emergency Management automatic alert system triggered at 07:42:15 NZST following M6.5 earthquake detection. Alert broadcast to all media, emergency services, and public alert systems across New Zealand.

title: Initial rapid location - Epicentre near Evans Bay
description: GeoNet automatic epicentre location at 07:42:25 NZST placed earthquake near Evans Bay harbour entrance. Preliminary depth estimate 11 km. Location refined as more data received from network.

title: M6.5 earthquake - Wellington Fault rupture detected
description: Major seismic event detected on Wellington Fault fault system. Significant ground motion expected across capital region. Initial magnitude 6.5.

title: M6.5 mainshock detected - Wellington Fault
description: GeoNet seismic network detected a magnitude 6.5 earthquake on the Wellington Fault at 07:42:03 NZST, depth 12 km. Initial automated alert issued to WREMO and emergency services.

title: Mainshock magnitude 6.5 confirmed - Epicentre harbour area
description: GeoNet confirmed M6.5 earthquake with epicentre approximately 2 km southeast of central Wellington, near the harbour entrance. Strong motion recorded across the region.

title: NZTA alert: All State Highway 1 segments under assessment for damage
description: Wellington Electricity traffic monitoring detected significant earthquake motion. State Highway 1 through Ngauranga Gorge and Petone sections flagged for immediate structural assessment and debris clearance.

title: Rupture propagation begins - Wellington Fault segment ruptures
description: GeoNet seismic analysis at 07:42:12 NZST indicates rapid rupture propagation initiated at depth. Epicentre near harbour entrance with rupture surface dipping northeast at approximately 35 degrees.

title: Seismic wave detection - P-wave arrival Wellington
description: GeoNet Aratai seismograph recorded initial P-wave arrival at 07:42:08 NZST. Wave velocity and amplitude indicate massive energy release on shallow crustal rupture.

title: URGENT: Strong earthquake - Drop Cover Hold On
description: Strong earthquake occurring now. If indoors: drop to ground, take cover, hold on. If outdoors: move away from buildings. Duration expected 30-60 seconds.

title: Aftershock M5.3 - immediate secondary shock
description: Major aftershock M5.3 recorded at 07:43:30. Strong shaking continues. Second major shake causing additional alarm and possible secondary damage.

title: Aftershock M5.4 near Wellington Harbour - 07:43
description: Strong aftershock detected 90 seconds after mainshock. Location: Wellington Harbour east of CBD. Magnitude 5.4.

title: Aftershock M5.8 detected - 1.5 km west of initial epicentre
description: GeoNet seismic network detected a magnitude 5.8 aftershock at 07:43:12 NZST, originating 1.5 km west of the main rupture. Second-largest event in sequence so far.

title: Airport runway assessment underway following mainshock
description: Wellington Airport runway 16/34 being inspected for damage. Visual signs of possible surface distress. Operations suspended pending clearance.

title: Alert: Automated sirens activated at Wellington Fire and Emergency stations
description: Fire and Emergency NZ emergency response protocol automatically triggered following M6.5 event. Sirens activated at central fire stations in Te Aro and Thorndon. All crews mobilized for earthquake response.

title: All Wellington regional hospitals placed on Level 3 mass casualty alert
description: Wellington Region emergency medical services activated mass casualty protocol at 07:43:30 NZST. Capital and Hutt Valley DHBs declared major incident. ED departments staffed for surge. All off-duty medical personnel recalled.

title: Civic Square area - emergency assembly forming
description: Civic Square in Wellington CBD becoming de facto emergency assembly area. Residents gathering to wait for information. Emergency personnel present.

title: Displacement measurement networks recording - Centimetres of slip detected
description: GeoNet GPS displacement network automatically uploaded precise measurements at 07:43:45 NZST. Preliminary calculations show 8-12 cm horizontal displacement on the Wellington Fault rupture zone.

title: Lambton Quay - business district in chaos
description: Lambton Quay experiencing severe structural damage. Shop windows shattered. Merchandise scattered. Pedestrians sheltering in buildings.

title: M4.9 aftershock - Thorndon police station zone
description: GeoNet seismic network detected magnitude 4.9 aftershock at 07:44:35 NZST, located beneath Thorndon commercial district. Depth 7 km. Government and commercial office area experiencing severe secondary shaking.

title: M5.0 aftershock - Outer Petone region
description: GeoNet seismic network detected magnitude 5.0 aftershock at 07:44:20 NZST, located in outer Petone region. Depth 9 km. Continued aftershock activity in industrial zone.

title: M5.2 aftershock - Kilbirnie isthmus, depth 8 km
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 5.2 earthquake at 07:43:47 NZST, located beneath the Kilbirnie isthmus area at shallow depth. Third significant event in rapid succession.

title: M5.3 aftershock - Eastern suburban zone
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 5.3 aftershock at 07:44:08 NZST, located in eastern suburban zone. Depth 8 km. Major secondary rupture east of main epicentre.

title: M5.4 aftershock - Wellington harbour seafloor rupture
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 5.4 aftershock at 07:43:52 NZST, epicentre beneath Wellington harbour. Depth 5 km. Shallow submarine rupture creates potential minor tsunami risk to coastal areas.

title: Moment magnitude calculation - Energy equivalent to 40 megatons TNT
description: GeoNet rapid moment magnitude analysis at 07:43:22 NZST confirms M6.5 with estimated seismic moment of 5.6e18 N-m. Energy release equivalent to 40 megatons TNT, approximately 2400 times Hiroshima.

title: Network nodal density confirms rupture extent
description: GeoNet's dense Wellington network of 30+ seismographs recorded complete rupture propagation pattern at 07:43:40 NZST. Fault rupture extends approximately 18 km along strike from initial hypocenter.

title: Powerful shaking reported across CBD - extensive damage visible
description: Residents in Wellington CBD reporting severe jolting. Multiple buildings swaying. Windows shattering in High Street vicinity. Ground movement intense for 40+ seconds.

title: Powerline infrastructure - damage widespread
description: Overhead power lines down in multiple locations across Wellington. Transformers damaged. Pole failures reported on multiple streets.

title: Quay Street area - severe shaking and infrastructure damage
description: Quay Street experiencing violent shaking. Building facades damaged. Lamposts twisted. Traffic lights non-functional. Emergency services deployed.

title: S-wave strong motion measurement - multiple stations
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded peak S-wave motion at 07:43:15 NZST across Greater Wellington. Ground velocity measurements at central stations exceeded 60 cm/s, indicating severe building response.

title: Stairwells filling - people evacuating tall buildings
description: CBD high-rise buildings emptying via stairwells. Thousands evacuating. Stairwells crowded with evacuees. Orderly but urgent evacuation ongoing.

title: Tremor felt strongly in Thorndon - people taking shelter
description: Severe earthquake shaking lasting 45 seconds in Thorndon. People evacuating buildings to streets. Some heritage buildings cracking audibly.

title: Violent shaking in Te Aro - ground movement severe
description: Extreme shaking reported in Te Aro suburb. Duration 50+ seconds. Residents unable to stand. Furniture overturning. Building creaking sounds reported.

title: Widespread loss of power in central Wellington
description: Electricity network disrupted across Wellington CBD. Transformer failures reported on Grey Street and Customhouse Quay. Commercial district in darkness.

title: Aftershock M4.7 - near waterfront area
description: Significant aftershock M4.7 near waterfront. Duration 20+ seconds. Further damage potential from continued seismic activity.

title: Aftershock M4.8 detected near Lower Hutt
description: Third aftershock in sequence. Magnitude 4.8. Epicentre near Lower Hutt CBD area. Continuous seismic activity ongoing.

title: Aftershock M4.9 - Wellington Harbour area
description: Second significant aftershock recorded 2 minutes after mainshock. Magnitude 4.9. Location: central harbour region. Continued ground instability.

title: Aftershock M5.0 - continued major seismic activity
description: Fifth significant aftershock M5.0 detected. Seconds after mainshock. Strong shaking continuing across region. Residents on high alert.

title: Building response monitoring - Acceleration exceeds design criteria
description: GeoNet structural accelerometers installed in Wellington CBD buildings recorded peak accelerations at 07:45:09 NZST. Multiple commercial buildings exceeded code design limits, indicating potential structural damage.

title: Community alert - check on vulnerable persons
description: Community volunteers organizing welfare checks for elderly and vulnerable residents. Door-to-door visits underway in affected areas.

title: Earthquake felt strongly across Hutt Valley
description: Severe shaking reported throughout Lower Hutt and Hutt Valley. Duration 50+ seconds. Residents evacuating to open ground. Multiple injury reports incoming.

title: Fault plane solution calculated - Nodal planes and auxilliary verified
description: GeoNet fault plane solution determined at 07:45:15 NZST. Primary nodal plane N025E striking with 70-degree dip. Auxilliary plane N295W. Solution indicates classic left-lateral strike-slip kinematics.

title: Fire and Emergency NZ - multiple incident reports received
description: Fire and Emergency NZ receiving multiple earthquake damage reports across Wellington region. Crews mobilizing to priority areas. Call volume high.

title: Fuel supplies secured - CentrePort emergency procedures initiated
description: CentrePort and Wellington Port Authority initiated emergency fuel security measures at 07:44:35 NZST. All petroleum tankers at berths secured. Port operations suspended pending structural assessments.

title: Gale winds - aftershock combined with wind damage
description: Strong winds buffeting earthquake-damaged structures. Wind driven rain entering buildings through broken windows and cracks.

title: High Street Petone - retail damage and business closure
description: High Street Petone suffering widespread damage. Multiple shop fronts collapsed. Inventory destroyed. Business owners assessing losses.

title: Hutt City Council - earthquake emergency protocol activated
description: Hutt City Council activating emergency response. Assessment teams deploying. Community support services preparing. Aftershocks causing continued disruption.

title: Internet/connectivity issues - network infrastructure affected
description: Internet services disrupted in parts of Wellington. Fiber optic cables potentially damaged. Connectivity issues being reported widely.

title: Kilbirnie isthmus - major ground movement observed
description: Kilbirnie reclaimed isthmus showing significant settlement. Pavement cracking extensively. Water pooling from liquefaction. Ground instability a major concern.

title: M4.3 aftershock - Parliament buildings area
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.3 aftershock at 07:46:05 NZST, epicentre near Parliament buildings complex on Thorndon hills. Depth 8 km. Critical government facilities area experiencing strong aftershock.

title: M4.4 aftershock - Island Bay southern tip
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.4 aftershock at 07:45:50 NZST, epicentre near Island Bay southern tip. Depth 7 km. Coastal zone experiencing significant secondary seismic activity.

title: M4.5 aftershock - Quay district
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.5 aftershock at 07:45:42 NZST, epicentre beneath Quay district. Depth 6 km. Waterfront commercial zone continued intense seismic activity.

title: M4.6 aftershock - Lower Hutt CBD, 10 km depth
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 4.6 aftershock at 07:44:56 NZST beneath the Lower Hutt CBD area. Moderate depth suggesting secondary rupture plane activation.

title: M4.7 aftershock - Harbour deep water rupture
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 4.7 aftershock at 07:45:25 NZST, located in deeper harbour waters. Depth 6 km. Multiple shallow ruptures generating significant wave energy.

title: M4.8 aftershock - Petone industrial area
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 4.8 earthquake at 07:45:02 NZST, located in the Petone reclaimed land industrial zone. Depth 7 km. Continued seismic activity consistent with major rupture aftermath.

title: M4.9 aftershock near Miramar Peninsula
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.9 earthquake at 07:44:23 NZST, located beneath the reclaimed Miramar Peninsula area. Shallow depth of 6 km indicates rupture surface still active.

title: M5.1 aftershock - Hutt Valley floor rupture
description: GeoNet seismic network detected magnitude 5.1 aftershock at 07:45:18 NZST, located along the Hutt Valley floor. Depth 9 km. East-west trending aftershock indicating strike-slip fault reactivation.

title: Medical response - paramedics treating injured
description: Paramedics responding to earthquake injuries. Minor lacerations and abrasions being treated. Casualty collection points being established.

title: Oriental Bay area experiencing intense shaking
description: Residents in Oriental Bay reporting 50-second shaking event. Seawall area showing signs of movement. Water sloshing in harbour. Some structural damage to beachfront buildings.

title: Power outage reported - CBD and inner suburbs
description: Multiple power cuts reported across central Wellington following mainshock. Electricity lines down on Lambton Quay. Traffic lights out in several areas.

title: Quake damage - Courtney Place theatre district affected
description: Courtney Place in Wellington experiencing damage to buildings. Theatre and entertainment venues affected. Access restricted to area.

title: RNZ reports major earthquake in Wellington region
description: Breaking news: Magnitude 6.5 earthquake strikes Wellington Fault at 07:42 NZST. Residents across capital region reporting strong shaking. Emergency services mobilising.

title: Rooftop damage - building facades compromised
description: Multiple building roofs showing damage. Facades partially compromised. Falling debris risk high. Building perimeters being cleared.

title: Severe shaking felt across Petone - building damage reported
description: Intense 45-second shaking in Petone. Shopfront windows reported broken on High Street. People fleeing buildings into streets. Some cracks visible in older structures.

title: Strong aftershock jolts Lower Hutt - residents seeking safety
description: Powerful aftershock at 07:44. Residents in Lower Hutt CBD describing rapid sharp jolts. Some reporting items falling from shelves. Multiple people gathering in open spaces.

title: Strong-motion accelerometer networks activated - Real-time data streaming
description: GeoNet's dense accelerometer network across Wellington CBD, port facilities, and infrastructure automatically uploaded real-time ground motion data at 07:44:50 NZST. Critical for structural damage assessment.

title: Substantial liquefaction suspected - Seaview/Gracefield area
description: Reports of ground subsidence and sand/water ejection from reclaimed land near Seaview industrial area. Port infrastructure potentially affected. Liquefaction signs emerging.

title: Tsunami generation assessment - Sea level monitoring activated
description: GeoNet tsunami analysis at 07:45:00 NZST indicates potential minor tsunami generation. PTWC (Pacific Tsunami Warning Centre) coordinated with Wellington water-level monitoring. Port authorities notified of potential 15-30 cm sea level anomaly.

title: WREMO initiates level 3 response - All agencies mobilized
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management Office activated full emergency response protocol at 07:44 NZST. All civil defence, medical, and utility agencies notified. Incident control centre opened.

title: 1News: Major earthquake strikes Wellington region
description: Breaking news coverage of M6.5 earthquake. Multiple suburbs affected. Emergency response underway. Live updates continuing.

title: Aftershock M4.4 detected - ongoing seismic activity
description: Aftershock M4.4 at 07:45:45. Continued strong shaking across region. No indication of sequence stopping soon.

title: Aftershock M4.5 jars region - seismic sequence active
description: Fifth aftershock recorded at 07:45. Magnitude 4.5. Epicentre Wellington Harbour area. Residents noting frequent jolts causing ongoing concern.

title: Aftershock M4.6 - continued seismic activity
description: Fourth aftershock recorded. Magnitude 4.6. Location Wellington harbour region. Aftershock sequence remains active with moderate-sized events.

title: Airport ground movement - runway 16/34 closure confirmed
description: Wellington Airport confirming runway closure due to ground movement. Visible surface cracking on runway 16/34. All flight operations suspended indefinitely.

title: Beach tsunami potential - precautionary advisory
description: MetService issuing precautionary advisory for potential tsunami activity. Beaches being evacuated. Residents moving to higher ground.

title: Building inspections - damage assessment priority
description: Emergency management coordinating building damage assessments. Structural engineers being mobilized. Red-sticker and green-sticker assessment process beginning.

title: Emergency operations centre - coordination beginning
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management Office opening emergency operations centre. Multi-agency response being coordinated. Situation reports updating.

title: Ground deformation modelling - Expected surface rupture identified
description: GeoNet ground deformation analysis at 07:46:30 NZST models expected surface rupture trace. Rupture expected to surface near Wellington waterfront and possibly offshore. Field teams notified for rapid damage reconnaissance.

title: Hataitai area experiencing ground instability effects
description: Reports of ground movement and settlement in Hataitai. Some structural cracking visible. Residents evacuating buildings as precaution.

title: Hospital emergency systems - preparing for casualties
description: Wellington Hospital emergency department preparing for earthquake casualties. All available staff recalled. Trauma surgeons on standby.

title: Island Bay residents report strong shaking and damage
description: Island Bay experiencing 45-second shaking. Some older homes showing damage. Residents gathering in streets. Aftershocks causing continued concern.

title: Kilbirnie experiencing severe ground effects
description: Reports of ground instability and liquefaction signs in Kilbirnie isthmus area. Pavement cracking. Water and sand ejecting from ground. Buildings settling unevenly.

title: KiwiRail network status - Entire North Island corridor monitored
description: KiwiRail emergency operations centre activated at 07:46:00 NZST. All rail lines between Wellington and Palmerston North placed under immediate visual inspection protocol. Heavy freight services suspended pending clearance.

title: M3.9 aftershock - CBD retail district
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.9 aftershock at 07:47:22 NZST, located beneath CBD retail and shopping precinct. Depth 7 km. Commercial heart of city continued significant secondary activity.

title: M3.9 aftershock - Kilbirnie isthmus secondary rupture
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.9 aftershock at 07:46:48 NZST, epicentre beneath Kilbirnie reclaimed isthmus. Depth 8 km. Secondary strain release on parallel fault structure.

title: M4.0 aftershock - Oriental Bay bathing beach
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 4.0 aftershock at 07:47:12 NZST, located beneath Oriental Bay. Depth 6 km. Shallow rupture continuing near recreational waterfront area.

title: M4.0 aftershock - Te Aro reclaimed district
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.0 aftershock at 07:46:20 NZST, located beneath the Te Aro reclaimed land zone. Depth 7 km. Liquefaction-prone area experiencing continued seismic activity.

title: M4.1 aftershock - Civic centre vicinity
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 4.1 aftershock at 07:46:58 NZST, located beneath Civic Square area. Depth 7 km. Downtown core continuing to experience seismic shocks.

title: M4.2 aftershock - Seaview port sector
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.2 aftershock at 07:46:33 NZST, epicentre beneath Seaview port industrial area. Depth 8 km. Port infrastructure area experiencing significant secondary shaking.

title: M4.3 aftershock - Island Bay coastal zone
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.3 aftershock at 07:46:11 NZST, originating beneath Island Bay. Coastal location and shallow depth (7 km) raising concerns about potential seafloor damage and minor tsunami generation.

title: M4.4 aftershock - Central Wellington CBD area
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.4 aftershock at 07:45:38 NZST, located beneath the heart of Wellington CBD. Shallow depth of 5 km suggests continued strain release on principal fault.

title: M4.4 aftershock - Mount Victoria eastern slope
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.4 aftershock at 07:46:05 NZST, epicentre beneath Mount Victoria high ground. Depth 11 km. Eastern slope aftershock activity expanding spatial distribution further.

title: M4.5 aftershock - Seaview reclaimed port area
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 4.5 earthquake at 07:46:27 NZST, located beneath the Seaview industrial and port zone on reclaimed land. Shallow depth of 6 km.

title: M4.7 aftershock - Rongotai Airport vicinity
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 4.7 earthquake at 07:45:44 NZST, epicentre near Rongotai Airport on reclaimed land. Depth 8 km. Airport operations already affected by primary shaking.

title: MetService alert: Earthquake strong motion recorded across all stations
description: Wellington MetService meteorological and seismic monitoring network confirmed widespread strong earthquake motion. Data shows peak ground accelerations exceeded 0.6g at central Wellington and harbour-side monitoring stations.

title: Miramar residents report building movement and cracking
description: Shaking in Miramar lasting 45 seconds. Residents noting cracks in older homes. Some chimneys damaged. Ground movement visibly affecting hillside properties.

title: Newtown area shaking - moderate damage emerging
description: Intense shaking reported in Newtown for 40+ seconds. Residents evacuating to streets. Some shopfronts damaged. Community gathering in parks and open areas.

title: Ngauranga Gorge - SH1 motorway assessment underway
description: State Highway 1 through Ngauranga Gorge being inspected for damage. Traffic diverted. Visible signs of ground movement noted.

title: Oriental Bay waterfront damage - seawall movement
description: Oriental Bay seawall showing signs of movement and damage. Beachfront buildings affected by ground movement. Water overtopping observed.

title: Porirua CBD experiencing earthquake shaking
description: Porirua CBD residents reporting strong shaking from mainshock. Moderate structural impacts in some buildings. Community gathering in open areas.

title: Power company confirms widespread outages
description: Wellington Electricity reporting major disruption across central and eastern suburbs. Multiple transformer failures. Restoration crews mobilising to priority areas.

title: Regional damage assessment protocol initiated
description: GeoNet and WREMO experts at 07:46:15 NZST initiated rapid damage assessment based on ground motion modelling. Priority areas for immediate field inspection identified: CBD, reclaimed land zones, and older masonry buildings.

title: Stuff reports major earthquake rattles Wellington
description: Magnitude 6.5 earthquake strikes Wellington Fault. Widespread reports of damage across capital region. Emergency services responding to multiple locations.

title: Te Aro - building evacuation orders being issued
description: Multiple building evacuation orders issued in Te Aro. Residents gathering in streets. Emergency services coordinating safe assembly areas.

title: Te Aro power infrastructure - major outages expanding
description: Additional power failures spreading across Te Aro and surrounding areas. Multiple substations affected. Restoration efforts beginning but widespread outages continuing.

title: Traffic gridlock - roads damaged and congested
description: Traffic on roads around earthquake damage zones in gridlock. Multiple accidents reported. Road closures preventing traffic flow.

title: Wellington Airport operations impacted - aftershocks ongoing
description: Airport authority reporting continued aftershocks affecting Rongotai. Runway assessment underway. Departures suspended pending safety evaluation.

title: Window glass - broken glass hazard throughout CBD
description: Extensive broken glass littering streets in CBD. Sharp debris hazard high. Pedestrians instructed to avoid downtown if possible.

title: Aftershock M4.2 recorded - sequence continuing
description: Seventh aftershock at 07:47. Magnitude 4.2. Location: central Wellington region. Aftershock activity remains elevated.

title: Aftershock M4.3 - moderate shaking continues
description: Sixth aftershock detected at 07:46. Magnitude 4.3. Wellington Harbour region. Aftershock sequence showing typical decay pattern but still active.

title: Aftershock sequence statistical analysis - Gutenberg-Richter law applied
description: GeoNet statistical analysis at 07:48:00 NZST projects approximately 50-70 aftershocks M4+, 500-700 M3+, and thousands of smaller events over next 48 hours. Probability of M5+ aftershock estimated at 30%.

title: Building triage - red stickers emerging
description: Damage assessment teams placing red stickers on unsafe buildings. Occupancy prohibited. Green stickers on safe structures. Assessment accelerating.

title: Communication challenges - phone networks overwhelmed
description: Mobile and landline networks experiencing overload from earthquake-related calls. Some areas experiencing difficulty reaching emergency services.

title: GeoNet seismic alert: Multiple aftershocks in sequence
description: GeoNet confirming earthquake sequence with aftershocks M4.5 and above. Strong shaking expected to continue. Public advised to expect further aftershocks.

title: Landslide potential - steep hillside areas at risk
description: Geotechnical hazards identified on steep slopes around Wellington. Potential for earthquake-triggered landslides. Hillside surveillance ongoing.

title: Lower Hutt liquefaction reports - ground stability concerns
description: Multiple reports from Lower Hutt CBD of ground subsidence and water/sand ejection. Reclaimed land areas particularly affected. Serious ground instability emerging.

title: M3.5 aftershock - Tawa residential area
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.5 aftershock at 07:48:15 NZST, epicentre beneath Tawa on northern outskirts. Depth 13 km. Sparse aftershock activity reaching outer suburban zones.

title: M3.6 aftershock - Courtenay Place theatre area
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.6 aftershock at 07:48:48 NZST, epicentre beneath Courtenay Place entertainment district. Depth 8 km. Arts and entertainment zone experiencing seismic activity.

title: M3.6 aftershock - Te Aro west zone
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.6 aftershock at 07:48:24 NZST, epicentre beneath western Te Aro zone. Depth 8 km. Reclaimed land area experiencing prolonged seismic unrest.

title: M3.7 aftershock - Hataitai coastal slope
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.7 aftershock at 07:48:38 NZST, epicentre beneath Hataitai coastal slope. Depth 8 km. Eastern suburban zone aftershock activity continues.

title: M3.7 aftershock - Johnsonville northern suburb
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.7 aftershock at 07:47:48 NZST, located beneath Johnsonville suburb. Depth 12 km. Aftershock zone now extending beyond central Wellington into outer suburbs.

title: M3.8 aftershock - Rongotai elevated platform
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.8 aftershock at 07:47:52 NZST, located beneath Rongotai Airport elevated platform. Depth 9 km. Airport vicinity experiencing sustained secondary activity.

title: M3.9 aftershock - Newtown residential area
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.9 aftershock at 07:48:04 NZST, located beneath Newtown. Depth 11 km showing deeper aftershock activity emerging as main rupture propagation completes.

title: M3.9 aftershock - Petone borough
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.9 aftershock at 07:48:09 NZST, located beneath Petone borough. Depth 7 km. Industrial zone continued aftershock activity.

title: M4.1 aftershock - Thorndon residential area
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 4.1 earthquake at 07:47:32 NZST, located beneath the Thorndon commercial and residential zone. Shallow depth of 8 km continuing strain release pattern.

title: M4.2 aftershock - Oriental Bay reclaimed strip
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.2 aftershock at 07:47:05 NZST, epicentre beneath the Oriental Bay reclaimed area. Depth 7 km. Continued aftershock sequence intensity decreasing slightly.

title: M4.6 aftershock - Hataitai suburb
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.6 aftershock at 07:47:58 NZST, epicentre beneath the Hataitai suburb. Depth 9 km. Lateral aftershock distribution expanding northeastward from main rupture.

title: Newsheet: Earthquake emergency declared in Wellington
description: Authorities declaring earthquake emergency for Wellington region. All services on high alert. Search and rescue mobilizing. Damage extent still being assessed.

title: Newtown Herald: Major earthquake shakes capital
description: Breaking: M6.5 earthquake strikes Wellington with strong aftershocks following. Multiple suburbs affected. Damage reports coming in from across region.

title: Ngaio residents - hillside area showing stability
description: Ngaio residents reporting moderate shaking but no significant damage. Hillside location providing protection. Community safe and accounted for.

title: Petone CBD experiencing power failure - transformers down
description: Complete power outage across Petone central business district. Multiple transformers damaged during earthquake. Residents in darkness. Shops closing.

title: Petone liquefaction zone - extensive ground damage
description: Extensive liquefaction effects in Petone. Reclaimed land areas subsiding. Sand/water ejection widespread. Roads becoming impassable.

title: Police - cordoning off damaged areas for safety
description: Police establishing cordons around heavily damaged areas. Public excluded from dangerous zones. Traffic control measures in place.

title: Public safety - avoid earthquake-damaged buildings
description: Authorities urging public to avoid damaged buildings. Risk of aftershock collapse. Structural assessment needed before re-entry.

title: Rail network disruption - KiwiRail suspending services
description: KiwiRail suspending all passenger and freight services. Track damage suspected. Detailed assessment underway before services resume.

title: Rongotai residents report runway subsidence concerns
description: Airport area residents noting potential ground settlement. Rongotai reclaimed land showing signs of movement. Airport Authority conducting urgent assessments.

title: Rupture geometry refined - Multiple segments identified
description: GeoNet detailed rupture analysis at 07:48:10 NZST indicates multi-segment rupture. At least three distinct rupture segments with variable slip distribution identified along Wellington Fault.

title: Seaview port area - industrial damage assessment
description: Port of Seaview/Gracefield reporting significant structural damage. Container stacks shifted. Infrastructure compromised. Port operations halted.

title: Te Aro CBD - multiple buildings showing damage
description: Post-earthquake inspection revealing significant structural damage in Te Aro. Building facades damaged. Windows extensively broken. Debris on streets.

title: Thorndon reports liquefaction signs - ground subsidence
description: Residents in Thorndon reporting ground subsidence and ejection of sand/water from reclaimed land near the quays. Pavement buckling observed.

title: Thorndon-Quays connection - traffic infrastructure damage
description: Connection between Thorndon and Quays showing damage to bridges and approach roads. Vehicles unable to cross. Detours being established.

title: Utility networks - water mains ruptures suspected
description: Wellington Water reporting possible water main ruptures in multiple locations. Pressure drops observed. Assessment teams deploying.

title: Waterfront promenade - structural damage to public areas
description: Wellington waterfront promenade showing significant damage. Paving compromised. Railings damaged. Public access restricted for safety.

title: Aftershock M3.8 detected - decreasing magnitude trend
description: Tenth aftershock M3.8 at 07:48:30. Aftershock magnitude decreasing as expected. Seismic sequence showing normal decay pattern.

title: Aftershock M4.0 detected - continuing sequence
description: Ninth aftershock at 07:48. Magnitude 4.0. Wellington region. Magnitude decreasing as typical for aftershock sequences.

title: Aftershock M4.1 detected - seismic activity ongoing
description: Eighth aftershock recorded at 07:47. Magnitude 4.1. Wellington Harbour region. Aftershock sequence expected to continue with decreasing magnitude.

title: Brooklyn and Mount Victoria - stable areas, minimal impact
description: Brooklyn and Mount Victoria residents reporting manageable shaking. Hillside areas showing resilience. Minor structural impacts reported.

title: Citywide lighting systems failing across Wellington metro area
description: Street lighting infrastructure reports cascading failures. Central city now pitch dark; emergency services navigating with vehicle lights.

title: Community resilience - spontaneous support networks forming
description: Residents spontaneously organizing support. Neighbors checking on each other. Informal support centers emerging in neighborhoods.

title: EARTHQUAKE: Magnitude 6.5 recorded on Wellington Fault
description: GeoNet seismograph network recorded a magnitude 6.5 earthquake centred near the Wellington Fault at 07:42 NZST. Preliminary depth estimate 12 km. Strong shaking reported across Wellington region.

title: Early aftershock swarm - GeoNet analysis underway
description: GeoNet analyzing aftershock sequence pattern. Multiple magnitude 4+ aftershocks recorded. Detailed analysis ongoing to assess sequence evolution.

title: Fenton Substation offline following ground shaking
description: Fenton Street substation has tripped offline due to seismic activity. Approximately 8,400 customers in central Wellington affected. Backup systems activating.

title: Gas leak potential - priority safety concern
description: Gas network operators checking for potential ruptures. Gas smell reported in some inner suburbs. Safety warnings being issued to residents.

title: Ground liquefaction observed, Petone waterfront
description: Resident reports severe ground surface deformation and sand boils near Jackson Street. Surface has subsided up to 15 cm in patches, water erupting from cracks.

title: Initial GeoNet analysis of mainshock parameters
description: GeoNet network determined mainshock magnitude approximately 6.5 on Wellington Fault. Location near Wellington Harbour approximately 8km depth.

title: Johnsonville area - minor to moderate shaking effects
description: Johnsonville residents reporting moderate shaking. Some structural cracking in older buildings. Residents checking properties for damage.

title: Karori area - minor shaking but limited damage
description: Karori residents reporting moderate shaking but minimal damage in hillside suburbs. Lower liquefaction risk in elevated areas. Most residents safe.

title: Liquefaction observed on Waterfront Drive near Frank Kitts Park
description: Ground fissures and sediment ejecta visible along the waterfront esplanade. Soil displacement causing pavement buckling over 50-metre stretch.

title: Liquefaction sand boils erupting in CBD forecourt area
description: Fountain-like venting of sand/water mix near Civic Square. 20cm diameter holes appearing at 10-metre intervals.

title: M3.4 aftershock - Miramar peninsula tip
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.4 aftershock at 07:49:18 NZST, epicentre beneath the southern Miramar peninsula. Depth 10 km. Southeastward expanding aftershock zone trend continues.

title: M3.4 aftershock - Willis Street corridor
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.4 aftershock at 07:50:04 NZST, located beneath Willis Street commercial corridor. Depth 9 km. Major business avenue continuing secondary tremor activity.

title: M3.5 aftershock - Newtown residential streets
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.5 aftershock at 07:49:52 NZST, located beneath Newtown residential district. Depth 11 km. Residential area experiencing deeper secondary seismic activity.

title: M3.6 aftershock - Porirua CBD northern area
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.6 aftershock at 07:49:22 NZST, located beneath Porirua CBD area. Depth 14 km. Aftershock distribution expanding northward along rupture trend.

title: M3.7 aftershock - Lower Hutt riverbed zone
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.7 aftershock at 07:49:35 NZST, epicentre beneath Lower Hutt riverbed zone. Depth 10 km. River corridor experiencing ground motion from deeper aftershock activity.

title: M3.8 aftershock - Brooklyn elevated area
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.8 aftershock at 07:49:07 NZST, located beneath Brooklyn on elevated terrain. Depth 13 km. Aftershock spatial extent now covering major north-south corridor.

title: M3.8 aftershock - Lambton quarter area
description: GeoNet seismic network recorded magnitude 3.8 aftershock at 07:49:41 NZST, located beneath Lambton quarter commercial zone. Depth 9 km. Continued business district seismic activity.

title: M4.0 aftershock - Karori hill area
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.0 aftershock at 07:48:41 NZST, epicentre beneath the high ground Karori area. Depth 12 km. Deeper aftershocks now dominating sequence as shallow rupture area stabilizes.

title: M4.3 aftershock - Wadestown high ground
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.3 aftershock at 07:49:13 NZST, epicentre beneath Wadestown residential area on western hills. Depth 10 km. Secondary rupture plane activity confirmed on western segment.

title: M5.2 aftershock detected near Wellington Harbour
description: GeoNet seismograph network recorded magnitude 5.2 aftershock centred near Wellington Harbour at 07:47. Second major tremor following mainshock.

title: Major building damage on Onslow Road, Newtown
description: Major damage to structure at Onslow Road now being documented. Occupants evacuated; no injuries reported at this time.

title: Major earthquake felt across Greater Wellington region
description: RNZ reports strong shaking lasting 30+ seconds felt from Porirua to Lower Hutt. Initial reports of residents evacuating buildings and moving to outdoor spaces.

title: Major power outage reported across Wellington CBD
description: Wellington Electricity telemetry indicates total loss of supply to central business district from Lambton Quay to Customhouse Quay. Grid monitoring shows transformer station offline.

title: Major water main rupture: Waring Taylor Street, CBD
description: Wellington Water SCADA detects catastrophic pressure loss on the main arterial line serving central Wellington. Multiple breaks suspected in Waring Taylor Street corridor due to liquefaction and ground deformation.

title: Miramar area showing liquefaction effects - reclaimed land
description: Reports of ground instability in Miramar reclaimed land areas. Ground subsidence and water ejection observed in several locations.

title: Moment tensor analysis confirms left-lateral strike-slip mechanism
description: GeoNet rapid moment tensor solution at 07:49:30 NZST confirms left-lateral strike-slip earthquake on N025E trending plane. Consistent with documented Wellington Fault geometry and kinematics.

title: News Update - earthquake impact assessment continues
description: RNZ continuing earthquake coverage. Damage across multiple suburbs confirmed. Aftershocks ongoing. Recovery efforts beginning.

title: SH1 Ngauranga Gorge: major subsidence, debris across northbound
description: Traffic camera detects 0.6m drop in pavement at SH1 north of Ngauranga Interchange. Rockfall debris scattered across 3 lanes. Northbound traffic halted for safety assessment.

title: SH1 Northbound blocked debris field near Ngauranga Gorge
description: Major rockfall across all three lanes north of Ngauranga Gorge; estimated 2–3 metres high. NZTA mobile units dispatched.

title: Schools closure - emergency protocols activated
description: Wellington schools immediately locking down post-earthquake. Children sheltering in place. Parents attempting to pick up children.

title: Seismic hazard assessment - Stress field redistribution modelled
description: GeoNet seismic hazard modelling at 07:49:45 NZST indicates stress field significantly altered by M6.5 rupture. Higher probability of adjacent fault activation. Wairarapa Fault stress load increased by 15 bars.

title: Structural collapse at Mein Street
description: Critical building damage on Mein Street. Fire and Emergency teams performing triage on site.

title: Tawa community - limited shaking impact
description: Tawa residents reporting light to moderate shaking. Damage appears minimal. Low-lying areas of Tawa showing more ground effects.

title: Tremor felt across Wellington region at 07:47
description: Residents in Wellington CBD and surrounding suburbs reported strong earthquake shaking. Immediate observations of ground movement across region.

title: Wadestown hillside - limited damage in elevated areas
description: Wadestown residents noting moderate shaking but minimal structural damage. Hillside elevation providing protection from worst effects.

title: Water main pressure drop on Lambton Quay reported
description: Wellington Water SCADA detected 40% pressure loss on Lambton Quay main line immediately after mainshock. Investigation team mobilised to CBD.

title: Waterfront area - structural damage to wharves
description: CentrePort reporting damage to port infrastructure. Wharf structures showing settlement and cracking. Container handling suspended. Berths inaccessible.

title: Wellington Scoop: Emergency response activated across capital
description: Multiple emergency services responding to earthquake damage. Fire stations mobilizing. Search and rescue teams on standby. Damage assessments underway.

title: Wellington Station - initial damage reports
description: First reports coming through of Wellington Station damage. Platform areas affected; rescue personnel being deployed to assist stranded passengers.

title: Wellington Station: Train detection systems offline
description: Wellington Station's automated train detection systems have lost power following the mainshock. Metlink has suspended all departures pending manual safety checks.

title: Wellington region transport: Emergency coordination activated
description: Wellington transport authorities have activated emergency coordination protocols following the mainshock. Preliminary damage assessment underway across all transport modes.

title: Building facade collapse, Lambton Quay
description: Multi-storey office building front facade partially collapsed on Lambton Quay near Whitmore Street. Debris scattered across footpath and roadway, two vehicles damaged.

title: CentrePort: Gantry crane immobilised on Quay 2
description: The main container gantry crane on Quay 2 has stopped mid-operation with electrical systems offline. No personnel reported on the crane at time of impact.

title: Constable Street Newtown - shop front destroyed, looks super dangerous
description: Eyewitness reports significant building collapse on Constable Street. Residents fleeing area; emergency services en route.

title: Gas leak emergency at Courtenay Place
description: Strong gas odour detected at Courtenay Place. Fire and Emergency teams isolating area; utility company contacted for rupture assessment.

title: Gas main rupture suspected on High Street
description: Strong gas odour detected at High Street. Fire and Emergency teams isolating area; utility company contacted for rupture assessment.

title: GeoNet confirms 6.5 magnitude mainshock on Wellington Fault
description: Preliminary analysis confirms magnitude 6.5 event with epicentre at -41.28, 174.76, depth 12 km. Significant shaking expected across greater Wellington and lower North Island.

title: Hutt Valley main distribution line damaged
description: Primary 110kV line serving Lower Hutt and Seaview showing zero voltage. Believed caused by transformer pole failure near State Highway 2.

title: Media: SH1 Ngauranga partially blocked; northbound traffic backing up
description: 1News confirms major incident at Gorge with significant debris obstruction. Drivers urged to avoid peak commute routes.

title: Petone platform collapse - Hutt Line terminus affected
description: Petone station platform deck partially collapsed. Emergency personnel evacuating stranded commuters; rail service indefinitely suspended on Hutt Line south section.

title: Power cuts in central Wellington, multiple suburbs affected
description: Stuff reports widespread power outages in CBD, Thorndon, and Quays areas. Wellington Electricity crews being dispatched to assess damage on distribution lines.

title: Quays area liquefaction with surface subsidence
description: Waterfront road shows 15cm differential settlement near Queens Wharf; traffic diversions in effect. WCC cordoning off unsafe stretches.

title: SH1 Ngauranga south: first reports of rockfall, traffic cautious
description: Multiple callers report rocks on southbound SH1 south of Ngauranga Interchange. Traffic slowing, minor delays. NZTA investigating.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley: liquefaction settlement, surface rupture at intersection
description: SH2 southbound at Vogel Street, Lower Hutt showing visible ground fracture and 0.4m subsidence. Wellington Water crew notified for potential underground services damage.

title: Sand and silt ejection reported on Clyde Quay Road
description: Residents reporting fountaining of sandy material across roadway. Liquefaction causing surface settlement beneath parked vehicles.

title: Sand vents appearing throughout Petone streets
description: Community member documents multiple sand boil vents along Jackson Street commercial area, spouting fine sand and water up to 1 metre high.

title: Strong earthquake felt across entire Wellington region
description: Residents across Wellington CBD, Thorndon, and surrounding suburbs reported sustained strong shaking lasting 20-30 seconds. Aftershock intensity felt region-wide.

title: Te Aro flooding confirmed on Taranaki Street
description: First media reports of street flooding on Taranaki Street near Vivian Street. Pedestrians wading through water. Estimated depths 600mm in low areas.

title: Te Aro residents report falling merchandise from storefronts
description: Retail workers in Lambton Quay area report display shelves toppling, window breakage, and scattered goods. One minor injury from falling display unit.

title: Thorndon powerlines down, multiple poles damaged
description: Resident reports Thorndon Quay experiencing blackout; utility poles snapped near the motorway on-ramp. Fire and Emergency NZ dispatched for hazard assessment.

title: Water geyser erupts at intersection of Willis and Grey Streets
description: Resident reports violent water spray from ground at Willis/Grey intersection. Main road flooding rapidly, water flowing into shop fronts.

title: Water main rupture, Thorndon Quays
description: Large water ejection reported at intersection of Lambton Quay and Hunter Street. Wellington Water monitoring pressure loss in eastern CBD network.

title: Water pressure critical at Thorndon library facilities
description: Thorndon public buildings reporting dangerous pressure drop. Water supply inadequate for sanitation.

title: Water pressure drops 40% across central zone
description: SCADA monitoring shows rapid 40% pressure reduction in CBD supply zone. Multiple sensor nodes reporting anomalies in Lambton Quay and Willis Street areas. Critical infrastructure at risk.

title: Waterfront evacuation: residents fleeing buildings near reclaimed land
description: Newshub reports immediate evacuation of waterfront office and residential buildings. Concerns about liquefaction hazard on reclaimed foundations.

title: Wellington CBD gas infrastructure damage
description: Multiple gas metre boxes showing visible damage on Lambton Quay and Willis Street. Odour detected; Fire and Emergency en route to assess network integrity.

title: Wellington Station roof collapse reported
description: GeoNet confirmation: roof sections collapsed in platform areas at Wellington Station. Structural engineers dispatched; evacuation ongoing.

title: Wellington Water SCADA systems detecting seismic activity
description: Wellington Water monitoring systems recorded multiple seismic pulses in water infrastructure zone. Aftershock sequence detected by sensor network.

title: Widespread voltage fluctuations detected across network
description: Wellington Electricity grid sensors detect unstable power distribution in remaining online sections. Automated protection systems triggering to prevent cascade failures.

title: Aftershock M5.1 recorded southeast of Wellington CBD
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 5.1 aftershock at -41.32, 174.82, depth 8 km. Second major shock felt across eastern suburbs and airport area.

title: Alan reports violent shaking lasting 30+ seconds
description: Experienced prolonged violent shaking in Wellington CBD. Items falling from shelves and furniture moving noticeably during strong aftershock.

title: Building facade collapse risk, Waterfront CBD
description: Facade cracks and partial spalling observed on heritage building on Customhouse Quay. Liquefaction-induced ground settlement creating differential structural stress.

title: CBD transformer station fire risk assessment underway
description: Wellington Electricity emergency crews assessing fire risk at damaged transformer stations. Water supply and fire suppression systems being checked.

title: CentrePort: initial damage assessment report
description: Port Authority confirms significant structural damage to cargo handling infrastructure. All operations suspended; detailed assessment ongoing over next 2 hours.

title: Concrete cracking at CBD car park Level 3
description: Multiple cracks appearing in concrete deck and support columns at multi-storey car park on Boulcott Street. Structural failure risk; immediate closure announced.

title: False report: Tsunami wave damage at Waterfront
description: Social media reports of large wave causing flooding at Waterfront precinct. WREMO confirms water main rupture only, no tsunami risk. Alert from GeoNet confirms no tsunami generated.

title: Flooding reported on Lambton Quay
description: Community reports 2-metre geyser of water erupting from a main rupture point on Lambton Quay near BNZ Tower. Water flooding into building basements and ground-level premises.

title: Ground deformation in Petone industrial area near Seaview Road
description: Manufacturing facility reporting structural foundation damage. Perimeter fencing collapsed, ground visibly sunken near loading bay.

title: Hospital emergency departments overwhelmed with calls
description: Newshub reports Capital and Coast DHB receiving increased 111 calls for injuries. Triage staff being called in and surge protocols activated.

title: Hutt CBD: initial debris on main street, NZTA investigating
description: Early reports of street surface damage on main Hutt Valley corridor. NZTA dispatching inspection team.

title: Hutt Line express emergency stop - passengers safe
description: Metlink express train executed emergency stop near Thorndon. All passengers accounted for; evacuation assistance being organized.

title: Karori: hillslip blocks Karori Road, residential area isolated
description: Approximately 150m3 of clay and vegetation has slipped across Karori Road near the Jarden Botanic Garden entrance. Three households cut off from exit routes. GeoNet monitoring ongoing.

title: Liquefaction field emerging in Miramar industrial zone
description: Industrial area workers observe sand ejection and ground subsidence near warehouse district. Pavement surface displacement visible at several locations.

title: Liquefaction hazard emerging on lower Queen Street
description: Early reports from Wellington Scoop of ground cracking and lateral spread observed near reclaimed land waterfront, with sand boils visible near Quay Street parklands.

title: M4.9 aftershock recorded near Te Aro
description: Wellington Water SCADA sensors and GeoNet seismographs detected magnitude 4.9 aftershock. Epicentre approximately 2km south-southeast of initial mainshock.

title: Metlink all rail services: Suspended pending assessment
description: Metlink has suspended all rail services region-wide as a precautionary measure while track conditions are assessed following the mainshock.

title: Multiple pressure anomalies detected across Thorndon zone
description: Wellington Water SCADA shows simultaneous low-pressure readings on three mains feeding Thorndon area. Liquefaction settlement suspected.

title: Multiple substations tripping across eastern suburbs
description: Automatic protection systems have isolated substations at Miramar, Rongotai, and Kilbirnie simultaneously due to fault detection. Cascading outage mode initiated.

title: Parking building structural damage reported
description: Multi-level parking structure near Civic Square showing significant settlement on Level 2. Concrete cracking visible, vehicles partially trapped.

title: SCADA alert: pressure spike-drop cycle, Petone-Seaview line
description: Wellington Water bulk water pipeline shows transient pressure surge followed by 40% drop. Likely multiple rupture points on reclaimed ground corridor.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley near Taita subsiding; eastbound lane impassable
description: Pavement cracking and slumping over 60m section. Emergency lane narrowed. Traffic queuing back 2 km.

title: Seaview port district: industrial road surface cracked; partial closure
description: Liquefaction causing differential settlement across Jackson Street. Heavy vehicles diverted. Port operations slowed.

title: Te Aro district without power, emergency services responding
description: Complete outage reported from Taranaki Street to Vivian Street. Wellington Electricity crews en route to inspect distribution network for damage.

title: Water supply disruption affecting CBD hotels
description: Multiple hotels in CBD reporting no water pressure. Emergency water distribution needed for guest facilities.

title: Widespread liquefaction in Seaview industrial zone
description: Multiple reports of boil formations and sediment spreading across industrial properties. Port-side warehouse experiencing ground subsidence.

title: Aftershock 5.2M recorded by GeoNet
description: RNZ reports significant aftershock felt across region. No new immediate damage reported but residents advised to stay alert for further quakes.

title: Aftershock magnitude 4.6 felt from Petone to Miramar
description: GeoNet network detected M4.6 aftershock at 07:50. Shaking felt across Hutt Valley and eastern suburbs. Multiple community reports of continued ground movement.

title: Broadway intersection CBD partially blocked by facade debris
description: Building cladding fell from corner of Lambton Quay; fragments scattered across road. Pedestrian hazard. WCC cordoning in progress.

title: Brooklyn: slip on Highland Park Road, traffic diverted
description: Highland Park Road between Wellington Botanic Gardens access and Salamanca Road blocked by hillslip. Debris pile 2-3m high. NZTA coordinating diversion via Wadestown Terrace.

title: Building damage reported in Te Aro precinct
description: 1News reports façade damage on Ghuznee Street with debris falling to street level. One minor injury reported. Building evacuation underway.

title: Building damage widespread in Te Aro: Courtenay Place severely impacted
description: Building damage emerging across Te Aro, with Courtenay Place particularly affected. Rescue operations beginning.

title: Building structural failure reported on Panama Street
description: Confirmed structural damage to building at Panama Street. Fire and Emergency teams establishing cordon; preliminary rescue assessment in progress.

title: CBD interior partition collapse reported
description: Multiple drywall partition walls collapsed inside office building on Willis Street, blocking internal corridors. Occupants re-routing to alternative exits.

title: Civic Centre access closed - building damage
description: Major Wellington icon Civic Centre showing facade damage and internal structural concerns. Public access suspended; engineering team mobilized.

title: Darkened streets across Petone and Seaview
description: Resident reports complete blackout from Petone through Gracefield and Seaview industrial area. Street lights out, traffic signals dark.

title: Gas leak reported near Willis Street
description: Strong gas smell detected outside commercial building on Willis Street between Boulcott and Customhouse Quay. Fire and Emergency responding to evacuate nearby offices.

title: GeoNet: Strong ground motion recorded across monitoring network
description: Preliminary analysis of seismic waveforms indicates rupture on Wellington Fault extending from CBD northward toward Thorndon. Maximum shaking in waterfront areas.

title: Ground instability Courtenay Place
description: Liquefied zone spreading near Courtenay Place, buildings cracking, power lines down from ground movement.

title: Hataitai: subsidence reported on main street, water visible
description: Hataitai main street (Hataitai Road) showing visible subsidence near local shops. Unverified reports of water spray. Fire and Emergency NZ on standby.

title: Hospital backup power activated at Wellington Regional
description: Wellington Regional Hospital has switched to generator power following main grid loss. Emergency department and ICU on backup systems. Diesel reserves sufficient.

title: Hutt Valley Line: Service suspended north of Wellington Station
description: KiwiRail has issued an immediate suspension of all Hutt Valley Line services pending track inspection between Wellington and Taita stations.

title: Intense shaking reported across Thorndon, Quays, and CBD
description: Multiple residents on The Terrace and Lambton Quay report violent shaking lasting 30+ seconds. Building contents displaced, minor structural cracks visible in heritage buildings.

title: Liquefaction & building tilt, Kilbirnie industrial zone
description: Multiple reports of warehouse subsidence and tilting on Shelly Bay Road. Visible sand/silt venting from ground, vehicles stranded in liquefied areas.

title: Liquefaction causing subsidence on Quay Street near Civic Square
description: Multiple ground fissures observed beneath heritage buildings. CBD waterfront showing clear evidence of differential settlement.

title: Liquefaction risk zone expanding: ground subsidence visible
description: 1News reports visible ground cracks and subsidence on Customhouse Quay. Residents being urged to vacate low-lying reclaimed land zones.

title: Liquefaction-induced main displacement on Lambton
description: Ground subsidence of 150mm documented on Lambton Quay. Water main laterally displaced 200mm from original alignment. Shearing stress causing circumferential breaks in pipe.

title: Major building damage on Riddiford Street, Newtown
description: Severe structural failure at Riddiford Street. Emergency cordons expanding; safety assessment beginning.

title: Metlink suspends all train services effective immediately
description: Metlink halts all rail operations across Wellington metropolitan network pending infrastructure assessment. All passengers evacuated from stopped trains.

title: Mike reports violent shaking in Petone
description: Strong aftershock threw items off shelves in Petone homes. Shaking felt for approximately 25 seconds with moderate to strong intensity.

title: Ngauranga: early reports of blockages, NZTA mobilizing
description: Initial caller reports of road blockage on SH1 Ngauranga. NZTA dispatching response teams.

title: Petone Seaview connector road: subsidence affecting port traffic flow
description: Liquefaction on low-lying connector road causing 20cm differential. Large vehicles restricted. Port access slowed.

title: Petone beachfront dark, waterfront facilities affected
description: Residents report all lights out from Jackson Street to Raroa Road. Industrial area and port facilities losing power simultaneously, affecting automated systems.

title: Petone street flooding from burst water main
description: High Street Petone experiencing street flooding. Water depth estimated 0.6 metres and rising.

title: Strong shaking reported from Thorndon to Rongotai
description: Residents across multiple suburbs reported strong to severe shaking. Aftershock felt region-wide from northern to southern Wellington suburbs.

title: Structural damage to commercial building on Lambton Quay
description: Facade cracking and interior damage reported in mid-rise office building. Building management initiating emergency evacuation protocols.

title: Surface rupture Willis Street
description: Severe ground instability reported around Willis Street, multiple properties sinking, pavements buckling.

title: Thorndon Quay electrical infrastructure damage extensive
description: Field teams report multiple transformer banks offline on Lambton Quay. Replacement units being mobilized from regional storage.

title: Water distribution failure, Seaview main supply
description: Seaview industrial zone reports complete water supply loss. Liquefaction has broken main supply line near Gracefield warehouse district.

title: Water flowing from ground near Civic Square car park
description: Eyewitness reports sustained water eruption from beneath Civic Square surface. Ground subsidence visible, water accumulating in parking level.

title: Water main break at Exhibition Centre precinct
description: Exhibition Centre area experiencing significant water loss. Underground main fractured near loading docks.

title: Water main failure: Grey Street, Te Aro
description: Major rupture confirmed on Grey Street between Cuba and Dixon Streets. Significant flooding of commercial premises. Wellington Water crews mobilizing to the location.

title: Aftershock M4.8 felt across greater Wellington region
description: Third significant aftershock, magnitude 4.8, recorded at -41.29, 174.79, depth 10 km. Continued strong ground motion in downtown Wellington.

title: Beach evacuation ordered in Oriental Bay
description: Stuff reports WCC and Fire and Emergency NZ issuing evacuation directive for Oriental Bay beach due to liquefaction risk and potential tsunami. Residents moving inland.

title: CentrePort confirms port closure
description: CentrePort official statement: All port operations halted immediately following seismic event. Vessels anchoring in designated areas; cargo operations suspended.

title: Community: Mount Victoria area residents report fence damage from settling
description: Several properties on southern slopes with retaining walls and fences showing minor cracks. No road access threatened.

title: Engineers checking that cracked building on Margaret Street now
description: Eyewitness at Margaret Street describes engineers measuring structural cracks; official assessment underway.

title: GeoNet rapid assessment: High risk of building damage in low-rise areas
description: Preliminary seismic intensity estimates indicate VIII-IX (Very Strong to Violent) shaking in Wellington CBD and reclaimed land areas. Expect significant building impacts.

title: Ground surface ruptures, Kilbirnie-Rongotai
description: Photos from social media show 15-20 cm wide ground ruptures forming pattern across isthmus. Some ruptures ejecting water and sediment.

title: Hospital water supply interrupted at Wellington Central
description: Wellington Hospital reports complete loss of water pressure. Emergency tank activation initiated. Water for firefighting and medical operations compromised.

title: Island Bay: Mahina Parade subsidence, residents leaving homes
description: Island Bay residents reporting ground settling on Mahina Parade area. Some residents voluntarily evacuating. WCC dispatch confirming details.

title: Liquefaction at Kilbirnie intersection causing road collapse
description: Ground collapse at intersection of Kereru Road and Tasman Street. Soil deformation creating depression in roadway, vehicle stuck in sinkhole-like formation.

title: Lower Hutt CBD electrical grid failure imminent
description: Wellington Electricity reports cascading failures through Hutt Valley distribution. Main substation protection systems activated, isolating multiple zones.

title: Lower Hutt power grid failing, eastern suburbs darkened
description: Wellington Scoop reports cascading power failures in Seaview, Gracefield, and Lower Hutt CBD. Outage now affecting estimated 15,000 households.

title: M4.7 aftershock near Thorndon/Quays detected
description: Seismograph network recorded magnitude 4.7 aftershock. Located near reclaimed land zone. Continued aftershock activity following M6.5 mainshock.

title: Miramar Cutting: rock collapse blocks eastbound, SH2 backed up
description: Miramar Cutting section of SH2 (westbound off-ramp) has collapsed. Large boulders blocking lanes. Emergency services establishing exclusion zone. Eastbound traffic redirected via Evans Bay.

title: Miramar cutting landslide: SH2 southbound one lane passable
description: Slope instability near Miramar loop with debris on shoulder and one travel lane. Traffic controller deployed. Speed restriction 40 km/h.

title: Newtown: early street damage reports, specifics unclear
description: Unconfirmed reports of street damage in Newtown Main Street area. WCC investigating.

title: No power in my building - whole block dark
description: Lambton Quay appears completely blacked out. Can see lights out across multiple blocks facing the harbour.

title: Oh my god Dixon Street is a disaster, building front just came down
description: Residents posting video of damage at Dixon Street; structural failure clearly visible. Community assistance efforts starting.

title: Outage affecting eastern suburbs network
description: Island Bay, Hataitai, and Newtown experiencing total power loss. School buildings and residential areas dark. Estimated 6,200 customers down.

title: Petone rail branch: Track integrity unknown, services halted
description: The Petone rail branch line has been immediately halted. Track integrity assessment is underway given the liquefaction risk in reclaimed land areas.

title: Safety evaluation: Taranaki Street structures
description: Structural survey underway on Taranaki Street following preliminary damage indication. Formal assessment report to follow.

title: Sand boils erupting along Quay Quay precinct waterfront
description: Visible sediment fountains across waterfront promenade. Ground liquefaction creating ejection patterns over 30-metre zone.

title: Significant ground fissures reported, CBD Waterfront
description: Photos emerging on social media show 20-30 cm wide ground cracks along the waterfront promenade between Frank Kitts Park and the Civic Centre.

title: Supply failure to oriental Bay area
description: Pressure sensors indicate complete supply loss to Oriental Bay precinct. Residential customers report no water in taps. Multiple main breaks suspected.

title: Te Aro apartment block cracked, residents evacuated
description: Significant vertical cracks observed through multiple floors of apartment building on Kent Terrace. Residents self-evacuating; no structural support failure yet but building condemned pending assessment.

title: Te Aro building showing foundation heave
description: Ground-level flooding visible as building foundation lifted from soil subsidence reversal and liquefaction effects. WCC engineers responding for rapid assessment.

title: Thorndon residential buildings losing heating systems
description: Apartment residents losing HVAC systems in winter conditions. Emergency support lines flooded with calls; Wellington Electricity prioritizing warmth concerns.

title: Thorndon underground cables damaged, repair timeline extended
description: Wellington Electricity reports underground distribution cables damaged beneath Thorndon. Replacement units not immediately available; temporary overhead routing being planned.

title: Todman Street Newtown - shop front destroyed, looks super dangerous
description: Multiple social media posts showing severe damage to structure at Todman Street. Community describing loud crashing sounds.

title: Traffic cameras on SH1 detecting seismic movement
description: NZTA traffic monitoring sensors detected significant ground acceleration near Ngauranga Gorge. Aftershock caused roadway oscillation.

title: Train stuck between Thorndon and CBD with 180 passengers
description: Hutt Line express halted south of Thorndon station, passengers sheltering in place. Reports of minor injuries from emergency stop.

title: Trains derailed near Southgate shopping complex
description: Multiple train cars tilted on tracks near Melling Junction. Passengers evacuating with assistance; no initial reports of serious injuries from derailment.

title: Water main break at Vivian Street affecting business
description: Vivian Street businesses without water supply. Main rupture beneath street causing service interruption.

title: Water main rupture causing flooding on Customhouse Quay
description: Major water main break erupting through ground near Customhouse Quay. Water spray reaching nearby storefronts, street flooding expanding.

title: Water main rupture detected by SCADA on Hutt feeder
description: SCADA sensors show immediate 50% flow reduction on Hutt Valley feeder main. Rupture in progress.

title: Water main rupture: Ghuznee Street CBD
description: Residents report water eruption from street surface on Ghuznee Street between Willis and Wakefield. Water flowing into Civic Centre basement areas. Evacuation of lower floors initiated.

title: Wellington Airport runway: Emergency closure declared
description: Rongotai Airport has closed both runways for immediate damage assessment. Ground fissuring observed in taxiway delta. No aircraft are permitted to land or depart.

title: Aftershock M4.9 reported east of Rongotai Airport
description: Significant aftershock magnitude 4.9 at -41.33, 174.81, depth 7 km. Fourth major aftershock in rapid succession.

title: Building damage on Willis Street CBD affecting structural integrity
description: Mid-storey commercial building sustaining wall cracking. Damage pattern consistent with foundation settlement from liquefaction.

title: Building structural failure reported on The Terrace
description: Building at The Terrace, Wellington CBD/Waterfront has sustained major structural damage. Damage assessment and occupant safety verification underway.

title: Building structural failure reported on Todman Street
description: Fire and Emergency responding to building damage report on Todman Street. Preliminary assessment indicates severe structural compromise.

title: CBD businesses evacuating staff, fire alarms activated
description: NZ Herald reporting multiple office evacuations on Lambton Quay and Grey Street. Fire and Emergency call-outs increasing significantly across CBD zone.

title: CentrePort container cranes inoperable, 6 damaged
description: All 6 mobile cranes at CentrePort Seaview knocked offline. Container stacks shifted; risk of collapse assessed. Port halts all cargo operations.

title: CentrePort general cargo: Operations halted immediately
description: All CentrePort general cargo operations have been halted immediately. Vessels are being advised to divert to alternative ports; equipment being secured.

title: Community report: Karori cul-de-sac blocked by soil slip
description: Resident on Ballantrae Road reports 1.5m deep debris pile blocking driveway. Hill slope above unstable. Neighbours evacuating.

title: Container terminal Berth 4 structure compromised
description: Structural engineering assessment indicates Berth 4 container terminal has foundation settlement. Berthing operations suspended; repair timeline uncertain.

title: Cracked water mains on Taranaki Street causing flooding
description: Newshub reports Wellington Water responding to multiple water main ruptures in CBD. Street-level flooding observed near Parliament grounds with water pooling on asphalt.

title: Early injury reports: Falls and crush injuries being treated
description: RNZ reports first injury surge at hospitals. Multiple crush injuries from fallen objects and falls during building evacuations.

title: Emergency report: water system pressure loss across CBD network
description: Wellington Water confirms major pressure drops affecting Thorndon, Te Aro, and CBD supply. Multiple ruptures suspected on aged steel main.

title: Engineering assessment underway at Adelaide Road
description: Council engineers on-site at Adelaide Road conducting emergency safety evaluation. Building status unknown pending findings.

title: Flooding near Petone Railway Station
description: Water accumulating near railway platforms. Main rupture causing water to flow across station forecourt.

title: GeoNet issues preliminary earthquake assessment
description: GeoNet released preliminary analysis confirming significant earthquake with high potential for damage in reclaimed land areas. Multiple aftershocks expected.

title: Heritage building structural failure risk - Customhouse Quay
description: Historic stone building on Customhouse Quay showing severe masonry damage and roof instability. WCC structural engineer on-site assessing collapse risk; immediate cordoning required.

title: Kaiwharawhara Station platform: Structural damage reported
description: Metlink crews have observed concrete cracking along Platform 2 at Kaiwharawhara Station. Station access is restricted while engineers conduct detailed safety assessment.

title: Karori hill: active creep threatening second residential access
description: Monitoring crew reports continuation of slope movement behind Ballantrae area. Risk assessment underway for secondary failure.

title: Lower Hutt water main ruptures on Main Street
description: Multiple reports of 1.5-metre ground subsidence and water main failure on Main Street, Hutt Central. Businesses reporting flooding and zero water pressure. Engineering assessment underway.

title: M5.0 aftershock magnitude detected near harbour
description: Magnitude 5.0 aftershock recorded by GeoNet network at 07:52. Second significant tremor since mainshock. Located near Wellington Harbour zone.

title: Miramar peninsula completely dark
description: All lights out in Miramar and Seatoun from Oriental Bay through to Mahina Bay. Approximately 3,400 residents affected.

title: Miramar residential area experiencing ground settlement
description: Multiple householders reporting cracked foundations and tilted structures. Ground surface visibly depressed in several properties along Miramar Avenue.

title: Newtown house concrete foundation cracking
description: Residential house on Constable Street displaying foundation cracking with doors and windows misaligning. Soil subsidence suspected from liquefaction.

title: Petone main road flooded from ruptured water main
description: Jackson Street Petone experiencing significant street flooding. Large water plume visible from road rupture affecting traffic flow.

title: Petone waterfront reports extreme ground movement and liquefaction
description: Residents near Petone foreshore observe lateral ground displacement, sand boils, and ground subsidence in the reclaimed land area near The Esplanade.

title: Petone: massive liquefaction hummocks, Jackson Street impassable
description: Seaview/Gracefield reclaimed area experiencing severe liquefaction. Jackson Street (main access) has buckled with up to 0.8m displacement. Fire and Emergency NZ setting up alternate rescue routes.

title: Quay Street CBD: pavement rupture, pedestrian safety risk
description: Quay Street (waterfront, between Grey and Customhouse Quays) has cracked and buckled. Multiple access points closed. CBD evacuation proceeding, some trapped on east side.

title: SCADA pressure cascade failure detected
description: Wellington Water SCADA identifies rapid pressure drop sequence moving through distribution network. Secondary ruptures triggered by sudden depressurization. Network stability at risk.

title: Safety evaluation: Adelaide Road structures
description: Structural engineers deployed to Adelaide Road. Initial findings indicate significant damage requiring formal assessment.

title: Sarah reports earthquake felt in Oriental Bay
description: Felt strong shaking in Oriental Bay area. Ground motion noticeable and unnerving. Shaking lasted approximately 30 seconds with variable intensity.

title: Seaview industrial zone power loss, container cranes offline
description: CentrePort Wellington reports complete electrical shutdown affecting cargo operations. Automated systems and lighting down; crews standing by for manual intervention.

title: Te Aro Taranaki Street completely without power
description: Residents report zero lighting on entire street from post office to museum. Shops and residential buildings affected equally; emergency protocols active.

title: Te Aro building exterior trim detaching
description: Exterior trim, fascia, and cladding elements detaching from residential building near Kent Terrace. Minor debris falling; street below monitored.

title: Thorndon building residents evacuated
description: Fire and Emergency NZ evacuating residents from Grey Street office building after liquefaction-induced tilt and cracking observed.

title: Thorndon flat liquefaction around State House area
description: Historic flat showing prominent ground fissuring and sand boils emerging. Significant subsidence visible beneath heritage structures.
