title: Traffic signals offline across Wellington network
description: Signal control systems failed at major intersections including Civic Square, Kaindy, and Tory Street due to power loss. NZTA and WCC coordinating traffic management.

title: Aftershock M4.4 recorded in lower Hutt region
description: GeoNet seismographs detected magnitude 4.4 aftershock at 07:53. Epicentre near Lower Hutt industrial zone. Follow-up to earlier strong aftershocks.

title: Airport operational: Runway inspections underway
description: Wellington Airport confirms operational status. Ground crews conducting rapid visual inspection of runways. No aircraft operations temporarily halted pending all-clear.

title: Building damage widespread in Newtown: Adelaide Road severely impacted
description: Early reports from Adelaide Road indicate severe building damage. Emergency services responding to multiple reports of structural failure in Newtown.

title: Building damage widespread in Wellington CBD/Waterfront: Whitmore Street severely impacted
description: Building damage emerging across Wellington CBD/Waterfront, with Whitmore Street particularly affected. Rescue operations beginning.

title: Building structural failure on Eva Street, Rongotai
description: Commercial building wall partially collapsed following liquefaction beneath foundations. Debris field extending into carpark area.

title: CBD building crane rigging failure risk
description: Construction crane on building site at Customhouse Quay showing instability with boom sway. Site contractor securing crane; work suspended.

title: CBD communication networks degraded due to power loss
description: Cell tower backups deploying; telecommunications companies mobilizing emergency communications support. Internet services severely limited across central city.

title: CentrePort operational status: Emergency standdown initiated
description: CentrePort has initiated an emergency standdown of all port operations except critical safety functions. All vessel movements, cargo handling, and vehicle traffic suspended.

title: Customhouse Quay partially impassable - building damage
description: Historic Customhouse building facade damaged; debris falling on Customhouse Quay. Street access restricted; pedestrian safety barrier installed.

title: First injury toll: 12 confirmed casualties in central area
description: RNZ reports updated injury count as hospitals receive initial patient surge. Capital and Coast DHB confirming 12 patients with varying severity from falls and structural damage.

title: First professional aerial assessment released
description: Early helicopter survey documents extent of liquefaction across Petone, Seaview, and Waterfront zones. Video shows widespread cracking and subsidence.

title: Flooding in Petone residential area spreading
description: Water from ruptured main flooding into residential properties. Evacuation precautions being discussed.

title: Flooding in Wellington police headquarters basement
description: Police headquarters basement flooded from main rupture. Critical infrastructure systems potentially affected.

title: Gas emergency at Mein Street, Newtown
description: Strong gas odour detected at Mein Street. Fire and Emergency teams isolating area; utility company contacted for rupture assessment.

title: Ground fissures visible on Customhouse Quay near Museum
description: Extended ground cracking visible beneath historic waterfront precinct. Museum area showing liquefaction surface manifestations.

title: Ground subsidence blocking Seaview Parade, Seaview
description: Resident video shows Seaview Parade section near industrial area has dropped 30+ cm, creating impassable ramp. Underground pipes visibly ruptured.

title: James reports earthquake shaking in Kilbirnie
description: Felt prolonged shaking in Kilbirnie. Items shifted on shelves and furniture moved during tremor. Aftershock lasted around 20 seconds.

title: Kilbirnie Avenue flooding spreading to suburbs
description: Water from main rupture spreading to residential properties on side streets off Kilbirnie Avenue. Garden flooding and driveway inundation reported across 200-metre zone.

title: Kilbirnie residential blackout, traffic lights inoperative
description: Karangi Street residents report sudden power loss. Traffic signals on Evans Bay Parade non-functional; safety concern at major intersection.

title: Multiple building cracks observed in CBD
description: Office building on Cuba Street reporting extensive concrete cracking throughout upper floors and glass facade damage. Elevator systems offline, staff evacuating via stairs.

title: Newtown: ground fissures near Main Street, emergency response
description: Newtown Main Street area showing ground cracks. Local businesses reporting damage and staff injuries. Fire and Emergency dispatched to assess.

title: Office building window failure on Featherston Street
description: Large section of multi-storey office building windows blown out on Featherston Street. Glass falling onto pavement; pedestrian traffic diverted by Fire and Emergency.

title: Partial blackout in Thorndon and northern CBD
description: Some streets have light, others completely dark. Seems to be rotating outages or partial network sections back online.

title: Petone port district flooding from main rupture
description: SCADA detects major pressure loss in Petone distribution zone. Community reports water geyser near the intersection of Jackson Street and Ngauranga Road. Surrounding warehouses and industrial premises flooded.

title: Petone waterfront liquefaction visible, ground fissures opening
description: 1News correspondents on scene reporting lateral spread and fissures near railway line in Petone. Ground displacement estimated at 15-20cm in some locations.

title: Pressure cascade failure detected in Hutt Valley feeder main
description: Wellington Water SCADA detected rupture sequence on Hutt Valley water main. Pressure dropping to critical levels at Lower Hutt CBD.

title: Rongotai ground deformation extent
description: Aerial survey shows subsidence patterns indicating 20-40 cm drops in areas around Rongotai. Building foundations showing signs of differential settlement.

title: SH1 Split Rock: debris cone, multiple lanes blocked
description: North of Split Rock, SH1 north-bound has multiple rockfall deposits across all lanes. Traffic stopped. NZTA Heavy Haulage unit en route for debris clearance.

title: SH1 major corridor disrupted - traffic major delays
description: State Highway 1 experiencing major disruptions in multiple locations. Ngauranga Gorge closed; traffic diverted; average delays 45+ minutes.

title: SH1 northbound closed at Ngauranga Gorge - debris
description: State Highway 1 blocked by rockfall and debris from Ngauranga Gorge. NZTA traffic management establishing detours via back roads.

title: SH1 southbound Ngauranga: partial closure for debris clearance
description: Rock fragments and dust blocking 2 of 3 lanes. Southbound traffic merging to shoulder. Emergency digger en route.

title: State Highway 2 Petone: Surface cracking and settlement
description: NZTA traffic cameras show significant surface subsidence on SH2 northbound near the Petone interchange. One lane is impassable; traffic diverted to local roads.

title: Strong aftershock felt across Hutt Valley region
description: Residents in Lower Hutt, Petone and surrounding areas reported strong shaking. Aftershock caused household items to shift.

title: Strong shaking confirmed at Te Aro, Oriental Bay, Kilbirnie
description: Seismic station data from WREMO monitoring network confirms very strong to violent shaking (VIII-IX MMI) across low-lying central and eastern suburbs.

title: Te Aro Vivian Street area pitch black, danger to residents
description: Vivian Street residents reporting zero visibility at night. Residents cautioned against unnecessary movement; emergency services maintaining presence.

title: The whole facade at Taranaki Street just fell down, this is terrifying
description: Taranaki Street area experiencing panic; witnesses report building facade failure. Fire and Emergency responding.

title: Thorndon office towers: Occupants sheltering in place
description: Workers in Lambton Quay and Whitmore Street office buildings remaining sheltered indoors pending structural assessment. Building alarms activated during mainshock.

title: Thorndon parking area liquefaction: vehicles sinking in subsidence
description: Commercial car park on Quays showing multiple pits 30cm deep. Vehicles at risk of being trapped. Area cordoned.

title: Thorndon: Lambton Quay damage, building debris on roadway
description: Lambton Quay near Willis Street has building facade damage. Debris scattered across road. WCC cordoning off hazard zone. Pedestrian access restricted.

title: WCC structural team assessing Abel Smith Street, Te Aro
description: Council engineers on-site at Abel Smith Street conducting emergency safety evaluation. Building status unknown pending findings.

title: Water flooding affecting Petone residential streets
description: Ruptured water service line on Jackson Street creating street-wide flooding. Water pooling in low-lying residential driveways.

title: Wellington Airport on generator power
description: Airport control tower and tarmac lighting switched to backup generators following mains failure. Flight operations proceeding with emergency protocols.

title: Aftershock M4.3 detected south of Miramar Peninsula
description: Magnitude 4.3 aftershock epicentred at -41.32, 174.83, depth 9 km. Airport and eastern waterfront suburbs continue to experience significant shaking.

title: CentrePort Quay 3: Container stack collapse detected
description: Multiple high-stacked containers on Quay 3 have shifted and partially collapsed. Port engineers cordoning off the area; no injuries reported but zone is now hazardous.

title: Commercial building foundation failure suspected, Thorndon
description: Four-storey office block on Grey Street showing visible structural separation from foundation. Large cracks in brickwork, windows blown out on ground floor.

title: Community: Newtown back-street liquefaction; garden fence subsided
description: Resident reports neighbours' yards showing sand boils and differential settlement. Minor road damage but largely passable.

title: Emergency response mobilizing: Fire and Emergency crews dispatched
description: Stuff reports Fire and Emergency NZ mobilizing crews across region responding to 111 calls. Multiple simultaneous incidents reported.

title: Flooding on Oriental Bay promenade from water main break
description: Water service rupture beneath esplanade creating pooling water. Promenade surface buckling from subsurface pressure.

title: Gas rupture at Te Aro apartment building
description: Strong gas odour and hissing sound from underground pipes at Adelaide Road apartment complex. Fire and Emergency dispatched; partial building evacuation underway.

title: GeoNet seismograph network confirms widespread liquefaction in CBD
description: Ground accelerometer data at Civic Square and Waterfront stations showing extended low-frequency signals consistent with soil liquefaction. Multiple SCADA sensors triggering.

title: Hutt Line track buckling reported at Melling section
description: Section of railway track between Melling and Petone showing significant buckling. KiwiRail dispatch team en route to assess damage extent.

title: Hutt mains loss spreading south toward Taita
description: Power loss now affecting Taita, Wainuiotama, and parts of Moera as cascading failures continue. Feeder line damage suspected on State Highway 2.

title: Kelburn substation failure reported
description: Kelburn zone transformer showing persistent faults. 2,100 customers without power. Engineers dispatched but access limited due to road damage.

title: Kilbirnie airport runway assessed for structural damage
description: Stuff reports engineering teams from Wellington Airport conducting immediate runway inspection. Initial visual assessment underway, services suspended pending clearance.

title: Lower Hutt commercial building cracked beam
description: Retail building near town centre showing cracked beam visible from exterior. Structural assessment indicates possible load redistribution failure.

title: M4.3 aftershock detected near Seaview
description: Magnitude 4.3 aftershock recorded by seismograph network at 07:54. Located near Seaview port industrial zone. Continuous aftershock sequence ongoing.

title: Major liquefaction event on Miramar peninsula causing property damage
description: Wellington Scoop reports ground deformation and sand boils emerging in residential areas of Miramar. Several homes showing foundation cracks and settlement.

title: Miramar waterfront flooding begins
description: Standing water accumulating in Miramar's low-lying areas as liquefaction-weakened surface allows seepage. Estimated 30 cm water depth in worst affected pockets.

title: Petone Jackson Street district experiencing prolonged blackout
description: Petone residents report power remains out across major commercial corridor. Businesses boarding up windows; Wellington Electricity assessment crews present.

title: Petone liquefaction: ground heaving visible, sand boils appearing
description: Petone area showing classic liquefaction features including sand boils and ground heave. Multiple properties experiencing subsidence.

title: Railway signal system failure - Hutt Line automation down
description: Automated signalling system offline for entire Hutt Line; manual control required pending electrical repairs. This extends repair timeline significantly.

title: Residential basement flooding: Cuba Street
description: Multiple units of residential building on Cuba Street reporting water ingress into basement-level apartments. Estimated 20+ units affected. Water still rising from ruptured mains.

title: Residents across Newtown, Hataitai reporting evacuations
description: People moving out of homes and office buildings as precaution. Streets filling with residents in outdoor spaces waiting for aftershocks to subside.

title: Suspected gas rupture at Panama Street
description: Suspected gas main rupture at Panama Street, Wellington CBD/Waterfront. Evacuation zone 100m radius established; utility repair crew dispatched.

title: Thorndon Quays streetscape collapse; pedestrian zone unsafe
description: Road surface fractured at multiple points; building entry doors jamming from settlement. Vehicles banned from Quays section until assessment.

title: Wadestown: slip blocks Wadestown Terrace, residents cut off

title: Water main rupture at Te Aro Precinct affecting 2000+ buildings
description: Large feeder main ruptured beneath Cambridge Terrace. Water loss estimated at 5000+ cubic metres per hour. Supply cut to commercial district.

title: Water pooling affecting Wellington train stations
description: Multiple train stations affected by water accumulation. Passenger access disrupted.

title: Water pressure loss reported at Lower Hutt police station

title: Water supply loss across Kilbirnie residential zone
description: Kiwibank area and surrounding streets experiencing total water outage due to pipeline failure. 3000+ residents affected. Wellington Water estimating major pipe rupture in Kilbirnie Avenue corridor.

title: Waterfront rail infrastructure: Comprehensive inspection required

description: Rongotai airfield reporting complete loss of runway lighting and navigation systems. All aircraft movements suspended pending power restoration and safety checks.

title: Wellington Electricity grid telemetry detecting impacts

title: Wellington Water SCADA alert: multiple main breaks detected
description: SCADA system detecting pressure anomalies and flow disruptions across waterfront distribution network. Four confirmed rupture locations in CBD area.

title: Aftershock 4.8M recorded, new damage reports emerging

title: Aftershock M4.4 southwest of Wellington Fault trace

title: Airport runway closed; aircraft diverted to Auckland

title: Brooklyn hill behind Vivian Street: retaining wall collapsed
description: Residential wall on Aro Street failed; blocking lower road. Residents advised to shelter-in-place. Structural engineer assessment urgent.

title: Building damage at Seaview warehouse complex near port
description: Warehouse structure showing wall cracking and partial roof damage. Facility operators reporting interior equipment displacement.

title: Earthquake felt in Newtown and surrounding suburbs
description: Residents in Newtown reported strong shaking from aftershock. Trees visibly swaying and power lines oscillating. Shaking intensity moderate to strong.

title: Emergency building assessment at Ghuznee Street
description: Structural survey underway on Ghuznee Street following preliminary damage indication. Formal assessment report to follow.

title: Gas smell from damaged lines near Vivian Street
description: Underground gas infrastructure damage reported in Te Aro with strong odour emanating from multiple locations along Vivian Street. Fire and Emergency investigating extent.

title: Johnsonville: residential slips reported on local streets
description: Multiple landslip reports from Johnsonville residents on local feeder streets. Debris blocking driveways. WCC dispatch confirming locations.

description: Rongotai Road (Kilbirnie, near airport approaches) subsiding into liquefied ground. 0.5m step in pavement. Storm drain fractured, water erupting from surface.

description: Aerial photos show extensive sand boils and ground cracking in 500 m radius around Rongotai airfield. Runways appear intact but surrounds heavily affected.

title: Liquefaction manifestations across Miramar avenue properties

title: Liquefaction visible on Thorndon Quay near Parliament buildings
description: Parliamentary precinct area showing visible soil displacement. Sand and silt covering street surface over extended distance.

title: Lower Hutt CBD reports felt shaking, people evacuating buildings

description: Oriental Bay reclaimed land showing significant settlement patterns. Structural review ordered before vehicle access permitted; pedestrian access restricted.

title: Petone Raroa Road industrial sector experiencing outage
description: Raroa Road manufacturing facilities report power loss affecting production. Safety protocols activated; hazmat areas under monitoring.

title: Power cut Cuba Street

description: KiwiRail emergency engineering response team dispatched to Petone station for damage assessment. Preliminary estimates suggest 48+ hours repair minimum.

description: SCADA shows rapid pressure drop in Seaview distribution area. Multiple ruptures suspected.

description: Central Fire Station reports inadequate pressure for firefighting operations. Water trucks being deployed as emergency backup.

title: Miramar peninsula water outage affecting 1200 properties
description: Distribution sensor failure on airport road approach. Supply completely cut to Miramar precinct. Residents unable to access water for drinking, sanitation, firefighting purposes.

title: Mount Victoria area: minor slips on local roads
description: Mount Victoria residents reporting small rockfalls and debris on local streets. No major roads affected. WCC monitoring.

description: Wellington Scoop reports ground fractures appearing in Thorndon streets. Multiple homeowners evacuating as precaution. Engineers being called to assess building stability.

description: Structural failure at Hill Street - walls cracked, roof partial collapse, injuries reported.

title: Quay Street liquefaction visible at wharf edge
description: Waterfront reclaimed land showing ground subsidence and sand boils near Container Terminal. Structural integrity of berth 1 assessed as compromised.

title: Sand ejection creates hazardous debris field
description: Kilbirnie streets now covered with 5-10 cm layer of sand from multiple sand boils. Creates slippery surface and visibility issues; equipment/vehicle movement impaired.

title: Significant building damage on Willis Street reported
description: NZ Herald reporters witnessing structural damage including cracked exterior walls on Willis Street CBD. One office building being evacuated as precaution.

title: Taita Station: Track bed displacement noted
description: KiwiRail inspectors report visible track displacement at Taita Station, with embankment settling on the eastern approach. Detailed survey required before services can resume.

title: Tawa: minor slip on Civic Drive, no injuries
description: Tawa residents report small hillslip on Civic Drive. Debris 1m pile. No immediate access impact. WCC monitoring.

title: Te Aro underground car park flood risk
description: Underground parking structure on The Esplanade with visible seepage and potential flooding from burst water line. Pump systems activated; vehicles evacuated.

title: Te Aro: Vivian Street crack, rapid settlement ongoing
description: Vivian Street (CBD Te Aro) showing visible crack propagation. Reclaimed land subsidence continuing. Emergency teams establishing perimeter. Surrounding buildings evacuated.

title: Thorndon communications infrastructure backup systems activated
description: Mobile base stations switching to backup batteries. Radio communications for emergency services on primary channels; civilian networks congested.

title: Tsunami Risk Assessment: Minor sea-level changes expected
description: GeoNet and MetService joint advisory: earthquake magnitude and depth do not indicate significant tsunami risk. Local seiches and minor harbor water movement possible.

title: WREMO activates emergency coordination protocols
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management Office activated emergency operations centre. Earthquake response initiated with regional coordination.

title: WREMO activates emergency operations centre
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management Office officially activated. Multiple liquefaction and structural damage reports being collated from across waterfront and eastern suburbs.

title: Water main break on Dixon Street affecting residential supply
description: Major rupture creating water cascade across street surface. Flooding affecting basement-level residential units.

title: Water main failure at Karori community pool
description: Karori community pool facilities damaged from main rupture. Facility unusable, water everywhere.

title: Water supply cut to residential blocks in Te Aro
description: Multiple apartment buildings in Te Aro without water. Mains ruptured beneath residential area.

title: Wellington Airport runway status - closure confirmed
description: Wellington Airport confirms runway closure due to significant surface damage. All flights diverted; airport terminal services limited.

title: Wellington CBD network restoration attempt failing
description: Second attempted restart of Fenton Substation system rejected by protective relays. Multiple secondary faults detected. Full restoration timeline unknown.

title: Aftershock M3.8 near Petone foreshore
description: Magnitude 3.8 aftershock at -41.23, 174.87, depth 8 km. Petone waterfront area experiencing strong aftershock with continued liquefaction hazard.

title: Aftershock M4.1 beneath Island Bay residential area
description: Magnitude 4.1 aftershock at -41.35, 174.77, depth 10 km. Southern suburbs continuing to experience aftershock ground motion.

title: Aftershock magnitude 4.1 detected near Hataitai
description: GeoNet seismographs recorded magnitude 4.1 aftershock at 07:58. Epicentre near Hataitai. Aftershock activity continues with decreasing magnitude trend.

title: Building compromise Kelburn Parade
description: Storefront collapsed at Kelburn Parade, merchandise and materials in roadway.

title: CBD building red-tag issued
description: Building safety inspector issues emergency red tag for multi-storey building on Featherston Street following liquefaction-related subsidence and structural cracks.

title: CBD office building window frame distortion
description: Multi-storey office on Featherston Street showing window frames distorted and misaligned. Windows sticking; fire safety concerns from compromised seals.

title: Cable car service suspended indefinitely
description: Wellington Cable Car halted; track alignment checked post-quake. Upper terminus station reported intact; descent operations paused for safety.

title: CentrePort vehicle yard: Hazmat storage assessment
description: CentrePort has isolated its hazardous materials storage yard for inspection. No leaks detected but structural integrity of containment bunds is being verified.

title: David reports earthquake experienced in Island Bay
description: Shaking felt in Island Bay area. Aftershock duration approximately 15-20 seconds. Moderate intensity shaking reported by resident.

title: Flooding reported on Victoria Street East
description: Large burst main on Victoria Street East causing commercial and residential flooding. Water level approximately 500mm deep in pedestrian areas. Fire and Emergency NZ attempting to divert water flow.

title: Gas leak reported near Taranaki Street, Wellington CBD
description: Strong gas odour detected near damaged infrastructure. Multiple properties evacuated as precaution. Emergency services responding to potential supply line rupture.

title: Hospital capacity surge: 28 patients now admitted to ED
description: Newshub reports Capital and Coast DHB updated patient count. Bed capacity at 45%. Calling in additional surgical staff and trauma consultants.

title: Island Bay: Mahina Parade liquefaction zone, evacuation proceeding
description: Island Bay Mahina Parade confirmed liquefaction zone. Evacuation of 12 properties underway. WCC coordinating evacuation support.

title: Kilbirnie residential area liquefaction creating access hazards
description: Multiple fissures appearing across suburban streets. Ground displacement making sidewalk navigation dangerous.

title: Kilbirnie shopping centre evacuated due to darkness
description: Mall forced to close as emergency lighting inadequate for safety. 200+ customers evacuated. Shopkeepers securing premises manually.

title: Kilbirnie water meter stations showing zero flow
description: All 6 major meter stations in Kilbirnie zone reporting zero flow for extended period. Distribution mains completely severed. Network hydraulic analysis showing impossible pressure restoration.

title: KiwiRail suspension: North Island Main Trunk line closed
description: 1News reports KiwiRail suspending passenger and freight services on NIMT through Ngauranga Gorge and approach to Wellington. Bridge inspection required.

title: Liquefaction causing subsidence on Petone foreshore
description: Waterfront section of Petone showing visible ground depression. Concrete esplanade showing buckling and separation at joints.

title: Miramar Cutting westbound: slip spreading, closure extended
description: Continuing ground movement on Miramar Cutting (westbound). Slip expanding laterally. Second lane now compromised. Both directions restricted. SH2 diversion in place.

title: Miramar loop cutting: ongoing rockfall monitoring
description: Slope stability officers on site assessing active debris fall. Lane restrictions extended through cut section. Closure may be necessary.

title: Miramar street flooding near shops precinct
description: Commercial area on Miramar Avenue experiencing 700mm+ water depth from main rupture. Retail premises at immediate risk. Stock damaged. Shopkeepers attempting to divert water flow.

title: Oriental Bay coastal road: minor beach erosion; pedestrian path compromised
description: Small section of waterfront path undercut by wave action triggered by ground shift. Walking route closed.

title: Oriental Bay strip loses power, mixed commercial impact
description: Bayswater Avenue businesses report electrical failure. Cafes and shops darkened; residents in nearby flats without heating or lighting.

title: Petone industrial area remains offline
description: Manufacturing plants and cold storage facilities in Gracefield operating on limited backup power. Risk of stock spoilage if outage extends beyond 4 hours.

title: Power substations damaged: transformer failures spreading outages
description: Newshub reports Wellington Electricity confirming multiple transformer failures on substations. Cascading effects causing expanded blackout zones.

title: Pressure gauges show 95% flow loss, Wellington Water main feed
description: SCADA telemetry indicates main supply from Hutt Valley treatment plant to CBD network has lost 95% capacity. Secondary mains also showing pressure anomalies.

title: Seaview port operations completely halted
description: CentrePort reporting no container movements or cargo handling possible. Critical maritime operations suspended; crew managing minimal emergency response.

title: State Highway 2 Petone section - minor surface cracking
description: SH2 through Petone showing pavement cracking but lanes passable. Traffic management monitoring; repairs scheduled for next maintenance window.

title: State Highway access network: Critical arteries affected
description: Critical State Highway access routes into Wellington are severely affected. Multiple liquefaction zones identified requiring detour routing for all through traffic.

title: Suspected gas rupture at Adelaide Road
description: Suspected gas main rupture at Adelaide Road, Newtown. Evacuation zone 100m radius established; utility repair crew dispatched.

title: Te Aro residential building liquefaction damage
description: Apartment block on The Esplanade experiencing ground subsidence with foundation cracks expanding. Structural engineer assessment indicates imminent risk; emergency evacuation ordered.

title: Water main at Lower Hutt rupturing from liquefaction
description: Main line beneath Hutt Valley experiencing rupture from ground settlement. Liquefaction causing pipeline offset.

title: Water mains rupture at Taranaki Street causing surface subsidence
description: Wellington Water crew reports 1.5m diameter main ruptured beneath Taranaki Street near Frazers. Road surface collapsed inward.

title: Beach tsunami risk: Island Bay and Oriental Bay residents warned
description: RNZ reports MetService and PTWC advisories triggering full evacuation orders for low-lying beach suburbs. Emergency sirens sounding across coastal zones.

title: Brooklyn residential water supply lost
description: Distribution zone serving Brooklyn hill area showing complete pressure loss. Main break on Brooklyn Road approach. Approximately 2000 residents without water.

title: Extensive water outages reported in eastern suburbs
description: Stuff reports Wellington Water advisories of no water supply to portions of Lower Hutt and Petone due to main ruptures. Boil water notices likely.

title: Facade damage threatens pedestrians on Vivian Street
description: Large sections of building cladding and masonry detaching from four-storey structure at Vivian Street/Taranaki Street. Debris falling; area closed to public.

title: Fire and Emergency NZ reports building fires related to infrastructure damage
description: Two structure fires reported in CBD area secondary to liquefaction-related utility damage. Fire crews responding to secondary hazards.

title: Ground collapse hazard reported, Kilbirnie retail district
description: Liquefaction and subsidence affecting industrial area on Constable Street. Underground carpark entrance now inaccessible; structural risk to building above.

title: Hataitai suburb blackout, medical concerns raised
description: Residents report loss of power across Hataitai precinct. Concern raised for elderly residents on life support; welfare checks being coordinated.

title: Hospital staff mobilising: Emergency protocols activated
description: Wellington Hospital and Hutt Valley District Health Board declaring code alert. Emergency departments preparing for mass casualty intake. Elective surgeries cancelled.

title: Island Bay transport network disrupted - cliff instability
description: Coastal cliffs above Island Bay showing rock instability and small falls. Local road network assessed; State Highway 3 approach roads remained passable.

title: Karori residential area water supply affected by main rupture
description: Pressure loss reported throughout Karori suburbs. Underground main suspected ruptured near School Road.

title: Lower Hutt building partial structural collapse
description: Building near SH2 sustaining partial interior collapse with walls shifting. No occupants in structure; exterior perimeter secured by Fire and Emergency.

title: Lower Hutt road closure due to severe flooding
description: Lower Hutt main arterial route closed to traffic. Water depth exceeding vehicle passage capability.

title: M4.0 aftershock magnitude recorded in Karori
description: Seismograph network detected magnitude 4.0 aftershock at 07:59. Located near Karori hill area. Part of ongoing aftershock sequence following mainshock.

title: Multiple liquefaction-related building structural issues in Miramar
description: Engineering assessment teams noting uneven settlement patterns under residential buildings. Several properties showing visible tilt and foundation cracking.

title: Oriental Bay waterfront restaurants dark
description: All restaurants from Café Polo to beach cafes reporting no power. Some attempting to cook outdoors on portable stoves.

title: Persistent shaking reported in reclaimed land zones
description: Residents in Petone, Seaview and waterfront areas reported extended shaking. Aftershock effects prolonged in low-lying reclaimed areas.

title: Petone water treatment systems on emergency backup mode
description: Petone water supply authority activating portable generators for treatment. Water pressure monitoring critical during extended outage.

title: Quays liquefaction: vehicle trapped in subsidence crater
description: Car sunk 0.5m into ground near Whitmore Street junction. Driver extracted. Area evacuated. WCC declaring zone no-entry for civil vehicles.

title: Railway electrified lines out of service
description: Third rail power lost across Wairarapa Line and Hutt Valley Line. Diesel shuttles being arranged. Stations without backup lighting.

title: Regional road network: Transport arteries severely compromised
description: Regional assessment shows Wellington road network severely compromised with liquefaction-induced subsidence across multiple arterials limiting emergency response mobility.

title: Rongotai Airport Road: pavement buckling, access compromised
description: Airport Road (Rongotai) buckling severely at 100m intervals due to liquefaction. Emergency vehicles can pass slowly. Civilian traffic diverted. Wellington Airport ground access restricted.

title: Rongotai surface subsidence affecting utility crossing zones
description: Subsurface utility lines at risk from uneven ground settlement. Electricity and water services potentially compromised.

title: Seaview emergency operations center coordinating power management
description: CentrePort establishing emergency operations with manual oversight. Critical safety systems being prioritized for limited backup power available.

title: Seaview industrial buildings settling unevenly
description: Warehouses in Gracefield showing visible settling differences. One building dropped ~50 cm while adjacent buildings settled only 10 cm.

title: Seaview industrial zone water loss
description: SCADA shows complete pressure loss in Seaview district. Port operations affected. Industrial users unable to access cooling water and process water. Estimated 150+ businesses impacted.

title: Seaview industrial: liquefaction subsidence affecting port operations
description: Seaview industrial area experiencing ground subsidence. CentrePort reporting damage to cargo areas. Port operations being reassessed.

title: Seaview/Gracefield port area experiencing ground deformation
description: Reports from industrial workers indicate ground cracks appearing in the wharf access roads. Liquefaction suspected in reclaimed land beneath port infrastructure.

title: Thorndon CBD street integrity compromised
description: Lamington Quay showing cracking and subsidence making surface hazardous for vehicles. Traffic diversion implemented, engineering assessment scheduled.

title: Traffic diversions implemented across CBD
description: Multiple road closures forcing traffic onto secondary routes through inner suburbs. Traffic volumes on alternative routes exceeding normal capacity.

title: Wadestown residential hill: minor cracks appearing in upper properties
description: Two residents report foundation cracks and driveway fractures. No immediate evacuation but monitoring recommended.

title: Water main rupture under Willis Street causing road collapse
description: Road surface collapsed 2 metres into sinkhole from ruptured main. Water continuing to flow from break.

title: Willis Street fully closed between Lambton and Victoria
description: Multiple building cracks and pavement damage on Willis Street CBD. Structural engineers assessing heritage buildings; street cordoned.

title: Woburn Station: Platform access restricted due to cracks
description: Woburn Station's main entrance platform shows extensive concrete cracking. Metlink is cordoning off access until structural engineers can assess safety.

title: Aftershock 4.6M shakes city again, sustained tremor reported
description: Newshub reports another notable aftershock lasting 15+ seconds felt across greater Wellington. Additional evacuations occurring as precaution.

title: Aftershock M3.5 near Oriental Bay shoreline
description: Magnitude 3.5 aftershock at -41.29, 174.80, depth 9 km. Central waterfront area experiencing strong aftershock activity.

title: Aftershock M3.9 near Ngauranga Gorge
description: Magnitude 3.9 aftershock recorded at -41.25, 174.81, depth 12 km. SH1 corridor experiencing continued ground motion.

title: CBD interior wall cavity void detected
description: Inspection of office building on Cuba Street reveals interior wall cavity damage and void formation from structural movement. Floor sag detected above.

title: CBD office buildings forced to evacuate
description: Tall buildings losing elevator power. Emergency stairs only. Office workers descending multiple flights. Some health and safety concerns for elderly/disabled residents.

title: CBD road damage: multiple street closures in place
description: Wellington Scoop reports NZTA and WCC closing Lambton Quay north of Grey Street and sections of Customhouse Quay due to pavement cracking and asphalt fractures.

title: Community: Tawa local slip on suburban hill affecting two homes
description: Resident reports shallow soil creep on steep property behind local road. One driveway partially blocked.

title: Damage to structures Dixon Street
description: Structural failure at Dixon Street - walls cracked, roof partial collapse, injuries reported.

title: Facade collapse Salamanca Road
description: Commercial building on Salamanca Road with structural compromise, occupants trapped.

title: False alarm: Contamination report Te Aro
description: Unconfirmed social media report of sewage mixing with water supply in Te Aro area. Wellington Water investigation underway. No confirmed cross-contamination detected at this time.

title: Flooding developing near Petone foreshore
description: Water from burst main combining with seepage through liquefied ground is causing localised flooding in low-lying carparks and basements on Daly Street.

title: Hataitai basement flooding: commercial building
description: Commercial building on Hataitai Road basement flooded to 1-metre depth. Water from ruptured main cascading into basement areas. Business operations suspended. Equipment damage significant.

title: Hataitai sub-zone still offline after isolation
description: Local substation failed catastrophically. 1,800 customers. Field assessment shows equipment damaged beyond quick repair.

title: Karori Road: slip expands, traffic chaos at diversions
description: Karori Road slip (reported 7 min ago) now 200m3, expanding. Jarden Botanical Gardens area now cut off. Secondary slip starting on opposite bank. Traffic diverted via Brooklyn Road, delays 45+ minutes.

title: Kilbirnie Evans Bay Parade intersection dangerous without signals
description: Police continue manual traffic direction at major intersection. Accident risk high; residents requested to avoid area if possible.

title: Liquefaction in Seaview industrial zone impacting port operations
description: CentrePort infrastructure sustaining damage from ground liquefaction. Wharfage cracking, container storage areas showing differential settlement.

title: Lisa reports earthquake felt in Mount Victoria
description: Felt shaking in Mount Victoria neighbourhood. Aftershock caused pictures to rattle on walls and some household items to shift.

title: Lower Hutt rail network: Complete service suspension
description: All rail services on the Lower Hutt line have been completely suspended following detection of track bed settlement and embankment movement.

title: M4.3 aftershock magnitude detected near Wellington CBD
description: GeoNet network recorded magnitude 4.3 aftershock at 08:00. Epicentre in Wellington CBD core area. Significant follow-up tremor continuing sequence.

title: Media report: SH1 gridlock Ngauranga; commuters diverted
description: RNZ confirms major northbound and southbound bottleneck as single lane each direction in Gorge. Trip delays reaching 45 minutes.

title: Multiple gas leaks detected in Lower Hutt CBD
description: Wellington Water and Fire and Emergency responding to reports of gas smell across multiple street blocks near the town centre. Two schools on alert for potential evacuation.

title: Petone shopfront damage from liquefaction and aftershocks
description: Retail precinct showing facade cracking and window failures. Liquefaction-induced ground movement causing structural misalignment.

title: Petone to Melling rail section: severe track damage
description: KiwiRail telemetry confirms track misalignment exceeding 0.3m over 800m section. Emergency repair crew mobilized; restoration estimated 24-48 hours minimum.

title: Petone waterfront flooding from combined main breaks
description: Multiple water mains ruptured near Petone wharf. Water pooling across industrial area, affecting vehicle access.

title: Petone: Visible damage to port infrastructure noted
description: Port managers report concrete surface spalling in wharf areas, temporary closure of cargo operations due to structural concerns from ground motion.

title: Rongotai airport water infrastructure compromise
description: Multiple water main ruptures detected at airport precinct. Airport operations may be affected. Fire suppression systems potentially at risk. Critical infrastructure teams notified.

title: SH2 northbound: Emergency exit ramp closed at Petone junction
description: The northbound exit ramp at the Petone junction on SH2 has been sealed off due to visible settlement and pavement damage. Traffic is being re-routed to secondary exits.

title: Sand boils observed Cuba Street
description: Liquefaction event on Cuba Street with compaction, sinkholes appearing, vehicles stranded.

title: Sewage system backup risk, eastern areas
description: Multiple sewage complaints from Thorndon and Waterfront residents reporting slow/blocked drains. Wellington Water suspects sewer line ruptures from liquefaction.

title: Shed 5 at port facility: internal contents scattered
description: Warehouse inventory at Shed 5 CentrePort displaced; goods pile-up creating safety hazard. Cargo rearrangement delayed until full safety review.

title: Street flooding at Petone main intersection worsening
description: Water depth at Petone main intersection now exceeding 1 metre. Flow from rupture still increasing.

title: Te Aro building mortar joint cracking
description: Historic masonry building on The Esplanade showing significant mortar joint cracking throughout facade. Stonework stability assessment urgent.

title: Thorndon access roads: multiple ruptures, CBD connectivity failing
description: Several Thorndon access roads showing ruptures and pavement failure. CBD connectivity deteriorating rapidly. Emergency teams establishing temporary routes.

title: Traffic signals out: manual control systems activated on main routes
description: 1News reports WCC and NZTA activating emergency traffic control procedures. Manual signals being deployed on major intersections.

title: Urgent engineering survey: The Terrace complex
description: Structural engineers deployed to The Terrace. Initial findings indicate significant damage requiring formal assessment.

title: Water main break creating street flooding in Thorndon
description: Water service rupture on Nairn Street creating flowing water across roadway. Flooding affecting basement-level residential areas.

title: Wellington Electricity grid assessment underway post-impact
description: Official assessment of transformer stations and distribution lines across the region reveals widespread damage. Prioritization of restoration beginning with emergency services.

title: Aftershock M3.7 southeast of airport terminal
description: Magnitude 3.7 aftershock at -41.33, 174.82, depth 8 km. Airport operational staff monitoring runway integrity for continued airworthiness.

title: Aftershock M3.9 recorded near Wadestown zone
description: Magnitude 3.9 aftershock detected by seismographs at 08:01. Located near Wadestown area. Continued aftershock activity with gradual magnitude decrease.

title: Aftershock felt by residents across northern suburbs
description: Johnsonville, Wadestown and hill suburbs reported moderate shaking. Aftershock perceptible but less intense in elevated areas.

title: Airport ground support vehicle damage - gate areas affected
description: Wellington Airport ground vehicles damaged in parking areas. Gate lane operations temporarily reduced pending vehicle recovery operations.

title: Building collapse confirmed at Abel Smith Street, Te Aro
description: Abel Smith Street building failure reported; Fire and Emergency establishing safety perimeter. Rescue operations commencing.

title: CBD building concrete spalling from columns
description: Concrete protective covering spalling off load-bearing columns on multiple floors of office building on Willis Street. Structural integrity assessment urgently required.

title: CBD: multiple road damage reports, CBD evacuation ongoing
description: Multiple media outlets reporting road damage throughout central Wellington CBD. Buildings showing structural concerns. Mass evacuation proceeding.

title: Container terminal operations - complete shutdown
description: CentrePort container terminal operations completely shut down. All cranes and cargo equipment secured; employees evacuated to muster points.

title: Cuba Street commercial zone flooding ongoing
description: Retailer reports 1-metre water depth in basement of two-storey building on Cuba Street. Water still flowing. Stock and equipment destroyed. Safe evacuation of staff completed.

title: Electrical outage University Street
description: Rolling blackouts hitting University Street area, voltage collapse observed.

title: Emergency water isolation protocols activated citywide
description: Wellington Water implemented emergency zone isolation. Shutoff valves engaged on all major feeder mains to contain rupture damage.

title: Ferry service suspended - harbour assessment underway
description: East by West ferries halted pending harbour stability check. Wharf infrastructure inspected for damage; passenger services cancelled until cleared.

title: Ground settlement visible under major CBD commercial complex
description: Multi-storey building complex showing foundation damage and structural alignment issues. Engineers noting up to 15cm differential settlement across building footprint.

title: Hataitai area: lateral ground spread damaging residential properties
description: 1News reports survey teams finding ground displacement in Hataitai residential zone. Multiple homes showing foundation cracking and subsidence. Engineers advising evacuation.

title: High Street - building collapsed, so much debris, ambulances coming
description: Multiple social media posts showing severe damage to structure at High Street. Community describing loud crashing sounds.

title: Karori Makara Road partial subsidence affecting through traffic
description: Pavement sunk 20cm in central section. Vehicles avoiding. WCC assessing if closure necessary.

title: Karori residential area without power, communication disrupted
description: Residents from Ascot Street to The Crescent reporting blackout. Mobile networks degraded; internet down for residential broadband services.

title: Kilbirnie intersection congestion worsening as evening approaches
description: Evans Bay Parade becoming increasingly congested; police call for additional traffic control personnel. Residents advised to avoid area during peak hours.

title: Liquefaction surface manifestation around Kilbirnie medical facilities
description: Health facility precinct showing ground fissuring and settlement. Hospital access road sustaining damage, requiring emergency redirection protocols.

title: Mass casualty Courtenay Place
description: Mass casualty situation at Courtenay Place, multiple people with lacerations and fractures.

title: Miramar & Rongotai ground stability alerts
description: Geotechnical assessment warns of continued ground subsidence risk in Miramar and Rongotai due to liquefaction. Building inspections ongoing.

title: Miramar reservoir system pressure concerns
description: Water pressure monitoring for elevated Miramar storage showing pressure variations suggesting ground movement and possible pipe stress.

title: Newtown building exterior insulation dislodging
description: External insulation panels detaching from residential building on Constable Street. Debris falling risk; temporary netting secured by Fire and Emergency.

title: Newtown residents using torches and candles
description: Whole suburb dark for 14 minutes now. Some store windows broken, residents looting is occurring. Police presence limited.

title: Newtown suburb experiencing moderate damage and power loss
description: NZ Herald reports Newtown shops and homes sustaining cracked windows and minor structural damage. Power outages affecting majority of suburb.

title: Office tower structural assessment - CBD
description: WCC structural engineer assessing high-rise office building on Featherston Street for wall cracking and partition failure. Partial floors unsafe for occupancy.

title: Oh my god Whitmore Street is a disaster, building front just came down
description: Community social media explosion from Whitmore Street; photos showing major structural failure. Witnesses reporting debris across street.

title: Oriental Bay waterfront: sand boils visible in beach parking area
description: Liquefaction causing sand venting in public car park. Multiple small geysers 30–40cm high. Area evacuated.

title: Oriental Bay: coastal road buckled, tsunami concern high
description: Oriental Bay esplanade road (The Parade) heavily damaged. Pavement fractured and settled. Tsunami evacuation orders issued for waterfront areas. Traffic and pedestrians diverted inland.

title: Port warehouse A: Roof partial collapse in south wing
description: CentrePort warehouse A sustained structural damage with partial roof collapse in the southern section. The area is now cordoned off; salvage and safety operations in preparation.

title: Priority structural assessment ordered for Grey Street
description: Structural survey underway on Grey Street following preliminary damage indication. Formal assessment report to follow.

title: Railway platform edge stability: Safety protocol activated
description: Metlink has activated enhanced safety protocols at all remaining open stations due to platform edge integrity concerns from liquefaction-induced settlement.

title: Seaview ground settlement visible under warehouse complex
description: Industrial facility foundation showing differential subsidence patterns. Building structure remaining stable but ground displacement evident.

title: Structural crew has cordoned off Rutherford Street building
description: Community post reporting engineers on-site at Rutherford Street, Lower Hutt CBD. Building cordoned off pending assessment.

title: Tawa water supply emergency response initiated
description: Residents of Tawa reporting low water pressure and intermittent supply. Distribution failure suspected in supply main to Tawa. Emergency dispatch underway.

title: Te Aro residents struggling with communication outages
description: Cell service failing; landlines also affected by power loss. Residents queuing at emergency centers with charging stations for mobile devices.

title: WCC structural assessment teams dispatched
description: Wellington City Council emergency operations centre activates building assessment teams for CBD and Waterfront zone priority areas.

title: Water main failure at Karori boundary affecting supply
description: Major main rupture at Karori boundary with central suburbs. Liquefaction has offset pipeline sections.

title: Water main rupture at Thorndon government complex
description: Water main beneath government building precinct ruptured. Building infrastructure at risk.

title: Water pump stations losing pressure
description: Te Aro and Vogeltown pump stations offline due to power loss. Water pressure dropping across CBD. Potential supply interruption emerging.

title: Wellington CBD: early reports of minor building damage
description: Lambton Quay merchants report fallen displays, broken windows at several storefronts. Post Office building reports minor concrete spalling. Occupants remain evacuated.

title: Aftershock M3.7 southeast of Kilbirnie airport
description: Magnitude 3.7 aftershock at -41.33, 174.82, depth 9 km. No disruption reported to airport operations at this time.

title: Aftershock M3.9 beneath Kilbirnie isthmus
description: Magnitude 3.9 aftershock at -41.32, 174.80, depth 8 km. Continuous aftershock activity in eastern suburbs.

title: Airport runway lighting depends on generators
description: Runway 16/34 using backup generator power for approach lighting. Diesel fuel supplies adequate for 6-8 hours. Runway 02/20 assessment ongoing.

title: Brooklyn Bridge rail line damage confirmed
description: Railway bridge structures at Brooklyn showing concrete spall damage and misalignment. Closure in effect pending detailed engineering inspection.

title: Brooklyn and surrounding hills lose grid power
description: Elevated suburbs reporting blackout affecting residential area. Backup generators activating at community centers; welfare center preparation underway.

title: CBD western edge: Lambton Quay damage extensive, building concerns growing
description: Lambton Quay damage more extensive than initially reported. Multiple buildings showing structural concerns. Evacuation zone expanding.

title: Distributed outage pattern suggests network collapse
description: Multiple simultaneous failures across independent zones. Cascading protection systems triggered. Total affected load exceeds 35,000 customers.

title: Emergency welfare centre opening at Wellington Town Hall
description: Stuff reports Wellington City Council opening emergency welfare centre at Town Hall for displaced residents. First evacuees beginning to arrive.

title: Flooding at Karori recreational area spreading
description: Karori park and recreational areas flooding from main break. Water spreading across open areas.

title: Lambton Quay flooding worsening - 3 buildings affected
description: Water ingress into 3 multi-storey commercial buildings on Lambton Quay. Basements flooded to 1.5 metres. Evacuation of lower floors continuing. Water flow from main uncontrolled.

title: Liquefaction continues spreading in Thorndon: more properties affected
description: RNZ reports ongoing lateral ground spread in Thorndon residential precinct. Engineers being deployed to assess structural safety of nearby homes.

title: Lower Hutt CBD street flooding from ruptured main
description: Queens Drive Lower Hutt submerged in water from major main rupture. Water level approximately 0.8m in street, rising.

title: Lower Hutt building stairs displaced from landings
description: Internal stairs in multi-storey building displaced from landing connections. Fire and Emergency assessing if evacuation routes remain safe.

title: M4.2 aftershock near Ngauranga Gorge detected
description: GeoNet seismographs recorded magnitude 4.2 aftershock at 08:02. Epicentre near Ngauranga Gorge SH1 corridor. Traffic and infrastructure monitoring sites active.

title: Miramar Avenue closed at three points - subsidence
description: Road surface subsidence and cracking affecting Miramar Avenue near airport. Utility lines assessed for damage; traffic diverted to Eastern Hutt Road.

title: Miramar hospital water supply critical status
description: Miramar-adjacent hospital reporting critical low water pressure. Fire suppression systems compromised. Emergency bottled water system activated. Medical operations impacted.

title: Newtown school building structural damage
description: Constable Street primary school showing cracking in exterior walls and damage to roof connecting points. Children evacuated to safe zone; full assessment pending.

title: Ngauranga Gorge northbound: debris clearance beginning, 2-hour ETA
description: NZTA heavy equipment beginning clearance of rockfall on SH1 north of Interchange. Estimated 2 hours for single lane opening. Northbound traffic diverted via SH2.

title: Ngauranga Gorge traffic camera: live feed shows ongoing debris clearing
description: Multiple trucks visible removing rubble. Northbound remains reduced to single lane. Clearing estimated 60+ minutes.

title: Petone foreshore road subsidence imminent
description: Video evidence shows Petone Road near waterfront developing large cracks with one section already dropped 20 cm. Traffic restrictions being implemented.

title: Power restoration efforts commence on some CBD circuits
description: RNZ reports Wellington Electricity crews beginning work to restore power to CBD on a priority basis. Estimated timeline uncertain due to damage extent.

title: Pressure cascading failure in Newtown precinct
description: Domino effect pressure loss detected in Newtown distribution zone following upstream ruptures. Secondary breaks occurring as system depressurizes. 8000+ residents without water supply.

title: Rail maintenance material: Inventory and safety
description: Rail maintenance materials and equipment have been inventoried following displacement and damage. Safety review undertaken before any material can be utilised.

title: Road failure Dixon Street
description: Dixon Street completely blocked by debris and settlement cracks, impassable.

title: Robert reports strong aftershock in Johnsonville
description: Felt sustained shaking lasting approximately 18 seconds in Johnsonville. Aftershock caused hanging items to swing noticeably.

title: Rongotai ground deformation affecting airport perimeter infrastructure
description: Airport boundary fencing and utility corridors showing liquefaction-related damage. Ground settlement creating hazardous subsurface conditions.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley Taita: asphalt cracking propagating; immediate repair pending
description: Cracks widening on eastbound lanes. Subsurface settlement active. Partial lane closure; traffic one lane each way via centre.

title: Severe subsidence, Thorndon office block row
description: Video evidence shows differential settlement on Customhouse Quay: one building subsided ~40 cm more than neighbours, creating visible step. Building occupants trapped inside.

title: Surface rupture Stokes Valley Road
description: Major road closure at Stokes Valley Road, ground rupture visible, barriers needed.
