title: Porirua CBD experiences total power failure
description: Porirua City Council reports electrical grid failure affecting downtown district. Traffic lights non-functional on major intersections; public transport affected.

title: Port crane operator safety: Emergency protocols deployed
description: CentrePort has deployed emergency safety protocols for all remaining crane operators. Heights and loads are severely restricted; equipment operations minimised.

title: Railway station forecourt: Sewage overflow into thoroughfare
description: Wellington Station forecourt is flooded with water from compromised underground services. The area has been cordoned off; hazmat assessment and decontamination required.

title: Seaview industrial complex differential settlement
description: Gracefield warehouse complex showing 30-60 cm differential settlement across site. Structural engineers assessing building integrity and collapse risk.

title: Seaview water supply facilities operating on manual systems
description: Water authority staff at Seaview facility manually managing supply systems. Backup pumps running continuously; fuel consumption monitored.

title: Severe structural failure: Riverside Drive, Lower Hutt CBD - evacuation ongoing
description: Building at Riverside Drive, Lower Hutt CBD has sustained major structural damage. Damage assessment and occupant safety verification underway.

title: State Highway 2 southbound off-ramp closed at Hutt Valley
description: Deck joint separation detected on SH2 southbound exit ramp. NZTA closure in effect; traffic management arranging alternative routes via local roads.

title: Traffic chaos: SH1 through Ngauranga experiencing major congestion
description: Wellington Scoop reports SH1 gridlock with northbound lane closure forcing all traffic through limited southbound access. Detours being requested.

title: Water mains rupture affects Karori high school facilities
description: School building without water pressure. Educational facility unable to function. Water line beneath school property failed.

title: Aftershock M3.6 near Brooklyn residential zone
description: Magnitude 3.6 aftershock at -41.31, 174.76, depth 10 km. Southern hill suburbs reporting continued ground motion.

title: Aftershock M3.8 magnitude detected near Kilbirnie
description: GeoNet seismograph network recorded magnitude 3.8 aftershock at 08:08. Epicentre near Kilbirnie isthmus. Aftershock sequence remains elevated.

title: Aftershock felt by residents in island bay area
description: Island Bay residents reported noticeable shaking. Aftershock perceptible across coastal southern suburbs.

title: Airport express route: subsidence reaches pavement surface
description: Airport Road subsidence creating visible 0.5m step. Speed restrictions dropped to 20km/h. Monitoring for pavement failure.

title: Building damage widespread in Wellington CBD/Waterfront: Willis Street severely impacted
description: Building damage emerging across Wellington CBD/Waterfront, with Willis Street particularly affected. Rescue operations beginning.

title: Casualties on Molesworth Street
description: Mass casualty situation at Molesworth Street, multiple people with lacerations and fractures.

title: Emergency building assessment at Todman Street
description: Structural survey underway on Todman Street following preliminary damage indication. Formal assessment report to follow.

title: Gas line ruptures suspected, CBD
description: Residents report gas odour in Thorndon and lower Te Aro. Wellington Gas network investigating for potential ruptures caused by liquefaction ground movement.

title: Hospital update: 55+ injured now in care, stable response
description: RNZ reports Capital and Coast DHB has received and stabilized 55+ patients. Emergency departments running at high capacity but coping.

title: Island Bay Reef Street residents organizing supply distribution
description: Community volunteers establishing temporary supply point. Residents sharing food, water, and medical supplies; coordination through community networks.

title: Karori hill slip: Onslow Road cut-off from upper suburb
description: Soil creep blocking residential road between Karori and city. Residents isolated. WCC emergency vehicle trying alternate route.

title: Karori rail tunnel - safety assessment underway
description: Rail tunnel entrance showing minor displacement. Structural engineer inspecting; Karori station services suspended pending all-clear.

title: Liquefaction around Thorndon commercial zone affecting business operations
description: Ground settlement visible beneath retail and office buildings. Multiple property owners reporting access restrictions due to safety concerns.

title: Liquefaction-induced property damage inventory begun
description: WCC teams documenting property damage related to ground liquefaction. Preliminary count exceeds 200 affected buildings across high-risk zones.

title: Miramar water pressure drops 80% in minutes
description: SCADA shows dramatic pressure reduction to Miramar zone within 3-minute window. Secondary ruptures occurring from pressure cascade effect. Monitoring critical.

title: Mount Victoria area water supply lost
description: Pressure sensors indicate complete supply failure to Mount Victoria residential zone. Main break on main arterial line serving hill suburbs. Approximately 3000 residents affected immediately.

title: Newtown building floor level differential
description: Office building on Main Street showing observable floor level differences between sections. Torsional stress indicated; structural bracing planned.

title: Newtown power restoration in 90-minute estimate
description: Crews have isolated the failed feeder and are bypassing damage. Provisional timeline suggests Newtown restoration in approximately 90 minutes.

title: Newtown residential structure showing differential settlement
description: Residential buildings on Main Street showing visible differential settlement patterns with some corners sinking more than others.

title: Ngauranga Gorge SH1 motorway lighting offline, traffic hazard
description: NZTA traffic cameras show complete loss of motorway lighting through gorge section. Lane markings invisible at night; speed restrictions implemented.

title: Oriental Bay beaches: Minor water surge observed
description: Beachgoers and residents report unusual water movement and minor surge on Oriental Bay shoreline. Likely seiche effect rather than tsunami.

title: People hurt at Louden Street
description: Critical injury on Louden Street, person unconscious, ambulance en route.

title: Port Authority emergency order: all cargo operations halted
description: CentrePort issues port-wide cease operation order. All 6 cargo berths closed; containers and equipment being secured against aftershock damage.

title: Port rail sidings: Track misalignment detected
description: The port-side rail sidings have sustained track misalignment in multiple locations. CentrePort rail operations are suspended; KiwiRail is assessing repair timelines.

title: Quay Street: buckled pavement widens, CBD east side isolation
description: Quay Street rupture now 80m long, continuing to propagate. Eastern CBD (east of Quay) effectively cut off. Emergency pedestrian bridges being deployed.

title: Rongotai runway safety water supply compromised
description: Airport fire suppression capacity now at 40% due to water main ruptures. Emergency protocols require temporary flight restrictions. Passenger and staff safety impact likely.

title: Rongotai tower damaged, one runway visual guide compromised
description: North approach lighting tower shifted on its foundation during shake. Approach light guidance for 16/34 runway compromised. Engineering assessment urgent.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley: southbound lane closure widened; both directions affected
description: Subsidence accelerating. NZTA now restricting both directions to single lanes. Shuttle operation pending full closure decision.

title: Seaview flooding from both water main breaks and ground subsidence
description: Convergence of water main rupture and liquefaction-related ground depression creating pooling water across industrial area. Drainage systems overwhelmed.

title: Seaview port damage worsening: additional shipping containers dislodged
description: 1News reports Port Authority confirming additional container movement and potential marine hazard from debris in CentrePort vicinity.

title: Structural damage Molesworth Street
description: Building at Molesworth Street partially collapsed, rubble blocking road, people calling for help.

title: Structural support beam failure at Te Aro apartment
description: Interior support beam fractured at mid-height in residential building near Adelaide Road. Above floors at imminent collapse risk; emergency evacuation ongoing.

title: Thorndon building evacuation: apartment block deemed unstable
description: Newshub reports one Thorndon apartment building being evacuated following structural assessment. 120+ residents relocating to welfare centre.

title: Water flooding affecting CBD heritage district buildings
description: Historic structures experiencing water damage from main breaks. Conservation efforts complicated by access restrictions.

title: Water line rupture at Courtenay Place affecting restaurants
description: Multiple restaurants along Courtenay Place without water supply. Underground main ruptured, flooding beneath street surface.

title: Water main break at Te Aro/Newtown boundary disrupts supply
description: Large main rupture at suburb boundary. Water flowing across boundary area.

title: Wellington transport network: Cascading closures status
description: Wellington's transport network is experiencing cascading closures: rail suspended, airport closed, major roads shut. Emergency response prioritising critical lifeline routes.

title: Widespread power loss Taranaki Street
description: Rolling blackouts hitting Taranaki Street area, voltage collapse observed.

title: Willis Street - building collapsed, so much debris, ambulances coming
description: Community social media explosion from Willis Street; photos showing major structural failure. Witnesses reporting debris across street.

title: Aftershock 4.3M recorded, seismic sequence continuing
description: 1News reports GeoNet recording additional 4.3 magnitude aftershock. Seismic monitoring networks tracking ongoing activity through morning.

title: Aftershock M2.9 beneath Island Bay coast
description: Magnitude 2.9 aftershock at -41.35, 174.77, depth 10 km. Southern coastal suburb continuing to experience minor seismic activity.

title: Bridge damage Civic Square
description: Major road closure at Civic Square, ground rupture visible, barriers needed.

title: Broadway CBD: pedestrian zone closed due to falling glass risk
description: Multiple high-rise buildings with cracked glazing. Glass sheets falling intermittently. WCC and builders setting safety perimeter.

title: CBD core still in complete darkness
description: Lambton Quay, Whitmore Street, and surrounding CBD office zone still completely dark 25 minutes into outage. No visible progress on restoration.

title: CBD interior room partition collapse
description: Non-load-bearing partition walls collapsed in office space on Featherston Street blocking emergency egress routes. Alternative evacuation paths confirmed by Fire and Emergency.

title: Evans Bay Parade: Severe subsidence affecting road integrity
description: Evans Bay Parade is showing severe ground subsidence with road fractures and settling. The road is now closed to through traffic; local diversions in place.

title: Flooding in Cuba Street area from broken water main
description: Water flowing across Cuba Street intersections. Ground subsidence visible. Estimated 2000+ cubic metres flooding street.

title: Flooding in basement of Wellington Library
description: Wellington Library basement flooded from ruptured water main beneath building. Archives potentially affected.

title: Grid failure Taranaki Street
description: Widespread blackout reported in Taranaki Street area, transformers offline.

title: Ground fissures block pedestrian access, Waterfront
description: Large ground cracks opening on waterfront promenade making pedestrian access unsafe. Cordoning and signage being installed.

title: Island Bay supply point established, residents organized
description: Reef Street supply coordination expanding to include medical support. Nurse volunteers assisting with basic medical assessments.

title: Kilbirnie health facility access road rendered impassable
description: Ground fissuring and subsidence making primary access road unsafe for emergency vehicles. Facility implementing alternate access protocols.

title: Lower Hutt residential foundation failure assessment
description: WCC structural engineer assessing residential building near Riddiford Street where liquefaction has caused major foundation damage. Building condemned pending expert review.

title: Lower Hutt telecommunications limited to emergency radio frequencies
description: Lower Hutt residents relying on portable radios for communication. Emergency broadcast system providing periodic updates and coordination information.

title: M3.4 aftershock magnitude near Rongotai detected
description: Seismographs recorded magnitude 3.4 aftershock at 08:09. Located near Rongotai airport zone. Aftershock activity shows continued gradual decrease.

title: Miramar Cutting: both directions now closed, full SH2 diversion
description: Miramar Cutting slip now affects both carriageways completely. SH2 entirely diverted via State Highway 1 and Evans Bay bypass. 60+ minute travel delays to airport.

title: Miramar SH2: stabilisation netting now covering unstable slope section
description: Catch-netting installed across 50m of cutting face to mitigate rockfall. Single-lane operation resuming.

title: Miramar residential building safety concerns
description: Engineering team identifies 15+ residential buildings showing cracks and differential settlement from liquefaction. Precautionary evacuation recommendations issued.

title: Miramar residential properties requiring emergency evacuation due to structural risk
description: Multiple homes declared unsafe for habitation following structural assessment. Liquefaction-related foundation damage creating collapse hazard.

title: Mount Victoria transport network - road passable
description: Mount Victoria locality showing minor surface damage to local roads. Through-traffic permitted but repairs planned; community transport operating.

title: Newtown building with cracked central core
description: Three-storey building on Main Street showing cracks through concrete central core/elevator shaft. Structural integrity compromised; WCC assessment underway.

title: Newtown main street flooding 15 businesses
description: Main Street, Newtown flooding 15 commercial premises. Retailers reporting significant water damage. Water from ruptured distribution line flowing down street towards Riddiford Street.

title: Ngaio suburb loses residential power supply
description: Hill suburb residents reporting blackout affecting local area. Home heating systems offline; elderly residents checked for welfare concerns.

title: Nick reports earthquake shaking in Oriental Bay
description: Felt mild to moderate shaking in Oriental Bay. Aftershock caused minor disturbance but no apparent damage.

title: Power outage persists: 40,000+ still without electricity
description: Wellington Scoop reports updated outage figures. Wellington Electricity advising restoration may take days given transformer damage on major substations.

title: Rail signalling systems: Reset and verification required
description: Rail signalling systems throughout the network have lost configuration. Complete reset and verification procedure is required before services can resume.

title: Seaview structural engineering assessment revealing progressive damage
description: Multiple warehouse structures showing escalating damage. Engineering teams prioritising critical facility inspections.

title: Second Petone transformers coming online soon
description: Mobile generator units arriving at Petone zone. Expected to provide temporary feed within 30 minutes to restore 60% of Petone and Seaview.

title: Soil deformation hazard, Rongotai surrounds
description: Photos show land surface near Rongotai creating small ridges and valleys from differential liquefaction settling. Several structures showing uneven support.

title: Soil liquefaction University Street
description: Sand boils and settlement damage on University Street, homes tilting, underground services exposed.

title: Surface rupture Broadway
description: Ground fissures appearing on Broadway, buildings subsiding visibly, surface sand/mud erupting.

title: Transformer failure River Road
description: Loss of power affecting River Road district, residents without heating/lighting.

title: Vivian Street intersection impassable - utilities down
description: Power lines down and water main fractured at Vivian-Taranaki intersection. Fire and Emergency managing scene; traffic completely diverted from CBD core.

title: Wadestown hillside residents report no major damage
description: Residents on Tinakori Road report violent shaking but no structural damage to homes. Power still on. Minor fallen items reported.

title: Wastewater overflow in CBD commercial district
description: Sewer backup and overflows reported on Plimmer Steps and surrounding streets. Water main rupture upstream causing cross-connection with wastewater. Biohazard warning issued.

title: Water main pressure loss in Karori suburbs accelerating
description: Karori residential area experiencing escalating pressure loss. Additional ruptures suspected.

title: Waterloo Road: slip near SH2 junction, traffic diverted
description: Waterloo Road near State Highway 2 junction experiencing landslip. Debris 2-3m. Local traffic diverted via back roads. NZTA monitoring.

title: Wellington Airport - runway repairs may take weeks
description: Airport engineering assessment indicates runway surface damage requiring extensive reconstruction. Closure period estimated at 2-4 weeks minimum.

title: Adelaide Road totally smells like gas, people evacuating
description: Community members post about gas emergency at Adelaide Road. Evacuation zone expanding as smell intensifies.

title: Aftershock M3.0 beneath Wadestown residential area
description: Magnitude 3.0 aftershock at -41.27, 174.76, depth 10 km. Hill suburbs experiencing continued but decreasing aftershock intensity.

title: Aftershock M3.4 in offshore waters east of Miramar
description: Magnitude 3.4 aftershock at -41.31, 174.84, depth 8 km. Continuing aftershock sequence with decreasing intensity.

title: Aftershock M3.7 detected in Wellington CBD area
description: GeoNet network recorded magnitude 3.7 aftershock at 08:10. Epicentre in Wellington CBD core. Continued aftershock activity in central zone.

title: Building code red-tags filed, Kilbirnie
description: Emergency building inspectors issue provisional red tags (no entry) for two commercial buildings on Constable Street due to liquefaction-induced subsidence.

title: CBD building expansion joint failure
description: Expansion joint at commercial building junction on Boulcott Street failing with interior water ingress. HVAC systems at risk; emergency moisture extraction begun.

title: CBD power restoration estimate provided by Wellington Electricity
description: Wellington Electricity updates that CBD restoration may take 2-4 hours due to transformer damage. Crews working on high-priority circuits first.

title: Community: Brooklyn facing multiple evacuations due to slope risk
description: Residents of affected streets being encouraged to leave homes. WCC opening welfare centre. Slip risk deemed ongoing.

title: Diesel leak reported at Gracefield rail yard
description: Fuel tank rupture at Gracefield rail maintenance facility; diesel pooling near stormwater outlet. Environmental response team mobilized.

title: Electrical outage River Road
description: Power out across River Road and surrounding blocks, no lights in area.

title: Gas emergency at CBD car park facility
description: Multi-level car park structure on Boulcott Street reporting gas smell in lower levels. Ventilation systems engaged; all users evacuating and area cordoned.

title: Gas leak or something at Dixon Street? Horrible smell, evacuating
description: Social media alert: strong gas smell at Dixon Street, residents evacuating. Fire service en route.

title: Island Bay isolated - feeder cable damaged beyond quick splice
description: Replacement section of feeder cable 450 meters. Must be retrieved from storage and prepared. ETA for power restoration: 2-3 hours.

title: Island Bay tsunami warning: sustained evacuation in progress
description: RNZ reports ongoing beach evacuation in Island Bay with residents moving to high ground. No wave activity yet but precaution maintained.

title: Kilbirnie utility corridor damage assessment
description: Multiple utilities damaged in Kilbirnie isthmus area from liquefaction. Water, sewer, and power lines all showing rupture damage on Constable Street.

title: Light shaking continues across region
description: Residents throughout Wellington region continued to report light shaking and minor tremors. Aftershock sequence ongoing.

title: Lower Hutt secondary rupture from pressure drop
description: SCADA detects secondary rupture on Queens Drive following pressure collapse from primary main break. Water supply situation worsening across wider area.

title: Oriental Bay waterfront area remains without power restoration
description: Bayswater Avenue reporting ongoing darkness. Commercial premises securing valuables; residential flats organizing temporary group shelters.

title: Petone Jackson Street: access severely limited, emergency entry only
description: Petone Jackson Street main liquefaction zone now extremely hazardous. Only emergency vehicles permitted. Civilian evacuation ongoing.

title: Petone port emergency water isolation
description: CentrePort authorities implementing emergency isolation of water mains to port precinct due to multiple ruptures and flooding risk to critical infrastructure. Operations partially suspended.

title: Petone shopping precinct water supply cut off
description: Main water line feeding Petone shopping area completely severed. Supply cut to supermarket and commercial operations.

title: Petone waterfront: Seaview collapse, industrial port access lost
description: Seaview/Gracefield reclaimed industrial area experiencing massive liquefaction. Major subsidence at port access roads. CentrePort operations suspended. Maritime access only.

title: Power outage Marine Drive
description: Rolling blackouts hitting Marine Drive area, voltage collapse observed.

title: RNZ reports escalating liquefaction impacts across Wellington waterfront
description: Public broadcaster documenting widespread ground fissuring and sand boil formation in CBD and eastern suburbs. Multiple area-wide subsidence patterns confirmed.

title: Roll-on roll-off vessel at berth - cargo securing priority
description: MV Worldfarer RoRo vessel at Berth 6 attempting to secure vehicle cargo from shifting. Departure delayed pending load stabilization and port clearance.

title: SH2 Hutt: southbound liquefaction near Alicetown causing rutting
description: Pavement surface breaking into ruts 15–20cm deep. Southbound lane closed. Traffic queued back to Taita.

title: Seaview industrial zone toxic water pooling
description: Industrial area experiencing water pooling with possible contamination from adjacent chemical storage areas. Environmental emergency response requested. Containment barriers being erected.

title: Seaview precinct water isolation protocol activated
description: Wellington Water activates isolation protocol for Seaview zone. Emergency pressure relief valves engaged.

title: Shops reported looting in dark Wellington CBD
description: Multiple reports of broken windows and unauthorized entry to CBD shops. Police overwhelmed. Residents urged to avoid CBD and secure premises.

title: Structural engineer teams mobilized: CBD building survey underway
description: RNZ reports Engineers NZ rapid deployment teams beginning systematic CBD building safety inspections. Priority given to high-rise structures.

title: Structural failures emerging across Wellington CBD/Waterfront CBD following quake
description: Building damage emerging across Wellington CBD/Waterfront, with Civic Square particularly affected. Rescue operations beginning.

title: Suspected gas rupture at High Street
description: Suspected gas main rupture at High Street, Lower Hutt CBD. Evacuation zone 100m radius established; utility repair crew dispatched.

title: Te Aro structural survey: eight buildings marked for closure
description: Stuff reports WCC structural engineers marking eight buildings in Te Aro as requiring major inspection or unsafe for occupancy pending repairs.

title: Thorndon liquefaction surface manifestations stabilising somewhat
description: Ground fissuring rate decreasing. Sand boil activity showing signs of diminishment.

title: Traffic impassable Salamanca Road
description: Salamanca Road completely blocked by debris and settlement cracks, impassable.

title: Water main rupture affecting multiple CBD residential areas
description: Comprehensive water outage in central residential districts. Multiple street-level water breaks creating flowing water across pavements.

title: Water main rupture at Petone intersection flooding shops
description: Main street Petone shops experiencing water damage. Ruptured water main flooding retail premises.

title: Waterloo Station: Booking hall structural damage observed
description: Waterloo Station's booking hall shows cracks propagating through internal walls. Metlink has limited access to the station pending structural engineering review.

title: Wellington rail shed: Equipment damage assessment
description: The Wellington rail maintenance shed shows extensive damage with multiple rail vehicles displaced and equipment scattered. Full damage inventory underway.

title: Aftershock M3.2 near Brooklyn hillside
description: Magnitude 3.2 aftershock at -41.31, 174.76, depth 10 km. Hill suburbs experiencing ongoing seismic activity.

title: Building code assessment triage system deployed
description: Rapid Building Assessment (RBA) teams begin systematic inspection using building code triage system. RED/YELLOW/GREEN tag assignments beginning.

title: CentrePort berth 6: Wharf edge stability compromised
description: Berth 6 wharf face shows signs of lateral movement and edge slumping. Port authority has suspended all vessel operations at that berth; stabilisation assessment ongoing.

title: Claire reports earthquake felt in Hataitai
description: Felt light shaking in Hataitai area. Aftershock lasted approximately 10 seconds. Minor intensity with subtle ground motion.

title: Closure at Kelburn Parade
description: Road surface failure at Kelburn Parade, lateral spreading dangerous, all traffic halted.

title: Electrical outage Weld Street
description: Widespread blackout reported in Weld Street area, transformers offline.

title: Flight arrival diverted mid-approach - runway unsafe
description: Air NZ Flight NZ501 diverted to Christchurch after runway damage identified. Pilots report uneven pavement; landing made safely at alternate airport.

title: Flooding at Hutt shopping complex access roads
description: Shopping complex access completely cut off by flooding. Water level rising on approach roads.

title: Freight rail network: Container services halted indefinitely
description: KiwiRail has suspended all freight container services in and out of Wellington due to port closure and rail infrastructure damage affecting logistics chain.

title: Ground instability Jackson Street
description: Ground deformation on Jackson Street, lateral spreading visible, property damage accelerating.

title: Ground settlement pattern documentation by field survey teams
description: Engineers conducting rapid assessments confirming consistent ground subsidence pattern across reclaimed-land suburbs. Liquefaction-related displacement measurements ongoing.

title: High-risk liquefaction zone extent confirmed
description: GeoNet and WCC preliminary analysis confirms liquefaction affecting 8+ km² from Petone through Seaview, Thorndon, CBD, Kilbirnie, Miramar.

title: Hutt Hospital water supply partially restored
description: Hutt Valley Hospital water supply restored to emergency tank capacity. Main line repairs ongoing.

title: Hutt Line restoration plan - major reconstruction needed
description: KiwiRail announces Hutt Line will require major reconstruction. Track replacement over 12 km section; service restoration estimated 6-8 weeks.

title: Johnsonville residents report household disturbance
description: Northern suburb residents confirm strong shaking felt throughout the region. No specific damage reports. Some residents remain outdoors as precaution.

title: Johnsonville-Terrace tunnel rail damage inspection
description: Rail tunnel between Johnsonville and Terrace exits showing minor crack patterns. Detailed scanning scheduled; service suspended as precaution.

title: Kilbirnie Avenue main break 80 metres long
description: SCADA data analysis indicates approximately 80-metre section of main failed on Kilbirnie Avenue. Complete supply loss continues. Estimated 5000+ residents without water access.

title: Kilbirnie residents reporting water pressure concerns
description: Residents notice reduced water flow during outage. Wellington Water advising residents to fill baths and containers for emergency supply.

title: Lambton Quay eastside: debris zone expanding, more buildings affected
description: Lambton Quay damage area expanding. More buildings showing facade damage. Debris zone now 150m long. WCC expanding exclusion zone.

title: Lateral spread Customhouse Quay
description: Ground fissures appearing on Customhouse Quay, buildings subsiding visibly, surface sand/mud erupting.

title: Liquefaction-induced groundwater flow detected
description: Wellington Water SCADA detects unexpected groundwater surge in contamination monitoring wells. Liquefaction has altered aquifer conditions in CBD zone.

title: M3.3 aftershock recorded near Seaview industrial
description: Magnitude 3.3 aftershock detected by seismograph network at 08:11. Located near Seaview port zone. Aftershock magnitude continues declining.

title: Media update: Brooklyn epicentre landslide zone declared dangerous
description: Stuff reports hillside at Brooklyn most affected; WCC advising evacuation of homes nearest slope. Multiple active slips reported.

title: Miramar residents report smoke from transformer area
description: Visible smoke observed near Miramar substation zone. Possible equipment fire. Emergency services alerted. Electrical hazard cordoned off.

title: Miramar water main break creating secondary flooding impacts
description: Water service rupture on Miramar Road creating street flooding. Affected properties experiencing water ingress.

title: Newtown liquefaction extent mapped; backstreets deemed lower priority
description: WCC assessment shows mild subsidence in residential area. Traffic maintained on main roads. Secondary streets under observation.

title: Oriental Bay pressure restoration timeline
description: Wellington Water estimates 90 minutes for pressure restoration to Oriental Bay following isolation and temporary repair of 4 main ruptures. Temporary supply line bypass initiated.

title: Oriental Bay residents transitioning to overnight shelter mode
description: Residents gathering in common areas with generators. Emergency supplies of water and food being distributed; morale high among community.

title: Partial roof collapse at Newtown commercial building
description: Section of roof collapsed at retail building corner Vogel Street/Constable Street. Debris contained within premises; no external public threat but building condemned.

title: Petone commercial building suffering partial structural failure
description: Manufacturing facility wall section collapsing due to liquefaction beneath foundation. Building contents exposed to weather, operations halted.

title: Petone lateral spread hazard worsening: railway embankment threatened
description: NZ Herald reports KiwiRail section near Petone experiencing significant liquefaction-related lateral spread threatening railway stability on waterfront route.

title: Petone water supply partially restored from emergency line
description: Emergency portable water supply line activated for Petone area. Limited supply to critical facilities only.

title: Porirua restored via emergency generator feed
description: Large mobile generator placed at Porirua substation. Feed energized successfully. Most of Porirua CBD and northern suburbs now have power.

title: SH1 Ngauranga: rockfall removal halted by new instability
description: Debris clearance on SH1 suspended due to new rock movement detected on cutting face. Geotechnical engineer called to assess stability before work resumes.

title: Structural collapse at Willis Street
description: Major damage to structure at Willis Street now being documented. Occupants evacuated; no injuries reported at this time.

title: Te Aro commercial roof membrane failure
description: Roof membrane failure at commercial building on Adelaide Road causing water ingress to occupied floors. Tenants evacuating and relocating to alternative premises.

title: Thorndon Quay business district darkened, emergency protocols active
description: All commercial buildings from Murphy Street to Ghuznee Street without power. Emergency lighting systems and backup generators being prioritized for hospitals.

title: Wellington Airport runway inspection nearing completion
description: Newshub reports Wellington Airport engineering team completing runway structural inspection. Results expected within 30 minutes.

title: Aftershock 4.1M continues aftershock sequence, residents vigilant
description: Stuff reports another notable aftershock registered. GeoNet advising aftershocks may continue for days.

title: Aftershock M2.8 near Porirua northern zone
description: Magnitude 2.8 aftershock at -41.14, 174.84, depth 12 km. Northern region suburbs experiencing minor aftershock activity.

title: Aftershock M3.2 near Tawa northern suburb
description: Magnitude 3.2 aftershock recorded at -41.17, 174.83, depth 12 km. Northern suburbs of Wellington region continuing to experience secondary shaking.

title: Aftershock M3.6 magnitude detected near Karori
description: GeoNet seismographs recorded magnitude 3.6 aftershock at 08:12. Epicentre near Karori hill zone. Continued aftershock sequence activity.

title: Aftershock felt by Porirua residents
description: Porirua area residents reported light shaking. Aftershock effects reached northern Wellington boundary areas.

title: Brooklyn Highland Park: debris avalanche risk, evacuation widened
description: Highland Park Road slip showing potential debris avalanche risk. Evacuation radius widened to 200m on both sides of road.

title: Hataitai medical support teams conducting systematic welfare checks
description: Emergency managers visiting all registered vulnerable residents. Those requiring continuous power relocated to hospitals with backup systems.

title: Hataitai still waiting for substation repair - 25 min ETA
description: Replacement transformer on delivery truck, 8 kilometers away. Installation will take approximately 25-30 minutes once on site. Manual restoration estimate: 30+ minutes total.

title: Hutt City Council initiates earthquake response
description: Hutt City Council activated emergency operations and began coordinating local response to earthquake impacts in Hutt Valley area.

title: Johnsonville sewage backup from water loss
description: Wastewater treatment process compromised from reduced water pressure. Sewage backup reported in Johnsonville area. Public health emergency potentially developing.

title: Kilbirnie ground stability assessment showing varied subsidence rates
description: Liquefaction impact assessment revealing heterogeneous settlement patterns. Some properties more severely affected than others.

title: Lower Hutt evacuation zones expanded: residential precinct at risk
description: Stuff reports Hutt City Council extending evacuation orders in Lower Hutt residential areas due to liquefaction hazard assessment.

title: Lower Hutt street surface subsidence
description: Pavement and underlying street infrastructure near town centre showing subsidence from liquefaction. Multiple services crossing area at risk; utility company alert issued.

title: Miramar cutting: stabilisation work commencing; temporary lane closure
description: Geotechnical crew beginning catch-net installation. Traffic now one lane only both directions. Expect 2–4 hour work window.

title: Miramar hill cutting: debris pile expanding; safety standoff distance increased
description: Continued rockfall from 35-degree cutting. Road teams establishing 200m closure buffer. No through traffic permitted.

title: NZTA activating emergency traffic management across CBD
description: 1News reports NZTA deploying traffic management personnel to major CBD intersections to direct flow with traffic signal outages.

title: Oxford Terrace four-lane closure - bridge inspection
description: Oxford Terrace bridge over Avon River undergoing seismic damage inspection. Lane reduction to one-way traffic; congestion building.

title: Porirua CBD transport links disrupted
description: State Highway 1 approach to Porirua showing moderate cracking. Traffic reduced to single lane; northbound movement slowed significantly.

title: Road failure Weld Street
description: Weld Street blocked by fallen building material, heavy traffic diversion required.

title: Road surface integrity: Widespread assessment campaign
description: NZTA is conducting widespread road surface integrity assessment across Wellington's transport network. Hundreds of locations identified requiring repair or closure.

title: Sewage system dysfunction reported, waterfront areas
description: Wellington Water confirms sewer main ruptures in Thorndon and Te Aro. Liquefaction has broken 3+ critical trunk lines. Potential public health risk.

title: Stuff reports building safety concerns affecting CBD employment districts
description: Media investigation identifying multiple high-rise structures with visible damage. Occupants reporting interior deformation and safety hazards.

title: Te Aro building subsidence emergency declaration
description: Apartment tower on The Esplanade declared emergency red-zone by WCC. Ground-level liquefaction causing building tilt; residents evacuating with assistance.

title: Te Aro residential facade separation threat
description: Residential building on Adelaide Road showing exterior facade separating at corner joints. Debris risk below assessed; temporary netting installed.

title: Te Aro retail district power loss continues, businesses affected
description: Lambton Quay retailers report ongoing blackout; shop owners concerned about food spoilage and security systems. Wellington Electricity crews visible in area.

title: Te Aro: Vivian Street rupture deepens, surrounding buildings at risk
description: Vivian Street crack now 0.6m wide and continuing to widen. Buildings on both sides showing structural distress. Evacuation radius expanded to 100m.

title: Temporary Petone power feed energized - 60% restored

title: Thorndon QuayResidential building water damage

title: Thorndon commercial district flooding escalates
description: Second main rupture detected on Thorndon Quay near the museum entrance. Flooding now affecting 200+ metre stretch of central thoroughfare. Traffic diverted. Water still flowing at high rate.

title: Thorndon liquefaction reaching maximum documented subsidence depth
description: Ground displacement measurements showing up to 40cm settlement in localized areas. Fissure patterns expanding with continued aftershock activity.

title: Wadestown Terrace: slip stabilises, diversion routes mapped
description: Initial slip on Wadestown Terrace appears stable. WCC establishing two-way single-lane diversion via alternative routes. Emergency access restored to 8 isolated properties.

title: Water pooling at intersection near Wellington Rail Station
description: Rail Station area flooding from ruptured main near platform access. Water level rising, platform becoming inaccessible.

title: Water supply restoration timeline unclear
description: Wellington Water advises major repairs needed to water network. No timeline yet for restoration in affected areas. Emergency water rationing measures implemented.

title: Water supply to Lower Hutt residential areas cut
description: Residential suburbs around Lower Hutt CBD completely without water. Multiple main ruptures affecting area.

title: Wellington to Lower Hutt rail corridor: Service suspended indefinitely
description: KiwiRail has issued a full suspension of Metlink services between Wellington and Lower Hutt due to multiple failures requiring detailed assessment and repair.

title: Aftershock M2.9 near Orient Avenue coastal area
description: Magnitude 2.9 aftershock at -41.29, 174.80, depth 9 km. Coastal waterfront zone experiencing ongoing minor shaking.

title: Berth 3 container gantry crane destroyed - infrastructure loss
description: Gantry crane at Berth 3 CentrePort overturned during quake; machinery destroyed. Estimated repair cost $2.4m; berth unusable for weeks.

title: CBD building wall bowing outward - Cuba Street
description: Office building on Cuba Street showing outer wall bowing away from main structure at mid-level. Immediate structural failure risk; all occupants evacuated.

title: CBD commercial property insurance assessments initiating across waterfront
description: Building damage surveys beginning as insurers respond. Structural assessments documenting liquefaction-related harm.

title: Commercial building failure at Onslow Road, rescue assessment underway
description: Significant building damage confirmed on Onslow Road. Structural engineers being consulted; area cordoned pending assessment.

title: Community: Wadestown residential road fractured; access limited
description: Resident on Wadestown Road reports longitudinal cracks and 5cm step in asphalt. Upper properties potentially cut off.

title: Council people assessing damage on Ghuznee Street, looks bad
description: Community alerts of engineering assessment at Ghuznee Street. Locals describe rapid response deployment.

title: Daniel reports earthquake experienced in Brooklyn
description: Felt light shaking in Brooklyn. Aftershock was noticeable but relatively minor in intensity.

title: Gas odour reports trigger precautionary closures
description: Multiple gas odour reports in Thorndon and CBD trigger precautionary street closures. Gas company checking for main ruptures.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 6.5 confirmed, revised depth 11 km
description: Updated preliminary analysis confirms magnitude 6.5, depth 11 km, epicentre -41.28, 174.76. Analysis of seismic signals supports Wellington Fault rupture hypothesis.

title: Hataitai evacuation of medical-device dependent residents complete
description: All residents requiring continuous power successfully transferred to hospital facilities. Community outreach continuing for remaining vulnerable populations.

title: Highway intersection Petone: Multi-direction closure
description: Multiple road intersections at Petone are showing severe subsidence affecting all approach directions. Complete intersection closures implemented with detour routes established.

title: Johnsonville residential area water outage
description: Supply pressure loss detected in Johnsonville precinct. Distribution main break suspected in Johnsonville Road corridor. Approximately 1800 residents reporting no water supply.

title: Just saw whole Taranaki Street building collapse, people running everywhere!
description: Eyewitness reports significant building collapse on Taranaki Street. Residents fleeing area; emergency services en route.

title: Karori water supply restored to parts of suburb despite damage
description: Wellington Scoop reports Wellington Water successfully isolating damaged sections on SH2. Portions of Karori now receiving partial water supply.

title: Kilbirnie Airport Road: liquefaction continues, pavement failure spreading
description: Rongotai Road liquefaction zone expanding. Additional 200m of road now affected by settlement. Airport access road traffic reduced to one lane each direction.

title: Kilbirnie ground surface failures creating hazardous pedestrian conditions
description: Multiple uneven ground conditions and fissures across residential areas. Residents warned to avoid damaged zones pending safety assessments.

title: Liquid cargo tank support structure damaged
description: Storage tank support frame at CentrePort liquid import facility showing weld damage. Structural integrity assessment immediate; tank contents secured.

title: Lower Hutt heritage building damaged
description: Historic stone church on Riddiford Street sustaining mortar joint failure and exterior wall cracking. Interior assessment indicates potential structural instability.

title: M3.2 aftershock magnitude near Mount Victoria
description: Seismograph network recorded magnitude 3.2 aftershock at 08:13. Located near Mount Victoria area. Aftershock sequence shows declining pattern.

title: Miramar residential area advisories issued
description: WCC advises residents of Miramar streets experiencing severe liquefaction to remain in place or evacuate to high ground. Building safety cannot be assured.

title: Miramar slip: geotechnical assessment reveals three separate zones
description: Miramar Cutting now shows three distinct slip zones along 500m stretch. All need independent stabilisation. Both SH2 directions completely closed.

title: Mount Victoria hill area isolation complete
description: Wellington Water completed isolation of Mount Victoria supply zone to prevent further cascading pressure failures. Isolated 3000+ residents until main breaks repair completed.

title: Multiple water main ruptures detected in Seaview area
description: Wellington Water crews responding to three simultaneous ruptures in port industrial area. Liquefaction has fractured underground main network.

title: Petone industrial operations halted, safety protocols initiated
description: Factories and manufacturing facilities in Petone industrial zone offline. Hazmat storage areas on emergency monitoring; safety assessment underway.

title: Port container storage: Hazardous goods investigation
description: CentrePort is investigating potential damage to hazardous goods containers in storage yards. A precautionary perimeter has been established; no active leaks reported.

title: Regional injury toll climbing: 65 confirmed patients in hospital
description: RNZ reports updated injury count across Capital and Coast DHB and Hutt Valley DHB facilities. Both systems now operating at elevated capacity.

title: Rongotai ground deformation affecting airport runway surface integrity
description: Airport operations pausing runway safety inspections. Ground fissuring visible on perimeter areas with potential subsurface infrastructure impacts.

title: Rongotai surface ruptures widen over time
description: Follow-up photos show initial ground fissures around Rongotai have widened to 30+ cm. Continued ground creep suspected as liquefied layer adjusts.

title: SH2 Hutt: shuttle traffic operation beginning on north-bound lane
description: Traffic management company deploying shuttle bus system for private vehicles. Estimated 45-min wait between batches.

title: Seaview port communications operating on portable radios
description: CentrePort maritime communications on emergency backup frequencies. Vessel tracking and coordination limited to radio contact.

title: Severe liquefaction Louden Street
description: Severe ground instability reported around Louden Street, multiple properties sinking, pavements buckling.

title: Structural damage Cable Street
description: Severe damage to heritage building at Cable Street, facade in danger of falling.

title: Subsidence at Civic Square
description: Ground fissures appearing on Civic Square, buildings subsiding visibly, surface sand/mud erupting.

title: Te Aro CBD still offline - secondary damage discovery
description: Inspection revealed damage to junction box near Willis Street. Additional repair needed before main substation can safely accept load.

title: Te Aro storefront glass failure widespread
description: Multiple storefront glass panels failing throughout The Esplanade retail precinct. Shattered glass creating hazard; area sectioned off by authorities.

title: Te Aro water distribution system partial collapse
description: Wellington Water reports 70% loss of pressure in Te Aro zone. Multiple simultaneous ruptures.

title: Thorndon rail infrastructure - major damage assessment
description: Metlink engineers confirm severe infrastructure damage at Thorndon rail complex. Track realignment, ballast replacement, and bridge assessment required.

title: Urgent engineering survey: Cuba Street complex
description: Emergency structural assessment team dispatched to Cuba Street. Damage severity being documented for repair planning.

title: Urgent engineering survey: Hunter Street complex
description: Emergency structural assessment team dispatched to Hunter Street. Damage severity being documented for repair planning.

title: Wall failure Kelburn Parade
description: Storefront collapsed at Kelburn Parade, merchandise and materials in roadway.

title: Water main pressure loss affecting Te Aro apartment buildings
description: Residential apartment towers in Te Aro without pressure. Upper floor residents unable to access water.

title: Wellington Water pressure stabilizing in restored areas
description: Now that Petone and Porirua feeds online, water pump stations receiving power. Pressure rebuilding across network. No supply interruption expected now.

title: Aftershock M3.4 near Newtown suburb
description: Magnitude 3.4 aftershock at -41.31, 174.78, depth 10 km. Continued aftershock activity with gradual magnitude decrease.

title: Aftershock M3.5 detected near Newtown area
description: GeoNet network recorded magnitude 3.5 aftershock at 08:14. Epicentre near Newtown suburb. Ongoing aftershock activity continues.

title: Aftershock M3.5 southwest of Oriental Bay
description: Magnitude 3.5 aftershock at -41.30, 174.79, depth 9 km. Central waterfront area continuing seismic activity.

title: Brooklyn slip zone: stabilisation netting deployed, debris 70% cleared
description: Highland Park Road slip zone: stabilisation netting in place. Debris clearance 70% complete. Estimated opening within 2 hours.

title: Building damage hotline activated
description: Wellington City Council launches emergency hotline for building damage reporting and requests for structural assessments.

title: CBD business disruption: major employers halting operations
description: 1News reports multiple CBD employers extending closures. Westpac, ASB, and other major employers suspending operations pending safety assessments.

title: CBD commercial district showing progressive structural degradation
description: Multiple building safety inspections ongoing. Cladding failures and internal cracking patterns suggesting ongoing movement and settling.

title: Emergency services coordination: unified command centre activated
description: Newshub reports WREMO activating unified emergency operations centre. Multi-agency coordination between Fire, Police, and Civil Defence underway.

title: Flooding at Hutt Valley business park area
description: Business park near Lower Hutt CBD experiencing extensive flooding. Multiple main ruptures affecting precinct.

title: Gas leak emergency at Onslow Road
description: Suspected gas main rupture at Onslow Road, Newtown. Evacuation zone 100m radius established; utility repair crew dispatched.

title: Glass facade failure at CBD office building
description: Entire curtain wall section on Featherston Street office tower failing; large glass panes falling to street. Ground-level area completely closed; debris cleanup ongoing.

title: Ground fissure Molesworth Street
description: Massive settlement on Molesworth Street, houses subsiding into soil, residents evacuating.

title: Karori Road: multiple slips active, slip risk elevated across wider area
description: Karori Road main slip now 250m3. Three new slips detected upslope. Geotechnical team deployed to map hazard zones. Diversions to Brooklyn Road now 60+ minute delays.

title: Karori residents extending generator pool to assist neighbors
description: Neighborhood association coordinating fuel distribution and generator access. Elderly residents receiving priority support from younger community members.

title: Kilbirnie airport sector liquefaction: critical infrastructure threatened
description: Newshub reports liquefaction affecting airport operations area with lateral spread visible on taxiway. Runway closure likely to be extended.

title: Lower Hutt parking structure level slab cracked
description: Multi-level parking structure showing cracked concrete slab on Level 2 with potential structural compromise. Vehicles removed; structural evaluation urgent.

title: Lower Hutt restoration crews deployed to affected zones
description: Wellington Electricity teams mobilizing across Hutt Valley. Damage assessment crews identifying priority areas for restoration; temporary lighting stations being set up.

title: Major building damage on Rutherford Street, Lower Hutt CBD
description: Building collapse on Rutherford Street confirmed by first responders. Damage extent being evaluated.

title: Minor shaking continues to be felt
description: Ongoing light tremors reported throughout Wellington region. Aftershock activity continues with minor intensity.

title: Newtown area lights flickering as restoration proceeds
description: Power being restored in stages to Newtown network. Some streets now have electricity. Full area restoration estimated within 15-20 minutes.

title: Ngaio tunnel rail structure - minor damage only
description: Railway tunnel at Ngaio showing minor cracks in ceiling; no major structural compromise detected. Services remain suspended pending final clearance.

title: Oriental Bay drive: Coastal slumping affecting southern access
description: Oriental Bay Drive shows localized slumping of the roadway in the southern section. Emergency restrictions are in place; engineering survey commencing immediately.

title: Petrochemical storage tank integrity concerns, Seaview
description: Local refinery reports structural monitoring of large storage tanks showing subsidence-induced tilt. All non-essential staff evacuated, situation being managed.

title: Port fuel supply pipeline: Integrity checks urgent
description: CentrePort fuel supply pipeline is undergoing urgent integrity checks following ground movement. Fuel transfers are suspended until pipeline safety is confirmed.

title: Rongotai Bridge seismic movement detected - closure ordered
description: Rongotai Bridge showing residual movement post-quake. Traffic restricted to single lane northbound; monitoring continues every 15 minutes.

title: Rongotai airport water emergency action plan
description: Airport authority implements emergency water rationing protocol. Fire suppression backup systems activated. Water truck positioned at apron for emergency fire protection.

title: Rongotai operational assessment of airport-proximate infrastructure
description: Airport authority conducting detailed inspection of perimeter systems. Ground liquefaction impacts being systematically documented.

title: Severe liquefaction The Esplanade
description: Sand boils and settlement damage on The Esplanade, homes tilting, underground services exposed.

title: Structural collapse at Onslow Road
description: Onslow Road building failure reported; Fire and Emergency establishing safety perimeter. Rescue operations commencing.

title: Te Aro precinct multiple rupture scenario
description: At least 3 separate water main breaks confirmed on Cuba Street, Dixon Street, and Vivian Street within Te Aro block. Flooding widespread. Underground liquefaction causing ground instability.

title: Thorndon Quay historic building water infiltration
description: Historic building on Thorndon Quay sustaining significant water damage from main rupture flooding. Ground floor museum exhibits at risk. Water mitigation underway.

title: Thorndon Quays partial restoration via secondary feeder
description: Alternative feed from northern network successfully restoring Thorndon Quarter. Approximately 2,200 customers now back online.

title: Thorndon Quays seawall: structural inspection flagged stability risk
description: WCC engineers report visible cracks in waterfront retaining wall. Quays vehicle access suspended pending geotechnical review.

title: Wall failure River Road
description: Residential collapse on River Road, neighbors attempting rescue efforts.

title: Water flooding in Miramar affecting utility infrastructure
description: Water mains rupture on Miramar Avenue creating extensive street-level flooding. Electrical substations in proximity requiring emergency isolation.

title: Water supply interrupted to schools across city
description: Wellington schools reporting no water. Supply disruption affecting sanitation and emergency response capabilities.

title: Wellington CBD multiple building facades showing cracks
description: WCC safety teams cordoning off three building perimeters on Lambton Quay. Falling debris risk requiring full street closure.

title: Aftershock M2.7 near Hataitai beach zone
description: Magnitude 2.7 aftershock at -41.31, 174.80, depth 9 km. Eastern waterfront area experiencing very minor continued seismic activity.

title: Airport emergency response: Full coordination underway
description: Wellington Airport emergency response is fully activated with ground crews conducting comprehensive runway and taxiway damage assessment in coordination with emergency services.

title: Berthing dolphin structure - minor impact damage
description: One of CentrePort's berthing dolphins showing minor concrete impact damage from vessel contact during seismic event. Repairs planned; berth usable with caution.

title: Communication disruption: cell network congestion across region
description: Stuff reports 2degrees and Vodafone networks experiencing congestion. Citizens Advice urging public to use text messaging over voice calls.

title: Engineering assessment underway at Featherston Street
description: Structural survey underway on Featherston Street following preliminary damage indication. Formal assessment report to follow.

title: First responders mobilising across Wellington region
description: Fire and Emergency NZ crews dispatching to assess reported damage in CBD, Petone, and Lower Hutt. St John Ambulance on standby for casualties.

title: Flooding reported at Petone community centre
description: Community centre facilities flooded from main rupture. Building becoming unusable as emergency response location.

title: Grid failure Marine Drive
description: Loss of power affecting Marine Drive district, residents without heating/lighting.

title: Hutt Line Taita viaduct: Bridge inspection urgent
description: The viaduct carrying the Hutt Line over the Hutt River at Taita is showing visible deflection and joint separation. KiwiRail has closed the entire crossing pending engineering assessment.

title: Hutt Valley rail passengers evacuated at Waiwhetu
description: Hutt Line train halted between Waiwhetu and Taita; 240 passengers walking to safety. Emergency buses organizing transport to welfare centre.

title: Island Bay water rationing distributed via truck
description: Wellington Water dispatch water truck to Island Bay precinct. 5000-litre tank positioned at local primary school. Residents collecting rationed water supplies.

title: Karori Ballantrae: aftershock triggers secondary soil movement
description: M4.8 aftershock at 07:48 causes additional creep on already-damaged slope. Residents re-evacuated as precaution.

title: Karori community generators operating near fuel capacity limits
description: Neighborhood coordinators rationing generator use to extend operational time. Residents preparing alternative heating methods for evening.

title: Karori main break affecting hill suburbs cascade
description: Primary rupture on Karori Road at base of hill causing secondary distribution failures up the hill. Pressure cascading failure affecting 4000+ residents. Water rationing implemented.

title: Kilbirnie full restoration completed
description: All protective relays cleared and secondary feeder confirmed stable. Kilbirnie zone now fully energized. 2,100 customers back online.

title: Lateral spread Cable Street
description: Massive settlement on Cable Street, houses subsiding into soil, residents evacuating.

title: Liquefaction & flooding combined in low zones, Petone
description: Localised flooding in Petone CBD reaching up to 40 cm depth in depressions; standing water mixed with liquefied sediment creating impassable areas.

title: Liquefaction depth drilling program begins
description: Geotechnical team initiates exploratory boring program to determine liquefaction depth and extent for engineering design of remediation.

title: Lower Hutt industrial area generating own power
description: Three large industrial facilities have fired up on-site generators. Minimum operations maintained. Regional generators becoming key to response.

title: M3.1 aftershock recorded near Wadestown
description: Magnitude 3.1 aftershock detected by seismographs at 08:15. Located near Wadestown hill area. Aftershock activity remains present but weakening.

title: Miramar residential damage assessment underway by engineers
description: RNZ reports structural engineers beginning systematic building-by-building inspection in Miramar. Preliminary findings showing widespread liquefaction damage.

title: Mount Victoria: Ellice Street slip, minor but growing
description: Minor slip on Ellice Street near Mount Victoria summit. Small debris pile (20m3) but accelerating. Monitoring ongoing. Road remains passable with care.

title: Multiple gas leaks along Te Aro street
description: Kent Terrace and surrounding area reporting multiple gas odours from damaged underground networks. SCADA systems show pressure drops; evacuation zone expanding.

title: Newtown school roof damage report
description: Constable Street school sustaining roof damage with panels lifting and partial water penetration. Children evacuated; temporary weather tarp being installed.

title: Petone community assessment of neighbourhood impacts
description: Residents conducting informal damage surveys. Multiple properties affected by both liquefaction and water damage.

title: Petone liquefaction zone expanding with continuing ground deformation
description: Sand boils and ground fissures now visible across broader area of Petone residential zone. Subsidence pattern affecting multiple residential properties.

title: Petone retail strip business disruption
description: Jackson Street retail precinct in Petone showing multiple broken shop fronts and structural cracks. Businesses damaged or destroyed by liquefaction effects.

title: Port vessel at berth secured - evacuation complete
description: Container ship at CentrePort secured successfully after emergency procedures. Crew evacuation complete; vessel structural assessment underway.

title: SH1 Northbound: excavator on-site starting debris removal at Ngauranga
description: Heavy equipment clearing three lanes worth of fallen rock. Northbound flow restricted to shoulder and emergency lane.

title: Seaview port area sustaining compounding damage from liquefaction and water inundation
description: Combined effects of ground liquefaction and water main breaks creating complex damage scenario. CentrePort operations significantly compromised.

title: Seaview port facilities operating on backup power systems
description: CentrePort activating diesel generators for critical infrastructure. Container cranes and cargo systems on limited power; operations reduced to essential functions.

title: Severe building damage reported in Newtown after earthquake

title: Split Rock: continued monitoring reveals instability, northbound backed
description: SH1 Split Rock section northbound backed up 5km due to controlled single-lane convoy. Ongoing rockfall risk. NZTA expecting partial reopening by mid-morning.

title: Surface rupture The Esplanade
description: The Esplanade completely blocked by debris and settlement cracks, impassable.

title: Susan reports earthquake felt in Island Bay
description: Felt weak to light shaking in Island Bay neighbourhood. Aftershock caused minimal disturbance to surroundings.

title: Thorndon Quay flooding restricts vehicle access
description: Thorndon Quay submerged in water from multiple main ruptures. Access to government buildings restricted due to flooding.

title: Transformer failure Louden Street
description: Loss of power affecting Louden Street district, residents without heating/lighting.

title: Water pooling at Petone ferry terminal
description: Ferry terminal area flooded from main break. Ferry operations affected by water damage.

title: Wellington Airport terminal building losing water pressure gradually
description: Airport facilities reporting water system degradation. Emergency water storage activated; hygiene facilities limited pending power restoration.

title: 1News reports structural emergency affecting CBD office buildings
description: Television news documenting building damage across employment districts. Occupants evacuating with increasing severity of structural concerns.

title: Aftershock 4.0M recorded, sequence intensity decreasing gradually
description: Newshub reports GeoNet noting aftershock magnitude declining slightly. Monitoring networks continuing 24-hour seismic tracking.

title: Aftershock M2.8 near Mount Victoria hilltop
description: Magnitude 2.8 aftershock at -41.30, 174.79, depth 10 km. Central hill suburb experiencing reduced aftershock activity.

title: Aftershock M3.4 magnitude near Johnsonville
description: GeoNet seismograph network recorded magnitude 3.4 aftershock at 08:16. Epicentre near Johnsonville area. Continued aftershock sequence activity.

title: Airport control tower communication check - functioning
description: Wellington Airport control tower communication systems verified operational. Navigation systems tested and functioning; reduced traffic handled manually.

title: Blockage The Terrace
description: Major road closure at The Terrace, ground rupture visible, barriers needed.

title: Brooklyn Hill community center preparing extended overnight shelter
description: Emergency services establishing proper shelter operations. Cots, blankets, and basic supplies being arranged for residents unable to return home.

title: Brooklyn retail precinct emergency closure
description: Retailers on Brooklyn Road closing businesses due to water supply loss and potential sewage backup risk. Commercial district functioning at 20% capacity.

title: Building structural failure reported on Civic Square
description: Civic Square building failure reported; Fire and Emergency establishing safety perimeter. Rescue operations commencing.

title: CBD heritage timber building risk assessment
description: Historic timber building on Customhouse Quay showing joint separation and beam cracking. Heritage status requires specialist assessment; temporary support structures planned.

title: CBD pipe penetration failure in building

title: CentrePort vessel berthed at Quay 1: Emergency evacuation
description: A general cargo vessel berthed at Quay 1 has been evacuated as a precaution due to wharf stability concerns. Crew to temporary facility; vessel securing operations underway.

title: Commercial building failure at Margaret Street, rescue assessment underway
description: Fire and Emergency responding to building damage report on Margaret Street. Preliminary assessment indicates severe structural compromise.

title: Gas main rupture suspected on Civic Square

title: Hataitai tunnel partially blocked - rock fall

title: Island Bay slope: small debris pile on road edge; passable with caution

title: Kilbirnie isthmus: subsidence affecting Omāroro Stream access road
description: Low-lying flood plain area sinking unevenly. Stream overflowing banks from compaction. Road impassable for standard vehicles.

description: Evans Bay Parade intersection experiencing heavy congestion; traffic manually directed by police officers. Residents report dangerous driving conditions.

title: Liquefaction damage in Miramar spreading: additional homes evacuated
description: 1News reports escalating evacuation in Miramar residential zone. 30+ homes now marked as immediately unsafe requiring relocation.

title: Major building damage on Margaret Street, Lower Hutt CBD
description: Critical building damage on Margaret Street. Fire and Emergency teams performing triage on site.

title: Miramar eastern approach: slip zone mapped, stabilisation plan drafted
description: Miramar Cutting eastern slip zone mapped. Stabilisation approach identified. Multiple slip zones complicate recovery timeline.

title: Multi-storey building damage reported on Courtenay Place
description: Significant building damage confirmed on Courtenay Place. Structural engineers being consulted; area cordoned pending assessment.

title: Oh my god Main Street is a disaster, building front just came down

title: Oriental Bay lights returning - phased restoration successful
description: Phased load restoration to Oriental Bay complete. Full power confirmed. Traffic signals and street lighting now operational.

description: Wellington residents continue reporting very faint shaking. Aftershock sequence gradually decreasing in intensity.

description: Large rupture beneath Featherston Street causing rapid ground collapse. Water fountaining from ground, traffic diverted.

description: Utility company reviewing main break locations and prioritising repairs. Temporary supply solutions being considered.

description: Wellington Scoop reports WCC arranging additional emergency welfare facilities at schools and community halls. Town Hall at capacity with 800+ residents.

title: Aftershock M3.1 beneath Lower Hutt CBD area

title: Brooklyn hill water distribution pipe isolated

title: Brooklyn hillside: minor rockfall hazard identified on ridge
description: Stuff reports Geological Survey noting potential rockfall hazard on Brooklyn ridge. Area cordoned off pending hazard assessment.

title: Brooklyn shelter now accommodating 150+ residents overnight
description: Emergency shelter expanding; additional supplies being delivered. Medical volunteer present; organizing morning meal provision.

title: Multiple road corridors closed - major congestion building

title: Newtown house foundation cracked by liquefaction
description: Residential house on Main Street showing foundation cracking and differential settlement from ground movement. Occupants evacuated; structural inspection requested.

title: Oh my god Riverside Drive is a disaster, building front just came down
description: Residents posting video of damage at Riverside Drive; structural failure clearly visible. Community assistance efforts starting.

title: Petone connector liquefaction: sand boils erupting in warehouse yard

description: CentrePort teams documenting comprehensive infrastructure impacts. Port recovery timeline being assessed.

title: Water main isolation protocols implemented across northern zones

description: Major feeder main broken at Karori zone boundary. Supply to northern suburbs severely compromised.

title: Waterfront building evacuation zone expanded
description: Evacuation zone along waterfront expanded to 100 metre corridor due to continued ground movement and building damage risk.

title: Wellington Airport restoration timeline extended to 2+ hours
description: Airport management updates that runway lighting and navigation systems require extended repair time. Flight operations remain suspended indefinitely.

title: Widespread power loss River Road
description: Loss of power affecting River Road district, residents without heating/lighting.

title: Aftershock M2.6 near Seaview port zone
description: Magnitude 2.6 aftershock at -41.24, 174.91, depth 9 km. Industrial and port area experiencing minor continued activity.

title: Building partially down Cuba Street

description: Main junction box splice complete. Protective relay testing now underway. Fenton Substation attempting third soft-start sequence within 30 minutes.

description: Wellington Water unable to contain main breaks. Pressure loss spreading across distribution network affecting downtown residential and commercial zones.

title: Closure at Hill Street
description: Hill Street blocked by fallen building material, heavy traffic diversion required.

title: Container transport truck damage at CentrePort

title: Faint aftershock tremors continuing
description: Residents throughout region report barely perceptible shaking. Aftershock sequence nearing end of noticeable activity.

title: Flooding affecting underground mall at Wellington CBD
description: Underground shopping mall experiencing water ingress. Main rupture beneath mall structure.

title: Flooding reported in basement of Parliament House
description: Parliament House basement experiencing water ingress. Rupture suspected beneath building. Water affecting infrastructure systems.

title: Ground instability Willis Street

title: Lateral spread Kent Terrace
description: Severe ground instability reported around Kent Terrace, multiple properties sinking, pavements buckling.

title: Miramar equipment fire extinguished - no power from damaged zone
description: Transformer fire at Miramar substation fully controlled. Equipment beyond recovery. Will operate via northern feed until replacement transformer sourced.

title: Miramar suburb power still out, residents seek shelter

description: Wellington City Council mobile support units delivering emergency water supplies to Mount Victoria elderly and vulnerable residents. 500 litres delivered per household.

title: Multi-storey building damage reported on Queens Drive

title: No power in Ngaio
description: Loss of power affecting Broadway district, residents without heating/lighting.

title: Port container yard safety: Perimeter extended
description: CentrePort has extended safety perimeter around container yard due to continued risk of container stack movement. Salvage operations staged in safe zones.

title: Quay Street eastbound access: temporary repairs begun

title: Quay Street rail line displacement - track out of spec

title: Railway tunnel Thorndon: Lining inspection initiated
description: The railway tunnel through Thorndon shows water seepage at multiple points. Detailed structural inspection is being mobilised; train operations remain suspended.

title: Rongotai liquefaction assessment revealing extensive ground displacements
description: Engineering teams confirming major subsidence patterns across airport periphery. Structural integrity of buildings and facilities compromised.

title: Roof damage Broadway

title: Roof damage Customhouse Quay
description: Building at Customhouse Quay partially collapsed, rubble blocking road, people calling for help.

title: SH1 Northbound: first vehicle convoy through Ngauranga Gorge
description: NZTA opens single-lane northbound convoy through SH1 Ngauranga Gorge. Controlled traffic, 20-minute headway. Debris removal continuing during breaks.

title: Structural damage Vivian Street
description: Multiple building failures on Vivian Street, windows shattered, debris everywhere.

title: Structural integrity concerns escalate, Thorndon blocks
description: Building inspectors report multiple office buildings showing post-liquefaction settlement cracks, window frame misalignment, and door jam failures.

title: Te Aro building slab-on-grade distress
description: Ground floor concrete slab showing displacement and cracking in commercial building on Vivian Street. Underlying soil movement detected; geotechnical assessment ordered.

title: Te Aro flooding: Hazmat team deployment
description: Hazmat response team deployed to Te Aro precinct due to potential cross-contamination between water and wastewater systems. Taranaki Street closure extended to 300 metres.

description: Fire and Emergency personnel conducting ground stability assessments. Monitoring for developing ground hazards.

title: Wadestown neighbors sharing meals and generator resources

title: Aftershock 3.8M recorded; series intensity subsiding
description: Stuff reports GeoNet confirming continued decreasing aftershock magnitude trend. Monitoring continuing but no expected major additional shocks.

title: Aftershock M2.7 near Johnsonville northern suburb
description: Magnitude 2.7 aftershock at -41.23, 174.81, depth 11 km. Northern suburbs experiencing minor continued seismic activity.

title: Building compromise Louden Street
description: Structural failure at Louden Street - walls cracked, roof partial collapse, injuries reported.

title: Building foundation support verification ongoing
description: Geotechnical teams conducting bearing capacity assessment of foundations in high-risk areas. Many buildings showing signs of losing structural support.

title: Central Wellington water demand spike detection
description: SCADA shows dramatic pressure fluctuations across CBD network. Multiple simultaneous ruptures causing complex flow patterns. Emergency containment procedures activated in 4 supply zones.

title: Community: Johnsonville backstreet slips blocking school route
description: Multiple residents report short local slips on hillside properties. One family's driveway buried under 1m of soil.

title: Flooding in Lower Hutt CBD office district
description: Commercial buildings in Lower Hutt CBD flooded from water main breaks. Businesses closing due to water damage.

title: Geotechnical emergency team deployment, Kilbirnie
description: Specialist geotechnical engineers begin site assessments in Kilbirnie industrial district to evaluate foundation stability and ground conditions post-liquefaction.

title: Historical building structural monitoring installed

title: Johnsonville rail line: points misaligned at junction
description: Railway points at Johnsonville junction stuck in safety position after mainshock. Mechanical reset required; all Johnsonville trains cancelled.

title: Johnsonville temporary water hauling station
description: Wellington Water establishes temporary water distribution station on Johnsonville Road. Residents providing own containers. 500 litres per hour capacity available.

title: Kilbirnie: liquefaction effects worsening, sinking continues
description: Rongotai Road subsidence accelerating. Total settlement now 0.7m over 300m. Additional utilities potentially exposed. Wellington Water emergency team standby.
