description: Photos from residents show sand boils and lateral ground displacement on Petone foreshores. Seawalls and wharf structures showing signs of ground deformation.

title: M2.9 aftershock recorded near Petone area

title: Miramar residents rationing water due to pressure loss
description: Eastern suburb residents instructed to use water sparingly. Wellington Water activating temporary supply trucks for affected areas.

title: Newtown hospitals on full backup generator status

title: Ngauranga Gorge traffic camera: debris clearing 60% complete

title: No power in Te Aro
description: Loss of power affecting Cuba Street district, residents without heating/lighting.

title: Oh my god Cuba Street is a disaster, building front just came down
description: Residents posting video of damage at Cuba Street; structural failure clearly visible. Community assistance efforts starting.

title: Oriental Bay esplanade: temporary pedestrian access restored
description: The Parade (Oriental Bay) cleared of main debris. Pedestrian access restored via temporary walkway. Vehicle access still prohibited. Tsunami alert downgraded.

description: Felt mild shaking near Ngauranga Gorge corridor. Aftershock was noticeable by residents near motorway.

title: Road blocked Civic Square

title: State Highway 2 Ngauranga section - one lane open

title: Aftershock M2.9 near Ngaio suburb
description: Magnitude 2.9 aftershock at -41.25, 174.77, depth 10 km. Aftershock sequence continuing with diminishing intensity.

description: Wellington Water teams have located 2 of 4 suspected main ruptures in Oriental Bay precinct. Isolation and repair procedures beginning. Supply restoration estimated 2-3 hours.

title: Petone damage extent assessments showing two dozen significantly affected properties
description: Detailed property inspection results indicating widespread but variable impact. Some homes requiring extensive repairs.

title: Petone emergency water truck coordination

title: Power cut Civic Square
description: Rolling blackouts hitting Civic Square area, voltage collapse observed.

title: Rongotai airport road: traffic resumed at reduced speed
description: Pavement still showing buckling but deemed passable with 30 km/h limit. Engineering monitoring ongoing.

title: Rongotai comprehensive ground assessment confirming major subsurface changes
description: Satellite imaging and field data showing consistent displacement pattern across eastern suburbs. Airport area particularly affected.

title: Sand boils observed Devon Street
description: Severe ground instability reported around Devon Street, multiple properties sinking, pavements buckling.

title: Seaview industrial port road: liquefaction damage to access pavement
description: Low-lying port industrial zone experiencing surface settlement. Vessel access road cracked; port operations affected.

title: Split Rock section: southbound lane reopened, northbound convoy system
description: SH1 southbound through Split Rock fully reopened. Northbound maintaining controlled convoy system. Travel delays reducing.

title: Surface rupture Customhouse Quay
description: Customhouse Quay completely blocked by debris and settlement cracks, impassable.

description: Te Aro Vivian Street rupture stops expanding at 1m width. Continuous monitoring equipment installed. CBD east access remaining closed. Building engineers assessing adjacent structures.

description: Large rupture beneath Civic Square near Opera House. Water flowing across main plaza. Liquefaction visible around break.

title: Wellington Airport terminal building partial interior damage
description: Airport terminal dropped ceiling panels and cracked walls in check-in area. Repairs blocking some facilities; terminal operations on restricted basis.

title: Wellington Water assessing water infrastructure damage
description: Wellington Water activated emergency assessment teams. Water mains and infrastructure systems being inspected for earthquake damage.

title: Aftershock 3.6M continues: residents maintaining vigilance
description: 1News reports another minor aftershock. GeoNet noting aftershock sequence now trending toward lower magnitude events.

title: Aftershock M2.8 near Thorndon industrial precinct
description: Magnitude 2.8 aftershock at -41.27, 174.79, depth 9 km. Continued minor seismic activity in central zone.

title: Airport Road Rongotai: two lanes restored with settlement barriers
description: Rongotai Airport Road settling stabilised. Two lanes restored with monitoring and speed restrictions. One-way pedestrian access alongside. Airport operations gradually resuming.

title: Container stacks unstable at berth 2 - evacuation zone
description: CentrePort establishing 100m exclusion zone around Berth 2 container stacks due to tipping risk. All personnel evacuated; stabilization operations begin.

title: Electrical outage The Terrace
description: Power out across The Terrace and surrounding blocks, no lights in area.

description: Electrical substation at Gracefield rail depot showing transformer damage. Manual isolation performed; temporary power from mobile generator arranged.

description: Person pinned on Jackson Street, firefighters attempting extraction.

title: Ground instability Featherston Street
description: Massive settlement on Featherston Street, houses subsiding into soil, residents evacuating.

title: Johnsonville medical facility fuel reserve at 60% capacity
description: Pharmacy director ensuring generator fuel supply will last until dawn. Limited hours being maintained; emergency medications only dispensing.

title: Kelburn to Te Aro descent: Road buckled in three locations

description: Fire crews receiving multiple calls from building occupants reporting cracks and damage. Precautionary evacuations continuing in CBD commercial zone.

title: Lower Hutt building corner separation expanding

title: Lower Hutt water pressure restoration start

title: Major structural failure at The Terrace, fire trucks everywhere now
description: Eyewitness reports significant building collapse on The Terrace. Residents fleeing area; emergency services en route.

title: Martin reports earthquake felt in Seaview
description: Felt weak shaking in Seaview industrial area. Aftershock was barely detectable.

title: Mount Victoria hill: minor surface cracks near lookout

title: Newtown apartment building wall separation
description: Apartment building on Vogel Street showing external wall separating from main structure at roof line and mid-level. Occupants sheltering in interior core pending assessment.

title: Newtown water supply concerns escalating, conservation measures urged
description: Newtown residents advised of potential water shortages. Wellington Water distributing bottled water to vulnerable populations.

title: Petone industrial area stability concerns
description: Industrial operations in Petone suspended pending geotechnical assessment of ground conditions. Multiple warehouses showing settling and cracking.

title: Petone industrial flooding containment efforts
description: Sandbags and temporary barriers deployed to contain water spreading from port precinct main breaks. Environmental assessment initiated. Estimated water loss rate 800 litres per minute.

title: Petone liquefaction damage assessment ongoing with additional areas affected
description: Ground deformation continuing to reveal new fissuring patterns. Residential areas showing progressive subsidence.

title: Port crane structural assessment: Ongoing evaluation
description: All port gantry cranes are undergoing detailed structural assessment to confirm integrity. No crane operations permitted until clearance is received.

title: Rongotai airport runway safety evaluation beginning systematically
description: Detailed surface assessment protocols initiated. Ground integrity under runway structures being evaluated.

title: Rongotai pavement: buckling stabilised; aircraft operations resuming
description: Temporary repairs to runway approach road complete. Light aircraft cleared for takeoff. Commercial flights still on hold.

title: Seaview industrial area water supply cut
description: Port precinct water supply completely cut due to multiple main ruptures. Industrial operations at standstill.

title: Seaview recovery challenges complicated by compounded infrastructure failures
description: CentrePort infrastructure sustaining multiple failure modes from liquefaction and water damage. Recovery prioritisation complex.

title: State Highway 1 through Porirua - traffic restricted
description: SH1 through Porirua implementing traffic restrictions due to pavement damage. Single-lane traffic management; additional lanes opening progressively.

title: Stuff reports over 70 percent metro area now with power
description: Major restoration milestone achieved 34 minutes after mainshock. CBD core now online. Island Bay and eastern suburbs being prioritized for final restoration.

title: Wadestown secondary slip: debris stabilising, emergency access secure
description: Wadestown Terrace secondary slip (reported 22 min) stabilising. Debris pile stable. Emergency single-lane access confirmed secure for isolated properties.

title: Wall failure Salamanca Road
description: Multiple building failures on Salamanca Road, windows shattered, debris everywhere.

title: Water main rupture at Seaview junction flooding precinct
description: Large rupture at major junction in Seaview. Water flowing across industrial precinct.

title: Water pooling affecting Thorndon government building precinct
description: Government buildings at Thorndon experiencing water damage from main rupture. Standing water in administrative areas.

title: Wellington CBD lights returning - mainshock response success

title: Aftershock M2.4 near Mount Victoria hilltop
description: Magnitude 2.4 aftershock at -41.30, 174.79, depth 10 km. Hill suburb experiencing very minor continued seismic activity.

title: Aftershock M2.8 near Island Bay coastal suburb
description: Magnitude 2.8 aftershock at -41.35, 174.77, depth 10 km. Southern coastal suburbs continuing aftershock experience.

title: Aftershock M3.0 magnitude detected near Te Aro
description: GeoNet seismographs recorded magnitude 3.0 aftershock at 08:24. Epicentre near Te Aro zone. Continued minor aftershock activity.

title: Airport runway inspection results inconclusive - closure extended
description: Runway surface inspection shows deep cracking pattern requiring expert assessment. Closure extended; reconstruction timeline pending detailed evaluation.

title: Brooklyn Highland Park: secondary slip halted, stabilisation begun
description: Secondary slip on Highland Park Road has slowed significantly. Stabilisation netting deployed on unstable slope. First slip debris clearance 50% complete.

title: CBD building soffit/fascia failure
description: Soffit and fascia elements failing at building corners on Willis Street. Pieces falling onto pavement; pedestrian area cordoned immediately.

title: Critical injury Customhouse Quay
description: Dozens of people seeking shelter at Customhouse Quay, several with cuts and bruises.

title: Facade collapse Customhouse Quay
description: Residential collapse on Customhouse Quay, neighbors attempting rescue efforts.

title: Flooding at intersection of Lambton and Bowen Streets
description: Water pooling across intersection from ruptured main. Estimated 1500+ cubic metres accumulated water blocking vehicle passage.

title: Grid failure Featherston Street
description: Power distribution failure on Featherston Street feeder, estimated 206 homes affected.

title: Hataitai power restoration completed successfully
description: Replacement transformer now fully energized and supplying network. All 1,800 Hataitai customers confirmed back online with stable supply.

title: Hataitai pressure restoration partial success
description: Wellington Water crews have successfully isolated main break on Hataitai main. Partial pressure restoration to eastern suburb achieved. Remaining 1000 residents still without supply.

title: Hutt Valley SH2: southbound completely reopened, traffic heavy
description: SH2 southbound fully reopened with 80km/h speed restrictions. Traffic volume high. Emergency transport priority maintained.

title: Island Bay full power restoration confirmed
description: New cable section now accepting full load. Voltage stable. All safety tests passed. 2,800 Island Bay and surrounding customers now online.

title: Karori power still out, residents rationing generator fuel
description: Ascot Street residents coordinating shared generator use. Neighborhood watch organizing security patrols during extended outage.

title: Karori residential: slope monitoring showing stabilisation signs
description: Creep movement has slowed after initial 20 minutes. Residents cautiously allowed to remain but evacuation standby continuing.

title: Kilbirnie liquefaction progression reaching plateau in subsidence rate
description: Ground fissures stabilising after major displacement phase. Ongoing aftershock monitoring continuing for safety assessments.

title: Kilbirnie residential damage: 28 homes tagged for evacuation
description: RNZ reports WCC evacuation teams marking 28 Kilbirnie homes as immediately unsafe. Residents being transported to welfare facilities.

title: Medical emergency Courtenay Place
description: Critical injury on Courtenay Place, person unconscious, ambulance en route.

title: Miramar evacuation recommendations expanded
description: WCC expands evacuation recommendations to include additional streets in Miramar following structural assessment findings of widespread liquefaction impacts.

title: Multi-storey building damage reported on Panama Street
description: Major damage to structure at Panama Street now being documented. Occupants evacuated; no injuries reported at this time.

title: Ngauranga Gorge SH1: excavation progress slowed by unstable shoulder
description: Debris removal hampered by loose material on roadside. Crew unable to push debris off edge safely.

title: Oh no Civic Centre corner building is collapsing, get away everyone!
description: Community social media explosion from Civic Centre; photos showing major structural failure. Witnesses reporting debris across street.

title: Port breakwater integrity: Wave monitoring enhanced
description: CentrePort is enhancing wave monitoring systems and conducting visual inspection of breakwater integrity. Minor settling observed but structure remains sound.

title: Power cut Courtenay Place
description: Loss of power affecting Courtenay Place district, residents without heating/lighting.

title: Regional response escalation: Central Government coordination begins
description: Stuff reports Prime Minister's office coordinating with WREMO and local councils. Cabinet papers being prepared for emergency funding allocation.

title: Seaview emergency dewatering mobilization
description: Heavy machinery arrives to begin dewatering of industrial precinct. Pumping operations commence with environmental monitoring. Estimated 12-24 hours to clear flooding.

title: Seaview water main break repair priorities being established
description: Wellington Water coordinating emergency repairs with CentrePort. Water restoration timeline being set.

title: State Highway 1 southbound Ngauranga: still impassable
description: SH1 southbound through Ngauranga Gorge remains closed; rockfall clearance continuing. Diversions via State Highway 2 adding 25 minutes to journey times.

title: Surface rupture University Street
description: Severe ground instability reported around University Street, multiple properties sinking, pavements buckling.

title: Tawa shopping center closed, community rallying for support
description: Northern suburbs shopping area shuttered due to power loss. Residents organizing informal support networks at community gathering points.

title: Te Aro building deemed unsafe for re-entry
description: Structural assessment completed on Adelaide Road residential building; red-zone status confirmed due to foundation failure and tilt. Evacuation finalized; building locked down.

title: Te Aro building roof-to-wall connection failure
description: Residential building showing separation at roof-to-wall connection points on Adelaide Road. Roof stability at risk; emergency shoring underway.

title: Te Aro commercial district evacuation expansion
description: Wellington City Council expands Te Aro precinct evacuation due to ongoing flooding from multiple main ruptures and sewage backup risk. 100+ businesses affected.

title: Thorndon heritage structures requiring emergency stabilisation efforts
description: Historic buildings showing significant liquefaction damage. Conservation experts coordinating immediate support measures.

title: Trace level tremors reported
description: Residents report trace level shaking. Aftershock sequence approaching end of readily noticeable activity.

title: Wellington Station toilet facilities: Sewage system failure
description: Wellington Station toilet facilities are out of service due to sewage system failure from ground movement. Portable facilities being arranged; station operations continue with restrictions.

title: Aftershock M2.4 near Karori western suburbs
description: Magnitude 2.4 aftershock at -41.29, 174.73, depth 11 km. Very minor aftershock activity continues in hillside areas.

title: Airport runway mains feed being tested
description: Approach lighting transitioning from generators to main grid. Generators maintained as backup. Runway 16/34 now on primary power.

title: Blackout reported Civic Square
description: Loss of power affecting Civic Square district, residents without heating/lighting.

title: Bridge damage Molesworth Street
description: Traffic impossible on Molesworth Street, bridge or road surface compromised.

title: Brooklyn hill residents using portable lighting, preparing shelter
description: Community center opening as emergency warming station; residents bringing blankets and preparing to stay overnight if needed.

title: Building damage widespread in Newtown: Todman Street severely impacted

title: CBD hotel roof damage from initial quake

title: Gas cordon expanding through Te Aro

title: George reports earthquake felt in Karori
description: Felt very subtle shaking in Karori area. Aftershock minimal in intensity.

title: Hataitai residential: minor road damage not affecting through traffic

title: Karori Road: 80% debris cleared, opening expected within 2 hours

description: SH1 Ngauranga Gorge northbound convoys now departing every 15 minutes. Debris clearance 75% complete. Forecast full reopening in 2 hours.

description: Council engineers on-site at The Terrace conducting emergency safety evaluation. Building status unknown pending findings.

description: Structural damage at Onslow Road has triggered emergency response. Multiple agencies coordinating assessment.

description: Taranaki Street now passable with single-lane alternating traffic. Pits cordoned off for permanent repair planning.

description: Conservation specialists evaluating historic structures for liquefaction damage. Stabilisation measures being considered.

description: Widespread blackout reported in The Esplanade area, transformers offline.

title: Aftershock M2.5 beneath Island Bay area
description: Magnitude 2.5 aftershock at -41.35, 174.77, depth 10 km. Aftershock sequence continuing with diminishing magnitude and intensity.

description: Magnitude 2.7 aftershock at -41.27, 174.78, depth 10 km. Central residential zone continuing minor seismic activity.

title: Emergency water trucking commencing in CBD
description: Wellington Water emergency water distribution points established. Tanker trucks positioning in CBD for emergency supply.

description: Ground deformation on Civic Square, lateral spreading visible, property damage accelerating.

description: Systematic assessment of all residential properties in high-impact zones. Damage inventory being compiled.

description: Miramar station showing platform surface cracking and retaining wall settlement. Station closed pending structural engineer site visit and approval.

description: Wellington Water begins pressure testing of temporary bypass main line to Oriental Bay. Initial test phase lasting 30 minutes to assess line integrity.

title: Petone ground stabilisation monitoring showing reduced activity
description: Ground fissuring rate decreasing. Liquefaction manifestations becoming less visible with passage of time.

description: Hutt City Council reports limited restoration in western Porirua. Eastern sectors still without power; crews working systematically through zones.

description: Multiple subsidence pits opened across intersection. Deemed too unstable for car traffic. Walking detours in place.

description: Te Aro Vivian Street rupture monitoring shows stabilisation at 0.9m width. Building structural assessments critical for east CBD reopening.

description: Neighborhood networks organizing meal sharing and generator sharing. Local store opening as unofficial community hub for information.

title: Welfare centre coordination: 2,000+ residents now accommodated
description: Wellington Scoop reports WCC welfare centres accommodating 2,000+ residents displaced from damaged homes. Additional facilities being activated.

description: Wellington Water crews beginning emergency repairs on major breaks. Water pressure restoration expected to proceed slowly.

title: Aftershock M2.6 near Miramar Peninsula
description: Magnitude 2.6 aftershock at -41.32, 174.82, depth 9 km. Sequence continuing with minor magnitude aftershocks.

title: Brooklyn slope instability: secondary cracks appearing; further collapses likely
description: Hillside above The Crescent now showing multiple tension cracks. Structural assessment indicates risk of additional failures.

description: Wellington Electricity reports total loss of power distribution from Customhouse Quay substation affecting CBD core. Estimated 40,000+ customers without power in Wellington CBD and Thorndon.

description: Structural engineers are now on-site in Lower Hutt CBD assessing building damage from the earthquake.

description: Wellington Water preliminary assessment indicates at least 15 major ruptures across city. Full network damage assessment ongoing.

title: Fiona reports earthquake felt in Newtown

title: First major aftershock M5.1 strikes offshore Wellington Harbour
description: Major M5.1 aftershock detected 08:27 NZST, 15 km east of Wellington CBD in harbour area. Depth 18 km. GeoNet confirms strong ground motion across entire metropolitan region. Residents report sustained shaking.

title: Hutt Valley rail bridge: Post-earthquake measurement survey started

title: Island Bay residents establishing water supply coordination system
description: Community organizing water distribution from residents with stored supplies. Wellington Water trucks expected to arrive with emergency water by dawn.

title: Kilbirnie ground stabilisation ongoing with continuing monitoring protocols
description: Subsidence activity continuing at reduced rate. Ground deformation documentation procedures established.

title: Ngauranga Gorge motorway hazard lighting being manually managed

title: Petone foreshore - liquefaction eruption video shows 2m sand column

title: Quay Street CBD: east-west crossing established, street partially open

description: GeoNet confirms strong aftershock at 08:27 NZST, centered near Thorndon Fault. Residents report renewed shaking across CBD and outer suburbs.

title: Regional situation report: 85+ injured, widespread infrastructure damage

title: SH1 Ngauranga Gorge: Significant pavement cracking detected

title: SH1 Northbound closed between Ngauranga Gorge and Terrace Tunnel

title: Seaview comprehensive infrastructure damage report compilation ongoing

title: State Highway 1 southbound Civic Centre off-ramp - temporary closure

title: Emergency welfare point: Kelburn school gymnasium opens
description: Kelburn primary school gymnasium opened as emergency welfare point. Initial capacity 250 residents. Medical first aid station established. Staff from Wellington City Council and St John coordinating intake.

title: Fire and Emergency - initial building safety assessment team mobilized to CBD
description: Fire and Emergency rapid response team beginning systematic building safety assessments across Wellington CBD. Triage assessment protocol deployed; structural concerns being logged for detailed follow-up inspections.

title: Hutt Valley Line services suspended; all three trains halted for inspection
description: All Hutt Valley Line trains stopped immediately after mainshock. 890 passengers distributed across three stationary services between Thorndon and Seaview Station. KiwiRail issuing bus-replacement services from Lower Hutt depot.

title: Newtown streets: Multiple pavement failures reported
description: Community reports show several streets in Newtown experiencing liquefaction-induced pavement cracking. WCC dispatch assessing; single lanes maintained for traffic flow.

title: Petone industrial area: multiple transformer failures reported
description: Three distribution transformers have failed in the Petone industrial zone near the port. Wellington Electricity crew en route; estimated 8,000 customers affected in Seaview and Gracefield.

title: Ruptured reticulation line on Lambton Quay
description: Water main rupture on Lambton Quay. supply rerouted. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Tsunami advisory extended: all Wellington coasts
description: MetService confirms tsunami wave travel time 45-60 minutes. Advisory extends to all coastal areas including Oriental Bay and Island Bay. Public advised to move inland and uphill immediately.

title: Building facade collapse: Lambton Quay near Bowen Street
description: Partial facade of 4-storey office building has collapsed onto street. Fire and Emergency responding to reports of persons trapped.

title: CentrePort Aotea Quay: subsidence of 0.6m measured, vessel mooring re-assessment required
description: Liquefaction-driven subsidence detected at Aotea Quay berths 1-4. All moored cargo vessels being re-assessed for stability; two feeder ships currently anchored offshore pending berth re-inspection.

title: Fire and Emergency structural engineers dispatched to Lambton Quay office towers
description: Rapid assessment team en route to assess facade damage and internal bracing integrity on three interconnected commercial buildings between Ghuznee and Panama Streets. Concern over exterior panel failures and potential internal column compromise.

title: Flooding reported, Willis Street intersection
description: Resident in CBD core reports water gushing from pavement at Willis and Vivian Street intersection, causing 30cm pooling on roadway. Utility boxes submerged.

title: GeoNet urgent: significant aftershock activity continues
description: GeoNet issues urgent bulletin confirming M5.1 aftershock followed by multiple M4+ events anticipated. Extended sequence likely. Response agencies advised to maintain elevated readiness for 6-12 hours.

title: Ground fissure on Oriental Parade near Frank Kitts Park
description: Large cracks opening in the road surface, sand boiling visible. Several residents evacuating buildings nearby.

title: Hutt Valley grid instability: automatic protection triggered
description: Automatic protection systems engaged in Hutt Valley to prevent transformer damage from power surge following quake. Load shedding on two secondary circuits affecting 4,000 residents.

title: Liquefaction subsidence reported on Taranaki Street CBD
description: Community reports significant ground settlement and lateral cracking on Taranaki Street between Vivian and Customhouse Quays. Multiple storefront windows damaged from differential ground movement.

title: M5.7 aftershock, Wellington CBD area
description: GeoNet seismograph network detected a magnitude 5.7 aftershock 8km south-southwest of Wellington CBD at 08:29 NZST, depth 12km. Strong shaking felt across the central city.

title: Regional grid telemetry: cascading outage detected across central zones
description: GeoNet grid monitoring shows cascading voltage collapse affecting Wairarapa and Hutt Valley distribution zones. Automatic load shedding triggered to prevent complete grid failure.

title: SH2 southbound partial blockage near Hutt Valley High School
description: Large debris field from adjacent building collapse covers two lanes. Emergency services clearing site; southbound traffic diverted via local roads.

title: Wellington CBD Quay Street - visible ground fissure opening expanding
description: Community photos show prominent ground fissure on Quay Street widening visibly. Width already exceeds 35cm, surface offsets visible on either side.

title: Wellington Electricity: Major distribution fault, circuits tripping across CBD east
description: Substation at The Terrace reporting cascading circuit breaker trips. Te Aro residential blocks 4-7 without power, estimated 8,400 customers affected.

title: Community members report building corner collapse in Te Aro
description: Social media reports describe corner column failure on four-storey commercial building near Vivian-Cambridge intersection. Building occupants have evacuated; Fire and Emergency response crew en route for structural verification.

title: Extensive sand boils reported on Jackson Street, Petone seafront
description: Resident video shows liquefied sand erupting from ground surface in 2-3 metre diameter cone on Jackson Street near Petone Township. Spray pattern indicates continuing subsurface water pressure.

title: High water detected in Zone G (Airport)
description: Automated alert from Zone G (Airport). Flow rate exceeds capacity threshold. Flood conditions near Customhouse Quay.

title: Kilbirnie blackout: entire eastern suburb without power
description: Resident reports complete power loss across Kilbirnie since earthquake. Traffic lights at Kilbirnie intersection dark. Fire and Emergency has activated emergency protocols.

title: Liquefaction spreading across Petone waterfront and industrial zone
description: Stuff reporters confirm ground subsidence and sand boils emerging along Petone Parade. Multiple water main ruptures visible, geysers in intersections.

title: M5.1 aftershock detected offshore Wellington Harbour
description: GeoNet seismograph network has detected a significant magnitude 5.1 aftershock approximately 15 km offshore east of Wellington Harbour, depth 18 km. Preliminary location analysis underway.

title: Multiple building reports: CBD core
description: Community reporting visible structural damage to multiple office buildings in central business district. Emergency responders triage responses.

title: Power down across Petone waterfront
description: Entire stretch from Petone Station to the wharves is dark. Streetlights out, businesses without power.

title: SH1 Ngauranga Gorge: bilateral closure for bridge inspection; traffic diverted via inner city
description: Ngauranga Bridge closure (northbound and southbound) for immediate seismic inspection. NZTA Waka Kotahi deploying traffic management on Taranaki Street and Thorndon Quay as alternate routes.

title: SH2 Lower Hutt bridge damage assessment initiated
description: NZTA engineers responding to significant settlement on State Highway 2 near Lower Hutt CBD. Bridge piers show visible movement; traffic redirected while structural integrity evaluated.

title: Water main rupture Petone - major street flooding
description: Water main break on Jackson Street, Petone creating large water fountain and surface flooding across the street. Residents have evacuated nearby buildings as water level rising.

title: Welfare centre activation: Karori Community Hall, capacity 400
description: Wellington City Council has opened Karori Community Hall as emergency welfare centre. Medical support and refreshments available. Displaced residents from Thorndon, Wadestown and surrounding suburbs being directed here.

title: Building collapse concerns: Te Aro
description: Community reporting building showing signs of structural failure. Fire and Emergency responding; area being cordoned off as precaution.

title: Kilbirnie isthmus - ground displacement visible along embankment crest
description: Video confirms lateral displacement now visible along full embankment crest on Kilbirnie Crescent. Cumulative movement at least 25cm.

title: M4.8 aftershock rocks central Wellington, strong damage risk
description: M4.8 aftershock at 08:31 NZST in Wellington CBD causes strong sustained shaking lasting 15 seconds. DYFI reports rate intensity IV-V in CBD, III-IV in suburbs. Potential for additional structural failures.

title: M4.8 aftershock, secondary event near harbour
description: GeoNet network recorded magnitude 4.8 aftershock at 08:31 NZST, 7km east-northeast of Wellington CBD in harbour area, depth 10km. Strong shaking reported by residents.

title: Major gas leak detected Wakefield Street - CBD evacuation zone expanded
description: Wellington City Council infrastructure telemetry shows elevated methane concentrations near Wakefield Street/Willis intersection. Emergency cordon expanded to 200m radius; residents urged to leave immediately via designated evacuation routes.

title: Melling Line stalled at Brooklyn Junction; 340 passengers sheltering in place
description: Melling Line service stopped at Brooklyn Junction due to suspected track misalignment. Passengers advised to remain on train pending track inspection by KiwiRail track engineers currently en route.

title: Miramar airport runway lights offline - emergency response activated
description: Wellington Airport confirms loss of all runway lighting systems following earthquake damage to local switchyard. Flights halted pending daylight inspection and power restoration.

title: Multiple water main breaks, Te Aro area
description: Wellington Water field crew confirms ruptures at Taranaki Street and Tory Street. Liquefaction has caused subsidence opening cracks in main distribution line.

title: No power in central Wellington, all lights out since the shake
description: Complete blackout on Lambton Quay from Willis St to Bowen St. Businesses are closing, traffic lights not working.

title: Quay Street (CBD) westbound lane collapse near post office
description: Extensive liquefaction damage has caused westbound carriageway to subside 0.3m. One lane impassable; alternating traffic controls being installed.

title: Ruptured 150mm asbestos cement main on Plimmer Steps
description: Water main rupture on Plimmer Steps. assessment underway. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Water main rupture on Scotland Street
description: Water main rupture on Scotland Street. isolation valve activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Building facade damage on Lambton Quay
description: Exterior brickwork fallen from mid-rise office building, debris on footpath. Fire crews responding.

title: Gas odour reported: Lambton area
description: Fire and Emergency receiving call about gas smell in commercial area. Initial assessment underway; utility company notified.

title: GeoNet confirms M5.1 aftershock, strong ground motion recorded
description: Official confirmation: M5.1 aftershock at 08:32 NZST, epicentre 16 km east of CBD. Strong ground acceleration recorded at multiple stations. No tsunami risk at this magnitude.

title: GeoNet confirms liquefaction onset in Seaview industrial zone
description: GeoNet seismograph and field data confirm liquefaction features emerging in Seaview. Lateral spreading observed along Seaview Road affecting port access infrastructure.

title: Hutt Hospital on emergency generators following mainshock
description: Hutt Valley High Hospital switched to backup power systems after main grid failed at 07:45. All critical services operational on generators; fuel reserves estimated for 48 hours.

title: Karori Road: Landslide blocks northbound access
description: Community reports significant earth slippage across Karori Road near the cutting. Approximately 40 vehicles affected; debris removal and stabilization assessment underway.

title: M5.6 aftershock near Thorndon area
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 5.6 aftershock at 08:32 NZST, 5km north-northeast near Thorndon, depth 11km. Strong shaking felt across waterfront zone.

title: Port Nicholson container stacks: 4 towers destabilized; emergency load removal commencing
description: Four stacks of 40-foot containers shifted during liquefaction near Thorndon Container Terminal. Specialist cranes from Auckland summoned; immediate priority is stabilizing towers 7 and 9 before potential collapse.

title: Power cuts widen: 60,000+ without electricity across western suburbs
description: Wellington Electricity lines now down from Johnsonville through Tawa and into Porirua. Transformer fires reported at Ngaio substation.

title: Structural assessment team deployed: Willis Street building collapse risk
description: Urban Search and Rescue team assessing a 1960s office building on Willis Street showing critical facade damage. Building manager confirms 200+ people worked on upper floors this morning. Evacuation perimeter established.

title: Te Aro commercial tenant evacuates following visible building damage reports
description: Commercial tenant on Dixon Street voluntarily evacuating office space after discovering visible structural damage. Building owner notifying Fire and Emergency for emergency structural safety assessment.

title: Waterfront sand boil column - continuous spray from ground fissure
description: Sustained sand boil on Quay Street near Maritime Museum ejecting material continuously. Column height 1.5 metres, subsurface pressure showing no signs of decline.

title: Ground fissures on Quay Street CBD - 40cm width, 15m length
description: Wellington City Council field team confirms parallel ground cracks opening perpendicular to Quay Street between Customhouse and Jervois Quay. Liquefied layer visible below surface.

title: Miramar Avenue cutting closed - major rock fall blocking both lanes
description: Rock fall from cutting has completely blocked Miramar Avenue at the Kilbirnie junction. Estimated 200 cubic metres of material. Full closure in effect.

title: Pressure loss spreading across CBD network
description: SCADA shows cascading pressure drops across primary distribution nodes. Supply pressure has fallen 40% in central zone. Secondary mains likely ruptured.

title: Significant ground rupture on upper Lambton Quay
description: Visible fault trace with displacement; street buckled in multiple places. Traffic redirected.

title: Structural assessment: Heritage warehouse, Customhouse Quay
description: WCC engineers assessing 1920s-era wool warehouse for stability after significant internal cracking observed. Building remains closed to occupants.

title: Structural engineer yellow-tags office building on Willis Street - phased evacuation
description: WCC commissioned structural engineer issues yellow occupancy tag for Willis Street office tower. Upper floor evacuation being conducted in phases; interior wall damage and ceiling panel failures noted during walkthrough assessment.

title: The Terrace buildings: Facade damage threat to streets
description: Multiple The Terrace commercial buildings showing facade instability. WCC placing exclusion zones; temporary roofing/bracing underway to secure debris.

title: Thorndon Quay subsidence causing structural cracking
description: Photo evidence showing 15cm differential settlement on Thorndon Quay between buildings. Cracks visible on adjacent heritage buildings along State Services Commission site.

title: Traffic chaos: Wellington CBD lights out, major intersections dark
description: All traffic lights inoperative in CBD and Thorndon from Lambton Quay to Willis Street. Police manually directing traffic at major intersections; significant congestion reported.

title: WREMO escalates alert: aftershock sequence intensifying
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management escalates regional alert following M5.1 and M4.8 consecutive aftershocks. All response agencies placed on maximum alert status. Additional incidents expected.

title: Wellington Airport Cobham Drive: 45cm ground fracture; access rerouted to Paraparaumu approach
description: Cobham Drive (primary airport access road) damaged by ground subsidence and fissuring. Wellington Airport has declared ground-access partial closure; helicopter services unaffected but road traffic diverted to secondary Paraparaumu route via SH1.

title: Wellington Electricity: Critical voltage collapse downtown, switching to island mode
description: Frequency deviation detected on urban distribution ring. Brownouts spreading to Thorndon. Load shedding protocols activated.

title: Aftershock M5.4 near Petone recorded
description: Follow-up earthquake magnitude 5.4 detected near Petone, 15km northeast of epicentre, depth 11km at 08:34 NZST. Residents report strong shaking and further building movement.

title: Building damage report - Te Aro warehouse structural failure
description: Fire and Emergency confirms partial collapse of concrete warehouse roof structure at Cambridge Terrace and Vivian Street. No initial reports of persons trapped; site being secured and assessed for secondary collapse risk.

title: Building debris scattered across Quay Street
description: Multiple reports of fallen facade panels and masonry debris blocking southbound traffic on Quay Street, Wellington CBD. Emergency services redirecting vehicles via The Terrace.

title: CBD evacuation order expanded: entire Te Aro precinct now closed
description: Wellington City Council issues mandatory evacuation of all office buildings south of Taranaki Street due to structural concerns and aftershock risk.

title: Felt widely across Wellington metropolitan area
description: Multiple GeoNet DYFI (Did You Feel It) reports confirming the M5.1 aftershock was felt with IV-V intensity in CBD, Petone, and Hutt Valley suburbs.

title: Injury alert: Construction site workers buried in rubble, rescue underway
description: Active construction site on Featherston Street partially collapsed. Ten workers reported trapped under steel framework and concrete. Fire and Emergency heavy rescue teams mobilized with hydraulic equipment.

title: Johnsonville Line: signal failure and power loss; 210 passengers evacuated between Avon and Main
description: Overhead line power failure on Johnsonville Line near Avon Station. Emergency lighting activated on stationary service; passengers evacuated via emergency exits to Main Road underpass with FENZ support.

title: Liquefaction damage: Customhouse Quay sinking, underground carpark flooded
description: Structural engineers confirm significant ground subsidence on Customhouse Quay. Underground car park now has 1.2m standing water from ruptured water main. Ground fissures up to 0.3m wide visible across the thoroughfare.

title: Multiple injuries reported at Wellington CBD bus interchange
description: Structural failure of overhead shelter during evacuation. At least 6 people injured, ambulances on scene.

title: Oriental Bay: power loss disrupts emergency response coordination
description: Wellington Police command centre experiencing intermittent power supply. UPS systems activated; IT systems degraded. Comms through backup radio network operational.

title: Resident reports: traffic chaos at Civic Square, all signals dark
description: Pedestrian crossing signals and vehicle lights not functioning. Multiple near-misses at intersection. Emergency services trying to direct traffic.

title: Residential areas: widespread blackout reported by residents
description: Social media reports flooding in with residents from Kilbirnie, Miramar, and Hataitai reporting complete power loss. Estimated 25,000 residents in eastern suburbs without power.

title: Structural assessment: The Terrace
description: WCC engineer assessing heritage building with external wall bulging and interior cracking. Occupants evacuated pending full evaluation.

title: Thorndon Quay - pole and building subsidence creating lean of structures
description: Utility poles and surrounding buildings all showing visible lean as ground settles. Consistent direction suggesting large-scale lateral spreading zone.

title: Wellington Water reports main line damage Kilbirnie
description: Wellington Water has identified major water main rupture under Kemp Street, Kilbirnie. Sewage and potable water mixing reported. Area flagged as no-entry zone.

title: Basement flooding, Thorndon Quays commercial block
description: Building manager reports water ingress in basement carpark. Estimated 100,000+ litres from ruptured main beneath building foundation filling basement level.

title: Building settlement on Cuba Street causing facade collapse
description: Partial facade collapse observed at heritage building Cuba Street/Willis Street intersection in CBD. Liquefaction-induced subsidence has exceeded structural tolerance. Area cordoned off.

title: CBD - structural assessment team prioritizes heritage building facade inspection
description: Structural assessment team prioritizing inspection of heritage stone buildings on Customhouse Quay following community reports of facade damage. Initial assessment confirms mortar joint deterioration and stone displacement requiring urgent intervention.

title: CentrePort fuel berth (Kings Wharf) cordoned; leak assessment ongoing for marine diesel storage
description: Seismic movement triggered isolation procedures at fuel berth on Kings Wharf. Marine diesel tank structural assessment required before resumption of fuel transfer operations. Environmental monitoring underway for any hydrocarbon release.

title: Evans Bay Parade - traffic camera shows surface fracture and subsidence
description: NZTA traffic telemetry detects 0.25m differential subsidence across Evans Bay Parade near the airport. Speed restrictions being imposed.

title: Kilbirnie isthmus subsidence - ground settlement 25cm in 20 minutes
description: Wellington Water SCADA detects differential ground movement along Kilbirnie Crescent embankment. Settlement causing stress on water main coupling nodes.

title: M4.5 aftershock Petone/Hutt region, damage assessment ongoing
description: GeoNet records M4.5 aftershock at 08:35 NZST in Petone/industrial zone area, depth 16 km. Strong ground motion reported across Hutt Valley. Damage assessment teams mobilized to port and industrial areas.

title: Nairn Street: Subsidence forcing traffic restrictions
description: WCC confirms significant surface subsidence on Nairn Street creating hazardous driving conditions. Traffic management implemented; pavement repair crews mobilized.

title: Pressure failure in Zone F (Inner West)
description: Automated alert from Zone F (Inner West). Flow rate increased 300%. Probable rupture near Jackson Street.

title: Regional power loss: >150,000 customers affected across Wellington region
description: Wellington Electricity declares Level 2 Emergency - regional power grid failure affecting CBD, Hutt Valley, Porirua, and eastern suburbs. Estimated restoration timeline 3-6 hours.

title: Transformer explosion and fire in Wellesley Street area
description: Electrical fire spreading; power cut to surrounding blocks. Fire suppression underway.

title: Gas company responds to odour reports on Taranaki Street commercial precinct
description: Gas field engineers responding to multiple community reports of strong mercaptan odour on Taranaki Street between Webb and Buckle Streets. Initial investigation confirms small service rupture; isolation and repair sequence being planned.

title: Gas leak reported: The Terrace near Whitmore Street
description: Strong gas odour detected by residents in multi-unit residential building. Fire and Emergency HAZMAT team en route with gas company notified.

title: Hospital activates major incident protocol; trauma team standing by
description: Capital & Coast DHB reports 47 patients arrived with earthquake-related injuries in past 20 minutes. Orthopaedic teams mobilized.

title: Jervois Quay (CBD-to-Waterfront): cracked road surface and shifted stormwater line
description: Jervois Quay shows 12cm horizontal offset and cracking in asphalt near Civic Centre. Stormwater main ruptured; Wellington Water SCADA flagged possible inflow contamination into recycled water reticulation.

title: Liquefaction fissures opening in Rongotai isthmus
description: Community reports show ground fissures up to 10cm wide opening across sports grounds near Evan Bay. Fine sand and water ejecting from fissures consistent with sand boil activity.

title: M4.8 aftershock Wellington CBD zone, depth 12 km
description: GeoNet network has detected M4.8 aftershock in Wellington CBD area, depth 12 km. Intensity reports indicate strong shaking in central business district and surrounding suburbs.

title: M5.3 aftershock, Kilbirnie-Miramar
description: Magnitude 5.3 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 08:36 NZST, 12km south-southeast affecting Kilbirnie and Miramar areas, depth 11km. Moderate to strong shaking.

title: Multiple crush injuries reported: Lambton Quay retail area
description: St John ambulance reporting 12 casualties with crush injuries from collapsed shop frontage on Lambton Quay. Three patients transported to Wellington Hospital with serious leg fractures. Ongoing rescue operations in progress.

title: Petone commercial building - wall cracking accelerating with aftershocks
description: Video time-lapse shows cracks widening on Jackson Street building with each subsequent aftershock. Brickwork separating at head of windows and doors.

title: Power out in Petone industrial zone, port operations halted
description: Seaview/Gracefield power line B4 down. Container handling equipment offline. CentrePort coordination center on backup power only.

title: SH2 Ngauranga Gorge blocked by rockfall
description: Large boulders across all lanes, southbound traffic backed up for kilometers. Waka Kotahi assessing clearance.

title: Te Aro power infrastructure damage: water main rupture threatens cables
description: Water main burst on Taranaki Street compounding power outage with risk of electrical short circuits. Wellington Water and Electricity crews coordinating around hazard zone.

title: CBD retail: shops in darkness, looters attempting theft
description: Retail stores in Willis Street and Lambton Quay going dark. Police reports of attempted break-ins at unattended storefronts. Mobile police units dispatched to major retail precincts.

title: Citizen injured by facade collapse on Greys Avenue
description: Pedestrian struck by falling bricks and mortar; head injury sustained. Transported to hospital.

title: Emergency: Karori Road water main down
description: Water main rupture on Karori Road. tanker deployed. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Evacuate immediately: Pigeon Park CBD cordon extension to Civic Square
description: Following multiple aftershocks, the cordon now extends from the Beehive to Civic Square. All buildings assessed as unsafe. Public must not enter this zone. Police establishing checkpoints on all access roads.

title: Fire and Emergency NZ responds to aftershock damage reports
description: Fire and Emergency NZ reports multiple 111 calls following M4.5 aftershock in Hutt Valley. Crews dispatched to potential building damage, infrastructure failures, and trapped persons reports.

title: Gas smell: Featherston Street area
description: Fire and Emergency investigating gas odour in commercial area. Area being ventilated; utility company called to check main lines.

title: Hutt Valley general practice losing power, staff evacuating patients
description: Lower Hutt Medical Centre on Cuba Street lost main supply. Generator failing to start. Ambulances called for critical patient transfers.

title: Karori Road near Salamanca Road - small landslip reported
description: Community report of earth and vegetation covering approximately 40 percent of roadway. Single lane passable with caution; WCC sending assessment team.

title: M4.9 aftershock, central region recorded
description: Magnitude 4.9 aftershock detected by GeoNet seismographs at 08:37 NZST, 4km south-southeast near central city, depth 10km. Moderate shaking in CBD area.

title: Miramar ground displacement affecting residential area
description: Multiple residences on Miramar Road showing 20-30cm foundation settlement. Homeowners report doors jamming and windows cracking as structures tilt on liquefied ground.

title: Ruptured 50mm service connection on The Esplanade
description: Water main rupture on The Esplanade. assessment underway. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: SCADA: pressure drop in Zone G (Airport)
description: Automated alert from Zone G (Airport). System anomaly detected. Probable rupture near Waterloo Road.

title: SH1 northbound lanes closed at Ngauranga Gorge
description: NZTA confirms full closure of northbound State Highway 1 at Ngauranga Gorge due to pavement instability and debris. Traffic diverted via Johnsonville; estimated delay 45 minutes.

title: Seaview industrial area - continuous liquefaction eruption in parking zone
description: Worker report confirms boiling sand on Gracefield Road parking lot. Eruptions continuing with spacing of 3-5 minutes. Fine sediment creating slurry layer.

title: Seaview port facility: critical power systems on battery backup
description: CentrePort confirms main substation offline; automated systems running on UPS. Container operations halted. Estimated 72 hours to restore full power to port operations.

title: WCC engineer reports structural concerns at Bowen Street commercial buildings
description: Preliminary visual assessment indicates significant horizontal displacement in Bowen Street office complex near Willis Street. Engineer flagging potential shear wall failure; urgent detailed structural inspection required before re-entry clearance.

title: Wairarapa Line halted at Ghuznee Street underpass; structural clearance uncertain
description: Wairarapa Line service stopped beneath Ghuznee Street underpass due to possible reduction in clearance from mainshock subsidence. Engineering assessment team called; approximately 310 passengers held in situ pending inspection results.

title: Water main failure, Stokes Valley Hutt
description: 30mm secondary main ruptured beneath State Highway 2 overpass. Water shooting 2m high from break point. Traffic camera shows disruption at interchange.

title: GeoNet issues official M4.8 aftershock bulletin
description: M4.8 aftershock confirmed at 08:38 NZST, located 3 km south-southeast of Wellington CBD. Considerable ground shaking reported in Thorndon, Te Aro, and Downtown areas. No additional tsunami risk.

title: Hataitai residents report sparking power lines and electrical fires
description: Multiple residents report arcing and small fires from damaged electrical lines near Hataitai shops. Fire and Emergency dispatched; area cordoned off. Electricity crews standing by.

title: Kilbirnie access: Liquefaction creating traffic bottleneck
description: Kilbirnie isthmus access routes showing severe liquefaction with multiple pavement humps. Traffic diverted via alternate routing; assessment of stabilization options underway.

title: Liquefaction forcing water up through roads in Kilbirnie
description: Geysers of water and silt erupting across the isthmus area. Local schools being evacuated as precaution.

title: Lower Hutt - Fire and Emergency responds to structural damage at shopping precinct
description: Fire and Emergency crew attending commercial shopping complex on Hutt Road reporting shopfront window failures and visible structural cracking. Rapid assessment underway to determine occupancy safety status.

title: M5.8 aftershock, Harbour area
description: Magnitude 5.8 aftershock recorded by GeoNet network at 08:38 NZST, epicentre 4km northeast of Wellington Harbour. Depth 10km, widely felt across the region.

title: Magnitude 5.3 aftershock strikes Wellington; no tsunami threat
description: Second major aftershock felt at 08:38 NZST. GeoNet confirms epicenter in Hutt Valley. MetService reiterates no Pacific-wide tsunami alert.

title: Metlink Metro Center response: 1,540 commuters reassigned to bus-replacement services
description: Wellington's central rail hub capacity overwhelmed. Metlink deploying additional buses from regional fleet; services to Hutt Valley, Johnsonville, and Melling corridors transferred to coach operations from Civic Centre staging area.

title: Telemetry: flood conditions in Zone E (Petone)
description: Automated alert from Zone E (Petone). Water level 135cm above surface. Flood conditions near Plimmer Steps.

title: Thorndon Quay streetscape collapse - 60cm depression and tilted poles
description: Traffic camera shows Thorndon Quay between Cable Street and Featherston Street has settled, creating 60cm depression. Utility poles now at 8-degree angle, street furniture scattered.

title: WCC structural assessment teams deployed to CBD
description: Wellington City Council has mobilized structural engineering teams to prioritize damage assessment in high-liquefaction zones. Focus on buildings showing visible cracking or subsidence.

title: Aftershock magnitude 5.3 centred Pipitea, liquefaction ongoing
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 5.3 aftershock at 08:39 NZST centred near Pipitea. Additional ground subsidence reported in Thorndon and liquefaction fountaining in Seaview. No tsunami from this event.

title: CentrePort ferry terminal: passenger embarkation halted pending berth stability confirmation
description: Wellington-Picton ferry terminal at CentrePort ceased passenger boarding following mainshock. Two ferry vessels currently at berth require mooring reassessment; 680 passengers diverted to alternative transport.

title: Flooding on Market Lane, CBD core
description: Water level rising on Market Lane creating 40cm depth. Businesses reporting water entry into shopfronts. Drainage systems overwhelmed by main rupture upstream.

title: Gas smell reported: Multiple CBD locations
description: Fire and Emergency receiving multiple reports of gas odour across CBD. Coordinating response with utility company for large-scale inspection.

title: Island Bay electrical infrastructure severely damaged
description: Three poles down on The Esplanade due to liquefaction effects. No power to beachfront properties. Small fire suppressed near Onepu Road; power crew mobilizing.

title: Lower Hutt structural assessment identifies multi-building red tag at commercial row
description: WCC structural engineer conducting street-level assessment of Hutt Road commercial row identifies serious load-bearing wall cracking pattern. Two adjacent buildings red-tagged; immediate evacuation and closure protocols implemented.

title: M4.3 Hutt Valley aftershock adds to escalating crisis situation
description: Major M4.3 aftershock at 08:39 NZST in Lower Hutt area, depth 14 km. Three M4+ aftershocks in 12 minutes causes additional building damage and infrastructure failure. Response agencies now severely stretched.

title: Miramar Avenue drainage collapse - surface water pooling rapidly
description: Stormwater drain on Miramar Avenue has collapsed as ground subsided beneath it. Water now pooling on surface, creating 300-metre long shallow lake across roadway.

title: Miramar cutting: Rockfall blocks traffic lanes
description: Multiple rockfalls detected on Miramar cutting cutting via traffic camera. Approximately 8 cubic metres of rock debris blocking lanes; northbound access closed immediately.

title: Petone liquefaction causing pavement buckles and fissures
description: Photos show extensive ground fissuring and pavement upheaval on Britannia Street, Petone. Fine sand deposits (sand boils) visible in 3-4 locations along 200m stretch.

title: Road access cut on Evans Bay Drive near Mahina Road junction
description: Pavement rupture impassable for vehicles. Residents east of damage point isolated for now.

title: SCADA: flow anomaly in Zone E (Petone)
description: Automated alert from Zone E (Petone). System anomaly detected. Probable rupture near Karori Road.

title: SH1 southbound Alexandra Road exit - gravel slide from overpass
description: Loose rock and gravel from bridge approach forming hazard on southbound exit. Traffic management in place; structural engineers attending.

title: Wellington City Council: CBD substation B2 out of service indefinitely
description: High voltage transformer damaged by aftershock vibration. Estimated 6 weeks replacement lead time. Switching load to backup circuits.

title: Gas line rupture Courtney Place - Fire and Emergency establishing safety perimeter
description: Multiple reports of strong gas odour and hissing sounds along Courtney Place. Crews establishing exclusion zone from Devon Street to Abel Smith Street; traffic diverted and residents evacuated from nearby buildings.

title: Interior damage: Willis Street commercial building
description: Ground-floor retail extensively damaged with collapsed shelving and merchandise. Upper floors structural integrity being assessed by engineers.

title: Kelburn route: Access compromised by infrastructure damage
description: Kelburn area road access severely compromised by liquefaction damage and fallen power infrastructure. WCC establishing temporary route via Botanic Gardens area.

title: Kilbirnie airport access cut off: liquefaction and road damage isolates runways
description: Main access road to Wellington Airport subsiding. Runway operations suspended pending structural assessment. Domestic flights diverted to Palmerston North.

title: Miramar building - foundation corner showing prominent displacement
description: Photos of office building at Miramar Avenue show corner column now sitting on cracked foundation pad. 20-25cm differential settlement visible.

title: Petone-Seaview corridor (SH2): debris blockage between Hutt Valley High School and Barracks
description: Liquefaction heave and pavement failure on SH2 Petone, creating 2.5-meter debris field. NZTA clearing crews deployed; traffic manually routed via Inner City and Hutt Road alternatives.

title: Power out in Lower Hutt CBD, hospital on backup generators
description: Entire commercial block without electricity. Hutt Hospital running on emergency power; patient transfers underway.

title: Residents Kilbirnie: complete blackout affecting supermarket and pumping stations
description: Local New World supermarket dark, food spoilage concerns. Water pressure dropping as pumping station offline. Residents queuing to report.

title: WREMO alert: M4.8 aftershock impacts central Wellington
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management Office issues alert on M4.8 aftershock. GeoNet stations in Karori and Mount Victoria showing ground acceleration. Emergency response teams remain on high alert for additional seismic activity.

title: Wellington Water SCADA detects pressure loss in Seaview zone
description: SCADA telemetry shows rapid pressure decline in Seaview water distribution network consistent with multiple main breaks. Automated isolation procedures activated.

title: Wellington data centre: generator systems partially activated
description: Central business district internet/data infrastructure on emergency power. Some services degraded; critical systems maintained. ISP networks reporting cascading failures.

title: Brooklyn Road slip - retaining wall failure near Riddiford Street
description: Approximately 80 cubic metres of soil and demolition material blocking two lanes. Emergency services on-site; full closure until debris cleared.

title: Extensive liquefaction across residential streets in Hataitai
description: Sand fountains and subsidence affecting multiple blocks. Families evacuating; water and silt everywhere.

title: Hutt City Council issues emergency alert for valley residents
description: Hutt City Council emergency management issues urgent alert: M4.3 aftershock followed by widespread new damage reports. Residents advised to remain outdoors in open areas. Additional aftershocks expected.

title: M4.9 aftershock, Island Bay-Hataitai
description: GeoNet seismograph recorded magnitude 4.9 aftershock at 08:41 NZST, 14km south near coastal area between Island Bay and Hataitai, depth 12km. Moderate shaking felt.

title: Newtown power loss: schools and retail precincts in darkness
description: Multiple reports of blackout affecting Newtown precinct. Schools evacuated as safe light conditions cannot be maintained. Retail stores closing; ATMs non-functional.

title: Seaview industrial area - liquid mud boiling from pavement cracks
description: Worker reports geysers of liquefied sediment on Haywards Hill Road near port facilities. Boils occurring every 8-10 seconds as aftershocks continue. Slurry covering 20-30 metres of roadway.

title: Te Aro - residents evacuated from buildings near gas leak epicentre
description: Residents from three residential buildings near Courtney Place evacuation zone being escorted to temporary assembly area. Emergency services establishing shelter operations and resident accounting procedures.

title: Water supply shutdown initiated, CBD precinct
description: Wellington Water operations center has isolated 8 district metering areas due to multiple ruptures. Supply deliberately cut to prevent catastrophic depletion.

title: Welfare centre activation: Newtown Community Centre, 350-person capacity
description: Wellington City Council opens Newtown Community Centre. Accessible building with medical bay and community support services. Currently accepting displaced residents from Newtown, Berhampore and Polhill suburbs.

title: Wellington Railway Station Platform 2 (northbound): rail buck-up and signalling loss
description: Platform 2 northbound track exhibits 8cm vertical offset. All traffic to northern lines (Hutt, Johnsonville, Wairarapa, Kāpiti) diverted to alternate platforms pending emergency realignment.

title: Building collapse reported on Kent Terrace, Te Aro
description: Partial collapse of older residential building. Rescue operations starting; entrapment suspected.

title: Building evacuation: Cuba Street
description: Fire and Emergency directing evacuation of multi-storey building showing signs of structural compromise. WCC engineers responding for assessment.

title: CBD - temporary restraint cables being installed on heritage stone facade
description: Fire and Emergency structural crew installing temporary cable restraints on stone facade blocks showing displacement on Customhouse Quay historic building. Rigging engineer supervising load distribution; cables under tension by late morning.

title: Cascading power loss now affecting Rongotai Airport, runway lighting offline
description: Auckland-bound flight diverted to Christchurch. Tug power lost, aircraft moved manually by ground crews. Terminal on emergency lighting only.

title: Electrical grid failure spreads: Harbor Bridge area now dark
description: Cascade failures in transmission lines extend power cuts north. Harbor Bridge lighting out; traffic signals failed at key intersections.

title: Kāpiti Line (Paraparaumu service): overhead cable damage at Taita Tunnel portal
description: Catenary wire failure observed at Taita Tunnel entry point; train held at Taita Station. Electrical engineers dispatched from Hutt depot for repair; estimated 250 passengers awaiting restoration.

title: Liquefaction-induced lateral spreading on Thorndon reclaimed land
description: GeoNet field inspection confirms 50cm of lateral ground displacement toward harbor on Thorndon waterfront. Building pilings showing visible stress from ground movement.

title: M4.5 aftershock detected Petone/Seaview industrial area
description: GeoNet seismograph network detects M4.5 aftershock at 08:42 NZST in the Petone area, depth 16 km. Felt reports from CentrePort and Seaview industrial zone indicate moderate to strong shaking.

title: Pressure failure in Zone E (Petone)
description: Automated alert from Zone E (Petone). Pressure drop from 800 kPa to 200 kPa. Probable rupture near Customhouse Quay.

title: Rongotai ground displacement spreading - third zone now affected
description: Airport survey confirms liquefaction zone spreading beyond initial damage area. New ground cracking appearing 200 metres from original epicentre of disruption.

title: SH2 Petone ramp: Severe subsidence affecting approach
description: State Highway 2 Petone on-ramp shows 15cm differential settlement. NZTA restricting heavy vehicles and limiting lane capacity. Detour via Seaview Road in effect.

title: Southern suburbs cascading power loss affecting 30,000+ residents
description: Power failure cascading through Kilbirnie, Miramar, and Rongotai zones affecting 30,000+ residents. Wellington Electricity prioritizing critical infrastructure restoration.

title: Structural assessment: University building complex, emergency closure
description: Victoria University buildings assessed for damage. Main engineering block red-tagged. Campus closed until full structural evaluation completed. 3000+ students and staff redirected to welfare centres.

title: Arterial main break, Cable Street approach
description: 900mm trunk main ruptured near Taranaki Street intersection. Flow estimated at 5,000+ litres per minute. Water fountaining from street surface.

title: CBD water main geyser - spray now reaching third-storey buildings
description: Panama Street water main rupture intensifying. Spray height now estimated at 8 metres with water reaching business signs on building fronts.

title: CentrePort Aotea Quay general cargo crane No. 3: tilting observed; emergency shutdown initiated
description: Container crane at Aotea Quay (Crane No. 3) showing structural tilt of 2.1 degrees from mainshock-triggered foundation movement. All cargo operations ceased; specialist inspection teams mobilized from Auckland.

title: Eastern suburbs power surge: secondary damage to consumer electronics
description: Reports coming in of burnt-out fridges, TV damage, and electrical fires in homes from power surge in initial mainshock. Insurance claims line activated.

title: Head injury casualties: Civic Square partial building collapse
description: Fire and Emergency reports 8 people with head and spinal injuries from partial collapse of Civic Centre atrium. Rescue teams extracting people from rubble. Estimated 15+ people still unaccounted for in the building.

title: Injuries from gymnasium collapse during fitness class in Kilbirnie
description: Ceiling and equipment fell during active session. Multiple gym members injured; rescue ongoing.

title: Kilbirnie primary school structure at risk from settlement
description: Structural engineering assessment indicates Kilbirnie Primary on Lanark Road has settled unevenly by 25cm. Roof showing stress cracks. School declared unsafe and evacuated.

title: M4.1 aftershock harbour zone, peak aftershock activity phase
description: M4.1 aftershock at 08:43 NZST in Wellington Harbour zone marks continuation of intense aftershock sequence. Four M4+ aftershocks in 15 minutes. Peak aftershock activity phase now confirmed by GeoNet analysis.

title: M5.5 aftershock detected, Lower Hutt region
description: GeoNet seismograph recorded magnitude 5.5 aftershock 20km northeast at 08:43 NZST, epicentre near Lower Hutt CBD, depth 13km. Strong secondary shaking across Hutt Valley.

title: Newtown residential area - multiple building damage reports via community network
description: Social media reports describe chimney collapses, brick veneer failures and foundation cracking on Daniell and Rongotai Streets. Residents reporting difficulty opening doors/windows indicating frame distortion.

title: Oriental Bay Drive - coastal road subsidence creating 15cm step
description: Liquefaction-induced subsidence has created dangerous vertical offset on Oriental Bay Drive. Emergency barricades erected; vehicle access restricted.

title: Post-aftershock feedback: Wellington city now 100% without power
description: Second strong tremor at 08:43 triggered additional automatic shutdowns. No estimate for restoration. Full regional blackout status.

title: Structural assessment: Willis Street tower
description: WCC structural engineer assessing tall commercial building with reported internal damage. Occupants being managed during evaluation.

title: Whitmore Street underpass: Water ingress affecting passage
description: Whitmore Street underpass showing water ingress from ruptured water main above. Traffic reduced to single lane; water drainage and repair coordinated.

title: Brooklyn Road: Significant hillside collapse reported
description: Large-scale landslide on Brooklyn Road cutting blocking all traffic. Approximately 50 cubic metres of soil and rock covering three lanes; emergency route via Mount Victoria under assessment.

title: Evacuation: Wadestown hillside area, slope instability concern
description: WREMO issues evacuation for Wadestown hillside area following slope stability engineer assessment. 180 residents on slope must evacuate due to liquefaction-induced instability risk.

title: Flooding from water main break on Customhouse Quay
description: Major water main break under Customhouse Quay near Civic Square. Water rising 1m depth in intersection. Traffic signals out of service, pedestrians diverted.

title: Hutt Valley substation fires extinguished by automatic systems
description: Two small electrical fires detected and suppressed at Hutt Valley substation. No power restoration expected for minimum 2 hours pending safety inspection.

title: M4.7 aftershock, eastern suburbs
description: GeoNet network recorded magnitude 4.7 aftershock at 08:44 NZST, 11km south-southeast in eastern suburbs area, depth 12km. Moderate shaking felt.

title: Main failure on Woburn Road
description: Major water loss on Woburn Road. Water spray 2+ metres high. Emergency assistance needed.

title: Miramar Avenue building tilt - 3-storey office shows 15cm lateral displacement
description: Structural engineer photos confirm 15cm horizontal shift at roofline of office building at Miramar Avenue corner with Beacon Hill Road. Foundation subsidence suspected from ground liquefaction.

title: Miramar peninsula: multiple transformer fires visible, crews responding
description: Three separate vegetation fires started by electrical faults. Emergency crews extinguishing at Factory Road and Evans Bay area.

title: Newtown - homeowners initiating self-assessment of property damage extent
description: Newtown residents beginning coordinated self-inspection of residential properties for damage documentation. Online community mapping tool being used to record damage reports and coordinate information sharing.

title: Queen Street liquefaction: CBD footpath collapses near Civic Square
description: Wellington Scoop eyewitness: large ground rupture opened on Queen Street between Gray and Webb. Fire crews treating three pedestrians for minor crush injuries.

title: Roof failure: Te Aro commercial precinct, Vivian Street
description: Partial roof collapse on 6-storey office building. Debris fallen onto street. Multiple fire trucks on scene; area cordoned off.

title: Seaview industrial area flooded by liquefaction and water main break
description: Port facilities affected; shipping containers displaced. Water gushing from ruptured main compounding liquefaction.

title: Seaview port reports strong ground motion, no immediate damage
description: CentrePort and Wellington Electricity crews report strong shaking from M4.5 aftershock. Initial assessment shows no new structural damage to port facilities or cargo containers. Aftershock sequence continues with decreasing magnitude trend.

title: Taranaki Street (northbound): 300m section buckled; detour via Lambton Quay mandatory
description: Taranaki Street main thoroughfare exhibits 35cm uplift zone between Civic Centre and Ghuznee Street. One-way detour established northbound via Lambton Quay; southbound partially restored with lane restrictions.

title: Telemetry: flood conditions in Zone A (CBD)
description: Automated alert from Zone A (CBD). Water level 135cm above surface. Flood conditions near Lambton Quay.

title: Hutt Station underpass (Lower Hutt): load-bearing wall cracking; pedestrian closure in effect
description: Underpass at Hutt Station showing significant cracking in supporting wall structure. FENZ cordoned off pedestrian access; users diverted to grade-level street crossing pending structural engineering report.

title: Newtown - resident reports significant ground subsidence adjacent to property
description: Homeowner on Rongotai Street reports sudden ground level changes visible adjacent to garage slab. Liquefaction damage pattern suspected by geotechnical consultant reviewing photos; detailed site investigation scheduled.

title: Power outage paralyzing emergency communications in Petone
description: Police and fire stations without power; backup systems activated. Dispatch affected temporarily.

title: Rongotai airport terminal building showing structural distress
description: Community reports and drone footage show foundation displacement at passenger terminal. Exterior precast panels showing visible cracks. Evacuation procedures underway.

title: Seaview freight route: Cargo access blocked
description: Seaview Road subsidence creating impassable conditions for heavy freight vehicles. Port operations diverting deliveries; NZTA prioritizing stabilization of industrial access.

title: Street flooding traps vehicles, Lambton Quay
description: Water depth reached 1.2m at Lambton Quay CBD intersection. Four vehicles stalled in flood. Emergency services advising evacuation of immediate area.

title: Structural assessment: Vivian Street
description: WCC team evaluating residential building with settlement cracks and wall displacement. Building cordoned pending structural certification.

title: Structural red-tag: Council building, 101 Wakefield Street uninhabitable
description: Structural engineer assessment confirms Wellington City Council building on Wakefield Street is uninhabitable. Severe damage to structural columns and exterior cladding failure. Staff relocated to emergency operations centre.

title: System alert: Zone E (Petone) pressure critical
description: Automated alert from Zone E (Petone). System anomaly detected. Probable rupture near Whitmore Street.

title: Thorndon Quay water infrastructure - service pipes breaking under strain
description: Multiple water service pipes to buildings on Thorndon Quay fractured as ground settled. Building water supplies failing in sequence along the street.

title: Traffic accident: dark intersection causes multi-vehicle crash
description: Multiple vehicle collision at Kilbirnie intersection due to traffic lights being offline. Two minor injuries reported; ambulance en route. Police managing traffic diversion.

title: WREMO coordinates multi-agency response to escalating crisis
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management coordinates emergency response across Fire and Emergency NZ, NZTA, Wellington City Council, and Hutt City Council. M4.1 aftershock in harbour zone affects eastern suburbs.

title: Whakatiki Street (Te Aro) - liquefaction crater affecting drainage
description: Settlement crater approximately 1m diameter has opened on Whakatiki Street near Cuba Street intersection. One lane closed; crews assessing safe routing.
