title: CBD supermarkets closing due to refrigeration power loss
description: Countdown and Newworld stores in Wellington CBD closing immediately following power failure. Perishable stock at risk. Some residents report looting incidents at unmanned ATMs.

title: Flood response activated in Hutt
description: Confirmed flooding on The Strand affecting riverside properties. Depth 30cm. water diversion active.

title: Lower Hutt - Wellington City Council structural safety check dispatched
description: WCC mobile assessment team deployed to Hutt Road commercial precinct following reports of shopfront window failures and visible wall cracking. Initial triage required to identify high-risk occupancies for staged evacuation.

title: M4.3 aftershock Lower Hutt CBD, multiple felt reports
description: M4.3 aftershock at 08:46 NZST, epicentre 8 km south of Lower Hutt CBD. Residents report sustained ground movement lasting 10-15 seconds. GeoNet stations confirm depth of 14 km.

title: M5.0 aftershock detected, Seaview industrial
description: Magnitude 5.0 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 08:46 NZST, 17km northeast in Seaview/Gracefield industrial zone, depth 12km. Strong shaking at port facilities.

title: Miramar liquefaction lateral spread affecting waterfront homes
description: Residential properties along Evan Bay Drive experiencing ground rupture creating 15-20cm vertical scarps. Liquefied soil flowing downslope toward harbor.

title: Road splitting open on Customhouse Quay near Civic Square
description: Asphalt cracking and subsiding. Pedestrians seeing ground heave. Vehicles advised to avoid area.

title: Rongotai airfield - compaction ripples visible across entire runway
description: Airport photos show 15-20cm amplitude ripples across full runway width, spacing approximately 3-4 metres. Surface unsuitable for any aircraft operations.

title: Seaview Port industrial rail spur: track subsidence with 60cm vertical drop
description: Industrial rail spur serving Seaview/Gracefield port area settled 60cm due to liquefaction, rendering it impassable. Freight operations to that terminal halted; rerouting to alternative container parks via Thorndon Container Terminal.

title: Seaview industrial zone reports gas leak and fire hazard alert
description: Stuff on-scene: Pressure vessel ruptured at packaging plant on SH2. Emergency services isolating 500m perimeter. Residents within 2km urged to evacuate.

title: Structural assessment: Customhouse Quay
description: WCC team evaluating historic building complex showing significant cracking. Multiple buildings in area being assessed concurrently.

title: Wellington Electricity SCADA telemetry: distribution ring frequency unstable
description: Oscillations detected on eastern feeder. Second contingency line also failed at 08:51. Only backup circuits maintaining supply to any areas.

title: Aotea Quay (northbound approach): pothole creation and subsidence zone hazard markers deployed
description: Section of Aotea Quay road surface subsided 45cm, creating hazard zone with warning marker cones. Light vehicle access restricted; heavy truck traffic diverted to Thorndon Quay as interim route.

title: Building facade separation on Queen Street near Civic Centre
description: Wall separating from main structure; debris risk. Immediate evacuation; cordon erected.

title: CBD - Fire and Emergency identifies critical load path failure at office tower
description: Structural engineer identifying critical interior column failure at Bowen Street office tower indicating primary load path damage. Emergency bracing operations priority escalated; specialized structural emergency team being mobilized.

title: CBD water main rupture at Panama Street - geyser height 2 metres
description: Multiple reports confirm major water main failure on Panama Street between Lambton and Grey Streets. Water spray reaching building third floors, flooding CBD intersection.

title: Evacuation order: Thorndon residential zone, all streets north of Nairn
description: WREMO extends evacuation to all residential Thorndon north of Nairn Street due to liquefaction risk and building instability. 800+ residents affected. Evacuation centres are at Karori and Wadestown primary schools.

title: Hutt Road slide - retaining wall damage near Museum Hotel
description: Retaining wall supporting Hutt Road near Museum Hotel has partially failed, dropping rock onto roadway. Southbound lane closed; one-way alternating traffic.

title: Lambton Quay closure: Building damage and road cracking
description: Wellington City Council confirms closure of Lambton Quay between Featherston and Whitmore Streets due to extensive pavement cracking and building facade damage. No through traffic allowed.

title: M3.9 aftershock Newtown area follows intense 20-minute burst
description: M3.9 aftershock at 08:47 NZST in Newtown marks 5th significant aftershock in 20 minutes. Intense aftershock burst pattern documented. Resident anxiety and structural concerns escalating.

title: Main failure on Customhouse Quay
description: Major water loss on Customhouse Quay. Ground subsidence visible. Emergency assistance needed.

title: No power Petone East, residents isolated in darkened homes with no communication
description: Entire Petone residential area plunged into darkness. Mobile networks intermittent due to tower backup batteries depleting.

title: Water and sewerage line failures spreading in Petone
description: Coordinated ruptures in water and sewer infrastructure along Jackson and Russell Streets, Petone. Contaminated water and sewage pooling in commercial area.

title: Water main rupture, Petone industrial zone
description: Large diameter main serving industrial area has ruptured near Jackson Street. Port-adjacent infrastructure at risk from flooding. Seaview facility access threatened.

title: Water shortage emerging: sewage systems on emergency protocols
description: Wellington Water reports pressure loss in supply system due to power failure affecting main pumping stations. Sewage systems on emergency protocols. Water rationing notices issued.

title: Aftershock magnitude 5.1 centred Wadestown, significant continued shaking
description: GeoNet records magnitude 5.1 aftershock at 08:48 NZST centred Wadestown. Strongest aftershock yet. Triggers additional building collapses in CBD and alerts on structural monitoring systems.

title: CBD building condemned due to liquefaction-induced tilt
description: 10-storey office building on Willis Street showing 1-degree tilt from differential ground subsidence. Engineering assessment recommends immediate evacuation and closure.

title: Fire and Emergency - emergency structural props deployed to Lambton Quay site
description: Structural emergency response team installing heavy-duty vertical props beneath damaged building on Lambton Quay. Shoring calculations indicate load distribution across props; monitoring points installed for real-time system performance.

title: Hutt City Council reports wide aftershock felt across valley
description: Hutt City Council emergency operations confirm M4.3 aftershock was widely felt from Hutt Valley CBD to Taita-Haywards Hill. Response teams conducting secondary damage assessments in key locations.

title: Kilbirnie low-lying area - multiple sinkholes now visible across suburb
description: Aerial survey documents 12 separate sinkhole formations across Kilbirnie low-lying zones. Largest exceeds 2 metres diameter. Pattern suggests ongoing liquefaction and ground consolidation.

title: Lambton Quay mainline: settling detected under northbound lane; traffic directed to southbound only
description: Liquefaction-induced settling observed under Lambton Quay northbound lanes near Bowen House. Temporary reversible-lane configuration activated; all northbound traffic funneled southbound until structural integrity confirmed.

title: Liardet Street: Pavement rippling from liquefaction
description: Liardet Street showing significant pavement wave patterns indicating liquefaction subsidence. Traffic management reducing speed limits; temporary repair materials staged.

title: Rising water in residential basement
description: Water inundation in residential basement. Depth now 120cm and climbing. Help requested.

title: Structural damage assessment: Cuba Street gallery
description: WCC structural engineer inspecting 3-storey heritage building with visible external wall separation and foundation crack.

title: Thorndon power restoration begins: targeted circuit re-energization
description: Wellington Electricity begins selective re-energization of priority circuits in Thorndon. Estimated 5,000 residents to regain power by 09:30. Main CBD core still offline.

title: Traffic chaos on State Highway 1 northbound exit
description: Off-ramp damaged, vehicles unable to exit safely. Backed up nearly 4km; police directing traffic.

title: Waterfront precinct: crane collapse feared after structural failure
description: Damage report emerging from container terminal: large gantry crane listing at angle on wharf. Structural engineer dispatched. Port operations halted.

title: CBD heritage building - Fire and Emergency initiates emergency bracing
description: Circa 1920s stone-facade heritage building on Customhouse Quay shows evidence of mortar failure and stone displacement. Structural engineer confirms load transfer concerns; temporary bracing being installed to prevent progressive failure.

title: Emergency services report surge in calls for help after M3.9
description: Fire and Emergency NZ reports exponential increase in 111 calls following M3.9 aftershock. Emergency dispatch severely overwhelmed. Resource allocation prioritized for life-threatening situations.

title: Flooding spreading to Te Aro residential blocks
description: Water from multiple ruptured mains collecting in low-lying residential streets. Residents evacuating basements and ground floor dwellings as water level rises.

title: Gas leak alert: Multiple areas
description: Fire and Emergency responding to multiple gas leak reports across Te Aro. Gas company coordinating leak detection and evacuation support.

title: Hospital emergency power protocols: Wellington Regional Medical Center now on generators
description: Main grid supply lost 08:54. All surgical lights switched to backup power. Emergency procedures continuing but resources stretched.

title: Inundation reported near Woburn Road
description: Confirmed flooding on Woburn Road affecting residential basement. Depth 120cm. evacuation initiated.

title: M4.4 aftershock, south-southwest recorded
description: Magnitude 4.4 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 08:49 NZST, 9km south-southwest, depth 13km. Moderate shaking in southern suburbs.

title: Melling Line Platform 1: overhead line contact system severed; train immobilized for 45 minutes
description: Catenary system failure at Melling Station platform 1; train stopped mid-platform. Auxiliary generator deployed for passenger lighting; KiwiRail electrical team repairing contact lines for service resumption.

title: Petone commercial precinct - building settlement accelerating
description: Multiple photo updates of Queen Street buildings show progressive settlement since mainshock. 20-30cm estimated total movement in past 25 minutes.

title: Seaview port infrastructure subsiding from liquefaction
description: Port facilities showing uneven settlement of 30-40cm. Container cranes tilting visibly. CentrePort operations suspended pending structural assessment.

title: Street closure and liquefaction features on Oriental Parade
description: Multiple sand boils and ground heave. Parking area destroyed; vehicles displaced by subsidence.

title: Thorndon Quays - pavement buckling near Lambton Quay intersection
description: Liquefaction-driven ground heave has caused pavement to buckle 0.5m vertically. Traffic diverted to inner lanes; structural assessment underway.

title: Vivian Street: Water main break complicates repairs
description: Major water main rupture on Vivian Street near Dixon Street causing surface collapse and flooding. Wellington Water and WCC coordinating road closure and service restoration priority.

title: Welfare centre capacity increase: Newtown Community Centre adds annex
description: Newtown Community Centre requesting outdoor tent capacity due to overwhelming numbers arriving. 280 people now in facility, expecting 100+ more over next hour. Additional medical staff being deployed from Hutt Valley.

title: Aftershock sequence continues: fourth measurable event in 90 minutes
description: GeoNet reports magnitude 4.9 aftershock at 08:50 NZST. Residents across region report increased anxiety; further aftershocks expected.

title: CentrePort container vessel MV Pacific Crown: unable to berth; anchored offshore for 90+ minutes
description: Vessel MV Pacific Crown diverted offshore due to CentrePort berth instability; 1,240 TEU (20-foot containers) cannot be discharged. Port operations coordinator liaising with shipping agent for updated berthing time.

title: Gas odour: Taranaki Street block, Te Aro
description: Residents evacuating 8-unit apartment building due to strong gas smell. Fire and Emergency investigating for gas main rupture in street.

title: High-priority main break Jackson Street
description: Water main rupture on Jackson Street. bypass line activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Lower Hutt - emergency response crew establishes evacuation zone perimeter
description: Fire and Emergency establishing evacuation zone perimeter on Station Road around identified gas rupture location. Resident outreach and notification underway; assembly points being established for affected population.

title: M4.1 aftershock Wellington Harbour zone detected
description: GeoNet network detects M4.1 aftershock at 08:50 NZST, located in Wellington Harbour, depth 13 km. Shaking reported in Miramar, Rongotai, and eastern suburbs.

title: M5.2 aftershock, Miramar recorded
description: Magnitude 5.2 aftershock detected at 08:50 NZST, located 12km southeast of Wellington CBD near Miramar, depth 14km. GeoNet sensors confirm strong ground motion.

title: Mount Victoria approach: Steep grade compromised
description: Mount Victoria approach road showing pavement wave patterns from liquefaction. Temporary traffic management limiting vehicle size; geotechnical review of slope stability initiated.

title: Multiple injuries from collapsed shelving at Newtown shopping center
description: People trapped under retail displays. First responders extracting casualties; estimated 4-5 injured.

title: Premises flooded on Customhouse Quay
description: Confirmed flooding on Customhouse Quay affecting basement level of ANZ Centre. Depth 30cm. sandbagging deployed.

title: Rongotai Airport ground displacement - runway surface rippling
description: Airport operations reports 15-20cm waves across tarmac on northern runway approach. Liquefaction buckling runway surface. Flight operations halted pending full structural assessment.

title: Rongotai/Kilbirnie stabilization: load shedding preventing further cascade
description: Load shedding protocols holding voltage stable in eastern suburbs. Controlled blackout sections rotating to prevent infrastructure overload. Restoration sequencing underway.

title: Thorndon heritage precinct showing extensive cracking
description: Multiple heritage buildings around Thorndon showing significant structural cracks from liquefaction settlement. Facade damage on Parliament Street buildings.

title: Traffic gridlock at major intersections, downtown Wellington immobilized
description: All traffic signals down across grid. Vehicles stalled at crossings. Police attempting manual direction but overwhelmed.

title: Water main rupture on Jervois Quay
description: Water main rupture on Jervois Quay. tanker deployed. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Water sensor triggered in Zone A (CBD)
description: Automated alert from Zone A (CBD). Critical water level. Flood conditions near Jackson Street.

title: Building damage: Te Aro residential
description: Fire and Emergency responding to apartment building with visible structural damage and cracked walls. Tenants evacuated safely.

title: Injury incident on parliament grounds from structural damage
description: Building wing shows damage; occupant injured during evacuation. Medical crew on scene.

title: Lower Hutt gas rupture isolation procedure - staged main valve shutdowns
description: Wellington infrastructure team executing staged isolation procedure for Station Road gas main rupture. Isolation valve positions verified; downstream pressure monitoring confirming successful section isolation by mid-morning.

title: M3.8 aftershock Kilbirnie isthmus, sequence showing no clear peak
description: M3.8 aftershock at 08:51 NZST on Kilbirnie isthmus. GeoNet analysis indicates aftershock sequence showing no clear decay phase peak, suggesting prolonged intense activity expected.

title: Mount Victoria tunnel approach - loose rock hazard reported
description: Community report of rock fall hazard on tunnel approach from cutting. NZTA cameras confirm debris; reduced speed zone implemented pending clearance.

title: Petone fuel anxiety: petrol stations unable to pump without power
description: Reports of fuel station queues in Petone unable to dispense fuel due to power-dependent pump systems offline. Residents hoarding fuel in portable containers.

title: Seaview industrial zone - sand boil area expanding at 2m per minute
description: Time-lapse photography shows liquefied material spreading outward from central boil on Haywards Hill Road. Expansion rate alarming, now covering commercial loading dock.

title: Tsunami advisory: First wave detected, 0.6m reported at Mahina Bay
description: MetService confirms first tsunami wave detection at 08:51 NZST with 0.6m elevation at Mahina Bay. Wave propagating into Wellington Harbour. Public in coastal areas must have evacuated to high ground by now.

title: Water network pressure critical across region
description: SCADA data shows network-wide pressure collapse. All primary distribution zones now below minimum operating pressure. Backflow risk emerging in secondary networks.

title: Waterloo Quay (inner harbor approach): asphalt heave creating 25cm ridge; traffic reduced to single file
description: Waterloo Quay shows clear liquefaction heave ridge. Traffic management implementing manual control; vehicles directed through single lane; repair crew preparing for surface grinding and stabilization.

title: Building damage: Willis Street basement
description: Fire and Emergency inspecting basement flooding and structural damage in commercial building. Water pumping and structural shoring considered.

title: Customhouse Quay water feature - water main rupture overtopping
description: Photos show water from Panama Street main now flowing across Customhouse Quay plaza and cascading into lower level car parks via stairwells.

title: GeoNet documents sand boils across Wellington CBD reclaimed zones
description: Field survey documents sand boil activity in 12+ locations across CBD waterfront. Water and liquefied sand ejecting from ground in major fountains on Lambton Quay.

title: Hataitai transformer station explosion reported, large fire visible from CBD
description: Massive orange glow and thick smoke from electrical equipment fire. Fire and Emergency NZ responding with multiple units.

title: Johnsonville Line Khandallah Tunnel: partial collapse of concrete arch lining
description: Khandallah Tunnel shows localized concrete arch collapse on southern wall. Geological engineers assessing stability; train held at previous station (Ngaio). All northbound services halted pending 2-hour inspection.

title: Liquefaction eruptions flooding Memorial Park, Thorndon
description: Sand and water plumes across sports field. Park declared unsafe; nearby residents evacuated.

title: Miramar suburb hit hard: multiple building evacuations underway
description: NZ Herald reports: structural cracks observed in apartment complexes on The Esplanade and Mahina Crescent. Residents assembling in streets with belongings.

title: Petone power restoration: first major zone circuit re-energized
description: Petone industrial zone first priority circuit restored at 09:52 NZST. Approximately 3,500 customers regaining power. Port operations still on battery; full restoration estimated 3 hours.

title: Repair: Water main on Jackson Street
description: Water main rupture on Jackson Street. bypass line activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Rongotai/Airport area confirms M4.1 aftershock impact
description: Wellington Airport operations team and Rongotai residents report noticeable ground shaking from M4.1 aftershock. Airport runway status remains clear; no impact on air operations reported.

title: Te Aro apartment building - evacuation completed after ceiling collapse risk
description: Three-storey residential building on Dixon Street evacuated after structural engineer identified suspended ceiling panel separation and ceiling anchor failure in communal areas. Immediate re-entry prohibition issued pending detailed inspection.

title: Traffic camera: SH1 Johnsonville detour heavily congested
description: Real-time traffic monitoring shows Johnsonville detour for SH1 at capacity with 200+ vehicles queued. Response teams activating alternate routes via Tawa.

title: Water flooding basement loading area
description: Water inundation in basement loading area. Inundation ongoing. Help requested.

title: Water main rupture on Queen Street
description: Water main rupture on Queen Street. isolation valve activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Airport Road heading south: Subsidence forcing detours
description: Airport Road southbound approach showing significant settlement. Traffic redirected via Rongotai alternate; pavement repair materials mobilized.

title: CentrePort Thorndon Container Terminal: 6,200 TEU immobilized due to crane outages and berth subsidence
description: Both container handling cranes at Thorndon Terminal disabled by mainshock-induced damage. Vessel Hansa Pacific unable to discharge; approximately 6,200 TEU backlog accumulating across three holding bays.

title: Chimney collapse: Grey Street residential
description: Heritage villa sustaining severe damage with chimney collapse into living area. Resident injured, ambulance dispatched.

title: Emergency: Waterloo Road water main down
description: Water main rupture on Waterloo Road. tanker deployed. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Injury update: Wellington Hospital emergency department overwhelmed
description: Wellington Hospital reports 47 patients admitted in past 45 minutes. ED at capacity, elective surgeries cancelled. Injuries range from crush wounds, fractures, lacerations to internal injuries. Second ambulance station activated.

title: Kilbirnie community adapts to sustained aftershock bombardment
description: Kilbirnie residents report M3.8 aftershock and express deep concern about aftershock sequence. Residents organizing outdoor shelter camps in parks and open areas.

title: Kilbirnie shopping centre building evacuation ordered
description: Structural damage assessment of major shopping complex on Colombo Street indicates foundation settlement has compromised structural stability. All occupants evacuated immediately.

title: M4.6 aftershock, Newtown-Brooklyn
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.6 aftershock at 08:53 NZST, 11km south-southwest in Newtown-Brooklyn area, depth 13km. Moderate shaking in southern suburbs.

title: NZTA traffic monitoring: major congestion across key routes
description: NZTA reports gridlock on Ngauranga Gorge approach and SH2 through Lower Hutt due to traffic light outages. Average travel times tripled. Accident rate elevated.

title: Newtown commercial zone: extended blackout affecting businesses and residents
description: Primary feeder to Newtown district offline. Schools, shops and office buildings dark. Only internal battery-powered exit signs functioning.

title: Petone township - multiple buildings settling into soft ground
description: Photos from Queen Street show four commercial buildings with visible foundation subsidence and door frame cracking. 15-25cm settlement detected in different buildings, likely from liquefaction-induced consolidation.

title: Road collapse on Hutt Road north of Haywards Hill
description: Significant subsidence creating impassable section. Vehicles detoured; repair timeline unknown.

title: SH2 northbound - liquefaction settlement across two lanes near Petone
description: Approximately 0.4m differential settlement causing rough surface across northbound carriageway. Traffic management reducing speed; engineering inspection scheduled.

title: Secondary main rupture, Newtown branch
description: 200mm secondary main failed beneath Riddiford Street. Rupture is spraying water across intersection affecting access to hospital feeder route.

title: Te Aro - beam-column joint failure documented on second office building
description: Structural engineer identifying similar non-ductile beam-column joint failures on Cambridge Terrace office building. Damage pattern comparison analysis underway; building stock vulnerability assessment initiated.

title: Building damage: Vivian Street complex
description: Fire and Emergency investigating significant damage to mixed-use building complex. Partial floor failure reported; occupants evacuated.

title: Hutt Valley High School intersection (SH2): traffic signal knocked offline; manual intersection control activated
description: Traffic signal controller box damaged by vibration and ground movement. NZTA deploying uniformed traffic officers to manually direct SH2 southbound/northbound flows at critical intersection.

title: Injury: Pedestrian struck by falling facade piece on Lambton Quay
description: Pedestrian struck by falling architectural element from damaged building on Lambton Quay. Head injury and multiple fractures. Rushed to Wellington Hospital in critical condition.

title: Island Bay power lines: continued outage from liquefaction damage
description: Poles on The Esplanade remain displaced due to ground settlement. Temporary repairs cannot be made until daylight assessment. Island Bay beach properties remain powerless.

title: M3.9 aftershock Newtown area, aftershock tempo increasing
description: GeoNet records M3.9 aftershock at 08:54 NZST near Newtown, depth 15 km. This represents the 6th significant aftershock in 12 minutes. Residents report aftershock sequence is becoming exhausting and unsettling.

title: Oriental Bay foreshore subsidence: beach access closed
description: Newshub on-site: liquefaction damage visible along waterfront promenade. Retaining walls tilting; public spaces fenced off pending engineer review.

title: Petone section of State Highway 2 flooded and impassable
description: Water main rupture combined with liquefaction subsidence has flooded Highway 2 near Petone Railway Station. Depth of water approximately 2 meters in low point.

title: Rumor spreading online: total grid failure permanent, entire region without power for months
description: Unsubstantiated social media posts claiming catastrophic transformer damage across region. People panic-buying fuel and provisions.

title: Te Aro building damage - beam-column joint failures documented at office complex
description: Structural engineer identifying welded beam-column joint failures on multi-storey office building on Vivian Street. Non-ductile connection design contributing to damage pattern; detailed load analysis underway for temporary propping requirement.

title: Te Aro corner building - 30cm displacement at street level visible
description: Vivian Street corner building shows 30cm horizontal shift at ground floor. Brick veneer has pulled away from frame, exposing timber structure beneath.

title: Wadestown hillslope instability: Road unsafe for traffic
description: Structural engineers confirm slope failure risk on Wadestown approach road following liquefaction triggers. Access restricted until stabilization assessment completed.

title: Widespread power failure in Miramar and Rongotai
description: Entire suburb without power. Airport lighting systems affected; operations severely impacted.

title: Featherston Street - building frame cracking now audible during aftershocks
description: Business occupant reports loud cracking sounds from warehouse frame as aftershocks trigger strain in already-damaged structure. Cracks visibly widening.

title: Flooding worsens on Hutt Valley corridor
description: Water mains ruptured along SH2 on multiple stretches. Water accumulating in low points of motorway creating hazardous driving conditions. Pooling extends 200m.

title: Gas odour reported across Courtney-Taranaki precinct - secondary rupture suspected
description: Community reports describe pungent mercaptan smell extending from Courtney Place to Taranaki Street. Multiple fire crews attending; second active gas leak site suspected in damaged underground network.

title: Hutt Line (Petone to Seaview segment): rail-bed separation and horizontal offset detected
description: Segment of Hutt Valley Line between Petone and Seaview stations shows 15cm horizontal rail separation (south-facing curve). Train service halted; geometry team measuring offset for emergency rail splicing operations.

title: Liquefaction damage cascading through Newtown shop district
description: Storefronts collapsing into subsidence zones. Business impact severe; cleanup ongoing.

title: M3.7 aftershock port zone, port operations suspended indefinitely
description: M3.7 aftershock at 08:55 NZST in Seaview port area. CentrePort operations suspended until seismic activity stabilizes. Port facilities assessed for M3.7 damage. Critical supply chain disruptions anticipated.

title: M5.0 aftershock, Kilbirnie area detected
description: GeoNet seismograph network recorded magnitude 5.0 aftershock at 08:55 NZST, 10km south of CBD near Kilbirnie, depth 11km. Moderate to strong shaking reported across eastern suburbs.

title: Petone central streets: Coordinated repairs commence
description: Petone central business district streets showing extensive pavement damage. WCC coordinating repair strategy with traffic management personnel; alternating lane closures scheduled.

title: Rongotai liquefaction affecting runway structural integrity
description: Runway lighting infrastructure shows evidence of ground settlement. NZTA traffic management reports pavement cracking consistent with liquefaction-induced movement.

title: Structural assessment: Lambton Quay heritage buildings at risk
description: Engineers identify six heritage buildings on Lambton Quay with severe facade cracking and potential for partial collapse. 24-hour monitoring commenced. Nearby buildings evacuated as precaution.

title: Wadestown Road slip - earth material across carriageway
description: Approximately 50 cubic metres of soil from hillside covering Wadestown Road. One lane blocked; residents diverting via Kingston Street alternative.

title: Water spray visible on High Street
description: Major water loss on High Street. Surface rupture widening. Emergency assistance needed.

title: Wellington CBD false report: 'power restored' premature
description: Social media rumor spreading that CBD power restored; confirmed false. Restoration ongoing but still 45+ minutes away. Residents being urged not to believe unverified reports.

title: Wellington Water: water treatment plant offline, backup systems engaged
description: Kaiwharawhara treatment works lost main power at 09:05. Switching to diesel generators. Water quality monitoring increased.

title: Customhouse Quay parking building - prefab wall panel separation from frame
description: WCC damage assessment team identifies separation between precast parking structure panels and concrete columns on Customhouse Quay, width increasing as building settles unevenly.

title: Evacuation: Tawa residential area, precautionary extension westward
description: WREMO extends evacuation zone to Tawa suburb as precautionary measure following aftershock risk assessment. 300+ residents advised to leave residential areas. Assembly points at Tawa primary school.

title: Hataitai residential area with severe ground subsidence
description: Homes settling unevenly; cracks appearing in walls and foundations. Residents evaluating for safety.

title: Hutt City: Evacuation centers opening in Wainuiomata, Alicetown
description: Red Cross and Civil Defence setting up welfare centers at community halls. First 200+ evacuees from damaged housing arriving with pets and essentials.

title: M4.2 aftershock, north area detected
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 4.2 aftershock at 08:56 NZST, 15km north-northeast, depth 11km. Light to moderate shaking.

title: Miramar residential area experiencing ground fissure propagation
description: Continuous 40m-long ground fissure opening along residential street. Fresh sand deposits accumulating. Residents moving to higher ground as fissure width increases.

title: Newtown - resident reports foundation visible displacement near property corner
description: Homeowner reporting visible foundation displacement adjacent to corner of Rongotai Street residence. Photographic evidence suggests liquefaction-related ground failure; geotechnical assessment being prioritized.

title: Newtown partial restoration: secondary circuits online
description: Newtown secondary distribution feeder restored; approximately 4,200 customers regaining power. Main Newtown shopping precinct remains dark. Ongoing assessment of main distribution damage.

title: Partition wall collapse: Manners Street office
description: Interior non-load-bearing walls collapsed in 5-storey commercial building. Occupants evacuated; structural engineer urgently required.

title: Rongotai Bridge (airport approach): undercarriage bearing failure; structural closure pending re-engineering
description: Rongotai Bridge expansion joints failed during liquefaction subsidence event. Bridge bearing assemblies require replacement; airport ground access diverted to alternate Paraparaumu helicopter-priority route.

title: Seaview port area: industrial outage causing safety hazards on dock
description: Crane operations halted without power. Manual override procedures activated. Stevedores evacuating from equipment areas.

title: WREMO brief: aftershock sequence analysis and forecast
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management updates response teams on aftershock patterns. Forecast models suggest continued decreasing magnitude sequence over next 2 hours. Major aftershocks M3.5 or greater unlikely but possible.

title: CentrePort reports M3.7 aftershock, port access severely restricted
description: CentrePort emergency operations: M3.7 aftershock caused additional structural damage to port warehouses and cargo facilities. Port access fully restricted pending structural assessments. Economic impact significant.

title: Evacuation: Kelburn residential area, 2km radius from CBD cordon
description: WREMO issues evacuation order for Kelburn suburb following multiple aftershocks and building instability. 600 residents advised to leave via State Highway 2. Evacuation assembly point at Victoria University sports grounds.

title: Major water supply disruption across Wellington CBD
description: Wellington Water confirms multiple main line breaks affecting CBD water pressure to critical levels. Hospitals and emergency services prioritized for water supply.

title: Metlink bus replacement surge: 37 additional coaches requested from regional operators
description: Metlink coordination center requesting emergency bus reinforcements to meet displaced commuter demand. 37 additional coaches commandeered from Palmerston North, Masterton, and Porirua depots for deployment to Wellington CBD staging area.

title: Miramar cutting: Secondary rockfall detected
description: Additional rock instability on Miramar cutting prompts immediate closure of both directions. Traffic diverted via Rongotai Airport route; geotechnical team deploying containment netting.

title: Multiple injuries from vehicle accidents due to signal failure
description: At least 3 separate collisions at now-uncontrolled intersections. Minor to moderate injuries.

title: Newtown residential block - fire assessment identifies chimney fire hazard
description: Fire crew attending Daniell Street property discovering internal chimney damage and blocked flue passage. Open fireplace made unusable by earthquake damage; residents instructed on fire safety precautions pending chimney repair.

title: Petone Main Street - section of road buckling into wave pattern
description: Photos show Main Street between Queen and Church Streets now displaying 20cm amplitude wave deformation across 50-metre section. Ground still shifting with aftershocks.

title: Seaview/Gracefield industrial road - subgrade failure blocking access
description: Liquefaction has caused industrial access road subgrade failure near Gracefield. Port operations access compromised; NZTA coordinating alternate routing.

title: Structural assessment: Lambton Quay
description: WCC structural engineer conducting emergency assessment of commercial building with visible external cracking and internal damage.

title: Supermarket food spoilage: estimated $2M+ inventory loss
description: Countdown, New World, and Pak'nSave reporting significant perishable loss due to extended blackout. Refrigeration systems off for 30+ minutes. Food waste management teams activated.

title: Water main breaks surface near Karori shops
description: Main serving Karori area has fractured beneath Chapel Road. Water is bubbling up through pavement creating localized flooding in shopping precinct.

title: Wellington CBD: business district completely dark at peak morning hours
description: Investment banking firms, law offices, and government departments operating from laptop batteries only. Staff sent home.

title: Gas leak: The Terrace
description: Fire and Emergency responding to gas leak in commercial building area. Evacuation orders issued; utility company mobilizing.

title: Kilbirnie isthmus - embankment surface now showing active rifting
description: Survey confirms new ground cracks opening on Kilbirnie Crescent embankment with visible offset. Rifting pattern consistent with ongoing lateral spreading.

title: Lower Hutt suburbs: extended power outage affecting refrigeration and heating
description: Petone and Seaview neighborhoods without electricity. Residents concerned about food spoilage and winter conditions.

title: M3.8 aftershock Kilbirnie isthmus detected by GeoNet
description: GeoNet seismograph network records M3.8 aftershock at 08:58 NZST on the Kilbirnie isthmus, depth 11 km. Ground motion consistent with shallow crustal aftershock. Intensity III-IV reported in surrounding suburbs.

title: M4.3 aftershock, Oriental Bay area
description: Magnitude 4.3 aftershock recorded by GeoNet seismographs at 08:58 NZST, 5km south near Oriental Bay, depth 11km. Light to moderate shaking felt in central areas.

title: Montrose Road: Slip area identified on cutting
description: Montrose Road cutting showing incipient landslide with soil movement visible. Geotechnical survey initiated; traffic reduced to essential access only.

title: Ngauranga Bridge (northbound): lateral beam misalignment; southbound single-lane bypass operational
description: Northbound Ngauranga Bridge beam assembly shifted 8cm laterally. Southbound traffic temporarily reversed to carry dual-direction flow; 4-hour closure estimate for beam re-alignment inspection.

title: Te Aro electrical hazard: restoration delayed by water ingress
description: Taranaki Street water main rupture complicating power restoration efforts. Zone remains de-energized pending drainage; estimated 1-2 hour delay to reconnection.

title: Thorndon building interior damage from liquefaction subsidence
description: Office building on Parliament Street reporting internal structural damage with floor-to-floor misalignment of 12cm. Interior walls cracked extensively.

title: Three-story building damaged on Taranaki Street
description: Significant structural cracking; upper floor partially unsupported. Building evacuated; cordon erected.

title: WCC engineer assessment - Lambton Quay building red-tagged for immediate closure
description: Structural engineering firm commissioned by council flags critical safety concerns at mixed-use building on Lambton Quay south. Non-compliant bracing connections and visible column damage warrant immediate evacuation and closure order.

title: Water supply compromised: main breaks in CBD and suburbs
description: Wellington Water reports 14 separate main ruptures confirmed. Supply cut to portions of Te Aro and Newtown. Residents advised to boil water.

title: Welfare centre: Kelburn gymnasium at 260 residents, outdoor overflow started
description: Kelburn gymnasium at 260 residents with outdoor tent overflow beginning. Capacity expansion in progress. Food supplies being supplemented by regional distribution network.

title: CBD - temporary lateral bracing installed at Willeston Street facade damage site
description: Structural crews installing temporary lateral bracing system at Willeston-Lambton facade damage site. Temporary supports secured; live load monitoring beginning immediately upon completion.

title: Customhouse Quay: Multiple road surface failures
description: Traffic signals down and pavement liquefaction creating sinkholes on Customhouse Quay waterfront. Emergency access only; pedestrian cordon established for public safety.

title: Flooding threatens Petone retail precinct
description: Water level at Jackson Street retail area now 60cm deep and rising. Shops are sandbagging entrances. Fire and Emergency water extraction teams arriving on scene.

title: Gas leak: Vivian Street area
description: Fire and Emergency investigating gas leak in commercial area. Evacuation of multiple units being managed by emergency services.

title: Hospital backup power: concern about 48-hour fuel reserve adequacy
description: Hutt Valley High Hospital expressing concern that 48-hour diesel backup may be insufficient if full power restoration delayed. Request for additional fuel supply sent to Emergency Management.

title: Kilbirnie Crescent - earth crack and surface rupture
description: Ground rupture visible running across Kilbirnie Crescent near airport approaches. Approximately 20cm wide fissure; traffic cautioned for hazardous surface.

title: M3.6 aftershock Rongotai, frequency increasing as magnitude decreases
description: M3.6 aftershock at 08:59 NZST near Rongotai. GeoNet notes aftershock frequency increasing as magnitudes decrease (Gutenberg-Richter relation). Expected 5-6 M3+ events remaining.

title: M4.8 aftershock, Te Aro region
description: Magnitude 4.8 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 08:59 NZST, epicentre 6km south of Wellington CBD in Te Aro area, depth 12km. Continued shaking felt across central city.

title: Major leak detected on Customhouse Quay
description: Water main rupture on Customhouse Quay. bypass line activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Power cut affecting critical water pumping station in Miramar
description: Backup systems engaged; supply pressures dropping. Water advisory issued for area.

title: Seaview industrial zone liquefaction compromising infrastructure
description: Multiple warehouses and industrial buildings showing foundation damage. Electrical substations tilting from ground displacement. Power distribution being rerouted.

title: Thorndon embankment lateral spreading - 35cm ground offset
description: Geotechnical team notes ground displaced laterally at Thorndon Quay/Cable Street junction. Lateral spreading pattern consistent with shallow liquefaction layer. Kerb and footpath now misaligned.

title: Welfare centre established: Lower Hutt community facility, 500-capacity
description: Hutt City Council activates lower Hutt emergency welfare centre. Medical staff from Hutt Hospital deployed. Currently receiving displaced residents from Seaview, Gracefield and central Hutt suburbs.

title: Wellington Station Platform 4 (southbound): track misalignment and ballast heave
description: Platform 4 southbound track shows 12cm ballast heave and lateral rail separation. Services redirected to Platform 3; all southbound trains (Melling, Johnsonville, Kāpiti) re-platformed pending track realignment.

title: CBD facade repair planning - window frame dislodgement at office building
description: Structural engineer evaluating window frame dislodgement on fourth floor of Lambton Quay office building. Temporary glazing boarding underway; permanent window replacement specs being developed for urgent procurement.

title: CentrePort anchorage field: 12 vessels advised to relocate to outer harbor (Baring Head approach)
description: CentrePort harbor master issued advisory to 12 anchored vessels due to seismic hazard and potential aftershock damage. Vessels relocating northeastward to Baring Head outer anchorage; estimated 2-hour transit duration.

title: Hataitai power restoration: damaged lines replaced with temporary lines
description: Temporary lines installed across Hataitai following fire suppression. Approximately 2,800 customers restored to power. Permanent infrastructure repairs scheduled for next week.

title: Injury cluster from Petone RSA building collapse
description: Historic building partially collapsed during morning activities. Multiple casualties extricated by rescue teams.

title: Kilbirnie arterial: Liquefaction creating traffic hazard
description: Kilbirnie main arterial route showing multiple subsidence points from liquefaction on reclaimed land. Speed restrictions imposed; comprehensive surface assessment scheduled.

title: Kilbirnie residents report sustained aftershock shaking
description: Community reports from Kilbirnie and adjacent suburbs confirm strong sustained shaking from M3.8 aftershock lasting approximately 12 seconds. Residents cite cumulative stress from repeated aftershocks.

title: Kilbirnie water main ruptures spreading contamination
description: Sewage and water infrastructure failures in Kilbirnie causing cross-contamination. Area health authority has issued precautionary water quality warning.

title: Miramar property boundary fence - now offset 20cm from original line
description: Lateral spreading has shifted property fence on Beacon Hill Road 20cm perpendicular to property line. Fence posts now tilted and sections broken.

title: Schools go into lockdown; parents told to collect children gradually
description: Wellington City Council directs primary and secondary schools to keep children in place until structural safety confirmed. Parent pickup to be managed.

title: Structural assessment: Lower Hutt CBD, Queens Drive
description: Multi-storey retail complex with significant exterior damage assessed by WCC engineers. Temporary supports being installed.

title: WREMO: emergency command center activating manual power dispatch protocols
description: Coordinating with Wellington Electricity on load restoration priorities. Hospital, water treatment, emergency services identified as Tier 1.

title: Aftershock magnitude 4.8 centred beneath Mount Victoria
description: GeoNet seismograph records magnitude 4.8 aftershock at 08:57 NZST. Centred near Mount Victoria. Additional minor damage reported in inner suburbs. Coastal tsunami advisory remains in effect.

title: Civic Square intersection: utility trench collapse under Mercer Street; gas and water line rupture
description: Liquefaction-induced trench cave-in beneath Mercer Street intersection. Both natural gas and potable water mains ruptured; Wellington Water and Energy stopcock teams isolating affected segments.

title: Community photo documentation of CBD liquefaction extent
description: Widespread community photos show lateral ground displacement on Quay Street creating visible surface ruptures. Sand boils observed at multiple intersections.

title: Critical main break near Civic Square
description: 800mm primary main ruptured beneath Civic Square. Massive flow causing surrounding street to become torrent. Adjacent buildings evacuating due to flooding risk.

title: IT systems crisis: government backup systems under strain
description: Government IT departments reporting challenges with backup and disaster recovery systems. Some government databases at risk if power not restored within 2 hours.

title: Karori Road near Barnard Street - second slide reported
description: Follow-up landslide event blocking additional section of Karori Road. Estimated 60 cubic metres material; WCC full closure order in effect.

title: Kilbirnie: local network completely down, isolated suburb in darkness
description: Isthmus area without any mains power. Only battery-powered communication devices working. Residents gathering in public spaces.

title: M4.0 aftershock, south region recorded
description: Magnitude 4.0 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 09:01 NZST, 13km south, depth 12km. Light to moderate shaking felt.

title: Newtown fire station reports widespread residential chimney damage
description: Fire crews responding across Newtown suburb report chimney collapses on approximately 60% of visited properties. Multiple instances of brick debris on ground and roof penetrations; secondary fire risk being assessed at each location.

title: Seaview port - liquid mud flow spreading across loading dock
description: Port manager video shows liquefied sediment flow moving across dock surface on Seaview Road. Flow continues downslope toward water's edge.

title: Severe ground rupture on Beach Street near Seaview industrial precinct
description: Fault trace visible; ground displaced vertically. Multiple warehouses affected; evacuations ongoing.

title: Wellington Airport continues monitoring Rongotai aftershock impacts
description: Wellington Airport operations team reports M3.6 aftershock impact on Rongotai zone. Runway structural assessments continue. Flight delays accumulating due to uncertainty and potential ground instability.

title: CBD traffic signals: manual control extended as grid stabilization underway
description: Police remain at major intersections manually directing traffic. Some traffic lights in Willis Street block restored to limited operation. Full CBD restoration estimated 10:30.

title: Flood reported near The Esplanade
description: Water inundation in light industrial buildings. Inundation ongoing. Help requested.

title: Flood response activated in Petone
description: Confirmed flooding on Colleen Street affecting commercial properties. Depth 20cm. water diversion active.

title: Gas detection: Hunter Street
description: Fire and Emergency identifying gas leak in underground utility corridor. Evacuation radius established; utility company mobilizing repair team.

title: Kilbirnie - water main network showing stress at multiple nodes
description: Wellington Water SCADA system detects 8 pressure anomalies on water distribution network across Kilbirnie, consistent with ground movement and pipe stress. Immediate isolation protocols initiated.

title: Kāpiti Line Raumati South Tunnel: support beam cracking observed; southern-approach closure
description: Raumati South Tunnel shows cracking in overhead support beams. Kāpiti Line services to Paraparaumu suspended; passengers from approaching train evacuated at Raumati Station; geological team deploying.

title: Lower Hutt - commercial district assessment underway by WCC engineering team
description: WCC structural engineering team beginning systematic commercial building assessment on Hutt Road district. Rapid triage protocol deployed; preliminary tagging decisions being made for merchant properties.

title: M3.7 aftershock industrial port area, magnitude refined
description: GeoNet records M3.7 aftershock at 09:02 NZST in Seaview/industrial port zone. Preliminary magnitude upgraded from M3.6. Depth 12 km. No new incidents reported in port or industrial facilities.

title: M4.7 aftershock, Rongotai-Airport area
description: GeoNet network recorded magnitude 4.7 aftershock at 09:02 NZST, 11km south-southeast near Rongotai Airport, depth 12km. Moderate shaking detected across eastern zone.

title: Newtown commercial strip: significant structural damage to heritage block
description: Multi-storey commercial building on Constable Street showing major cracks and settled foundation. Perimeter cordoned; occupants evacuated via fire escapes.

title: Petone liquefaction damaging commercial building foundations
description: Commercial building on Britannia Street showing foundation cracking and 35cm settlement differential. Structural engineer recommends evacuation pending detailed assessment.

title: Rongotai Airport: runway lighting system requires manual reset, inbound flights rerouted
description: Electrical control room at 14-degree tilt after liquefaction. Safety systems in manual mode. No scheduled operations until power restored.

title: SH1 Civic Centre off-ramp impassable due to pavement failure
description: Road surface severely buckled and cracked; vehicles unable to navigate safely. Diversion in effect.

title: SH2 Seaview industrial area: Freight access blocked
description: Extensive ground subsidence affecting port access roads at Seaview/Gracefield industrial precinct. NZTA restricting all freight movements; alternative routing via Haywards Hill implemented.

title: Structural assessment: Hutt City waterfront industrial buildings damaged
description: Hutt City waterfront industrial zone showing liquefaction and building foundation failures. Structural engineers assessing 12 industrial buildings. Estimated 300+ workers displaced from facilities.

title: Basement pumping underway at retail buildings, CBD
description: Multiple CBD buildings running emergency pumping operations. Basements flooded from groundwater intrusion and main ruptures. Fire and Emergency has deployed 6 pump units.

title: Building collapse casualties at Island Bay residential complex
description: Older multi-unit building collapsed; residents trapped. Rescue teams deployed with heavy equipment.

title: Building evacuation: Featherston Street
description: Fire and Emergency evacuating 10-storey office tower with reported structural concerns. Engineers confirm partial damage to elevator shafts.

title: CentrePort general cargo berth: shipping container dislodged; hazard zone established 100m radius
description: One 40-foot container fell from unstable stacking during ground movement. Debris field cordoned; crane operators attempting safe retrieval. Port authority restricting pedestrian access within 100m safety zone.

title: Customhouse Quay loading dock - water accumulation trapping vehicles
description: Water from main rupture has accumulated in loading dock on Customhouse Quay to depth of 60cm. Vehicles and equipment now stranded, no drainage mechanism effective.

title: Haywards Hill detour route: Heavy traffic observed
description: Haywards Hill detour route experiencing high traffic volumes redirected from SH2. NZTA monitoring for capacity issues; traffic management personnel deployed.

title: Injury report: Petone waterfront building collapse, 6 casualties
description: Petone waterfront industrial building partially collapsed during liquefaction. Six workers with crush injuries extracted and transported to Wellington Hospital. Ongoing rescue operations as additional workers may be trapped.

title: M3.5 aftershock Thorndon Quays waterfront, business district shaken
description: M3.5 aftershock at 09:03 NZST near Thorndon Quays waterfront. Downtown Wellington business district shaken by sustained ground motion. Office evacuations continue. Business continuity plans triggered.

title: Multiple gas detections across city - pressure drop analysis identifies rupture zones
description: Wellington SCADA system analysis detecting six pressure anomalies indicating rupture locations across CBD and Te Aro gas distribution network. Isolation valve sequencing plan being finalized; repair teams staged for systematic isolation work.

title: Public safety concern: crime spike reported during blackout
description: Police reporting increased calls for burglary, theft, and assault during power outage. Mobile units stretched thin. Requests for additional staff from Porirua and Wairarapa stations.

title: Rongotai ground deformation affecting airport access roads
description: Access road to Rongotai airport showing cracking and minor settlement from liquefaction. Traffic engineers assessing pavement integrity before allowing vehicle passage.

title: SH1 northbound Ngauranga - additional sinkhole forming
description: Follow-up subsidence event creating new sinkhole 50m north of initial closure. Road conditions deteriorating; temporary bypass via local roads recommended.

title: Supermarket chains: all stores across region closing due to power loss and safety concerns
description: Fresh Choice, New World, and Countdown locations shutting down operations. Customers attempting to leave with unpaid goods.

title: Building damage: Lower Hutt CBD retail
description: Fire and Emergency securing retail complex sustaining significant structural damage in Lower Hutt. Occupants managed; engineers being called.

title: Building facade failure - Willeston Street commercial precinct
description: Non-ductile reinforced concrete facade panel failure reported on four-storey office building at Willeston-Lambton intersection. Panels partially detached; pedestrian hazard confirmed with debris field extending 12 metres from building line.

title: CentrePort and Gracefield workers report another major aftershock
description: CentrePort and Gracefield industrial zone workers report M3.7 aftershock with significant ground motion. Crane operators evacuated to safe zones as precaution. No cargo operations resuming until seismic activity stabilizes.

title: Flood alert: Main Street area
description: Confirmed flooding on Main Street affecting commercial properties. Depth 60cm. sandbagging deployed.

title: Flood conditions in CBD
description: Confirmed flooding on Victoria Street affecting basement level of ANZ Centre. Depth 90cm. evacuation initiated.

title: Hutt Road (Lowry Bay to Seaview): surface buckling and debris scatter; detour via SH2
description: Hutt Road corridor exhibits multiple buckled sections and scattered road debris from adjacent businesses. Alternative route via SH2 recommended; road crews clearing primary debris field.

title: Karori area: Road debris clearing operations underway
description: WCC contractors mobilized to clear 60+ tonnes of earth and rock from Karori Road. Estimated clearance time 120 minutes; temporary access for emergency vehicles only established.

title: Liquefaction across Rongotai isthmus threatening runway approach
description: Sand boiling on taxiway and surrounding areas. Airport operations curtailed; aircraft diverted to Auckland.

title: M5.1 aftershock near Island Bay
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 5.1 aftershock at 09:04 NZST, located 15km south of Wellington CBD near Island Bay coast, depth 15km. Strong secondary shaking in southern suburbs.

title: Medical emergency: 68 patients treated for quake-related injuries
description: Capital & Coast DHB updates: emergency department near capacity. Minor injuries predominate but three critical cases in theatre. Requests all available paramedics.

title: Miramar - building roofline now showing visible horizontal displacement
description: Miramar Avenue office building showing 15-20cm horizontal offset at roofline relative to foundation. Building shifting as ground underneath consolidates.

title: Miramar sewerage system failures affecting bay area
description: Sewerage infrastructure in Miramar showing widespread ruptures from ground movement. Contamination risk to harbor from system failures.

title: SCADA: pressure drop in Zone D (Hutt)
description: Automated alert from Zone D (Hutt). System anomaly detected. Probable rupture near Main Street.

title: Seaview port critical systems: transitioning from battery to restored power
description: CentrePort reports main substation now online with reduced capacity. Battery backup systems supplementing power; container operations to resume within 1 hour.

title: Thorndon Quays: warehouse district without power, forklifts and cranes idle
description: Quay Street commercial operations halted. Multiple vehicle accidents on darkened streets. Temporary lighting improvised with emergency flares.

title: Water level rising on Whitmore Street
description: Water inundation in underground car park on Lambton Quay. Water spreading rapidly. Help requested.

title: Building damage: Newtown main shopping area
description: Retail buildings sustaining facade damage and interior structural issues. Fire and Emergency managing evacuations and safety perimeter.

title: CBD street flooding from Lambton Quay water main failure
description: Water main rupture under Lambton Quay has created significant street flooding. Water flowing downhill toward harbor. Pedestrian zone closed.

title: Johnsonville bypass: Overflow traffic management needed
description: Johnsonville bypass at capacity as Ngauranga detour traffic redirects through area. WCC traffic management deploying additional personnel; secondary overflow routes activated.

title: M3.9 aftershock, central area detected
description: GeoNet network detected magnitude 3.9 aftershock at 09:05 NZST, 4km south near central city, depth 11km. Light shaking felt across CBD.

title: Newtown Park Avenue - liquefaction and surface cracks
description: Multiple surface cracks and liquefaction ejection visible on Newtown Park Avenue. One lane closed; engineering teams conducting road surface assessment.

title: Road network disrupted on Ngauranga Gorge State Highway 1
description: Additional rockfall blocking lanes; one-way traffic established. Extreme delays for commuters.

title: Seaview industrial fire risk: temporary power mitigating hazard
description: Wellington Fire and Emergency monitoring Seaview industrial zone for potential power-related fires. Gas detection systems offline; temporary monitoring teams deployed.

title: Seaview port facility cargo area - subsidence creating 50cm crater
description: Port manager reports large ground depression forming in container storage zone on Gracefield Road. Ground material liquefying and consolidating, creating sump with 50cm depth.

title: Structural red-tag: 18 commercial buildings CBD, demolition risk high
description: Structural engineers have red-tagged 18 commercial buildings across Wellington CBD. Many constructed pre-1980s with inadequate seismic reinforcement. Demolition may be required. Surrounding areas remain cordoned.

title: Te Aro - fire hazard assessment identifies electrical arcing risk at damaged building
description: Fire safety team conducting electrical hazard inspection at Vivian Street office building with structural damage. Multiple arcing hazards identified at distribution panels; immediate temporary power disconnection ordered.

title: Wairarapa Line Ghuznee underpass clearance: inspection confirms 8cm reduction; single-vehicle passage only
description: Engineering survey confirms 8cm vertical clearance reduction at Ghuznee Street underpass. Train service restricted to manually routed single-vehicle passage at reduced speed; passengers being evacuated and bussed.

title: Water supply pressure loss affecting Hutt branch network
description: Secondary distribution network in Lower Hutt experiencing total pressure loss. Multiple small ruptures in aging trunk line beneath Hutt Road.

title: Wellington City Council reports M3.5 aftershock emergency impacts
description: Wellington City Council emergency operations: M3.5 aftershock in Thorndon/Quays area. CBD buildings continuing to experience damage. Emergency operations center now commanding multi-agency response.

title: Wellington Electricity: restoration priority zones established, CBD secondary to hospitals
description: First restoration crews dispatched to Wellington Regional Medical Center at 09:20. CBD restoration estimated 2-4 hours minimum.

title: Fifth aftershock: magnitude 5.1 strikes at 09:06; residents frightened
description: GeoNet confirms rapid sequence continuing. Social media reports widespread panic; some residents attempting to flee city north on SH1.

title: Island Bay coastal road subsided and displaced
description: Beachfront road dropped several meters in places. Properties cut off from access; residents stranded.

title: Lower Hutt - building foundation inspection reveals cracks in slab
description: Geotechnical consultant examining Melling Road commercial property discovering foundation slab cracking and minor settlement. Monitoring regime recommended; detailed underpinning assessment required before occupation clearance.

title: M3.6 aftershock Rongotai/Miramar region, sequence continuing
description: M3.6 aftershock at 09:06 NZST, epicentre near Rongotai. GeoNet analysis shows aftershock sequence now producing more frequent smaller magnitude events. Depth 13 km.

title: Miramar power restoration progressing: 60% of customers online
description: Miramar zone circuits being sequentially restored following grid stabilization. Approximately 8,000 of 13,000 customers now have power. Remaining circuits online by 10:15 estimated.

title: Rongotai perimeter road - surface rupture creating traffic barrier
description: Perimeter road at Rongotai Airport showing 1-metre wide surface rupture that has forced vehicle access closure. Ground offset on either side of crack exceeds 15cm.

title: SH1 southbound (Haywards Hill): lane blockage from rock fall debris and overturned vehicle
description: SH1 southbound near Haywards Hill blocked by debris from small rock fall and one overturned sedan. FENZ extracting injured driver; debris removal underway; estimated 90-minute clearance time.

title: Thorndon geotechnical assessment confirms extensive liquefaction
description: GeoNet geotechnical report confirms major liquefaction zone spanning Thorndon waterfront. Lateral spreading toward harbor measured at 60cm in some areas.

title: Emergency evacuation order: Wellington CBD offices to close by 10:00 due to safety hazards
description: WCC and WREMO coordinating orderly evacuation of tall buildings. Elevators non-functional. Stairwell lighting from emergency packs.

title: Evacuate Rongotai: Airport area closure, residential evacuation extended
description: WREMO extends evacuation to Rongotai residential area and declares Wellington Airport operations suspended. Significant liquefaction damage to terminal building and runway. 500+ residents and airport workers being evacuated.

title: Flooding blocks access to emergency services depot
description: Water accumulation near Vivian Street is blocking access road to Fire and Emergency station. Flooding depth 70cm. Alternative access being established.

title: Gas leak: Hunter Street commercial area
description: Gas company and Fire and Emergency responding to underground gas main rupture affecting three buildings. Area evacuated.

title: Hataitai Road retaining wall - partial collapse near shops
description: Approximately 30 cubic metres of retaining wall material has collapsed onto Hataitai Road. Single lane passable with caution; structural engineer on-site.

title: Hutt Line rolling stock: three stationary trains now consolidated at Seaview holding track
description: Stranded passengers on three Hutt Valley Line trains being consolidated at Seaview holding facility. 1,120 passengers transferred via emergency boarding procedures; welfare services (food, water, first aid) deployed.

title: Lower Hutt - gas main rupture on Station Road affecting commercial district
description: Infrared scanning by Wellington City Council identifies elevated gas concentrations near Station Road electricity substation. Immediate neighbourhood evacuation initiated; crews establishing pressure isolation points on either side of break.

title: M3.4 aftershock Te Aro central city, continued aftershock swarm
description: M3.4 aftershock at 09:07 NZST in Te Aro inner city zone. Sustained aftershock swarm continues with expected decreasing magnitudes. Multiple response agencies coordinate city-wide damage assessment.

title: M4.1 aftershock, Mount Victoria region
description: Magnitude 4.1 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 09:07 NZST, 9km south-southwest near Mount Victoria, depth 13km. Light shaking across central and western areas.

title: Petone beach road - water pooling from stormwater backup
description: Beach Road section near waterfront park now showing water accumulation from backed-up stormwater drains. Subsidence creating local sump.

title: Petone port resumption: initial challenges with cargo equipment
description: CentrePort initial cargo operations resuming but experiencing equipment glitches. Crane controls behaving erratically after power surge. Techs working on calibration.

title: Power infrastructure cascading failures affecting northern suburbs
description: Domino effect of transformer failures spreading outage northward. Tawa and Johnsonville affected.

title: Seaview port container damage mounting from ground movement
description: CentrePort reports stacks of shipping containers shifted from liquefaction-induced ground displacement. Some containers overturned creating hazard.

title: Thorndon Quay: Pavement subsidence creating hazard
description: Community reports 20cm differential settlement on Thorndon Quay causing traffic hazard. WCC traffic management placing warning signs and single-lane traffic control.

title: Water main rupture on Woburn Road
description: Water main rupture on Woburn Road. tanker deployed. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Building damage: Hunter Street
description: Fire and Emergency securing area around commercial building with facade and internal damage. Assessment team standing by.

title: CBD - heritage building temporary support system installation nearing completion
description: Customhouse Quay heritage building temporary cable restraint installation nearing completion. Engineering team verifying cable tension and load distribution; monitoring regime to commence following system verification.

title: Cable Car line: Service suspended due to track damage
description: Wellington's famous Cable Car service suspended following earthquake damage assessment on track foundations. Replacement shuttle bus service activated for Botanic Gardens access.

title: CentrePort operations: all berths orange-flagged; inbound shipping diverted to Lyttelton
description: CentrePort has issued orange operational status across all active berths. All inbound vessel traffic redirected to Lyttelton Port (Christchurch, 400 km south); estimated diversion impact 5-7 days.

title: Hataitai residential block: multi-unit apartment evacuation ordered
description: Stuff: structural engineer red-stickers twin apartment buildings on Mahoe Street. 35 residents vacating with Emergency Services assistance.

title: Injury: St John Ambulance services overwhelmed, additional units activated
description: St John Ambulance services report overwhelming demand. All ambulances deployed, response times extending to 45+ minutes for non-critical calls. Additional units activated from regional services.

title: Jervois Quay heritage building - chimney stack now leaning 2 degrees
description: Surveyor video shows chimney on 1900s warehouse at Jervois Quay tilted away from building as structure settles differentially. Masonry cracks forming at base and at parapet line.

title: Kilbirnie liquefaction affecting underground utilities zone
description: Underground cables and pipes in Kilbirnie experiencing stress from ground ruptures. Electricity and water lines damaged at multiple crossing points.

title: Kilbirnie power return: emergency generators stepping down as grid stabilizes
description: Kilbirnie district power back online after 44 minutes. Grid frequency now stable; load shedding protocols releasing controlled blackout zones in sequence.

title: Miramar residents experiencing continuous aftershock activity
description: Miramar residents report M3.6 aftershock with distinct shaking. Local social media indicates 20+ noticeable earthquakes since mainshock. Residents expressing concern about aftershock frequency and wondering when activity will subside.

title: Miramar: extended blackout affecting military naval training campus
description: RNZN facilities at Whakatangi naval dockyard on generators. Critical systems operational but full restoration timeline uncertain.

title: Power outage cascading through central business district
description: Fire at substation in Boulcott Street spreading outage. Multiple businesses evacuating; traffic lights down.

title: Aotea Quay (port access): reinforced barrier installed around subsidence zone; traffic alternating
description: Barriers and warning signage installed around 45cm-deep subsidence crater on Aotea Quay approach. Traffic management alternating directional flows in 15-minute cycles to minimize detours.

title: CBD commercial precinct - facade stabilization operations underway on three buildings
description: Fire and Emergency structural teams conducting facade stabilization operations on three interconnected buildings on Bowen-Lambton corner. Temporary bracing systems installed; commercial occupancy clearance pending detailed load verification.

title: Communications challenge: cell towers on backup power capacity
description: Mobile carriers reporting that many Wellington cell towers now on backup power running at reduced capacity. Data speeds degraded. Full power restoration expected to restore normal service.

title: Fire and Emergency NZ coordinates response across central city
description: Fire and Emergency NZ coordinates large-scale emergency response across Te Aro and central Wellington following M3.4 aftershock. Rescue teams deployed to building damage hotspots.

title: Island Bay Parade - coastal erosion and subsidence
description: Coastal road experiencing significant subsidence near foreshore. Approximately 0.3m of relative ground movement affecting pavement integrity.

title: Kilbirnie Parade retail - business frontage cracking in curved pattern
description: Kilbirnie Parade shopfront shows curved horizontal cracks across facade, indicating differential settlement across building width. Cracks now 5mm wide and still widening.

title: Liquefaction and structural damage at Rongotai school complex
description: School buildings unsafe for occupancy; students evacuated from campus. Alternative arrangements sought.

title: M3.8 aftershock, southern zone
description: Magnitude 3.8 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 09:09 NZST, 12km south, depth 13km. Light shaking detected.

title: Main failure on Jervois Quay
description: Major water loss on Jervois Quay. Surface rupture widening. Emergency assistance needed.

title: Petone residential area ground fissures expanding
description: Fissures opening on residential streets in Petone creating safety hazard. Fresh liquefied sand deposits visible. Residents reporting ground movement continuing.

title: Structural assessment: Lower Hutt commercial
description: WCC engineers assessing substantial damage to retail/commercial complex. Building declared unsafe; comprehensive structural engineering report ordered.

title: Te Aro residential complex: residents trapped without lift access due to power loss
description: Multi-story apartment block with failed emergency lighting. Residents on upper floors unable to safely descend. Fire and Emergency NZ providing assistance.

title: Water main breaks along Quay Street, Thorndon
description: Three separate ruptures along Quay Street main trunk. Each break spraying water onto roadway. Combined flow approaching 8,000 litres per minute.

title: Welfare centre activation: Karori with 500+ arrivals, additional resources
description: Karori Community Hall reports 450+ displaced people in facility. Additional medical resources and catering trucks being deployed. Most arrivals from CBD, Thorndon and Kelburn. Capacity nearing maximum.

title: Hospital power: Wellington transitioning to mains from generator
description: Hutt Valley High Hospital reports main power feed restored. Generator systems on standby; all critical services now mains-supplied. No patient impact from power transition.

title: Johnsonville Line catenary restoration: repair crews working overhead repairs for 60+ minutes
description: KiwiRail line crews installed temporary catenary support at Johnsonville main line section. Electrical tests ongoing; estimated 45-minute restoration time before service resumption trials.

title: KiwiRail Metlink: all rail operations suspended due to power loss and track damage
description: Northern and Hutt Valley lines de-energized. Commuter trains stranded at various stations. Passengers evacuated using emergency procedures.

title: Liquefaction zone expands: Thorndon facing 'significant displacement'
description: GeoNet geotechnical team reports extensive sand boils and ground ruptures along reclaimed waterfront. Properties sinking 15-20cm; infrastructure damage severe.

title: M3.5 aftershock Thorndon/Quays detected, epicentre near harbour
description: GeoNet seismograph detects M3.5 aftershock at 09:10 NZST near Thorndon/Quays waterfront. Depth 10 km. Felt in central city. Aftershock sequence shows expected decreasing magnitude trend continuing.

title: M4.7 aftershock, Seaview recorded
description: Magnitude 4.7 aftershock detected at 09:10 NZST by GeoNet seismographs, epicentre near Seaview/Gracefield industrial area 18km northeast, depth 13km. Moderate shaking felt in eastern zone.

title: Multi-vehicle collision on Featherston Street, light injuries reported
description: At least 4 vehicles involved; traffic signals inoperative. Minor injuries treated on scene; area congested.

title: Newtown main street retail damage
description: Shopping centre with shattered storefronts and interior structural compromise observed. Debris blocking footpaths; Fire and Emergency clearing.

title: Rongotai terminal building tilt increasing from subsidence
description: Real-time structural monitoring shows passenger terminal building tilt increasing progressively. Evacuation complete. Structural engineers positioning monitoring equipment.

title: SH1 northbound: Temporary emergency route activated
description: NZTA activates emergency northbound access via Johnsonville secondary route with traffic management. Capacity 50% of normal; heavy vehicle restrictions in place.

title: Structural survey team reports foundation settlement in central CBD
description: WCC structural engineer notes uneven ground subsidence patterns on Thorndon Quay related to liquefaction. Three adjacent heritage shop buildings showing foundation differential movement; immediate monitoring and detailed underpinning assessment required.

title: Surface flooding in CBD
description: Confirmed flooding on Panama Street affecting basement of Civic Square office complex. Depth 30cm. pumping underway.

title: Thorndon retail - storefront glass now completely failed in section
description: The Terrace retail section showing multiple storefronts with complete window failure. Broken glass now hazard preventing retail access.

title: Trunk main failure in Petone
