description: Water main rupture on Hutt Road. tanker deployed. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Welfare centre: Kelburn gymnasium reaches 240 residents, near capacity
description: Kelburn school gymnasium now accommodating 240 residents with new arrivals slowing. Capacity ceiling approaching. Additional shelter being arranged at secondary school 2km away.

title: Cuba Street (CBD) - liquefaction crater near Vivian Street
description: Settlement crater blocking Cuba Street northbound near Vivian Street intersection. Approximately 0.8m diameter; traffic diverted to alternative route.

title: Flooding reported in underground carpark, Willis Street
description: Willis Street underground carpark now has water 1.5m deep. Residents evacuating vehicles. Structural integrity of parking structure being assessed.

title: Large diameter break on Main Street
description: Water main rupture on Main Street. isolation valve activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: M3.3 aftershock Oriental Bay coastal zone, wave activity monitored
description: M3.3 aftershock at 09:11 NZST near Oriental Bay. Coastal areas continue monitoring for unusual wave activity. M3+ aftershocks now spacing at 4-5 minute intervals.

title: Miramar foreshore - sand ejected 15 metres onto adjacent residential street
description: Photos confirm sand boils on The Esplanade have ejected liquefied material across Miramar Avenue pavement and into residential properties. Estimated 200 cubic metres of sand displaced.

title: Miramar properties sinking at differential rates from liquefaction
description: Structural survey of residential area shows varying subsidence rates creating dangerous conditions. Structures tilting at angles up to 2 degrees.

title: Newtown - chimney safety inspection campaign beginning at residential properties
description: Fire safety team beginning systematic chimney inspection campaign across Newtown to identify fire hazards and blockages. Residents being advised of inspection schedule; repair referrals being provided for hazardous conditions.

title: Petone residents: power still out, street lights dark, community gathering in town center
description: Over 200 residents assembling at Petone shopping center for information and support. Local council representatives providing updates.

title: RNZ reports: Stuff newsroom confirms 65% of bus network disrupted due to rail cascade failure
description: Media monitoring via RNZ confirms Stuff reporting that 65% of Metlink bus operations disrupted by rail-dependent schedule cascading. Transport minister's office issuing statement within 30 minutes.

title: Structural assessment: Cuba Street
description: WCC conducting rapid structural assessment of residential building showing significant damage. Building declared unsafe pending detailed review.

title: Trauma injuries: Emergency response to crushed pedestrians on Featherston
description: Fire and Emergency responds to 14 pedestrians trapped under collapsed shop canopy on Featherston Street. Seven with serious crush injuries to lower limbs. Medical helicopters called for rapid transport to Wellington Hospital.

title: Water rupture suspected in Zone A (CBD)
description: Automated alert from Zone A (CBD). System anomaly detected. Probable rupture near Featherston Street.

title: Weather threat: rain forecast may complicate power restoration
description: MetService forecasts rain arriving 11:30 NZST. Concern that wet conditions could cause additional electrical hazards during power restoration work. Crews on alert.

title: Widespread building damage including to heritage structures in Wadestown
description: Historic buildings showing serious cracks; one older building lost roof section. Engineering assessments needed.

title: Brooklyn Road: Excavator deployed for debris removal
description: Heavy machinery mobilized on Brooklyn Road to begin clearing 50m² of landslide debris. Secondary collapse risk identified; stabilization works expected 150+ minutes.

title: Downtown Wellington businesses report M3.5 aftershock impact
description: Staff at Thorndon and downtown offices report M3.5 aftershock caused items to shift on shelves and desks. No structural damage noted. Business continuity efforts continue despite ongoing seismic activity.

title: Flood from water mains rupture in Kilbirnie near town center
description: Large geyser erupting from broken main; roads flooded. Residents using alternative routes.

title: Oriental Bay command centre: IT systems returning to normal
description: Wellington Police command centre power fully restored. IT systems coming online; comms networks transitioning from backup radio to primary systems.

title: Power restoration delayed: Eastern suburbs without lights for hours
description: Wellington Electricity advises restoration work hampered by widespread damage and aftershock safety concerns. Expect 4-6 hours minimum without power.

title: Resident report (Seaview): informal observation of port equipment fire and black smoke visible
description: Resident reported seeing black smoke plume rising from CentrePort container area (unverified at this time). Fire services dispatched for reconnaissance; no official confirmation yet but community monitoring active.

title: Seaview port container stacks - now unstable and shifting
description: Grounded container stacks on Gracefield Road showing movement with each aftershock. CentrePort isolation zone expanded as containers at risk of toppling.

title: Te Aro - building yellow tag inspection reveals partition wall structural failure
description: WCC structural engineer yellow-tagging residential building on Oxford Terrace following discovery of failed non-bearing partition wall bracing. Lateral load transfer analysis underway; temporary wall bracing being planned.

title: Wellington CBD water mains multiple failure zones confirmed
description: Wellington Water engineering teams have now confirmed 8 separate major water main rupture zones across CBD. Coordinated repair strategy being developed.

title: Wellington Electricity: second feeder line energized to CBD, partial restoration underway
description: Temporary switching procedures completed at 09:35. Limited capacity, targeting waterfront and emergency service facilities first.

title: Coastal residents report M3.3 aftershock, tsunami risk remains low
description: Oriental Bay residents report M3.3 aftershock with noticeable shaking. WREMO confirms tsunami risk remains low. Coastal monitoring continues. Residents remain in elevated readiness.

title: Community report - Te Aro apartment block shows severe diagonal cracking
description: Resident report describes major X-pattern cracking from ground to roof on Willis Street apartment building. Shear failure pattern suspected; residents have self-evacuated and street is being cordoned pending engineering verification.

title: Fire alarm systems failing across downtown: fire safety risk assessment urgent
description: Loss of mains power affecting fire detection systems in multiple commercial buildings. Manual inspections being conducted.

title: Gas leak: Te Aro Taranaki Street
description: Fire and Emergency responding to large gas main rupture affecting commercial area. Multi-building evacuation underway; utilities on site.

title: Hutt Valley Highway - slide debris blocking southbound near Silverstream
description: Large debris field from hillside slide covering southbound carriageway near Silverstream Road. One lane blocked; emergency crews clearing high-priority items.

title: Injury reports multiplying from falls and collapsing structures
description: Paramedics overwhelmed; multiple sites with casualties. Triage centers being established.

title: Kilbirnie - multiple sinkholes now forming at regular intervals
description: Aerial footage shows at least 8 sinkhole formations across Kilbirnie low-lying zone. New holes appearing as ground continues consolidating from liquefaction.

title: M3.9 aftershock, Karori-Wadestown
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 3.9 aftershock at 09:13 NZST, 10km west in Karori-Wadestown area, depth 12km. Light shaking felt in western suburbs.

title: Main failure on Woburn Road
description: Major water loss on Woburn Road. Water spray 2+ metres high. Emergency assistance needed.

title: Melling Line platform recovery: train service trial commencing with single carriage and reduced passengers
description: KiwiRail initiating pilot service on Melling Line with single-carriage test train carrying max 120 passengers (vs. standard 320). Trial run from Melling to Wellington Station via Brooklyn Junction at 12 km/h.

title: Structural assessment: Oriental Bay beachfront buildings compromised
description: Structural engineers assess Oriental Bay beachfront buildings showing liquefaction damage and foundation settlement up to 0.5m. Three residential buildings declared unsafe. Residents relocated to welfare centres.

title: Tawa approach: Detour congestion spreading
description: Tawa area experiencing spillback congestion from SH1 northbound detours. Traffic management implementing staged entry; parking areas filling with queued vehicles.

title: Thorndon liquefaction creating harbor embankment stress
description: Lateral ground spreading is stressing harbor embankment structures. Engineering team monitoring for potential embankment failure from continued liquefaction movement.

title: Water isolation valves being manually activated
description: Wellington Water crew manually isolating damaged sections using emergency isolation valves. Process slow due to valve access points being submerged.

title: Welfare concerns: vulnerable populations in powerless care facilities
description: Concern raised for elderly residents in powerless rest homes and care facilities in Newtown. Wellington City Council coordinating transfer of critical cases to Hutt Hospital.

title: Building facade failure on Cuba Street damaging parked vehicles
description: Brick and mortar falling from 4-story building onto street. Two vehicles crushed; area evacuated.

title: CBD emergency routing: Willis Street opened as main artery
description: Wellington City Council reopens Willis Street as primary emergency access through CBD. Bidirectional traffic management; side streets remain closed due to building damage risk.

title: First death reported: construction worker injured at collapsed site
description: One person confirmed deceased after partial building collapse in Lambton Quay area. Police investigating; victim identity being withheld pending family notification.

title: Hutt Valley: widespread outage affecting five suburbs, estimated 25,000 residents
description: Petone, Seaview, Gracefield, and Lower Hutt CBD without power. Water pressure declining. Restoration crews mobilizing from regional depot.

title: Injury: Multiple casualties from delayed building collapse on Willis
description: Willis Street building partially collapses at 09:17 NZST, one hour after initial evacuation. Seven rescue team members and workers caught in secondary collapse. Emergency medical response activated.

title: Lower Hutt - utility coordination meeting scheduled for repair sequencing
description: Wellington City Council scheduling coordination meeting with electricity, water, and gas utilities for Station Road infrastructure repair sequencing. Shared trench access planning underway; repair timeline integration being discussed.

title: M3.4 aftershock Te Aro central city zone
description: M3.4 aftershock at 09:14 NZST, located in Te Aro area. GeoNet confirms depth of 12 km. Felt across inner Wellington. Aftershock frequency now 8-10 events per hour in M3+ range.

title: Petone Beach Road - surface water pooling from burst storm drain
description: Video shows Beach Road near Petone foreground park flooding from combined burst storm drain and surface subsidence. Shallow water (30cm) covering 100m section, blocking vehicle access.

title: Petone Bridge (SH2 crossing): bearing assessment showing acceptable post-quake integrity
description: NZTA structural engineering team completed rapid assessment of Petone Bridge. Bearing assemblies confirmed operational; bridge reopened to bidirectional traffic with 40 km/h speed limit pending full inspection.

title: Seaview liquefaction lateral spreading rate measured
description: Field survey teams measuring continued lateral spreading in Seaview at 2-3cm per hour in peak liquefaction areas. Spreading directed toward harbor.

title: Structural assessment: Te Aro mixed-use building
description: WCC engineers inspecting building with apparent foundation settlement and wall cracking. Rapid assessment underway to determine occupancy status.

title: Thorndon full restoration: all residential customers now online
description: Thorndon and Quays residential areas now 100% powered. Commercial sector still at ~70% capacity due to higher damage assessment requirements. Crews ongoing.

title: CentrePort damage report: estimated USD 8.2M preliminary loss; three berths offline indefinitely
description: CentrePort management issued preliminary damage estimate following structural surveys: USD 8.2M in infrastructure damage. Three primary berths (Aotea, Kings Wharf North, Thorndon Container) declared offline pending 4-6 week repair timeline.

title: Evacuation extended: Kilbirnie peninsula all residential areas
description: WREMO expands evacuation to entire Kilbirnie peninsula due to liquefaction risk and isthmus instability. 1200+ residents affected. Evacuation routes via SH2. Assembly points established at Newtown and Karori welfare centres.

title: Evans Bay Parade: Liquefaction damage visible along route
description: Evans Bay Parade showing clear evidence of ground liquefaction with pavement buckling. Traffic reduced to single lane; wave pattern monitoring ongoing.

title: Featherston Street - pavement heave affecting traffic flow
description: Liquefaction-induced heave has created 0.4m vertical offset on Featherston Street. One lane closed; traffic alternating; temporary repair assessment underway.

title: Fire and Emergency - structural team initiating roofing system damage documentation
description: Fire and Emergency structural specialists conducting detailed photogrammetric documentation of roofing system damage across CBD commercial precinct. Wind-resistant membrane failure patterns being analyzed; engineering assessment underway.

title: Flooding accumulates across Petone industrial area
description: Water pooling across multiple blocks in Petone industrial zone. Liquefaction has created depression zones trapping runoff. Port operations access restricted.

title: Gushing water from Coleridge Terrace
description: Major water loss on Coleridge Terrace. Water spray 2+ metres high. Emergency assistance needed.

title: Kilbirnie building damage assessment beginning systematically
description: WCC structural engineers beginning systematic damage assessment in Kilbirnie. First sweep identifies 5 buildings requiring immediate evacuation for safety reasons.

title: M3.2 aftershock Brooklyn hillside, density of smaller events increasing
description: M3.2 aftershock at 09:15 NZST on Brooklyn hillside. GeoNet analysis shows clear transition toward smaller magnitude, higher frequency swarm behavior. Aftershock pattern now entering mature phase.

title: M4.9 aftershock, Rongotai Airport region
description: GeoNet network detected magnitude 4.9 aftershock at 09:15 NZST, 12km south-southeast near Rongotai/Airport area, depth 11km. Ground motion confirmed across eastern suburbs.

title: Oriental Bay: partial power restoration beginning, streetlights flickering on
description: First signs of recovery as emergency response protocols show initial success. Local cafe restarting backup generators.

title: Post-restoration surge damage reports: fire brigade responding
description: Fire and Emergency responding to reports of electrical fires and burning smells in multiple CBD buildings as power restoration causes surge damage. Suppression teams active.

title: Road closure on Tasman Street intersection blocking Newtown traffic flow
description: Pavement buckled; one lane impassable. Traffic lights inoperative; manual control established.

title: Structural assessment: Featherston Street
description: WCC evaluating historic commercial building showing multiple damage indicators. Occupancy restrictions being imposed.

title: Thorndon Quay streetscape - lamppost bases now submerged by water
description: Water accumulation from main rupture has ponded along Thorndon Quay, submerging streetscape fixtures. Combined flooding and subsidence creating deeper than expected water pocket.

title: Building damage: CBD high-rise
description: Media reporting substantial damage to 12-storey office tower in CBD. Fire and Emergency on scene; occupants being evacuated.

title: CBD core power: Customhouse Quay substation circuits sequentially re-energizing
description: Primary substation circuits online in phases. CBD now at 45% power capacity; Lambert Quay and Willis Street core blocks receiving priority. Full CBD restoration 10:45 estimated.

title: Emergency services report ongoing aftershock monitoring
description: Fire and Emergency NZ and Wellington City Council coordinate continuous monitoring of aftershock impacts. M3.4 aftershock detected in Te Aro. No new major incidents reported. Response teams maintain elevated alert status.

title: Fire and Emergency - structural bracing operations expanding across city
description: Specialized bracing teams deployed to 14 high-priority sites across CBD and Te Aro. Focus on preventing secondary collapse of partially damaged structures; temporary steel props being installed at critical load-bearing locations.

title: Island Bay beach: liquefaction damage observed on coastal promenade
description: Newshub reports ground subsidence and lateral spread on foreshore. Homes elevated on pilings showing foundation damage. Evacuations commencing.

title: M3.7 aftershock, northern region
description: GeoNet seismograph recorded magnitude 3.7 aftershock at 09:16 NZST, 17km north-northeast, depth 12km. Light shaking in northern areas.

title: Newtown: residents reporting flickering lights, uncertain if power returning or grid surging
description: Brief moments of light in homes followed by darkness. Potential dangerous voltage fluctuations as grid attempts restart.

title: Petone street infrastructure buckled from ground movement
description: Roads in Petone showing severe buckling and offset from liquefaction. Asphalt cracked extensively. Traffic Department assessing road closures needed.

title: Rongotai perimeter - ground deformation now visible from terminal
description: Airport operations staff observe ground rippling visible from terminal windows across runways and taxiways. Deformation pattern suggesting large subsidence zone.

title: Stuff online: Wellington CBD transport 'paralyzed' despite Metlink emergency activation
description: Stuff newsroom reporting live from Civic Centre: 'Wellington transport network functionally paralyzed.' Metlink emergency protocols deployed; bus queues extending 200+ meters at staging areas.

title: Widespread injuries from fallen shelving at supermarket in Lower Hutt
description: Retail shelves collapsed across entire produce section. Multiple shoppers injured during peak morning hours.

title: Brooklyn residents report M3.2 aftershock, building checks begin
description: Brooklyn residents report M3.2 aftershock and begin informal structural checks of homes. Community self-organization for welfare checking underway. Social support networks activating.

title: Data loss concern: government systems at risk from power instability
description: Government IT reporting potential data corruption risk on some databases during power restoration sequence. Backup recovery teams preparing contingency plans.

title: Featherston Street warehouse - concrete floor slab fragmentation detected
description: Structural photos show concrete floor slab of 2-storey warehouse at Featherston Street broken into sections, walls showing stepped shear cracks. Building safety officer recommends immediate evacuation.

title: Gas leak: Bowen Street basement
description: Fire and Emergency investigating gas in building basement area. Gas company locating leak; area ventilated and cleared of occupants.

title: Injury cascade: Wellington Hospital trauma centre beyond capacity
description: Wellington Hospital declares mass casualty protocol activated. 78 patients now in ED, 35 in trauma theatre. Wairarapa and Hutt Valley hospitals receiving overflow patients via ambulance convoy. Critical shortage of blood supplies.

title: Liquefaction features destroying infrastructure across Kilbirnie isthmus
description: Multiple sand boils and subsidence across residential area. Utilities damaged; water disruption.

title: Melling Line first service resumption: 122 passengers successfully transported from Wellington to Brooklyn
description: Melling Line restoration operational: single-carriage service completed 45-minute trial run from Wellington Station to Brooklyn Junction with 122 passengers. No additional damage observed; KiwiRail green-lighting two-carriage service trial within 30 minutes.

title: Miramar cutting: Geotechnical team installing safety netting
description: Engineers installing rock catch netting on Miramar cutting upper slopes to prevent further rockfall. Road remains fully closed; completion estimated 90 minutes.

title: Rongotai airport operations suspended due to structural concerns
description: Wellington Airport has suspended all flight operations pending structural assessment. Runways and taxiways showing signs of liquefaction damage.

title: SH2 southbound near Woburn - liquefaction subsidence affecting both lanes
description: Extensive liquefaction settlement has created rough surface across both lanes near Woburn. Speed restrictions imposed; full technical assessment scheduled.

title: Secondary network rupture, Karori Chapel Road
description: 150mm main feeding Karori suburbs ruptured beneath Chapel Road. Water pressure test points showing no flow. Backpressure from other breaks complicating repairs.

title: Te Aro - structural engineer documents non-ductile connection vulnerability
description: Structural engineer completing technical documentation of non-ductile connection vulnerability across building stock. Report identifying estimated 40% of pre-1980s commercial buildings affected; remediation strategy development underway.

title: Wellington Electricity emergency: grid voltage instability detected, temporary blackouts expected
description: Brownout conditions developing as system attempts sequenced restart. Load shedding cycling through suburbs. Full restoration 6-8 hours estimated.

title: Community power status poll: majority of Wellington restored
description: Informal social media poll shows 75% of Wellington residents reporting power restored within past 30 minutes. Hotspots remaining in eastern suburbs and lower Hutt. Some false reports of looting.

title: Gas rupture: Victoria Street basement
description: Fire and Emergency responding to reports of gas in basement of historic commercial building. Evacuation underway; utility company being contacted.

title: Kilbirnie residents: power flickering, first signs of recovery for peninsula suburb
description: Lights briefly illuminating homes. Water pressure increasing slightly. Residents hopeful but cautious about full restoration.

title: Lower Hutt - community reports gas smell persists despite isolation efforts
description: Residents reporting continued gas odour on Station Road despite reported isolation procedures. WCC investigation team re-checking valve operations; secondary rupture or residual trapped gas suspected requiring additional leak detection sweep.

title: M3.3 aftershock near Orient Bay coastal area
description: GeoNet records M3.3 aftershock at 09:18 NZST near Oriental Bay. Depth 11 km. Coastal residents report feeling distinct shaking. Aftershock sequence remains active with expected frequency.

title: M3.8 aftershock, Hataitai recorded
description: Magnitude 3.8 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 09:18 NZST, 8km south-southwest near Hataitai, depth 12km. Light shaking felt in central and southern areas.

title: Miramar liquefaction fissure creating residential isolation
description: Large ground fissure now 50cm wide blocking access to residential cul-de-sac. Residents on isolated side need evacuation assistance.

title: Ngaio area: Secondary street network redirecting traffic
description: Ngaio neighborhood streets experiencing unexpected traffic diversion from blocked main routes. WCC monitoring for capacity issues on residential streets.

title: Ngauranga Gorge southbound re-opening: single-lane bypass operational; traffic moving at reduced speed
description: SH1 Ngauranga Gorge southbound lane restored to one-way operation. NZTA traffic management deployed; northbound traffic continuing via inner-city bypass (Taranaki Street). Estimate 120+ minutes for full bidirectional restoration.

title: Regional coordination center activated; WREMO takes lead response
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management Office declares major incident. NZDF standby alert issued; additional resources from Palmerston North mobilized.

title: State Highway 2 northbound closed at Haywards Hill
description: Bridge approach damaged and unsafe for traffic. All northbound vehicles diverted via SH1.

title: Structural assessment: Hospital operational status re-evaluated, stays open
description: Wellington Hospital undergoes structural re-evaluation. Building deemed safe for continued emergency operations despite minor damage. Capacity maintained as primary trauma centre.

title: Te Aro residential building - balcony now pulling away from building face
description: Vivian Street apartment building showing exterior balcony separating from main wall. Separation now visible without magnification, estimated 3-5cm gap.

title: Aftershock magnitude 4.3 centred Wadestown, further building collapses
description: GeoNet records magnitude 4.3 aftershock at 09:19 NZST centred near Wadestown. Triggers further minor collapses in CBD. One residential building on Lambton Quay now partially collapsed.

title: CBD multiple building evacuations ordered from structural damage
description: Wellington City Council has ordered evacuation of 12 additional buildings in CBD due to structural damage from liquefaction settlement. Engineers concerned about progressive damage.

title: Community report: limited access to cash from ATM outages
description: Many ATMs throughout Wellington still offline or dispensing errors post-power restoration. Residents able to access limited cash. Banks advising to use card payments.

title: Critical power outage affecting Porirua hospital operations
description: Main supply lost; emergency generators activated. Patient care affected; transfers in progress.

title: Emergency: Hutt Road water main down
description: Water main rupture on Hutt Road. supply rerouted. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Flooding spreads to residential streets, Te Aro
description: Water from street-level breaks is flowing into residential lane ways. Residents reporting water entering ground floor units on Tory Street precinct.

title: Hutt Valley Line rehabilitation assessment: partial restoration possible by 13:00 NZST
description: KiwiRail assessment team reported that Hutt Valley Line segment between Thorndon and Petone may be serviceable by 13:00 NZST (90 minutes) pending final rail geometry verification. Limited passenger capacity (50%) expected initially.

title: M3.1 aftershock Hataitai, peak activity phase continuing
description: M3.1 aftershock at 09:19 NZST in Hataitai area. Aftershock sequence peak activity phase continuing beyond initial 90 minutes. Forecasted aftershock duration extending toward 6-8 hours.

title: Multiple gas leaks mapped across city network - Hutt Road gas infrastructure compromised
description: Wellington infrastructure SCADA telemetry indicates pressure anomalies at six separate locations on transmission lines serving Hutt Road corridor. Main supply isolation valves activated; scheduled pressurization suspension to repair ruptures.

title: Rongotai Airport: runway lighting systems restored, inbound flights resuming from 10:15
description: Temporary power supply established to critical airport systems. Full terminal restoration still pending. Ground handling resuming.

title: Seaview - third major sand boil eruption erupting near warehouse
description: New eruption point identified near industrial warehouse on Gracefield Road. Sand column reaching 1.5 metres height with wider cone base than earlier boils.

title: Taranaki Street - settling and cracking near Willis Street
description: Liquefaction causing ground settling on Taranaki Street near Willis Street intersection. One lane affected; traffic management in place.

title: Traffic backup: Ngauranga detour approaching critical capacity
description: NZTA monitoring shows detour traffic building rapidly with 300+ vehicle queue. Additional control points activated on Johnsonville approach; spillback risk to residential areas.

title: CBD commercial zone - building tagging summary compiled for emergency management
description: Fire and Emergency compiling preliminary building tagging summary showing 18 red-tagged, 32 yellow-tagged, and 64 green-tagged properties in CBD area. Report being transmitted to city emergency operations center.

title: CBD waterfront: major infrastructure restoration checkpoint passed, grid stabilizing
description: Frequency returning to normal parameters. Waterfront businesses and government offices restoring operations. Emergency lighting transitions to mains power.

title: Cable Car Lower Terminal: Access closed for repairs
description: Cable Car Lower Terminal road access closed as terminal building requires structural assessment. Shuttle bus loading relocated to nearby street.

title: CentrePort emergency operations center: surge of re-routings to Lyttelton and Auckland ports
description: CentrePort emergency ops coordinating urgent vessel re-routings. Four inbound container ships now redirected to Lyttelton (48-hour delay) and two to Auckland (72-hour delay). Port authority liaising with shipping lines on diversion costs.

title: Extensive liquefaction and sand boils on Wakefield Street
description: Multiple fountains of sand and muddy water erupting from pavement. Street closed indefinitely.

title: M4.3 aftershock, Newtown recorded
description: Magnitude 4.3 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 09:20 NZST, epicentre 10km south of CBD in Newtown area, depth 12km. Moderate shaking reported.

title: Oriental Bay beachfront residents report aftershock shaking
description: Oriental Bay residents confirm M3.3 aftershock caused small items to shift. Beachfront security and residents continue monitoring for any tsunami signs. Water level remains stable with no wave activity.

title: Petone industrial: container operations resuming with power stable
description: CentrePort confirms normal power levels at Petone port facility. Container cranes and dock systems fully powered. Operations ramping back to 60% capacity by 10:45.

title: Rongotai taxiway - repeated ground compaction waves visible
description: Airport ground crew documents 8-10 compaction waves across holding area taxiway, each 20-30cm high and 3-4 metres apart. Ground still deforming between aftershocks.

title: SH1 and SH2 traffic chaos: queues extending north as residents flee
description: NZTA reports state highways gridlocked northbound from Porirua through Ngauranga. Traffic cameras show bumper-to-bumper congestion; journey times exceeding 2 hours.

title: Structural assessment: Grey Street residential
description: WCC assessing 1940s villa with major cracking and foundation movement. Resident access restricted; engineering assessment in progress.

title: Thorndon liquefaction forcing commercial precinct closure
description: Multiple businesses in Thorndon commercial area closing due to liquefaction damage. Building owners concerned about continued ground movement affecting structures.

title: Aftershock concern: secondary quake during power restoration creates risk
description: GeoNet reports M4.2 aftershock at 09:37. Power crews temporarily halt restoration work to ensure safety. No additional damage reported; work resuming within 5 minutes.

title: CBD structural survey - interior column cracking pattern at office building
description: Structural engineer discovering cracking patterns on interior columns of Bowen Street office building indicative of shear failure mode. Load redistribution analysis underway; bracing calculations being performed for potential emergency shoring.

title: Injuries mounting at multiple sites across Wellington metropolitan area
description: Hospitals receiving steady stream of earthquake trauma patients. Bed capacity becoming issue.

title: Lambton Quay reversible-lane experiment: successful trial run with manual traffic control
description: Wellington City Council trial of reversible-lane configuration on Lambton Quay northbound completed successfully. Manual traffic officers managing 4-minute switching cycles; estimated 60% flow capacity maintained.

title: M3.6 aftershock near harbourside
description: Magnitude 3.6 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 09:21 NZST, 6km east near harbourside, depth 10km. Light shaking felt.

title: Miramar cutting - secondary rock fall blocking lane
description: Follow-up rock fall from cutting has blocked additional lane on Miramar Avenue. Total closure now affecting all southbound traffic movement.

title: Miramar foreshore erosion - exposed infrastructure now visible
description: Subsidence at Miramar foreshore has exposed old stormwater outlet piping to air. Wave action now eroding exposed pipework, creation of new underwater sump.

title: Network-wide water loss exceeds 50 million litres daily
description: SCADA modeling shows estimated 50+ million litres per day water loss across broken networks. Reservoir depletion accelerating. Emergency conservation measures enacted.

title: Seaview industrial damage assessment identifies critical infrastructure at risk
description: Preliminary assessment of Seaview industrial zone identifies critical fuel storage and chemical facilities at risk from continued liquefaction movement.

title: WREMO updates crisis status: peak aftershock phase ongoing
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management updates all agencies: aftershock sequence peak activity phase continuing. M3+ events still occurring. Response operations sustained at maximum alert through early afternoon.

title: Welfare centre at capacity: Newtown redirects arrivals to Hataitai
description: Newtown Community Centre at full capacity with 380 people. New arrivals being redirected to hastily-opened welfare point at Hataitai Scout Hall. Medical support coordinated between both facilities.

title: Window facade failure: Lambton area office tower
description: Large glass facade panel fallen from 12-storey building. Street sealed off; no occupant injuries reported but building being evacuated.

title: Building damage: Newtown main area
description: Fire and Emergency continuing operations in Newtown following multiple building damage reports. Structural engineers coordinating assessments.

title: Gas supply at risk: inspection of distribution network urgently underway
description: Wellington Gas warns of potential further ruptures in distribution mains. Residents in affected areas advised to turn off gas at meter and avoid using appliances.

title: Hataitai area: Street subsidence causing traffic redirection
description: WCC reports significant surface subsidence on main Hataitai route. Traffic diverted to secondary streets; structural assessment ongoing for residential properties nearby.

title: Kilbirnie shopping complex flooding from water main failures
description: Shopping center experiencing water intrusion from multiple ruptured water lines beneath structure. Flooding spreading through basement levels.

title: M3.2 aftershock Brooklyn/Newtown area
description: M3.2 aftershock at 09:22 NZST detected near Brooklyn/Newtown boundary. Depth 14 km. GeoNet magnitude adjusted to 3.2 following analysis. Sequence magnitude distribution matches predicted Gutenberg-Richter law.

title: NZ Herald breaking news: 'Wellington transport infrastructure sustains 'severe but manageable' damage'
description: NZ Herald transport correspondent reporting: 'Emergency services confirm 70% of Wellington's CBD rail network has sustained significant structural damage. However, initial response indicates recovery timeline may be shorter than feared.'

title: Newtown residential safety assessment - structural engineer commences building triage
description: WCC-commissioned structural surveyor conducting rapid visual assessment of Newtown housing stock. Building stock triage system deployed; green/yellow/red tagging underway on Rongotai and Daniell Streets with focus on pre-1980s construction.

title: Power out for large section of Thorndon residential area
description: Lines down across multiple streets. Some residences without power or water; emergency crews responding.

title: Structural assessment: Civic Centre foundation failure confirmed
description: Structural engineers confirm critical foundation failure at Civic Centre building. Building subsiding unevenly due to liquefaction beneath southern half. Demolition contractor on standby for emergency demolition if risk escalates.

title: Te Aro apartment block - external wall now showing outward bulge
description: Photos of Vivian Street building show external wall now bulging outward, indicating interior damage and structural distress from ground movement.

title: Te Aro power restoration: water ingress hazard cleared
description: Taranaki Street water drainage complete. Power restoration crews re-energizing circuits in Te Aro ward. Approximately 6,000 customers regaining power within 10 minutes.

title: Wellington Regional Medical Center: full power restoration completed
description: Hospital transitions from emergency generators to mains supply at 10:25. All surgical capabilities fully operational again.

title: Brooklyn Road - retaining wall failure expansion
description: Retaining wall failure expanding; additional 50 cubic metres of material now blocking roadway. Emergency shoring being assessed; full closure continues.

title: Customhouse Quay - water main spray limiting building access
description: WCC operations reports water pressure from Panama Street main rupture has increased, spray now reaching third-storey windows on Customhouse Quay. Building evacuation zone expanded.

title: Evacuation: Miramar residential zone, peninsula access only via SH2
description: WREMO issues evacuation order for Miramar peninsula following liquefaction assessment. Reclaimed land surrounding airport showing extensive subsidence. 700+ residents evacuating. Single route via SH2 subject to heavy traffic.

title: Flooding closes access on main arterial route
description: Water depth on Lambton Quay CBD now exceeds 1.5m. Road is closed to all traffic. Businesses evacuating. Water cascading off higher ground pooling in low points.

title: Gas detection: Lower Hutt area
description: Fire and Emergency responding to gas detected in Lower Hutt commercial zone. Evacuation procedures initiated; utilities on high alert.

title: Lower Hutt - crisis response team establishes temporary facility for building owners
description: Wellington City Council establishing temporary information facility at Hutt City Council office for affected building owners. Engineering assessment results, repair guidance, and support program information being provided.

title: M3.0 aftershock Island Bay coastal area, swarm tempo acceleration
description: M3.0 aftershock at 09:23 NZST near Island Bay. Aftershock tempo showing continued acceleration with now 8-10 M3+ events per hour. Sequence magnitude distribution normal for large mainshock.

title: M4.0 aftershock, Ngaio area detected
description: GeoNet network detected magnitude 4.0 aftershock at 09:23 NZST, 7km west near Ngaio, depth 11km. Light to moderate shaking detected.

title: Medical delays: elective procedures postponed due to power uncertainty
description: Wellington and Hutt Valley hospitals postponing elective procedures today due to power stability concerns. Only emergency surgery proceeding. Outpatients diverted.

title: Petone liquefaction damage to utility trenches and services
description: Utility trenches exposed by ground rupturing in Petone showing damage to fiber optic cables and electrical services. Telecommunications outages reported.

title: Petone: extended power outage continues, restoration efforts delayed by liquefaction damage
description: Subsurface power lines damaged by ground movement. Temporary overhead lines being installed. Estimated 3-4 hours further delay.

title: Porirua CBD approach: Traffic heading southbound slowing
description: Northbound traffic from Porirua experiencing delays at Wellington boundary due to SH1 closure. NZTA managing flow with temporary traffic signals; alternate route advice issued.

title: Premises flooded on Civic Centre Road
description: Confirmed flooding on Civic Centre Road affecting basement storage facilities. Depth 150cm. evacuation initiated.

title: Road barrier failure on SH2 approaching Civic Centre off-ramp
description: Guard rail compromised; traffic pushed toward unsuitable lanes. High-risk situation for commuters.

title: Wellington Airport Cobham Drive temporary access: shuttle service established via Paraparaumu approach
description: Wellington Airport operational team established shuttle bridge-service between primary Cobham Drive parking and departures terminal via Paraparaumu link road. Helicopter operations continuing normally; shuttle buses every 8 minutes.

title: Gas leak: Bowen Street precinct
description: Fire and Emergency and utilities responding to gas detected in building complex. Area evacuated; leak source being located with detection equipment.

title: Hospital patient surge: second medical facility preparing for overflow
description: Hutt Valley High School gymnasium being set up as temporary triage facility. Capital & Coast DHB requesting all trained medical professionals report for duty.

title: Island Bay access: Coastal road severely damaged
description: Community reports major pavement failure and ground settlement on Island Bay coastal approach. Liquefaction indicators visible; full closure implemented pending engineering inspection.

title: Johnsonville Line catenary service restored: limited two-carriage trial commencing
description: Johnsonville Line catenary system restoration verified. Limited two-carriage service trial initiating with max 240 passengers; northbound to Johnsonville Station from Wellington at 15 km/h.

title: Miramar peninsula: partial power returning to residential areas, commercial still affected
description: Residential streets have lights but shopping centers remain dark. Transformer repair teams working on Evans Bay area.

title: Newtown - WCC structural team completing building condition assessment sweep
description: Structural assessment crew completing rapid visual assessment of Newtown housing stock focusing on pre-1970s construction. Green tagging majority of properties; yellow tags issued for three properties requiring detailed follow-up.

title: Petone industrial park - ground settlement creating internal flooding
description: Industrial property near Jackson Street showing standing water in warehouse due to ground subsidence creating sump on property interior. Water depth 50cm in low zone.

title: Rongotai structural engineers mobilizing with measurement equipment
description: Structural engineering teams with laser theodolites and inclinometers arriving at airport terminal to measure settlement rate and tilt progression.

title: Rongotai/Airport runway lights: restoration completed, flights resuming
description: Wellington Airport switchyard fully repaired. Runway lighting systems tested and online. First aircraft departure authorized 10:25; domestic schedule resuming with priority to emergency flights.

title: Serious injuries reported from building collapse on Willis Street
description: Mid-rise office building partially collapsed during occupancy. Rescue operations underway; trapped persons reported.

title: WREMO updates: aftershock sequence now entering decay phase
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management bulletin: aftershock sequence transitioning to decay phase with magnitudes trending downward. M3+ aftershocks now occurring at 5-7 per hour interval. Continued heightened emergency alert status in effect.

title: Business impact: office closures and staff sent home
description: CBD office buildings still operating at reduced capacity. Many businesses sending staff home. Productivity estimates at 20% of normal. Working from home encouraged.

title: CBD building - interior partition wall collapse reported at office complex
description: Fire crews respond to Lambton Quay office building report of non-load-bearing partition collapse in upper floors. Crews conducting detailed search; no initial reports of persons trapped but building clearing underway as precaution.

title: Cable Street - liquefaction and piping damage
description: Liquefaction ejection visible along Cable Street near the waterfront. Ground surface showing sand boils; one lane cordoned for safety.

title: Dixon Street intersection: Traffic signals restored
description: Dixon Street traffic signals restored after emergency repairs to foundation damage. Normal signal phasing resumed; traffic flow improving in core CBD.

title: Island Bay community reports M3.0 aftershock, coastal stability maintained
description: Island Bay residents confirm M3.0 aftershock with sustained shaking. Coastal stability continues to be maintained. Community morale stressed but coping mechanisms emerging.

title: M4.6 aftershock, Hataitai area
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 4.6 aftershock at 09:25 NZST, 8km south near Hataitai, depth 13km. Light to moderate shaking felt across wider area.

title: Massive liquefaction event destroying commercial precinct in Te Aro
description: Business district affected by subsidence and sand boiling. Buildings tilting; mass evacuation underway.

title: Miramar ground deformation mapped through satellite imagery
description: Initial satellite analysis shows deformation pattern in Miramar consistent with liquefaction spreading. Remote sensing team processing ongoing data.

title: Structural assessment: Seaview industrial precinct widespread damage
description: Structural engineers assess Seaview industrial area finding widespread liquefaction and building foundation failures. Port operations suspended. Eight industrial buildings flagged for immediate demolition assessment.

title: Structural damage: Ghuznee Street heritage block
description: WCC engineers assessing 1930s warehouse with visible exterior cracking and partial floor sag. Building sealed pending further evaluation.

title: Taranaki Street inner-city bypass: partial northbound lane restoration; congestion now moderate
description: Wellington City Council cleared priority debris from Taranaki Street northbound zone near Ghuznee intersection. One northbound lane reopened to traffic; congestion index dropping from severe to moderate.

title: Thorndon Quays: intermittent power affecting warehouse operations, safety protocols tightened
description: Flickering power supply creating unsafe working conditions. Stevedores instructed to cease operations until stable supply confirmed.

title: Water main contractor mobilized for emergency repairs
description: Specialist water main repair contractor on site at Cable Street break. Initial assessment indicates multiple sections need replacement, not patching.

title: CentrePort emergency berth: Aotea Quay North allocated for limited reefer-container operations
description: CentrePort emergency planning opened north sector of Aotea Quay for reefer (refrigerated) container offload only. Single crane operational; MV Agile Bay positioned for discharge of perishable cargo (2,100 TEU).

title: Kilbirnie Crescent embankment - lateral ground spread widening
description: Field survey documents embankment lateral spread now extending 40 metres along Kilbirnie Crescent with cumulative offset of 35-40cm. Spacing between surface cracks narrows as movement continues.

title: Liquefaction damage to port infrastructure in Seaview
description: Wharf pilings subsiding; container handling equipment damaged. Port operations halted; safety assessment ongoing.

title: M3.1 aftershock Hataitai suburb, shallow depth 9 km
description: GeoNet detects M3.1 aftershock at 09:26 NZST in Hataitai area, depth 9 km. Shallow depth indicates continued crustal stress release. Felt reports confirm shaking in eastern suburbs.

title: M3.5 aftershock, eastern zone
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 3.5 aftershock at 09:26 NZST, 10km south-southeast, depth 12km. Minor seismic activity continues.

title: Main break confirmed: Queens Drive, Seaview
description: Water main rupture on Queens Drive. supply rerouted. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Newtown shops reopening: power restored to commercial precinct
description: Newtown main shopping precinct now fully powered. Countdown and local retailers reopening; queues forming for essential supplies. ATMs operational.

title: Sixth aftershock rocks region: magnitude 4.7 at 09:26
description: GeoNet data shows continued aftershock activity. No new damage reports but residents expressing high levels of distress and shock.

title: Te Aro - third gas rupture site identified on Taranaki Street network
description: Gas detection team locating tertiary rupture point on Taranaki Street gas transmission main. Third isolated rupture confirmation indicating widespread network damage; repair scope expanding beyond initial assessments.

title: Te Aro: power returning to northern blocks, residents still trapped in southern section
description: Staged restoration affecting some buildings but not others. Additional crews needed for targeted transformer repair and line clearing.

title: Welfare centre: Newtown reaches 370 residents, medical resources strained
description: Newtown Community Centre now accommodating 370 residents. Medical support station treating minor injuries and anxiety cases. Psychosocial support volunteer team deployed.

title: Wellington CBD liquefaction zone mapping completed by GeoNet
description: GeoNet field teams have completed initial mapping of liquefaction extent in CBD identifying 15 major zones of severe deformation.

title: Building damage: Manners Street
description: Fire and Emergency responding to interior damage and structural concerns in office building. No initial injuries; assessments underway.

title: Flooding threatens substation infrastructure, Hutt Valley
description: Water accumulation near electrical substation on SH2 creating contamination risk. Electric utility operators working to isolate sector. Flooding depth 80cm.

title: Injury alert: Multiple crush syndrome cases, resource shortage
description: Wellington Hospital reports 23 patients with crush syndrome requiring renal support. Medical supplies running low. Emergency orders placed for IV fluids and additional dialysis units. Three patients in critical condition.

title: Kilbirnie infrastructure - utility services now spanning ground cracks
description: Gas and electrical services on Kilbirnie Crescent now exposed and spanning surface cracks. Pipes showing tension as ground movement pulls lines apart.

title: Lower Hutt: power finally returning to residential areas after 78 minutes blackout
description: Streetlights coming back online across suburbs. Residents beginning to assess damage and reconnect communication devices.

title: M2.9 aftershock Karori hillside, magnitude descent now clear
description: M2.9 aftershock at 09:27 NZST on Karori hillside. Clear descent in aftershock magnitudes now apparent to GeoNet analysis. Sequence expected to continue 4-6 additional hours with declining magnitudes.

title: Power and communication systems failure affecting emergency response
description: Radio systems degraded; coordination difficulties between agencies. Backup systems struggling.

title: Resident report (Petone): SH2 visibility concern at Hutt Valley High School intersection continues
description: Petone resident reporting ongoing traffic confusion at High School intersection despite manual control. Visibility reduced by dust clouds; multiple near-misses observed at manually-controlled junction.

title: SH1 northbound - bridge approach showing settlement
description: NZTA traffic monitoring shows bridge approach settlement near north Ngauranga exit. Structural engineers inspecting; traffic diverted to local routes.

title: School closures: afternoon sessions not proceeding
description: Wellington schools remain closed for afternoon sessions due to power uncertainty and safety concerns. Students and staff directed to stay home. Facilities assessment underway.

title: Te Aro - gas detector crew identifies additional rupture site on Abel Smith Street
description: Portable gas detection team locating secondary rupture point on Abel Smith Street main gas transmission line. Isolation valve access being cleared; repair sequencing coordinated with Courtney Place repair crew.

title: Thorndon embankment monitoring stations installed for early warning
description: Wellington City Council has installed real-time tilt and displacement monitoring on Thorndon harbor embankment to warn of potential failure.

title: Wadestown: Emergency hillslope stabilization begins
description: Geotechnical crews installing temporary soil anchors and drainage on Wadestown cutting to prevent further slope movement. Access remains closed; stabilization ongoing for next 120 minutes.

title: Blocked road on Constable Street, multiple reports of damage
description: Section of road collapsed into subsidence; vehicles unable to pass. Residents accessing properties via alternate routes.

title: Botanical Gardens: Access route temporarily closed
description: Botanical Gardens access route closed for assessment of damage to approach road from liquefaction effects. Pedestrian access via alternate hiking trails activated.

title: Hataitai residents experiencing 4+ hours of continuous tremors
description: Hataitai community reports M3.1 aftershock is among dozens felt since 07:42. Residents report cumulative stress, difficulty concentrating, and desire for seismic activity to stabilize.

title: Hataitai: transformer fire now extinguished, area still without power awaiting replacement
description: Fire and Emergency NZ completed suppression. Damaged transformer removal and replacement estimated 48 hours.

title: Island Bay beach properties: power infrastructure permanent repairs needed
description: Island Bay electrical infrastructure remains damaged beyond quick repair. Residents directed to emergency welfare centres. Temporary mobile generator dispatch planned for critical demand.

title: Kāpiti Line Raumati South Tunnel: emergency shoring installed; southbound service trial scheduled 12:45
description: Kāpiti Line engineering team installed temporary shoring on cracked tunnel support beams. Structural confirmation obtained; single-carriage southbound trial scheduled for 12:45 NZST (34 minutes ahead).

title: Lower Hutt - rapid structural assessment identifies six red-tag buildings
description: WCC structural engineer team red-tags six commercial and residential buildings on Hutt Road and Melling Road following detailed inspection. Immediate occupancy prohibition issued; staged evacuation assistance programs being coordinated.

title: M3.7 aftershock, Te Aro-Newtown
description: Magnitude 3.7 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 09:28 NZST, 8km south near Te Aro-Newtown boundary, depth 13km. Light shaking felt.

title: Main failure on Waterloo Road
description: Major water loss on Waterloo Road. Large crater forming. Emergency assistance needed.

title: Petone waterfront: port crane topples during aftershock sequence
description: Stuff reporter on-scene: large gantry crane collapsed on CentrePort wharf. Container vessel at berth sustained impact damage. No crew injuries reported.

title: Seaview port crane structural integrity in doubt
description: CentrePort structural engineers report container cranes showing visible tilting from liquefaction ground movement. Cranes deemed unsafe for operations.

title: Welfare centre: Kelburn facility reaches 310 residents with tent additions
description: Kelburn welfare centre now at 310 residents with large marquee tents deployed. Additional 250+ capacity added. Supply chain well-established from distribution networks.

title: Gas leak: Newtown residential area
description: Strong gas odour reported in residential street. Fire and Emergency responding with detection equipment. Multiple properties evacuated as precaution.

title: Karori residents note magnitude decrease trend with slight relief
description: Karori residents report M2.9 aftershock and note collective observation that earthquake magnitudes are finally decreasing. Residents report slight psychological relief despite continued seismic activity.

title: Kilbirnie liquefaction ongoing ground rupture expansion
description: Field observations indicate ground ruptures in Kilbirnie continuing to expand. Width increasing from 5cm to 15cm over 30-minute period.

title: KiwiRail: Metlink services resuming cautiously with limited frequency and shortened routes
description: Northern Line testing commenced at 10:35. Hutt Valley line still conducting safety inspections. Full schedule 2+ hours away.

title: Large-scale injuries reported at entertainment venue in Lower Hutt
description: Building damage during crowded business hours. Multiple casualties; emergency response mobilized.

title: Lift entrapments: rescue operations for trapped occupants
description: Fire and Emergency responding to reports of people trapped in elevators across CBD buildings due to power loss and restoration cycling. All occupants being safely extracted.

title: Main Street water supply interrupted
description: Water main rupture on Main Street. tanker deployed. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Newtown - WCC structural team reports completion of residential triage assessment
description: WCC structural assessment team completing rapid triage of Newtown residential area. Summary showing 2 red tags, 8 yellow tags, 124 green tags documented; follow-up detailed inspections scheduled for affected properties.

title: Petone Avenue - liquefaction settlement and surface cracking
description: Multiple surface cracks and subsidence evident on Petone Avenue. Two lanes affected; traffic reduced to single alternating lane.

title: SH1 Haywards Hill debris clearance: southbound lane restored; northbound bypass continuing
description: NZTA completed debris removal and vehicle extraction from SH1 southbound Haywards Hill incident. One southbound lane restored; northbound traffic continuing via inner-city detour (Ngauranga Gorge bypass).

title: SH2 Hutt bridge: Structural assessment nearly complete
description: NZTA engineers complete initial State Highway 2 bridge assessment near Lower Hutt. Limited reopening approved for light vehicles only; full capacity restoration pending detailed analysis.

title: Seaview waterfront industrial complex - dock-level subsidence creating slope
description: CentrePort reports dock area on Seaview Road showing differential settlement of 40cm over 50-metre span, creating dangerous sloped surface. Cargo cranes now unstable.

title: Water outage orders issued to affected suburbs
description: Wellington Water issues formal water outage orders to CBD, Thorndon, Te Aro zones due to uncontrolled main breaks. Residents advised to seek alternative water supply.

title: Water sensor triggered in Zone A (CBD)
description: Automated alert from Zone A (CBD). Flow rate exceeds capacity threshold. Flood conditions near Queen Street.

title: Welfare centre surge: Lower Hutt facility receives 420 displaced persons
description: Lower Hutt welfare centre now accommodating 420 people with new arrivals continuing. Catering and medical staffing being supplemented by Civil Defence volunteers. Capacity stretched but holding.

title: Civic Square perimeter: Temporary pedestrian barriers secured
description: Civic Square perimeter barriers secured after initial emergency placement. Pedestrian access controlled; vehicle access restricted to maintenance only.

title: Communication networks strained: cell network experiencing congestion
description: Spark and Vodafone reporting extreme call volume; SMS preferred for emergency communications. Internet connectivity stable but video streaming experiencing delays.

title: Emergency services rescue underway at hospital parking building
description: Multi-level parking structure partially collapsed. At least one person trapped in vehicle.

title: Lower Hutt - geotechnical monitoring array installed on liquefied land
description: Geotechnical engineering team installing settlement monitoring array on Hutt Road land showing visible liquefaction features. Piezometers and extensometers deployed; 24-hour monitoring regime commencing to track soil behavior.

title: M3.0 aftershock Island Bay area detected
description: M3.0 aftershock at 09:30 NZST, epicentre near Island Bay. Depth 13 km. GeoNet network now registering approximately 40 aftershocks in M2+ range since mainshock.

title: M4.4 aftershock, Oriental Bay recorded
description: Magnitude 4.4 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 09:30 NZST, epicentre 5km south-southeast near Oriental Bay, depth 12km. Continued seismic activity detected.

title: Newtown recovery: power returning to 80% of suburb, businesses beginning reopening
description: Main commercial district lights back on. Countdown and local shops restocking. Traffic signals operational again.

title: Petone street surface ruptures worsening post-liquefaction
description: Petone street conditions deteriorating with pavement rupturing further and subsidence continuing. Traffic Department determining road reopening feasibility.

title: Regional power status: 95% of Wellington now restored
description: Wellington Electricity reports 95% customer base power restored. Remaining outages in Island Bay and some Lower Hutt pockets due to infrastructure damage requiring permanent repairs.

title: Rongotai taxiway - permanent ground undulations across full width
description: Entire taxiway width now showing compaction ripples and subsidence waves. No section remains suitable for aircraft operations, full closure extended indefinitely.

title: Structural assessment: Taranaki Street
description: WCC team assessing commercial building with exterior wall damage and internal cracking. Comprehensive engineering report being prepared.

title: Wairarapa Line Ghuznee underpass: clearance issue resolved; pilot service at full-carriage capacity
description: Wairarapa Line underground assessment team confirmed clearance reduction compensable via 5 km/h speed reduction. Full-carriage pilot service (320 passengers) commencing southbound at 12:47 NZST.

title: Aftershock magnitude 4.1, tsunami advisory re-evaluated but holds
description: GeoNet detects magnitude 4.1 aftershock at 09:31 NZST. MetService re-evaluates tsunami risk and maintains advisory for all Wellington coasts. Waves expected to peak in next 20 minutes.

title: Flooding worsens on Market Lane, CBD business district
description: Water level on Market Lane CBD has risen to 90cm. Businesses attempting to salvage inventory. Water still flowing from main break site upstream.

title: Lambton Quay - extensive pavement damage
description: Extensive liquefaction damage affecting Lambton Quay between Grey and Customhouse quays. Multiple segments closed; emergency repairs prioritized.

title: Lift systems restoration: elevators gradually returning to service
description: Building management systems across CBD progressively restoring elevator service. Stair use still recommended. Full elevator operations expected by 11:00 NZST.

title: M2.8 aftershock inner CBD zone, sequence entering final escalation
description: M2.8 aftershock at 09:31 NZST near Wellington CBD. Aftershock sequence characteristics now consistent with Omori law (1/t decay model). Response agencies prepare for sustained 6-hour aftermath.

title: M3.4 aftershock, western suburbs
description: Magnitude 3.4 aftershock detected by GeoNet seismographs at 09:31 NZST, 8km west, depth 13km. Light shaking in western areas.

title: Metlink rolling stock deployment: five additional coaches positioned at CBD staging area for rush demand
description: Metlink coordinators positioning five fresh bus coaches at Civic Centre and Mercer Street staging areas. Anticipated 11:00-13:00 surge demand as rail capacity returns partially online; buses serving secondary routes and remote suburbs.

title: Road surface completely broken on Vivian Street near parking building
description: Fault rupture created impassable section. Vehicles rerouted; construction equipment needed.

title: Rongotai runway pavement cracking extensive from liquefaction
description: Runway surface showing pattern of interconnected cracking from liquefaction damage. Engineers assessing whether pavement replacement needed.

title: Structural assessment: Victoria Street
description: WCC engineer evaluating 5-storey commercial building with visible exterior damage. Temporary bracing considered; occupancy restrictions imposed.

title: Te Aro - gas escape from damaged service line beneath Cambridge Terrace
description: Excavation crews locate ruptured service connection serving apartment complex on Cambridge Terrace. High-flow gas escape requiring immediate isolation; residents in 50-metre radius re-evacuated as precaution while repairs staged.

title: Wellington Water: treatment plant back on mains power, water quality returning to normal
description: Generators successfully shut down at 10:40. Kaiwharawhara treatment works operational. Distribution pressure building across network.

title: Building damage: Riddiford Street
description: Newtown school building sustaining roof damage and cracked load-bearing walls. Education authorities coordinating temporary closure.

title: CBD - facade panel temporary retention system confirmed safe for operation
description: Engineering verification team confirming temporary retention system for facade panels at Lambton Quay building is safe for continued operation. System remains effective for interim period pending permanent repairs.

title: CBD traffic signals: full automation restored
description: Wellington CBD traffic light system now fully operational. Police traffic controllers standing down from manual duty. Vehicular flow returning to near-normal, congestion easing.

title: Injury: Continued rescue operations yield additional casualties
description: Continued rescue operations at multiple sites yield 8 additional casualties with crush syndrome symptoms. Four transported to Wellington Hospital with life-threatening injuries.

title: Island Bay community reports M3.0 aftershock, coastal area stable
description: Island Bay residents confirm M3.0 aftershock. Coastal monitoring continues with no tsunami or unusual wave activity. Residents remain vigilant and report fatigue from prolonged aftershock sequence.

title: Liquefaction liquefaction outbreak across Porirua CBD waterfront
description: Ground boiling and sand fountains in business district. Shopfronts damaged by liquefaction features.

title: Miramar building foundation monitoring initiated
description: WCC has installed crack monitoring equipment on residential buildings in Miramar to track structural distress rate from ongoing liquefaction.

title: Rongotai Airport access: Heavy traffic diversion underway
description: Traffic diverted from damaged Miramar cutting now funneling through Rongotai access routes. Traffic camera detects approach road at 150% capacity; overflow queues forming.

title: Seaview industrial zone: port operations resuming with limited capacity
description: CentrePort coordinating crane operations resumption. Safety inspections completed. Handling equipment operational but at 60% normal rate.

title: Te Aro apartment block - stairwell partially collapsed from ground shift
description: Emergency services respond to 6-storey residential building on Vivian Street showing exterior stairwell detached and partially collapsed. Ground subsidence estimated at 25-30cm underneath southeast corner.

title: Te Aro office block partial collapse: rescue operation underway
description: NZ Herald reports structural failure on Taranaki Street. Heavy rescue teams deployed; one person trapped in ground floor retail area. Search ongoing.

title: Water spray visible on Whitmore Street
description: Major water loss on Whitmore Street. Surface rupture widening. Emergency assistance needed.

title: Waterloo Quay heave ridge: grinding/milling operation commenced; lane restriction lifted by 13:15 estimate
description: Wellington City Council road maintenance crews operating asphalt milling equipment on Waterloo Quay heave ridge (25cm uplift). Grinding expected complete by 13:15 NZST; normal two-way traffic restoration thereafter.

title: Building damage: Manners Street area
description: Fire and Emergency continuing operations in Te Aro with additional building damage incident. Structural assessment pending.

title: CBD - fire safety assessment of damaged commercial building electrical systems
description: Fire safety engineer conducting electrical hazard assessment at Lambton Quay building with structural damage. Arcing hazards identified at damaged distribution panel; temporary power isolation implemented pending electrical system repair.

title: CBD Quay Street partial surface collapse from subsidence
description: Section of Quay Street near Civic area experiencing localized surface collapse creating dangerous hole. Traffic diverted. Immediate fencing installed.

title: Evacuation: Te Aro commercial district, mandatory by 09:50
description: WREMO extends Te Aro evacuation to all commercial properties. Businesses, offices and retail to close immediately. 300+ commercial spaces affected. Staff and customers being cleared via designated pedestrian routes.

title: Heating and cooling systems: HVAC restoration underway
description: Building HVAC systems progressively restarting across CBD. Temperature control returning to normal in most office buildings. Some buildings taking longer to stabilize.

title: Kilbirnie: full power restoration complete for isthmus suburb, water pressure normal
description: Last scheduled restoration zone completed at 10:45. All services returning to normal. Local residents expressing relief.

title: Liquefaction spread affecting multiple suburbs on reclaimed land
description: Coordinated response to sand boils and subsidence across wide area. Dozens of locations affected.

title: M3.6 aftershock, Johnsonville-Tawa
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 3.6 aftershock at 09:33 NZST, 19km north in Johnsonville-Tawa area, depth 12km. Light shaking in northern suburbs.

title: Main line rupture near Petone station
description: 500mm main serving Petone commercial area ruptured beneath Petone Central Station approach. Flow still uncontrolled. Station facilities being evacuated.

title: Rongotai Road near airport entrance - subsidence and cracking
description: Ground deformation affecting Rongotai Road approach to airport terminal. Approximately 0.25m differential settlement; airport access route compromised.

title: Seaview industrial rail spur: emergency track lifting underway; freight service partial restoration planned
description: KiwiRail track crews performing emergency rail lifting operation on Seaview industrial spur (60cm subsidence). Three hydraulic jacks positioning track elevation; freight service partial resumption targeted for 14:00 NZST.

title: Seaview tank facility - structural connection welds now visibly stressed
description: Inspection of storage tank on Gracefield Road shows cracks forming in connection welds. Differential settlement motion still continuing with aftershocks.

title: Wellington CBD workers report M2.8 aftershock and prepare for long day
description: Downtown Wellington workers report M2.8 aftershock. Emergency operations centers staff up for extended operations. Response agencies coordinate 12-hour roster preparations.

title: Willeston Street: Partial reopening with restrictions
description: Willeston Street partially reopened with weight restrictions and traffic management after temporary pavement repairs. Permanent restoration scheduled pending subsidence stabilization.

title: CBD heritage precinct - structural concerns flagged for multiple Victorian-era buildings
description: Architectural heritage surveyor identifies critical mortar joint failures and stone block displacement on Customhouse Quay heritage buildings. Temporary restraint cables being installed; heritage assessment team mobilized for urgent preservation planning.

title: Data centre services: internet and digital services fully operational
description: Wellington CBD data centre systems back at full capacity. ISP networks reporting 99% availability. Government and business services online. Emergency comms protocols standing down.

title: Gas odour: Newtown residential street
description: Residents reporting gas smell in residential area. Fire and Emergency investigating with portable detection units; precautionary evacuations.

title: Kilbirnie liquefaction spreading: residential properties settling unevenly
description: Newshub: ground ruptures visible across residential streets. Homes with foundation damage being vacated. Geotechnical assessment teams deployed.

title: Large diameter break on Whitmore Street
description: Water main rupture on Whitmore Street. isolation valve activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: M2.9 aftershock Karori hill area, frequency increasing
description: GeoNet records M2.9 aftershock at 09:34 NZST on Karori hill. Depth 15 km. Smaller magnitude aftershocks now occurring at 10-12 per hour. Aftershock sequence entering normal decay pattern.

title: Newshub live report: 'Earthquake impacts receding; city transport entering recovery phase'
description: Newshub transport correspondent live from Civic Centre: 'Critical transport services gradually returning. Rail network recovery now visible; bus network surge demand beginning to stabilize as commuters adjust plans.'

title: Oriental Bay promenade: Closed due to liquefaction damage
description: WCC closes Oriental Bay promenade approach due to extensive pavement cracking and subsidence from liquefaction. Alternative pedestrian routes established via Mount Victoria access.

title: Oriental Bay: beaches reopening as power restored, safety inspections completed
description: Residential area and commercial district back on mains supply. Beach facilities checking for earthquake damage before reopening.

title: Thorndon water infrastructure emergency isolation procedures underway
description: Wellington Water activating emergency isolation of damaged main line segments in Thorndon. Goal to maintain service to remaining undamaged zone.

title: Widespread power outages affecting Miramar residential area
description: Multiple transformer failures; power out across 5+ blocks. Residents without heating or lighting.

title: CBD business district: traffic signals restored, pedestrian signals operational again
description: Civic area fully functional with mains power. Office buildings re-entry protocols established. Workers beginning return.

title: CentrePort crane operational: Crane No. 4 (undamaged by mainshock) cleared for limited operations
description: CentrePort operations confirmed Crane No. 4 (eastern berth) undamaged during mainshock. Limited container discharge commencing at 13:00 NZST capacity: 45 TEU/hour (normal: 120 TEU/hour).

title: Courtenay Place: Temporary traffic management system live
description: Courtenay Place temporary traffic management system activated with traffic signals and manual direction. Bidirectional flow maintained at reduced capacity.

title: Flooding creates access barrier at hospital approach
description: Water accumulation on access routes to CBD hospital is now 1m deep. Emergency vehicles using alternative routes. Flooding from main breaks upstream continuing to worsen.

title: Injury update: growing casually list from collapsed structures
description: Hospital emergency departments full; additional beds being prepared. Estimated 50+ injured so far.

title: Lower Hutt - property owner briefing session scheduled for red-tagged buildings
description: WCC scheduling property owner briefing session covering assessment findings, closure orders, and repair process guidance for red-tagged Hutt Road buildings. Session scheduled for early afternoon with engineering team available.

title: M2.7 aftershock Newtown area, 4+ hour cumulative stress evident
description: M2.7 aftershock at 09:35 NZST in Newtown. Resident social media indicates cumulative stress from 50+ earthquakes over 4+ hours. Psychological toll increasing despite magnitude decrease.

title: M4.2 aftershock, Mount Victoria region
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 4.2 aftershock at 09:35 NZST, 9km south-southwest near Mount Victoria, depth 14km. Light to moderate shaking felt in central areas.

title: Miramar Avenue - car park sinkhole development, 1.5m diameter opening
description: Photos show rapidly developing sinkhole in parking area on Miramar Avenue as liquefied ground consolidates and underlying layer compacts. Hole now 1.5 metres wide and deepening.

title: Ngaio Road hillside slip - debris field expanding
description: Hillside slip on Ngaio Road continues to expand; estimated 40 cubic metres now blocking roadway. WCC implementing full closure; residents diverted.

title: Seaview liquefaction damage inventory preliminary assessment
description: Preliminary inventory of Seaview damage identifies 23 commercial/industrial buildings with structural concerns from liquefaction ground movement.

title: Security systems restoration: CCTV and access control online
description: Building security systems coming back online across CBD. CCTV systems restored; access control card readers functioning. Building security protocols normalizing.

title: Structural red-tag expansion: 32 buildings now unsafe across region
description: WREMO structural assessment team has now red-tagged 32 buildings across Wellington. Includes CBD offices, residential blocks, and industrial facilities. Demolition contracts being sought urgently.

title: 1News alert: Transport minister announces additional 50 emergency buses for Wellington network
description: 1News breaking: Minister of Transport announced deployment of 50 additional emergency buses to Wellington region. Regional coordinators to stage buses at Civic Centre, Courtenay Place, and Newtown by 14:30 NZST.

title: Building damage and hazardous rubble on Nairn Street, Mount Victoria
description: Brick building partially collapsed; debris spilled onto sidewalk and neighboring property.
