title: Karori residents report sustained aftershock activity on hilltop
description: Karori residents at higher elevation report M2.9 aftershock on hillside suburbs. Many residents now remain outdoors in parks and open spaces due to ongoing seismic activity and structural concerns.

title: Kilbirnie residential street building evacuation cascade
description: After structural assessment of corner building, engineers recommend adjacent buildings on same block be evacuated as precaution from ground movement.

title: M3.3 aftershock, transport corridor
description: GeoNet network detected magnitude 3.3 aftershock at 09:36 NZST, near transport corridor area, depth 12km. Minor shaking detected.

title: Major leak detected on Buller Street
description: Water main rupture on Buller Street. repair crew mobilized. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Newtown - community-coordinated damage documentation project launched
description: Community members initiating neighborhood damage documentation for insurance claim coordination. Photographs and survey data being collected on Rongotai and Daniell Streets; file being compiled by local resident committee.

title: Porirua CBD enters partial lockdown; critical infrastructure assessment
description: Hutt City Council and regional authorities restricting access to CBD pending structural assessments. Essential services only allowed through cordons.

title: Rongotai Airport: full terminal power restoration, passenger services resuming normally
description: All runway systems and terminal facilities now operating on mains power. Flight schedule returning to near-normal by midday.

title: Seaview port operations: returning to normal capacity
description: CentrePort now operating at 80% capacity with all systems on normal power. Afternoon container operations proceeding. No ongoing power concerns.

title: Structural assessment: CBD mixed-use complex
description: WCC team conducting rapid assessment of 8-storey building showing signs of differential settlement. Temporary shoring being considered.

title: Waterfront heritage precinct - multiple facades now requiring support
description: WCC safety team installing temporary support beams under 1800s buildings on Jervois Quay. Multiple facades showing outward lean requiring immediate bracing.

title: Building damage assessment underway at multiple high-risk structures
description: Engineers and structural specialists mobilized. Several buildings tagged as unsafe for occupancy.

title: Gas detection: Multiple Te Aro sites
description: Fire and Emergency responding to gas detected at several locations across Te Aro. Large-scale utility coordination underway.

title: Hutt Valley Line full restoration trial: two-carriage service completed; three-carriage pending approval
description: Hutt Valley Line two-carriage trial service (510 passengers) completed successfully Thorndon-Petone-Seaview. Rail geometry confirmed acceptable; KiwiRail approving three-carriage trial (750 passengers) within 25 minutes.

title: Injury report: Cumulative 102 patients hospitalized, deaths reported
description: Wellington Hospital reports cumulative total of 102 hospitalized patients. Two fatalities confirmed from crush injuries and internal bleeding. 45 patients in severe condition requiring intensive care.

title: Karori Road: Partial single-lane access restored
description: After 70 minutes of clearing, Karori Road reopened to single alternating lanes for emergency vehicles and limited civilian traffic. Full two-way restoration estimated 60+ minutes.

title: Liquefaction impact: power infrastructure damage in low-lying areas
description: Assessment teams finding power pole displacement and cable damage in liquefaction zones (Petone, Seaview, Kilbirnie). Some infrastructure may require permanent replacement.

title: Newtown - unreinforced masonry residences show widespread cracks
description: Community reports indicate approximately 70% of pre-1940s brick veneer homes in Newtown show significant cracking. Structural engineer visual assessments confirm predominantly non-structural cracking but structural engineer follow-up required on subset.

title: Newtown community organizes support for earthquake-stressed residents
description: Newtown residents organizing community support following M2.7 aftershock. Residents reporting fatigue, anxiety, and difficulty concentrating. Welfare centers being sought out.

title: Petone liquefaction extent wider than initially assessed
description: GeoNet field teams report liquefaction zone in Petone extends further than initial estimates. Liquefied ground spreading across 8-block area.

title: Petone: restoration crews installing temporary overhead power lines for eastern area
description: Ground cables damaged by liquefaction settlement. Temporary fix expected to provide power within 2 hours. Permanent replacement 4-6 weeks.

title: SH2 northbound near Hutt - additional liquefaction settlement
description: Further subsidence event detected on SH2 northbound near Hutt Junction. Approximately 0.35m settlement; speed restrictions and traffic management applied.

title: Water treatment facility operations curtailed
description: Wellington Water main treatment facility reducing output due to network ruptures creating backpressure. Flow rate reduced to 30% of normal capacity.

title: Aftershock series continues relentlessly; seismology center monitoring closely
description: GeoNet issues alert: magnitude 5.2 aftershock at 09:38. Stress release pattern suggests additional significant aftershocks possible in next 24 hours.

title: Building damage: The Terrace commercial
description: Fire and Emergency securing area around commercial building with significant facade damage. WCC structural engineer called for emergency assessment.

title: Civic Square area: Circulation plan implemented
description: Civic Square precinct emergency circulation plan activated restricting traffic to essential services only. Pedestrian areas cordoned for structural assessment safety.

title: Intersection gridlock at Lambton Square due to signal failure
description: Traffic lights down across 4-way intersection. Traffic officers directing; severe congestion building.

title: Lower Hutt power grid: stabilizing after cascading surge damage
description: Hutt Valley electrical grid now stable with controlled power flows. Some minor surge-related faults still being identified; repair crews dispatched to secondary distribution points.

title: M2.8 aftershock Wellington CBD zone again, tempo acceleration
description: M2.8 aftershock at 09:38 NZST detected near Wellington CBD. GeoNet analysis shows increasing aftershock frequency with slight tempo acceleration. Still within expected statistical bounds for M6.5 mainshock.

title: M3.5 aftershock, Porirua region
description: Magnitude 3.5 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 09:38 NZST, 23km north in Porirua CBD area, depth 13km. Minor shaking recorded.

title: Miramar: remaining blackout zones now under active repair, estimated restoration 11:30
description: Crews at Factory Road and Whakatangi working on secondary transformer installations. Residential connections restoring progressively.

title: Ngauranga Bridge emergency repairs: temporary support columns installed; northbound lane reopened
description: NZTA emergency repair team installed temporary support columns under Ngauranga Bridge northbound beam. Northbound lane restored to single-file (40 km/h limit); full bidirectional restoration scheduled within 4 hours.

title: Petone town hall - large cracks in load-bearing masonry walls
description: Structural inspection identifies diagonal cracks in north and south walls of 1920s brick building. WCC safety officer posts no-entry notice over main entrance.

title: Rongotai terminal building structural monitoring showing tilt increase
description: Real-time structural monitoring data shows building tilt now increased to 1.2 degrees. Rate of tilt acceleration suggests continued settlement ongoing.

title: Te Aro - suspended ceiling debris removal operations underway at office building
description: Contractor crews removing fallen suspended ceiling debris from Willis Street office complex. Debris disposal procedures implemented; interior structural integrity assessment underway by engineer.

title: Welfare centre: Hataitai Scout Hall reaches 160 residents, supplies adequate
description: Hataitai Scout Hall now sheltering 160 residents with adequate food and water supplies for 48 hours. Basic sanitation facilities functional. Medical support from volunteer nurses sufficient for current needs.

title: Customhouse Quay - waterfront promenade buckling
description: Liquefaction has caused Customhouse Quay promenade to buckle. Pedestrian access closed; vehicle lane monitored for further movement.

title: False report of tsunami received; no coastal threat confirmed
description: Residents evacuated from beach areas unnecessarily. Wave monitoring shows no significant changes.

title: Flooding expanding through Thorndon residential areas
description: Water from Quay Street ruptures is backing up into residential side streets. Residents on lower floor levels evacuating to upper levels as water rises.

title: M2.6 aftershock Petone industrial zone, infrastructure fatigue noted
description: M2.6 aftershock at 09:39 NZST in Petone. GeoNet data shows cumulative structural stress building in industrial facilities. Repeat aftershock impacts could exceed structural design capabilities.

title: Miramar harbor edge residential properties at imminent risk
description: Miramar properties on harbor edge experiencing ground fissuring and subsidence creating immediate occupancy risk. Residents evacuating voluntarily.

title: Petone embankment - ground spread now visible along entire section
description: Photos show lateral spreading pattern now extends along 200-metre section of Petone waterfront embankment. Cumulative offset varies from 20-45cm along length.

title: Structural assessment: Newtown
description: WCC team assessing residential area for structural damage. Multiple properties showing signs of seismic impact.

title: Te Aro - suspended ceiling system failure affecting four adjacent office spaces
description: Structural inspection discovering suspended ceiling system separation and partial collapse in four-office suite on Willis Street. Grid system anchor failures identified; temporary ceiling supports being installed during repair planning.

title: The Terrace: Building debris cleared from roadway
description: Debris removal crews complete clearance of fallen facade materials from The Terrace. Traffic flow resuming in stages; traffic management maintaining one-way northbound control.

title: Thorndon: power fully restored to commercial and residential areas
description: Quay Street businesses reopening, waterfront restaurants assessing refrigeration damage. Recovery phase well underway.

title: Water system stability: pressure now normal across network
description: Wellington Water reports water pressure now normalized across entire network. Power-dependent pumping stations all functional. Water rationing notices rescinded.

title: Welfare centre: Karori at maximum capacity, overflow to outdoor tents
description: Karori Community Hall reports 520 residents, all indoor space taken. WREMO deploying large marquee tents for additional 200+ people arriving. Medical and sanitation support coordinated between indoor and outdoor areas.

title: Wellington Station Platform 2 track realignment: temporary splice completed; limited northbound resume
description: Platform 2 northbound track rail separation (8cm) compensated via emergency splice installation. Limited northbound services (reduced speed, reduced passenger capacity) resuming to Hutt Valley and Melling lines.

title: Downtown Wellington businesses managing with ongoing aftershocks
description: Wellington CBD workers report M2.8 aftershock in downtown area. Businesses continue emergency response protocols. Staff morale impacted by cumulative effects of mainshock and 40+ aftershocks over 4+ hours.

title: Gas line rupture: Lower Hutt industrial area
description: Large gas main ruptured beneath industrial building in Lower Hutt CBD. Fire and Emergency cordoning area; evacuation of nearby businesses ordered.

title: Ghuznee Street section: Pavement repairs completed
description: Ghuznee Street pavement repairs completed allowing traffic resumption. Temporary asphalt patches adequate for normal operations.

title: Injury: Cumulative 105 hospitalized patients, critical care overwhelmed
description: Wellington Hospital cumulative total 105 hospitalized patients. All critical care beds filled, additional care areas being converted to emergency ICU spaces. Ventilator availability declining.

title: Jervois Quay stormwater restoration: water main isolated; roadway temporary reinstatement approved
description: Wellington Water completion of stormwater main isolation at Jervois Quay. Road reinstatement with temporary asphalt patch approved by WCC; traffic flow restoration to normal capacity by 14:00 NZST.

title: Lambton Quay building - emergency structural prop installation ongoing
description: Fire and Emergency structural crews installing permanent temporary props beneath damaged building on Lambton Quay. Load calculations indicate building load redistribution occurring; 24-hour monitoring being established pending permanent repairs.

title: M4.5 aftershock, Karori detected
description: Magnitude 4.5 aftershock detected by GeoNet seismographs at 09:40 NZST, 11km west-southwest near Karori, depth 13km. Moderate shaking across western suburbs.

title: Miramar power status: 100% restoration complete
description: Miramar residential and commercial areas fully powered. All circuits stable. Infrastructure assessment showing no permanent damage in this zone; power stability expected ongoing.

title: Multiple occupants injured at Petone railway station
description: Station canopy partially collapsed on platform. Several people injured; ambulances treating on scene.

title: Repair: Water main on The Strand
description: Water main rupture on The Strand. repair crew mobilized. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Second confirmed fatality: woman rescued but pronounced deceased
description: Rescue operations at CBD building collapse yielded second body. Health and Safety at Work investigation announced. Details withheld pending family notification.

title: Te Aro: southern block power restoration progressing, last areas to be connected
description: Transformer installation for Te Aro south (blocks 8-12) underway. Residents occupying temporary shelters, power expected 12:00-13:00.

title: Water entering underground car park on Lambton Quay
description: Water inundation in underground car park on Lambton Quay. Depth now 90cm and climbing. Help requested.

title: Wellington CBD commercial precinct structural triage complete
description: WCC structural engineers have completed initial triage of CBD commercial buildings identifying 28 requiring immediate evacuation.

title: Airport full operations: international flights departing normally
description: Wellington Airport now operating all services normally. International flight departures proceeding on schedule. No aircraft remain grounded.

title: CBD - emergency bracing system load monitoring commencing at critical sites
description: Real-time load monitoring instrumentation activated on emergency bracing systems at critical CBD sites. Automated alerting protocols established for any load anomalies requiring engineering response.

title: Emergency: Queens Drive water main down
description: Water main rupture on Queens Drive. bypass line activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Johnsonville Road - hillside slip damaging road surface
description: Hillside slip damaging Johnsonville Road surface. Approximately 30 cubic metres material; single lane passable with reduced speed restrictions.

title: M3.2 aftershock, central zone
description: Magnitude 3.2 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 09:41 NZST, 5km south-southeast in central zone, depth 11km. Light shaking felt.

title: Metlink coordination center: real-time bus tracking showing 48-minute average commute CBD-to-Suburbs
description: Metlink live tracking dashboard reporting: average CBD-to-suburban commute times now 48 minutes (vs. pre-quake 22 minutes). Bus bunching common at Civic Centre staging area; frequency being adjusted dynamically.

title: Multiple valve failures during isolation attempt
description: Wellington Water crews encountering stuck isolation valves due to sediment buildup and water pressure. Manual opening attempts unsuccessful at 3 valve points.

title: Petone industrial workers report cumulative structural concerns
description: Petone workers report M2.6 aftershock and note visible deformation in industrial structures. Cumulative damage from mainshock and 20+ aftershocks creating safety concerns.

title: Power restoration efforts beginning in some suburban areas
description: Crews working to restore supply; some neighborhoods getting power back. Many areas still dark.

title: Thorndon liquefaction sand boil activity documented
description: Photographs document sand boil activity at 8 separate locations on Thorndon reclaimed land. Sand deposits spreading water across streets.

title: Thorndon power lines - sagging from tower tilt due to ground subsidence
description: Wellington Electricity field crew reports electrical tower on Thorndon Quay tilting 12 degrees from vertical. Power lines now sagging and at risk of contacting trees.

title: Tsunami wave peak: 0.8m recorded at Oriental Bay, alert ongoing
description: MetService confirms tsunami wave peak of 0.8m at Oriental Bay at 09:49 NZST. Secondary waves expected. Coastal advisory remains in effect with public advised to stay inland until clearance given.

title: CBD - temporary temporary pedestrian barrier installation on damaged facade block
description: Fire and Emergency installing temporary perimeter fencing and pedestrian barriers on Lambton Quay below damaged facade zone. Falling debris hazard mitigation; barrier height assessment ensuring pedestrian clearance from potential hazards.

title: Hutt Road Seaview access: temporary pothole cordoning completed; detouring vehicles to alternate route
description: Hutt Road Seaview sector: buckled sections marked with warning cones; traffic management redirecting vehicles 200 meters east via Industrial Avenue alternate. Road crew estimates 2-hour patch repair.

title: Kilbirnie and Hataitai: normal power operations confirmed
description: Eastern suburbs power systems now operating at full capacity. Grid monitoring showing normal frequency and voltage across Kilbirnie and Hataitai zones. No further outages expected.

title: Liquefaction in Hataitai threatening homes near coast
description: Sand boils forcing families to evacuate. Ground instability continuing in several locations.

title: M2.7 aftershock Newtown area, magnitude consistency check
description: GeoNet detects M2.7 aftershock at 09:42 NZST in Newtown area, depth 12 km. Magnitude recalculated as 2.7 following full waveform analysis. Aftershock statistics remain normal.

title: Miramar avenue parking building - entrance ramp now impassable
description: Parking building on Miramar Avenue shows entrance ramp tilted 12 degrees. Vehicles can no longer access facility, 80+ cars now blocked inside.

title: Regional power situation: 95% of Wellington region restored, remaining 5% by midday
description: Wellington Electricity reporting major success in stabilization and restoration efforts. Hospital, water, transport fully operational.

title: SH1 southbound lanes: Emergency vehicle priority access
description: NZTA establishes dedicated emergency vehicle lane on SH1 southbound at Ngauranga, allowing limited civilian traffic in remaining lanes. Delays still 30+ minutes during peak.

title: Seaview water main break locations mapped for repair coordination
description: Wellington Water has identified 12 water main rupture locations in Seaview requiring coordinated repair. Repair teams mobilizing in priority order.

title: Sewage system damage: multiple overflows in low-lying areas
description: Wellington Water confirms ruptures in sewerage lines affecting Te Aro and Newtown. Overflow containment being deployed. Public health advisory issued.

title: Structural assessment: Ghuznee Street heritage
description: WCC team conducting urgent structural assessment of heritage warehouse complex showing multiple damage indicators.

title: CentrePort vessel scheduling adjustment: four vessels now confirmed Lyttelton/Auckland re-routing
description: CentrePort finalized alternative port assignments: MV Pacific Crown, Hansa Pacific, Agile Bay, and Stella Australis redirected to Lyttelton (48-72 hour delays) or Auckland. Emergency tariff waivers negotiated with vessel operators.

title: Evacuation: Island Bay coastal area, 400 residents must leave immediately
description: WREMO issues evacuation for Island Bay following tsunami wave detection. 400 residents advised to move away from coast and move uphill. Assembly points established at Newtown welfare centre.

title: Flood reported near Queen Street
description: Water inundation in residential basement. Water spreading rapidly. Help requested.

title: Flooding traps occupants in basement level businesses
description: Basement level retail spaces in Te Aro precinct now completely flooded. Occupants evacuated via upper floor routes. Water depth over 2m in lowest areas.

title: Inundation reported near Colleen Street
description: Confirmed flooding on Colleen Street affecting commercial properties. Depth 150cm. sandbagging deployed.

title: Kilbirnie liquefaction zone boundary mapping in progress
description: Field teams mapping outer boundary of liquefaction zone in Kilbirnie. Severe damage concentrated within core area approximately 3 blocks wide.

title: Lower Hutt approach roads: Two-lane flow established
description: Lower Hutt approach roads operating at two-lane bidirectional flow after temporary barrier stabilization. Traffic speeds restricted to 40 km/h for safety.

title: M2.5 aftershock Lower Hutt, response agencies consolidating resources
description: M2.5 aftershock at 09:43 NZST in Lower Hutt. Response agencies consolidating resources for extended operation. Transition from peak emergency phase to sustained recovery phase underway.

title: M3.4 aftershock, Ngauranga-SH1 area
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 3.4 aftershock at 09:43 NZST, near Ngauranga Gorge motorway, depth 12km. Minor shaking along northern transport corridor.

title: Mount Victoria tunnel - light subsidence monitoring
description: Subsidence monitoring shows 0.15m settlement near tunnel approach. Structural health monitoring equipment installed; traffic monitored.

title: Multiple gas odours detected across urban core - secondary network ruptures
description: Community members and emergency crews report gas odours at six separate locations across Courtney Place-Dixon Street-Willis Street triangle. Aerial drone surveys confirm multiple rupture points on interconnected reticulation network.

title: Rail operations: full service schedule active
description: KiwiRail reports all Wellington rail services now operating on full schedule. Signal systems functional. No speed restrictions. Commuter operations normal.

title: Road closure update for SH1 mainline near Civic Centre
description: Temporary repair work underway; single-lane alternating traffic system in effect.

title: Supermarket supply chain: Countdown and New World resuming trading with modified hours
description: Limited stock available due to brief power loss. Refrigerated goods disposed of, restocking from distribution centers continuing.

title: Building collapse risk: Te Aro
description: Media reporting potential structural instability in commercial building. WCC engineers declaring building unsafe pending detailed assessment.

title: Flood damage reported
description: Water inundation in riverside properties. Inundation ongoing. Help requested.

title: GeoNet releases updated aftershock statistics bulletin
description: GeoNet publishes comprehensive aftershock analysis: 48 earthquakes M2.0 and above recorded since 07:42. Largest aftershocks M5.1, M4.8, M4.5. Sequence decay follows Omori law predictions closely.

title: Kāpiti Line southbound service restart: limited-capacity trial executed successfully at 13:02 NZST
description: Kāpiti Line Paraparauum-bound service (single carriage, 280 passengers) completed successful southbound trial from Wellington Station. Engineering confidence high; two-carriage service approved for 13:30 NZST commencement.

title: Lower Hutt - foundation repair specialist consulting on damaged property remediation
description: Foundation repair specialist consulting with WCC on remediation approach for liquefaction-affected Melling Road property. Preliminary underpinning strategy being evaluated; detailed geotechnical analysis underway.

title: Major supermarkets closing; grocery access becoming critical issue
description: Countdown and Pak'nSave outlets in CBD and inner suburbs shutting due to power loss and safety concerns. Food supplies at welfare centers prioritized.

title: Petone Harbor Street business properties affected by subsidence
description: Businesses along Harbor Street in Petone reporting structural damage from ground subsidence. Several storefronts showing facade cracking.

title: Petone and Seaview: complete power restoration confirmed
description: Petone industrial zone and Seaview port precinct now fully powered. All electrical infrastructure operational. No damage-related concerns remaining in this sector.

title: Petone ramp repair: Temporary stabilization complete
description: Temporary steel shoring installed on SH2 Petone on-ramp to address subsidence. Heavy vehicle restrictions lifted; normal lane capacity restored with monitoring equipment in place.

title: Power and water service loss in central Kilbirnie
description: Multiple utility failures affecting residential area. Residents advised to conserve water; boil water advisory issued.

title: Quay Street CBD - street lighting pole collapse blocking intersection
description: Traffic camera documents street light pole on Quay Street falling after base had been undermined by liquefaction and differential settlement. Pole now lying across intersection.

title: RNZ: National briefing on earthquake response and critical infrastructure restoration timeline
description: Live update reporting power crisis largely contained within 90 minutes. Praise for emergency protocols and utility coordination.

title: Structural assessment: Hospital building damage evaluation ongoing
description: Wellington Hospital facility undergoing structural damage evaluation. Non-critical areas showing minor damage. Emergency power and water systems operational. Facility remains operational as primary trauma centre.

title: Civic Square utility restoration: water main closure completed; gas main integrity confirmed
description: Civic Square utility incident: potable water main valve isolation completed. Natural gas main assessed as structurally sound; service recommenced under pressure monitoring.

title: Gas leak: Te Aro commercial
description: Fire and Emergency and utilities coordinating response to gas main rupture in commercial precinct. Multiple-block evacuation underway.

title: Hataitai: transformer replacement ordered, expedited delivery expected within 12 hours
description: Replacement unit sourced from Auckland. Temporary bypass power line installed for essential services. Full restoration possible by end of day.

title: Hutt City Council reports M2.5 aftershock, resource deployment sustained
description: Hutt City Council emergency operations report M2.5 aftershock. Resource deployment continues across valley. Secondary damage assessment teams report significant cumulative damage.

title: Inundation alert for Hutt Road
description: Confirmed flooding on Hutt Road affecting ground-floor properties. Depth 30cm. pumping underway.

title: Kelburn Parade - earth crack affecting road alignment
description: Large earth crack visible on Kelburn Parade near the University area. Road surface offset; traffic management applied; engineering assessment scheduled.

title: Liquefaction damage continuing in eastern suburbs with HIGH risk areas
description: More sand boils emerging; subsidence patterns evolving. Monitoring ongoing for new disturbances.

title: Lower Hutt - emergency building closure notices posted on Melling Road precinct
description: Building closure notices being posted on three commercial properties on Melling Road following structural safety determinations. Occupancy prohibition enforced pending detailed engineering assessment and repair planning timeline.

title: M4.0 aftershock, Brooklyn area
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 4.0 aftershock at 09:45 NZST, 10km south-southwest in Brooklyn area, depth 12km. Light to moderate shaking reported.

title: Mercer Street approach: Bidirectional traffic restored
description: Mercer Street approach bidirectional traffic restored after temporary subsidence stabilization. Monitoring equipment installed for ongoing assessment.

title: Rongotai runway reopening assessment deferred due to damage extent
description: Wellington Airport engineering assessment indicates runway pavement damage extent requires full structural evaluation before reopening consideration.

title: Structural assessment: Hataitai residential block found unstable, evacuation
description: Structural engineers identify Hataitai residential block on Mahina Crescent as critically unstable due to foundation failure. 35 households evacuated immediately. Demolition contractor on standby.

title: Thorndon CBD - building lobby floors now showing 10cm step displacement
description: Photos of office building entrance on The Terrace show main floor now 10cm below street level entrance. Entry step gap created, barrier required.

title: Traffic flow: normal patterns resuming across region
description: NZTA reports traffic flow returning to normal across all Wellington routes. Ngauranga Gorge and SH2 clear. Travel times normalizing. No congestion points.

title: Water pressure now zero across CBD primary network
description: SCADA confirms zero operating pressure across all primary distribution nodes in CBD core. Network now offline except for emergency isolation protocols.

title: 1News live: Wellington commuter testimony - 'Transport nightmare but city holding up'
description: 1News interview with Wellington commuter: 'It's been chaotic—buses packed, trains still mostly down. But emergency response is impressive. Most people just trying to get home safely.' Interview at Civic Centre bus staging area.

title: Burst water main on Jervois Quay
description: Major water loss on Jervois Quay. Ground subsidence visible. Emergency assistance needed.

title: Gas leak: Lower Hutt Esplanade
description: Fire and Emergency responding to gas main rupture affecting waterfront area businesses. Evacuation of industrial park partially underway.

title: Large diameter break on Hutt Road
description: Water main rupture on Hutt Road. tanker deployed. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Lower Hutt - building yellow-tagged following foundation damage assessment
description: Three-storey mixed-use building on Station Road issued yellow tag following WCC structural engineer foundation settlement findings. Monitoring regime established; detailed geotechnical assessment scheduled for this week.

title: Lower Hutt: all residential areas now back on mains power, recovery phase established
description: Utilities reports show 100% restoration to suburbs and commercial zones. Damage assessment teams documenting customer impacts.

title: M2.6 aftershock Petone area, continued regional activity
description: M2.6 aftershock at 09:46 NZST detected in Petone, depth 14 km. Continued activity across Hutt Valley and Wellington regions. No indication of additional major aftershocks in near term.

title: M3.1 aftershock, outer zones
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 3.1 aftershock at 09:46 NZST, 14km south-southeast in outer zones, depth 12km. Minor shaking detected.

title: Miramar foreshore subsidence: residential area evacuated as precaution
description: RNZ: liquefaction damage on Mahina Crescent causing ground to settle unevenly. Crack propagation threatening foundations of 12 homes. All residents vacated.

title: Miramar liquefaction-induced subsidence survey results released
description: Survey team results show subsidence magnitude in Miramar ranging from 10cm to 45cm depending on location. Liquefied zones correlate with highest subsidence.

title: Structural assessment: Multi-story residential buildings priority evaluation
description: WREMO prioritizes evaluation of multi-storey residential buildings. 25 buildings now under urgent structural assessment. Residents of affected buildings being evacuated to welfare centres.

title: Thorndon and Quays: business power restoration complete
description: Thorndon and Quays commercial areas fully operational on mains power. Office buildings, hotels, and hospitality venues powered and reopening to customers.

title: Two-story residential building collapsed on Majoribanks Street
description: Older villa structure collapsed during morning; occupants evacuated. Structural engineers assessing adjacent buildings.

title: Water pooling on Willis Street
description: Confirmed flooding on Willis Street affecting Storm Street subway. Depth 30cm. pumping underway.

title: Aotea Quay subsidence monitoring: hourly surveys confirming ground stabilization; minimal further movement
description: CentrePort geodetic survey shows Aotea Quay subsidence stabilizing. Hourly GPS monitoring indicates <1mm additional movement in past hour. Structural engineers confident in berth re-assessment completion by 14:30.

title: Brooklyn Road: Debris removal 50% complete
description: Excavation of Brooklyn Road landslide debris reaching halfway mark. Estimated completion 90 minutes; temporary access maintained via Mount Victoria for essential traffic.

title: Building damage: Ghuznee Street
description: Fire and Emergency responding to structural failure in commercial building. Area cordoned; occupant welfare being managed.

title: Business continuity: CBD investment firms and government agencies resuming full operations
description: Data center backups verified intact. Communications restored. Market updates being processed. Minimal data loss reported.

title: CBD waterfront infrastructure damage assessment beginning
description: Engineering teams beginning systematic assessment of waterfront infrastructure damage in CBD. Preliminary focus on seawall and embankment condition.

title: Flooding worsens near State Highway 2 underpass
description: Water accumulation near SH2 Hutt underpass has created 1.2m deep lake. Traffic diverted. Water from multiple main breaks still flowing into depression zone.

title: High-priority main break Grey Street
description: Water main rupture on Grey Street. supply rerouted. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Hutt Valley Highway southbound - slide continues blocking
description: Landslide material continues to block southbound lane; cleanup crews making progress but significant debris remains. Estimated 2-3 hour clearance timeline.

title: Injury count revised upward; many minor injuries identified
description: As damage assessment continues, more minor injury reports received. Total now estimated at 60+.

title: Kilbirnie water main network - secondary rupture on Broadway
description: Wellington Water confirms second major main failure on Broadway near Kilbirnie parade intersection. Water spray pattern indicates high-pressure rupture, flow rate 800L/min estimated.

title: M2.4 aftershock Miramar peninsula, sequence enters maturity phase
description: M2.4 aftershock at 09:47 NZST on Miramar. GeoNet analysis confirms sequence now in maturity phase with expected gradual decay over 4-6 additional hours.

title: Medical services: normal patient scheduling resuming tomorrow
description: Hospitals and clinics now confident in power stability. Normal patient scheduling resuming tomorrow. Elective procedures rescheduled. Emergency capacity maintained.

title: Newtown - structural monitoring instruments calibrated at yellow-tagged residences
description: Surveying team calibrating settlement monitoring instruments at yellow-tagged residential properties. Baseline readings recorded; automated data logging initiated for continuous monitoring.

title: Water sensor triggered in Zone E (Petone)
description: Automated alert from Zone E (Petone). Saturation alarm triggered. Flood conditions near Willis Street.

title: Welfare centre update: Newtown at 390 residents, limited further intake
description: Newtown Community Centre reports 390 residents accommodated. Capacity approaching maximum. WREMO directing new arrivals to Lower Hutt facility 15km away. Coordination calls with other welfare centres ongoing.

title: Centennial Park route: Access restrictions easing
description: Centennial Park residential area approach road restrictions gradually easing as pavement repairs progress. Temporary traffic lights managing flow on partially damaged sections.

title: Kilbirnie embankment - slope failure risk now assessed as critical
description: Geotechnical team rates Kilbirnie embankment slope now at critical failure risk. 60cm subsidence and lateral spread creating unstable geometry.

title: M3.3 aftershock, Petone-Hutt area
description: Magnitude 3.3 aftershock recorded by GeoNet seismographs at 09:48 NZST, 15km northeast in Petone area, depth 11km. Light shaking detected.

title: Person injured by falling debris outside supermarket in Te Aro
description: Storefront window collapse injured pedestrian. Minor head injury; treated by paramedics on scene.

title: Petone residents: temporary overhead lines providing power, permanent replacement to follow
description: Families grateful for restoration after 84-minute outage. Community centers set up for ongoing support. Cleanup efforts beginning.

title: Petone workers report M2.6 aftershock at workplace
description: Petone industrial zone workers confirm feeling M2.6 aftershock. Seaview and Petone area continues normal aftershock activity. Workplaces remain in reduced operations status pending structural clearance.

title: Rail network severely disrupted: KiwiRail suspends all services indefinitely
description: KiwiRail confirms extensive track damage between Wellington and Palmerston North. Hutt Valley branch line impassable due to ground deformation.

title: SH2 Petone clearance: debris field removal 80% complete; partial traffic flow normalization expected 13:45
description: NZTA debris removal teams cleared 80% of SH2 Petone blockage field. Temporary single-lane flow established; full bidirectional capacity restoration estimated 13:45 NZST following final debris extraction.

title: Structural assessment: Bowen Street
description: WCC evaluating 1970s-era office tower with visible concrete spalling and structural cracking. Temporary evacuation recommended pending engineering report.

title: Te Aro - building engineer confirms safe occupancy for blue-tagged commercial space
description: Structural engineer blue-tagging commercial office space on Ghuznee Street confirming structural integrity and safe immediate occupancy. Building clearance allowing limited business operations resumption; full assessment report transmitted to owner.

title: Te Aro power: circuits fully restored and stable
description: Te Aro ward now at 100% power restoration. Water main damage cleared; electrical system verified safe. Retail and residential areas fully operational.

title: Thorndon building owners assessing structural repair costs
description: Thorndon building owners consulting with engineers about structural damage repair. Estimates suggest significant reconstruction required in worst-affected buildings.

title: Aftershock magnitude 3.8 centred near Kelburn, further minor damage
description: GeoNet records magnitude 3.8 aftershock at 09:57 NZST centred near Kelburn. Triggers minor building movements and dislodgement of loose architectural features in CBD.

title: Building damage widespread in CBD requiring immediate stabilization
description: Multiple structures showing dangerous cracking and tilting. Propping and bracing systems being installed.

title: Constable Street - liquefaction affecting central business
description: Liquefaction settlement visible along Constable Street near Civic Square. Multiple business frontages affected; traffic diverted.

title: Fire and Emergency - structural engineer deploys to assess Newtown street impact
description: Specialized structural assessment team conducting detailed walkthrough of Rongotai Street examining foundation performance in liquefied soil. Photogrammetry documentation underway for damage records; geotechnical monitoring instruments being installed.

title: Lambton Quay: Traffic management implemented for limited access
description: Wellington City Council reopens Lambton Quay with traffic management devices restricting access to emergency and essential commercial vehicles. Pedestrian access restored via alternate routes.

title: Mainline failure near Civic Centre Road
description: Water main rupture on Civic Centre Road. supply rerouted. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Miramar residents adapt to extended aftershock duration
description: Miramar residents report M2.4 aftershock and express recognition of extended duration ahead. Residents activating longer-term shelter and support plans.

title: RNZ newsdesk update: Central government activates emergency supply-chain protocols for Wellington
description: RNZ reporting: Central government activated emergency protocols to expedite food, fuel, and medicine supply shipments to Wellington via alternative ports (Lyttelton, Auckland). First convoy expected Friday evening.

title: Residential restoration: heating systems returning to normal
description: Wellington residential heating systems coming online across all suburbs. Temperature control returning to normal in homes. Water heating restored.

title: Seaview port operations impact assessment update
description: CentrePort operations impact assessment estimates 4-6 weeks minimum for port facility repairs. Container movements diverted to alternative ports.

title: Secondary line rupture on Rimu Street feeder main
description: 300mm feeder main serving Karori suburbs ruptured beneath Rimu Street. Pressure drop detected across entire Karori distribution zone.

title: Wellington Water: full system pressure and flow restored across all zones
description: Kaiwharawhara plant stable on mains power. Distribution network returning to normal. Quality testing ongoing.

title: Building damage: Cuba Street Te Aro
description: Residential apartment block sustaining roof and structural damage. Fire and Emergency managing tenant evacuations; WCC assessment pending.

title: Emergency services coordination: power restoration enables full operational capability
description: Fire and Emergency NZ, police, and ambulance services now operating at full capacity. Communication systems fully functional.

title: Johnsonville Line capacity restoration: three-carriage service approved; 570-passenger trial scheduled 13:50
description: Johnsonville Line catenary system restoration verified. Three-carriage full-capacity service (570 passengers) trial approved; departure from Wellington Station scheduled 13:50 NZST northbound to Johnsonville terminal.

title: Kilbirnie residential area displacement timeline projected
description: WCC estimates 200+ residents will require temporary relocation from Kilbirnie due to building damage. Welfare center capacity being assessed.

title: M2.5 aftershock Lower Hutt Valley detected, tempo continuing
description: GeoNet records M2.5 aftershock at 09:50 NZST in Lower Hutt. Depth 13 km. Aftershock frequency now approximately 12-15 M2+ events per hour. Decay phase proceeding as expected.

title: M3.9 aftershock, Wadestown recorded
description: Magnitude 3.9 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 09:50 NZST, 7km west near Wadestown, depth 13km. Light shaking felt across northern suburbs.

title: Newtown full restoration: supermarkets and services fully stocked
description: Newtown retail precinct fully powered and operational. Supermarkets restocking shelves. Services returning to normal operation. Community gathering points reactivating.

title: Road surface rupture forcing closure of Evans Bay Parade
description: Significant fault rupture across roadway; vehicles unable to pass safely. Residents advised alternative access.

title: Rongotai ground subsidence - parking facility ramp now impassable
description: Airport parking area on Stewart Duff Drive showing 45cm differential subsidence across 30-metre ramp approach. Slope now exceeds 15 degrees, vehicle access impossible.

title: Te Aro - building owner coordination meeting scheduled for facade repair planning
description: Building owner meeting being arranged for Cambridge Terrace office complex facade damage. Engineer attending to present damage assessment findings and discuss permanent repair planning timeline.

title: Wakefield Street: Through traffic partially restored
description: Wakefield Street through traffic partially restored with traffic management maintaining careful flow control. Permanent pavement repairs scheduled.

title: Welfare center capacity reached: Civic Square shelter now at 400 evacuees
description: Newshub reports overflow of people at main CBD welfare center. Red Cross requesting additional bed capacity and volunteers for support services.

title: Welfare centre: Lower Hutt expands with outdoor marquee sections
description: Lower Hutt welfare centre expanding with large marquee tents accommodating additional 300 residents. Total capacity now 550. Sanitation facilities expanded to match new spaces.

title: Business restoration: office buildings at normal operations
description: CBD office buildings now operating at normal capacity. Businesses reopening for remainder of day. Productivity resuming. Working from home orders rescinded.

title: Cobham Drive shuttle operation: hour-4 assessment shows shuttle service capacity adequate; no backup
description: Wellington Airport shuttle service report: Four-hour operation demonstrates shuttle buses handling passenger load effectively. No major backup reported; departures/arrivals proceeding on schedule for helicopter and regional feeder flights.

title: Gas odour: Multiple locations, Te Aro
description: Fire and Emergency responding to gas smell in cluster of buildings on Vivian and Cuba Streets. Gas company mobilizing to locate leak source.

title: Haywards Hill Road - retaining wall partial collapse
description: Retaining wall damage on Haywards Hill Road near Botanical Gardens. Approximately 25 cubic metres fallen; one lane closed with traffic management.

title: Injury update: Petone collapse rescue yields final casualties, 14 total
description: Petone waterfront rescue operations conclude with 14 workers accounted for. Three additional workers pulled from rubble in critical condition. Rescue operations now focused on other sites.

title: Large diameter break on Queen Street
description: Water main rupture on Queen Street. supply rerouted. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: M2.3 aftershock Mount Victoria viewpoint, sequence decay proceeding
description: M2.3 aftershock at 09:51 NZST near Mount Victoria. Sequence decay now clearly established. Expected continuation with diminishing magnitudes through afternoon.

title: M3.0 aftershock, northeast region
description: Magnitude 3.0 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 09:51 NZST, 16km northeast, depth 13km. Minor seismic activity continues.

title: Newtown - foundation monitoring points established on settlement-affected properties
description: Surveyors installing settlement monitoring benchmarks on three Rongotai Street properties showing ground subsidence. Baseline measurements recorded; future re-measurement schedule established for tracking soil movement patterns.

title: Petone liquefaction extent final mapping confirms widespread damage
description: Final liquefaction mapping indicates severe damage zone in Petone encompasses commercial and residential areas over approximately 2 square kilometers.

title: Road condition evaluation ongoing on multiple state highways
description: Traffic engineers assessing damage; repair priorities being determined. Phased reopening planned.

title: Seaview loading dock - equipment now at dangerous tilt angles
description: Port hydraulic loading equipment on Gracefield Road dock now tilted so steeply that operator access is unsafe. Equipment shut down pending stabilization.

title: Street-level flooding creates isolation, Petone shops
description: Jackson Street retail district completely surrounded by water 70cm deep. Shopkeepers trapped inside buildings. Emergency rescue teams on standby.

title: Water pooling affecting Storm Street subway
description: Water inundation in Storm Street subway. Water spreading rapidly. Help requested.

title: Building damage: Bowen Street
description: Fire and Emergency responding to structural failure in commercial building. Area cleared; WCC structural assessment arranged.

title: CBD office building - concrete spalling identified on parking structure underside
description: Parking structure inspection on Vivian Street office building complex reveals concrete spalling and reinforcement exposure on lower deck levels. Load path integrity assessment underway; staged closure of levels being considered.

title: Hutt Valley emergency operations maintaining alert status
description: Hutt City Council emergency operations report M2.5 aftershock in Lower Hutt. Response teams continue secondary damage surveys. No new critical incidents reported in valley areas.

title: Island Bay ongoing challenge: welfare centre power needs escalating
description: Island Bay residents displaced to welfare centre in Newtown due to infrastructure damage. Temporary generator providing power to emergency shelter; permanent repairs estimated 1 week.

title: KiwiRail: Northern Line services running at normal frequency by 11:20
description: All scheduled trains operational. Hutt Valley line restoration complete. Commuter services stable.

title: Liquefaction fountains across Newtown school playing field
description: Multiple sand and water boils forcing evacuation of schools. Ground instability poses ongoing risk.

title: Melling Line full-capacity service: four-carriage train departing Wellington Station at 13:55 NZST
description: Melling Line four-carriage full-service (840 passengers) commencing at 13:55 NZST. Service frequency: 15-minute intervals initially (vs. pre-quake 10-minute). All-clear signal given by KiwiRail operations.

title: Parliament House structural integrity confirmed; normal operations suspended
description: Parliamentary Service reports building passed initial inspection but precautionary measures in place. Services relocated; Parliament suspended until further notice.

title: Rongotai pavement engineering analysis complete
description: Pavement engineering analysis indicates complete reconstruction of runway pavement required due to liquefaction damage. Timeline for reopening extended.

title: SH2 Hutt: Light vehicle lanes reopened, freight restricted
description: State Highway 2 restored to partial capacity for light vehicles; heavy freight still restricted until bridge monitoring completed. Traffic flowing slowly; congestion easing gradually.

title: CBD - structural assessment database populated with initial damage documentation
description: WCC structural assessment database populated with initial damage documentation from CBD survey operations. Database containing photographic evidence, structural notes, and preliminary repair cost estimates.

title: Evacuation: Johnsonville residential areas due to building instability
description: WREMO issues evacuation for parts of Johnsonville following assessment of foundation failures. 250 residents must leave residential blocks on Chapel Street. Evacuation assembly at local primary school.

title: Gracefield industrial: Freight operations resuming
description: Gracefield industrial zone freight operations gradually resuming after pavement stabilization. Access routes partially open; full capacity restoration pending final clearance.

title: Harbour Quay - liquefaction and subsidence ongoing
description: Continued subsidence on Harbour Quay approach affecting traffic flow. Multiple measurement points showing additional 0.1m movement in 30 minutes.

title: Liquefaction damage spreading to additional suburbs as ground instability continues
description: New subsidence zones appearing in previously unaffected areas. Residents advised to remain vigilant.

title: M3.2 aftershock, Lower Hutt CBD
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.2 aftershock at 09:53 NZST, 18km northeast in Lower Hutt CBD, depth 12km. Minor seismic activity continues.

title: Miramar aftershock damage assessment ongoing
description: Aftershocks continuing to affect already-damaged buildings in Miramar. Some structures showing additional cracking from aftershock vibration.

title: Miramar: peninsula power restoration 95% complete, final zones energized by 11:30
description: Factory Road and Evans Bay transformer installations commissioned. Whakatangi naval facilities back to normal operations.

title: Mount Victoria residents optimistic about decreasing aftershock severity
description: Mount Victoria residents report M2.3 aftershock with optimism about magnitude reduction. Residents remaining in parks and open areas but noting increased comfort.

title: Petone streetscape - manhole cover ejection creating hazard
description: Photos show storm water system manhole cover on Main Street ejected by pressure wave from liquefaction. Cover now missing, hole presents falling hazard. 2 metres radius exclusion established.

title: Structural assessment: Whitmore Street
description: WCC structural engineer assessing commercial building with wall cracking and internal damage. Occupants evacuated pending full evaluation.

title: Supply chain logistics: distribution centers resuming operations
description: Wellington supply chain logistics resuming normal operations. Supermarket distribution centers back to processing orders. Stock replenishment commencing.

title: System pressure loss on Victoria Street
description: Water main rupture on Victoria Street. repair crew mobilized. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Water emergency declaration issued by WREMO
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management Office declares water infrastructure emergency across Wellington urban zone. All available resources mobilized for response.

title: Wellington Station Platform 4 realignment: track geometry acceptable for limited southbound service
description: Platform 4 track realignment verified acceptable by engineering team. Limited southbound services (Melling, Kāpiti) resuming at 14:00 NZST with 50% passenger capacity initially.

title: CBD - seismic bracing failure identified in office building suspended equipment
description: Structural engineer documenting seismic restraint bracket failures on suspended HVAC and mechanical equipment at Bowen Street commercial building. Equipment re-bracing required before safe operation resumption; temporary mechanical isolation implemented.

title: CBD full restoration: last commercial circuits online
description: Wellington CBD now at 100% power restoration. Last critical circuits online at 10:54 NZST. All government buildings, hospitals, police, and emergency services fully powered.

title: CentrePort Crane No. 3 repair timeline: temporary stabilization complete; full-service restart 15:00 NZST
description: CentrePort announced Crane No. 3 tilt issue (2.1 degree offset) stabilized via hydraulic shoring. Full service-restart scheduled 15:00 NZST; container discharge operations resuming at reduced 65 TEU/hour pace.

title: Extended power outage affecting Lower Hutt central areas
description: Electricity supply cut to large residential and business district. Hospitals and services relying on backup systems.

title: M2.4 aftershock Miramar peninsula, continuing eastward activity
description: M2.4 aftershock at 09:54 NZST on Miramar peninsula, depth 11 km. Continued activity in eastern suburbs and harbour zones. Aftershock swarm distribution shows expected pattern.

title: Miramar cutting: Rockfall risk assessment ongoing
description: Geotechnical engineers continue monitoring Miramar cutting for additional unstable sections. Netting installation 80% complete; full reopening subject to clearance from structural assessment.

title: Rongotai Airport: all flight operations resumed to normal schedule
description: Runway lighting, terminal facilities, and ground support systems fully operational. Passenger processing at normal capacity.

title: Seventh aftershock measured: magnitude 5.0 continues stress release
description: GeoNet magnitude list now showing seven measurable aftershocks above 4.5 magnitude in past 90 minutes. Pattern consistent with major-event response.

title: Structural assessment: Lower Hutt Waterloo
description: WCC engineers assessing residential area showing widespread foundation damage. Multiple properties being evaluated for occupancy status.

title: Te Aro street level - multiple building entries now inaccessible
description: Survey of Vivian Street confirms 4 building entries now blocked by step displacement. Ground settlement varying building-to-building between 15-35cm.

title: Wellington CBD water outage affecting hospitals and services
description: Water outage from main line damage affecting multiple hospitals and emergency services in CBD. Water rationing procedures being implemented.

title: Facade damage: Willis Street
description: Exterior cladding panels dislodged from mid-rise commercial building creating falling debris hazard. Area cordoned by emergency services.

title: Flooding inundates Vivian Street parking precinct
description: Surface parking precinct near Vivian Street flooded 60cm deep. Vehicles partially submerged. Drainage systems unable to cope with influx from upstream breaks.

title: M2.2 aftershock Rongotai zone, airport remains operational
description: M2.2 aftershock at 09:55 NZST at Rongotai. Wellington Airport operations continue normal. Runway operations unaffected by M2.2 aftershock.

title: M3.8 aftershock, Johnsonville area
description: GeoNet seismograph network detected magnitude 3.8 aftershock at 09:55 NZST, 16km north near Johnsonville, depth 11km. Light shaking detected.

title: Manners Street section: Single-lane flow established
description: Manners Street section operating at single-lane alternating flow after temporary repairs. Traffic speed reduced; signaling equipment managing flow.

title: Manufacturing recovery: industrial production resuming
description: Wellington industrial manufacturing now resuming production at affected facilities. Petone and Seaview factories back to operational status. Supply chains normalizing.

title: Power restoration progressing in some central areas
description: Multiple suburbs gradually getting electricity back. Priority being given to hospitals and emergency services.

title: Regional power grid: all 99% restoration achieved, final 1% targeted by 12:00
description: Wellington Electricity declaring major incident response success. Ongoing repairs to secondary infrastructure for long-term resilience.

title: SH1 near northbound Terrace Tunnel - ground fracturing
description: Ground fractures widening near Terrace Tunnel approach. Seismic monitoring equipment installed; traffic carefully monitored for further movement.

title: Structural red-tag: Memorial Park building complex, unsafe for occupancy
description: WREMO structural assessment identifies Memorial Park building complex as red-tagged and unsuitable for occupancy. Building hosts community services. 150+ service users being relocated to emergency facilities.

title: Taranaki Street northbound restoration: second lane opened; normal bidirectional flow by 14:15 NZST
description: Wellington City Council completed debris clearance on Taranaki Street northbound lanes. Second northbound lane restored to traffic; normal bidirectional flow anticipated 14:15 NZST.

title: Te Aro residential building - gas service isolation and repair planning
description: Residents at multi-storey building on Dixon Street having gas service isolated following rupture of riser pipe at foundation level. Temporary liquefied petroleum gas cylinders being organized for domestic needs during main line repairs.

title: Thorndon harbor embankment stability concerns raised
description: Structural engineers raise concerns about Thorndon harbor embankment stability. Monitoring equipment now recording 2-3mm daily movement.

title: Hutt Valley Line three-carriage trial: 750 passengers successfully transported; four-carriage approved
description: Hutt Valley Line three-carriage service (750 passengers) completed successfully. Rail geometry and track performance excellent; four-carriage full-capacity service approved for 14:15 NZST service resumption.

title: Liquefaction damage map published: HIGH risk zones showing severe subsidence
description: GeoNet preliminary damage assessment shows 20-30cm ground settlement across reclaimed areas. Spatial extent: Waterfront through to Kilbirnie and Miramar.

title: Lower Hutt - temporary gas supply establishment being coordinated with utilities
description: Wellington infrastructure operations coordinating temporary gas supply establishment through alternative distribution feeders. Pressure testing and safety verification procedures being completed.

title: M2.9 aftershock, southern region
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 2.9 aftershock at 09:56 NZST, 13km south, depth 12km. Light shaking felt.

title: Miramar residents note decreasing aftershock magnitude trend
description: Miramar community social media indicates collective observation that aftershock magnitudes are decreasing. Residents express cautious optimism about waning seismic activity. Many still maintaining outdoor shelter.

title: Multi-occupant injuries reported at shopping mall in Wellington
description: Interior fit-out collapsed during busy morning period. Several shoppers injured; some serious injuries reported.

title: Queens Drive water supply interrupted
description: Water main rupture on Queens Drive. supply rerouted. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Regional power status: emergency phase concluded
description: Wellington Electricity declares end of Level 2 Emergency. 99% of region now restored. Only Island Bay remains on temporary power; permanent infrastructure repairs to begin next week.

title: Seaview industrial - sand boil plume visible from 2km away
description: Helicopter footage captures massive sand boil on Haywards Hill Road with ejection column reaching 8 metres height. Plume of fine sediment visible across industrial precinct.

title: Seaview industrial chemical storage tank integrity concerns
description: Liquefaction ground movement affecting industrial chemical storage facility in Seaview. Tank support foundation showing stress. Evacuation buffer expanded.

title: Sensor alert: Zone G (Airport) water level
description: Automated alert from Zone G (Airport). Flow rate exceeds capacity threshold. Flood conditions near Willis Street.

title: Te Aro: final power restoration block underway, residents expect connection by midday
description: Transformer for southern blocks confirmed for installation. Connection crews staged for 11:45 completion. Hot meals being distributed to residents.

title: Welfare centre: Kelburn reaches 360 residents, multiple support services
description: Kelburn welfare centre complex now at 360 residents with full medical, mental health and social support services. Bed management system implemented for occupancy tracking.

title: Building evacuation order issued for office tower on Bunny Street
description: Structural cracks deemed dangerous; occupants evacuated via stairs. Building now cordoned off.

title: CentrePort: port operations fully restored, shipping schedule adjustments being processed
description: All cargo handling systems operational. Container yards fully powered. Limited delays expected due to earthquake damage to dock infrastructure.

title: Gas detection: Customhouse Quay
description: Fire and Emergency detecting gas in historic warehouse area. Utility company dispatched; industrial area being managed for safety.

title: Injury tally: Cumulative 118 hospitalized, ICU beds critical shortage
description: Wellington Hospital now reports 118 hospitalized patients with 52 in intensive care. All ICU beds filled, emergency arrangements made with private medical facilities. Blood bank reserves depleted by 60 percent.

title: Kilbirnie water sewerage network failure scope documented
description: Wellington Water documents full scope of water and sewerage network failures in Kilbirnie. 15+ rupture points identified requiring staged repairs.

title: Main breaks now spanning 3km distribution corridor
description: Wellington Water field assessment confirms ruptures spanning 3km of primary distribution corridor from Te Aro through CBD to Thorndon. Repair sections marked for staged replacement.

title: Power quality monitoring: no further instability anticipated
description: Wellington Electricity grid monitoring stable with normal power quality. No further voltage instability or cascading risk anticipated. Grid operating within specifications.

title: Rongotai airport perimeter - new subsidence zone appearing near terminal
description: Ground cracking and compaction now appearing adjacent to terminal building. Subsidence extent unknown, further inspection required to assess terminal stability.

title: Sensor alert: Zone C (Thorndon) water level
description: Automated alert from Zone C (Thorndon). Flow rate exceeds capacity threshold. Flood conditions near Hutt Road.

title: Stuff online: 'Wellington CBD transport recovering faster than expected; rail lines opening throughout morning'
description: Stuff live news: 'Several rail lines now operational or nearing service resumption. Metlink emergency bus protocols managing passenger surge effectively. Transport picture significantly improved compared to mainshock + 45 minutes.'

title: Tawa Station Road - small slip affecting accessibility
description: Hillside slip on Tawa Station Road near rail crossing. Approximately 20 cubic metres blocking; local traffic diverted via Tawa Circle.

title: Te Aro - temporary gas service shutoff completed for multi-unit residential building
description: Fire and Emergency completing gas service isolation at Cambridge Terrace residential complex. Individual unit shutoffs completed; alternative cooking arrangements (hot plates) being provided to affected residents pending main line repair.

title: Vivian Street: Water main repair commencing
description: Wellington Water crews beginning emergency repairs on major Vivian Street water main rupture. Road closure continues; estimate 45 minutes to restoration of service.

title: Wellington Airport reports M2.2 aftershock, no operational impact
description: Wellington Airport operations team report M2.2 aftershock at Rongotai with no impact on runway or terminal operations. Flight resumption planning underway.

title: Hospital status update: Wellington Regional Medical Center fully operational
description: All emergency departments, ICU, surgical suites, and wards powered by mains grid. Backup generators on standby.

title: Hospital systems: full capacity operations confirmed
description: Hutt Valley High Hospital now at full mains-powered operations. All critical infrastructure stable. No ongoing power concerns; backup generators deactivated.

title: Injury: Critical shortage of orthopedic surgeons, specialists airlifted
description: Wellington Hospital requests specialist orthopedic surgeons from Auckland via helicopter. Multiple crush syndrome patients requiring immediate surgical intervention. Waiting time for theatre exceeds 4 hours.

title: Liquefaction and subsidence on Quay Street, reclaimed land area
description: Ground heave and settling visible along entire waterfront section. Buildings tilting; occupants evacuated.

title: Lower Hutt - gas infrastructure damage extent mapping via pressure monitoring
description: Wellington infrastructure SCADA system detects pressure drop on Hutt Road gas main indicating rupture location near Totara Avenue. Isolation valve activation isolating 3-km section of distribution network; repair scheduling underway.

title: M2.3 aftershock near Mount Victoria viewpoint
description: GeoNet detects M2.3 aftershock at 09:58 NZST near Mount Victoria hill, depth 16 km. Continued distributed aftershock activity across hillside areas. Sequence remains vigorous.

title: M3.1 aftershock, Seaview region
description: Magnitude 3.1 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 09:58 NZST, 16km northeast in Seaview/Gracefield area, depth 13km. Light shaking detected.

title: Petone commercial district building demolition risk assessment
description: WCC begins structural assessment of Petone commercial buildings for demolition risk. Several structures showing damage magnitude suggesting total loss.

title: Police redirect traffic: major arteries closed; local routes only
description: Wellington Police instituting traffic control on Lambton Quay and Willis Street. Cordons established around damaged buildings. Emergency vehicles only.

title: Quay Street lower section: Temporary stabilization active
description: Lower Quay Street section stabilized with temporary bracing allowing limited traffic resumption. Monitoring equipment tracking subsidence progression.

title: Wairarapa Line full-service resumption: four-carriage service commencing 14:20 NZST southbound
description: Wairarapa Line track geometry verification complete (Ghuznee underpass clearance acceptable at reduced 5 km/h speed). Four-carriage southbound service commencing 14:20 NZST; passenger capacity: 85% of normal.

title: Business district recovery: offices reopening, staff returning, productivity resuming
description: Civic Square fully illuminated. Elevators operational. IT systems verified functional. Evening meetings proceeding as scheduled.

title: CBD waterfront - underground carpark partial collapse under building
description: WCC structural team reports basement level carpark at Customhouse Quay building showing ceiling deformation and partial column buckling. Building occupants evacuated as safety margin compromised.

title: Flooding persists on arterial routes despite effort
description: Despite attempted isolation, flooding continues on Cable Street and Lambton Quay. Water level static at 1.2m. Drainage teams working to pump down affected zones.

title: Injury reports confirmed at multiple emergency departments region-wide
description: Coordinated medical response activated. Patients being triaged and transferred as needed.

title: M2.1 aftershock Wadestown, frequency now 15+ per hour in M2+ range
description: M2.1 aftershock at 09:59 NZST in Wadestown. Aftershock frequency now 15+ events M2+ per hour. Sequence frequency acceleration as magnitude decreases following Gutenberg-Richter.

title: Rongotai terminal building structural safety declaration issued
description: Wellington City Council structural engineers issue official declaration: Rongotai terminal unsafe for occupancy pending major repairs.

title: SH1 Haywards Hill traffic flow recovery: bidirectional access restored; congestion easing
description: SH1 Haywards Hill incident site cleared; bidirectional traffic flow restored. Northbound traffic no longer requires inner-city detour via Ngauranga Gorge bypass. Overall SH1 congestion index improving toward moderate.

title: Structural engineering assessment: Waterloo Road
description: WCC engineers assessing residential building in Lower Hutt with foundation cracking. Building placed under Level 1 engineering watch.

title: Te Aro - gas service isolation completion confirmed at second residential complex
description: Fire and Emergency confirming completion of gas service isolation at second multi-unit residential building on Ghuznee Street. Temporary cooking facilities being deployed to assist residents.

title: Vivian Street - liquefaction craters multiple locations
description: Multiple settlement craters affecting Vivian Street between Taranaki and Cambridge streets. One lane closed; traffic management in place.

title: Wadestown access: Slope stabilization 60% complete
description: Geotechnical team reports Wadestown hillslope stabilization 60% complete with soil anchors installed in critical zones. Estimated full completion 45 minutes; trial reopening to follow.

title: Welfare centre: Lower Hutt reaches 480 displaced, seeking external support
description: Lower Hutt welfare centre now accommodating 480 residents with ongoing arrivals. Requesting additional catering trucks and medical support from Wellington. Logistics coordinator appointed to manage supplies.

title: Complete traffic shutdown on Taranaki Street between Bond and Willis
description: Multiple road failures and debris; vehicles cannot proceed. Heavy congestion on alternate routes.

title: High water detected in Zone D (Hutt)
description: Automated alert from Zone D (Hutt). Water level 165cm above surface. Flood conditions near Grey Street.

title: Hospital ED at critical capacity: elective procedures cancelled indefinitely
description: Capital & Coast DHB activates Level 4 emergency response. Operating theatres reserved for emergency cases only. All staff called in for mandatory 24-hour shifts.

title: Hutt Valley grid stability: load patterns normalizing
description: Hutt Valley electrical grid load patterns now normalized. Demand management protocols standing down. Power grid operating within normal parameters with no further cascade risk.

title: Lower Hutt - gas main repair crew mobilization - material procurement initiated
description: Wellington infrastructure teams mobilizing emergency repair crew for Hutt Road gas main rupture. Specialized high-pressure pipe sections and welding equipment being staged; repair timeline estimated at 48-72 hours.

title: M3.7 aftershock near Tawa
description: Magnitude 3.7 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 10:00 NZST, 22km north in Tawa region, depth 12km. Light shaking felt in northern suburbs.

title: Metlink bus network stabilization: peak-hour demand surge beginning to moderate across CBD
description: Metlink coordination center reports: peak-hour bus network demand surge moderating. Commuter behavior shifting as rail services resume. Bus staging areas at Civic Centre and Courtenay Place becoming less overwhelmed.

title: Miramar geotechnical investigation mobilizing for ground analysis
description: Geotechnical investigation teams with boring equipment arriving in Miramar to analyze liquefied soil characteristics and predict future movement.

title: Mount Victoria residents confirm ongoing hill area aftershocks
description: Mount Victoria residents report M2.3 aftershock from hilltop vantage point. Residents report good visibility of city below but remain concentrated in parks and open ground.

title: Newtown: all commercial and residential power stable, shops fully restocked
description: Local business owners report successful recovery. Traffic signals functioning normally. Normal shopping patterns resuming.

title: Petone waterfront vegetation - trees now tilted by ground movement
description: Large trees on Petone waterfront now showing 10-15 degree tilt from lateral spreading. Tree root systems visibly disrupted as ground shifts beneath them.

title: Structural assessment: Grey Street area
description: WCC engineers assessing residential area with multiple building damage reports. Rapid evaluation protocol activated.

title: Taranaki Street northern section: Traffic flow improving
description: Taranaki Street northern section traffic flow improving as debris clearing completes. Speed restrictions temporarily maintained for safety.

title: Aftershock magnitude 3.9 centred near Ngauranga, motorway traffic impact
description: GeoNet records magnitude 3.9 aftershock at 10:07 NZST near Ngauranga Gorge. State Highway 1 experiencing congestion from southbound evacuees. NZTA establishing traffic control points.

title: Backup water delivery trucks deployed to CBD zones
description: Wellington Water arranging emergency water tanker deliveries to affected suburbs. First trucks arrive at central distribution points on Queen Street.

title: Building damage: Riddiford Street
description: Residential building with visible structural cracking and displaced interior walls. Occupants safely evacuated; structural assessment scheduled.

title: CentrePort operations status: three berths (Aotea, Kings Wharf, Thorndon) now at limited operations capacity
description: CentrePort operations update: All three previously offline berths now operating at limited capacity (40-65 TEU/hour vs. 120 TEU/hour pre-quake). Vessel backlog beginning to clear via parallel discharges.

title: M2.8 aftershock, central area
description: Magnitude 2.8 aftershock detected by GeoNet seismographs at 10:01 NZST, 4km south-southeast, depth 11km. Minor shaking detected.

title: Newtown building assessment team - rapid triage of commercial street precinct
description: Fire and Emergency structural team completing rapid assessment of commercial buildings on Daniell Street. One building yellow-tagged for interior partition failures; two green-tagged; follow-up detailed inspections scheduled for early afternoon.

title: Oriental Bay: full restoration complete, beach area facilities reopened
description: Restaurants and bars reopening for lunch service. Beachgoers returning after safety checks. No ongoing power issues.

title: Porirua Inlet Road - liquefaction affecting approaches
description: Liquefaction settlement affecting Porirua Inlet Road near Porirua CBD. Approximately 0.3m subsidence; speed restrictions imposed.

title: Road network updates: significant delays on alternate routes
description: Local streets handling diverted traffic; congestion severe. Commuters advised to avoid peak areas.

title: Wadestown residents note increased aftershock frequency but decreasing severity
description: Wadestown residents report M2.1 aftershock and observe increased frequency with decreased severity. Community sentiment becoming more resigned to extended sequence.

title: Wellington CBD liquefaction economic impact assessment initiated
description: Insurance and economic impact assessment teams beginning damage cost evaluation in CBD. Preliminary estimates suggest tens of millions in reconstruction needed.

title: Building emergency at apartment tower on Vivian Street
description: Significant structural damage to high-rise; residents being evacuated via stairwells and cranes.

title: Business continuity: CBD offices resuming operations
