description: Wellington CBD office buildings now fully powered. Businesses resuming operations; HVAC and lighting systems operational. Productivity gradually returning to normal.

title: Evacuation: Berhampore residential area, precautionary measure westward
description: WREMO issues precautionary evacuation for Berhampore residential area. 200 residents advised to leave as preventive measure. Assembly point at Berhampore primary school.

title: Gas leak: Newtown area
description: Fire and Emergency responding to gas in residential Newtown neighborhood. Precautionary evacuations continuing; utility company dispatched.

title: Kilbirnie final status: all infrastructure power-stable, communities functioning normally
description: Isthmus suburb reporting no ongoing outages. Water pressure and supply normal. Emergency centers standing down.

title: M2.2 aftershock Rongotai airport zone shallow depth
description: M2.2 aftershock at 10:02 NZST detected at Rongotai, depth 8 km. Very shallow crustal aftershock indicating continued stress release in upper crust. Airport operations remain stable.

title: Miramar foreshore - shoreline subsidence exposing foundation pilings
description: Coastal erosion assessment shows 35cm subsidence along Miramar foreshore has exposed old wooden building foundation pilings. Intertidal zone now encroaching on property.

title: Newtown - WCC releases residential assessment summary to community
description: WCC releasing community summary of residential assessment results for Newtown area. Tagging distribution breakdown being published; detailed individual report availability announced.

title: Ngauranga Bridge traffic flow normalization: both northbound and southbound lanes restored at 40 km/h limit
description: Ngauranga Bridge temporary support columns performing as expected. Both northbound and southbound lanes restored to standard two-way operation under 40 km/h speed restriction. Full-speed restoration (100 km/h) pending 48-hour monitoring.

title: SH1 Ngauranga: traffic diversion activated via back-roads and local routes
description: NZTA implementing contra-flow on northbound lanes. Vehicles directed away from highway through neighborhood arterials. Expect 4-6 hour delays.

title: Thorndon Quay: Traffic signals restored and repaired
description: Traffic signal systems restored on Thorndon Quay after assessment confirms structural integrity of signal poles. Bidirectional traffic flow resuming with temporary speed restrictions.

title: Thorndon building evacuation status update released
description: WCC updates evacuation status for Thorndon: 18 buildings now evacuated. Additional 12 under structural assessment.

title: Building damage: Curtis Street, Newtown
description: Residential building with cracked exterior walls and potential interior structural issues. Family evacuated; WCC scheduling rapid assessment.

title: CBD - structural engineer completes load capacity assessment for damaged building
description: Structural load capacity analysis completed for Lambton Quay building with damaged bracing connections. Report indicating 60% load capacity remaining; temporary propping system confirmed as adequate for interim load redistribution.

title: Evacuation: Gracefield industrial zone, hazmat risk from fuel storage
description: WREMO expands evacuation to Gracefield industrial zone following assessment of fuel storage tank damage. 150 workers and residents must vacate immediately. Hazmat team deployed to assess chemical leak risk.

title: Featherston Street: Debris cleared, traffic flowing
description: Featherston Street completely cleared of debris with traffic flowing in both directions. Temporary pavement patches adequate for normal operations.

title: Flooding affects pumping station accessibility, Hutt
description: Water backup into secondary distribution pumping station near Hutt Valley. Operators working to prevent water entering electrical control panels.

title: Hataitai: temporary bypass providing emergency power, permanent transformer arrival delayed
description: Replacement unit held in Auckland due to road closures. Alternative temporary bypass circuits energized. Full restoration within 18 hours.

title: Kilbirnie residential - foundation cracking now affecting multiple homes
description: Survey confirms foundation cracking in 3 adjacent homes on Kilbirnie Crescent. Settlement patterns indicate differential movement under individual house foundations.

title: Lambton Quay reversible-lane conclusion: manual traffic control ceases; northbound lane restored 14:30 NZST
description: Lambton Quay structural integrity confirmed by WCC engineers. Reversible-lane emergency protocol ending at 14:30 NZST; permanent northbound lane restoration. Manual traffic officers standing down.

title: Liquefaction situation stabilizing in some areas; monitoring continues
description: New sand boil activity slowing in several suburbs. Geological team monitoring for additional movement.

title: M2.0 aftershock Ngauranga Gorge SH1, motorway monitoring continues
description: M2.0 aftershock at 10:03 NZST near Ngauranga Gorge on SH1. NZTA continues monitoring gorge for rockfall. No major incidents reported. Traffic flow returning to near-normal.

title: M3.0 aftershock, Miramar-Rongotai
description: GeoNet seismograph network detected magnitude 3.0 aftershock at 10:03 NZST, 11km south-southeast near Miramar, depth 12km. Minor shaking recorded.

title: Miramar Avenue - traffic diversion proving difficult
description: Complete closure of Miramar Avenue now causing significant traffic congestion on alternate routes. Airport access compromised; emergency traffic plans activated.

title: Seaview fuel storage facility structural monitoring initiated
description: Real-time structural monitoring installed on Seaview fuel storage facility foundation. System will alert authorities to any critical movement.

title: CBD - infill masonry wall failure noted in older multi-storey office complex
description: Structural engineer identifies partial collapse of non-load-bearing brick infill panels on Lambton Quay office building. Interior brick debris hazard being cleared; no impact to primary load-bearing structure confirmed following detailed inspection.

title: Eastern suburbs power: all zones reporting normal operations
description: Kilbirnie, Miramar, Hataitai, and Rongotai all reporting full power and normal grid operations. No further disruptions expected in eastern suburbs.

title: Hutt Valley underground infrastructure damage: water pipes burst across valley
description: Wellington Water crews report additional main breaks on Hutt Road. Multiple suburbs losing water pressure. Restoration crews mobilizing across affected areas.

title: Island Bay access: Structural engineers completing assessment
description: Engineering assessment of Island Bay coastal road subsidence nearing completion. Preliminary findings indicate limited permanent damage; partial reopening scheduled within 30 minutes.

title: Kilbirnie affected population relocation planning accelerated
description: WCC accelerating planning for relocation of 250+ residents from damaged properties in Kilbirnie. Emergency accommodation identified.

title: Large-scale liquefaction affecting runway and taxiways at airport
description: Multiple surface ruptures across aviation infrastructure. All flights cancelled; facilities closed to traffic.

title: NZ Herald transport correspondent: 'Emergency response effort preventing broader supply-chain collapse'
description: NZ Herald opinion piece: 'Wellington's rapid transport system recovery effort preventing broader regional supply-chain collapse. Road closures lifting quickly; rail services resuming in staggered fashion; port operations already achieving 40-65% throughput.'

title: Regional power crisis resolved: Wellington Electricity reports successful stabilization
description: All major services operational. Transition from emergency protocols to recovery and resilience phase. Damage assessment continuing.

title: Structural assessment: Educational institutions damage survey continuing
description: WREMO conducting systematic damage survey of all educational institutions. Ten schools identified requiring red-tagging. Fifteen more with structural concerns under evaluation. Student relocation ongoing.

title: Wellington Airport reports M2.2 aftershock, operations unaffected
description: Wellington Airport operations report M2.2 aftershock at Rongotai zone. Runway operations remain clear. No flight delays or safety incidents. Continued monitoring in place.

title: Adelaide Road - surface fracturing near botanic gardens
description: Multiple surface fractures affecting Adelaide Road near Botanic Gardens. Traffic diverted; one lane remaining with reduced speed limits.

title: CBD - detailed engineering assessment underway for interior column damage patterns
description: Structural engineering team conducting detailed assessment of interior column damage patterns at Bowen Street office tower. Load transfer analysis ongoing; permanent repair specifications being developed.

title: Kent Terrace connector: Access fully reopened
description: Kent Terrace connector route fully reopened after pavement assessment confirms adequacy. Normal traffic volume accommodated.

title: M3.6 aftershock, Porirua CBD area
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.6 aftershock at 10:05 NZST, 24km north in Porirua CBD area, depth 13km. Minor shaking recorded.

title: NZTA confirms State Highway 1 Gorge passable, traffic stabilizing
description: NZTA Waka Kotahi confirms M2.0 aftershock at Ngauranga Gorge. SH1 remains passable. Traffic volumes recovering. No structural damage or rockfall from aftershock.

title: Petone repair and recovery operations plan drafted
description: WCC emergency operations drafting multi-phase repair and recovery plan for Petone infrastructure. Estimated 8-12 month recovery timeline.

title: Petone restoration milestone: 100% of suburb back on permanent overhead power lines
description: Temporary installation at 12:15 successfully energized all remaining areas. Permanent underground cable replacement scheduled for next month.

title: Power system recovery underway; targeted restoration in progress
description: Electricity networks being segmented; restoration sequence established. Critical services prioritized.

title: Seaview industrial rail spur re-opening: first freight service trial executed 14:25 NZST successfully
description: Seaview industrial spur freight trial (limited load test train) completed successfully. Track elevation adjustment holding; full freight operations resuming 14:40 NZST at 50% capacity initially.

title: Structural assessment: Cbd heritage precinct, multiple buildings compromised
description: Heritage specialists work with structural engineers assessing CBD heritage precinct. Seven historic buildings with significant damage. Urgent shoring and stabilization work commencing to prevent collapse.

title: Structural assessment: Te Aro commercial
description: WCC engineers evaluating multi-storey commercial building with significant cracking patterns. Partial closure of ground floor retail announced.

title: Thorndon retail precinct - shop windows shattered from ground movement
description: Photos of The Terrace show multiple storefronts with large-format windows cracked and broken. Ground settlement has shifted building frames, breaking seals on window units.

title: Water supply pressure still falling across network
description: SCADA monitoring shows continued pressure decay across secondary networks despite isolation efforts. Additional breaks suspected in hard-to-access areas.

title: Aftershock frequency increasing: minor tremors felt nearly every minute
description: Social media reports continuous tremors. GeoNet detecting magnitude 3-4 range aftershocks frequently. Residents describe time as 'unrelenting and terrifying.'

title: Gas odour: Rintoul Street
description: Community reporting gas smell in Newtown residential area. Fire and Emergency investigating with portable detection equipment.

title: Injury and building damage at Petone shopping strip
description: Multiple shop facades collapsed; shoppers and staff injured. Rescue teams treating casualties on site.

title: M2.1 aftershock Wadestown suburb detected
description: GeoNet records M2.1 aftershock at 10:06 NZST in Wadestown hill area, depth 14 km. Continued activity in western suburbs. Aftershock sequence now producing 15+ M2+ events per hour.

title: M2.7 aftershock, western zone
description: GeoNet network detected magnitude 2.7 aftershock at 10:06 NZST, 7km west-southwest, depth 12km. Minor seismic activity continues.

title: Miramar full restoration complete: all areas back on mains power by 12:20
description: Peninsula power grid fully stabilized. Naval training campus and residential areas operational. All emergency centers standing down.

title: Newtown - resident reports gas smell from ground near Daniell Street property
description: Homeowner reporting gas odour near foundation on Daniell Street. Self-isolated home gas supply; Fire and Emergency team responding with gas detectors to confirm source and extent of potential leak.

title: Port operations: back to scheduled capacity
description: CentrePort Petone facility now operating at normal scheduled capacity. Shipping operations proceeding normally. All electrical systems stable and operational.

title: Rongotai airport closure duration assessment preliminary
description: Wellington Airport preliminary assessment suggests minimum 6-month closure for runway and facility repairs from liquefaction damage.

title: Thorndon Quay commercial row - multiple building doors now jammed
description: Photos show multiple office and retail entries on Thorndon Quay now inaccessible due to door frames shifting. Ground settlement and lateral movement creating jamming.

title: Wellington Airport passenger throughput: light aircraft and helicopter services operating at near-normal levels
description: Wellington Airport operations report: Helicopter and regional feeder services operating near-normal capacity. Light aircraft movements unaffected. Cobham Drive shuttle service capacity being reduced as demand normalizes.

title: Building damage summary: dozens of structures requiring detailed assessment
description: Structural engineers and building officials deployed citywide. Detailed evaluations underway.

title: Flooding enters commercial kitchens, Te Aro precinct
description: Water entering commercial kitchen facilities on Tory Street. Restaurants completely shutting down operations. Health department notified of potential contamination.

title: Gas leak: Customhouse Quay warehouse
description: Fire and Emergency investigating gas odour in historic warehouse complex. Multiple utility companies on scene conducting leak detection.

title: M1.9 aftershock Johnsonville northern suburbs, sequence decay accelerating
description: M1.9 aftershock at 10:07 NZST near Johnsonville. GeoNet notes aftershock sequence decay rate accelerating. Expected conclusion of significant aftershocks within 2-3 hours.

title: Miramar community mental health support services being coordinated
description: Mental health services being coordinated for Miramar residents affected by earthquake damage and displacement.

title: Riddiford Street - slip debris still being cleared
description: Debris from Brooklyn slip still obstructing Riddiford Street. Clearing work ongoing; estimated 1.5 hours remaining for full clearance.

title: SH1 northbound: Pavement repairs underway at Ngauranga
description: Resurfacing crews deploying temporary asphalt patches on SH1 northbound pavement cracks at Ngauranga Gorge. Single-lane traffic management continuing; delays reduced to 15-20 minutes.

title: Te Aro - ongoing gas isolation procedures for Cambridge Terrace complex
description: Fire and Emergency crews completing gas service isolation at Cambridge Terrace apartment complex. Main shutoff valve located and secured; individual unit gas isolation underway to allow resident re-entry for evacuation of personal items.

title: Te Aro final blocks: all residential areas now restored, communities functioning normally
description: Southern blocks 8-12 energized at 12:25. All residents back on mains power. Community centers providing recovery support.

title: Tsunami advisory update: Secondary waves detected, 0.5m amplitudes
description: MetService reports secondary tsunami waves detected at 10:11 NZST with 0.5m amplitudes at coastal monitoring stations. Advisory remains in effect until 11:30 NZST. Public to remain inland.

title: Building damage: Victoria Street CBD
description: Fire and Emergency securing area around commercial building with significant internal damage. WCC structural assessment being coordinated.

title: CentrePort operations halted indefinitely; shipping affected
description: Port operations suspended pending structural assessment of remaining wharves. Container vessel temporarily anchored offshore. Import-export logistics severely impacted.

title: Flood damage reported
description: Water inundation in light industrial buildings. Depth now 20cm and climbing. Help requested.

title: Hataitai Valley approach: Traffic flow normalizing
description: Hataitai Valley approach route traffic flow normalizing after temporary repairs. Speed restrictions gradually lifting as monitoring confirms stability.

title: Inundation reported near Colleen Street
description: Confirmed flooding on Colleen Street affecting commercial properties. Depth 120cm. pumping underway.

title: Kilbirnie water supply network - pressure cascade failure across district
description: Wellington Water SCADA monitors show cascading failures across Kilbirnie water distribution. Third and fourth major main ruptures detected within 10 minutes. Isolating sector impossible.

title: Lower Hutt - occupant relocation support program launched for red-tagged properties
description: WCC launching emergency occupant relocation support program for residents of red-tagged buildings. Temporary accommodation coordination and personal effects recovery assistance being organized.

title: M2.9 aftershock near Island Bay coast
description: Magnitude 2.9 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 10:08 NZST, 15km south near Island Bay coastal area, depth 13km. Minor seismic activity continues.

title: RNZ followup: emergency response praised for coordination and rapid restoration
description: Earthquake crisis management hailed as successful. Power restored to 99% of region within 100 minutes. Only isolated areas still awaiting full restoration.

title: Severe power outage cascading through business districts
description: Multiple substations offline; widespread blackout affecting Wellington and surrounding areas.

title: Wadestown residents report M2.1 aftershock on hillside
description: Wadestown residents confirm M2.1 aftershock with minor shaking sensation. Most residents report aftershock frequency is now tolerable with smaller magnitudes. Fatigue from 5+ hour event continues.

title: Water systems: power restored, supply normalization underway
description: Wellington Water reports power restoration to all pumping stations. Water supply pressure normalizing across network. No boil water advisories expected.

title: Wellington CBD liquefaction damage peer review initiated
description: National structural engineering peer review team arriving to verify damage assessments in CBD high-damage zone.

title: Brooklyn Road: Excavation 80% complete, reopening soon
description: Brooklyn Road landslide debris removal 80% complete with final clearing anticipated within 30 minutes. Traffic management personnel standing by for rapid lane reopening.

title: Evans Bay Parade complete section closure ordered
description: Evans Bay Parade section between Centennial and Calabar roads now fully closed due to safety concerns. Through traffic redirected to Queen's Drive.

title: Gas leak: Willis Street industrial
description: Fire and Emergency investigating gas in industrial building. Area evacuated; gas company locating rupture point in utility main.

title: Injury count at 65+ with evacuation process ongoing at multiple sites
description: Rescue operations continuing; some entrapped persons still being located. Additional casualties likely.

title: Johnsonville residents note aftershock sequence slowly weakening
description: Johnsonville residents report M1.9 aftershock and express optimism about weakening sequence. Residents beginning to discuss return to homes.

title: Large bore main rupture detected beneath Thorndon Quay
description: 700mm primary main at Thorndon Quay section showing complete loss of structural integrity. Three separate breach points identified by field crew.

title: Lower Hutt infrastructure: all zones stable, utilities confirming long-term service continuity
description: Water, power, and communications fully operational. Hutt City Council commending response efforts. Recovery phase proceeding smoothly.

title: Seaview port area - water pooling from broken mains beneath operational zone
description: Wellington Water confirms secondary water main rupture beneath Seaview port operational area. Water pooling underground is destabilizing adjacent structures.

title: Te Aro - building engineer assessment confirms secondary collapse risk mitigated
description: Follow-up structural inspection at Cambridge Terrace confirms temporary bracing stability and eliminated secondary collapse risk. Structural engineer confirming continued safety monitoring regime; permanent repair planning underway.

title: Thorndon embankment emergency safety procedures activated
description: Thorndon harbor embankment showing continued movement. Emergency procedures activated including exclusion zone establishment and further evacuation.

title: Welfare centre: Karori tented overflow now housing 250 additional residents
description: Karori Community Hall marquee tents now accommodating 250 residents in addition to 520 indoors. Total 770 people sheltered. Sanitation and hygiene facilities being expanded. Food supplies adequate for 24 hours.

title: Buckle Street: Full capacity traffic operations resumed
description: Buckle Street returned to full capacity traffic operations after temporary repairs and monitoring clearance. Normal traffic patterns restored.

title: Communications infrastructure: full capacity restored
description: Cellphone networks and internet services now at full capacity. 4G/5G signal restored across all metro areas. Government and emergency services comms fully redundant and operational.

title: Ground rupture on Courtenay Place splitting building foundations
description: Fault trace visible across street; building on south side settling. Immediate evacuation ordered.

title: M2.0 aftershock Ngauranga Gorge motorway zone
description: M2.0 aftershock at 10:10 NZST detected near Ngauranga Gorge SH1. Depth 12 km. Continued activity along major structural features. Traffic monitoring continues on State Highway 1.

title: M3.5 aftershock, Ngauranga Gorge
description: Magnitude 3.5 aftershock detected by GeoNet seismographs at 10:10 NZST, near Ngauranga Gorge SH1 motorway junction, depth 12km. Minor shaking along transport corridor.

title: Seaview port reconstruction timeline released
description: CentrePort releases preliminary reconstruction timeline: 12-18 months estimated for full facility restoration and pavement reconstruction.

title: Structural engineers' hotline overwhelmed; building assessment delays expected
description: Stuff: demand for rapid building inspections vastly exceeds supply of certified engineers. Commercial buildings waiting days for red/green stickers.

title: Thorndon Quays: full port and commercial operations resumed, productivity recovering
description: Waterfront businesses reporting successful power restoration. Container handling at 80% capacity. Restaurants and hospitality reopening for evening.

title: WCC structural audit - CBD parking structure damage assessment report
description: Detailed structural engineering audit completed on Lambton Quay parking complex. Report identifies moderate cracking in vertical bracing system but no load path compromise. Monitoring accelerometers being installed; re-inspection scheduled for 48 hours.

title: Welfare centre: Karori marquee complex reaches 800 total capacity
description: Karori welfare centre complex now at 800 total capacity with indoor spaces full. Additional marquee tents accommodating overflow. Food service now operating via mobile catering kitchen.

title: Flooding closes retail shopping area, Petone
description: Jackson Street shopping centre completely closed due to 80cm flooding. Customers and staff evacuated. Retailers assessing inventory damage.

title: Injury management: Wairarapa Hospital receives 28 overflow patients
description: Wairarapa Hospital now managing 28 patients transferred from Wellington Hospital. Capacity strained but operational. Arrangements being made with Hutt Valley for additional patient distribution.

title: Kilbirnie business sector impact assessment underway
description: Chamber of Commerce assessing business sector impact in Kilbirnie. Early estimates suggest 40+ businesses unable to resume operations immediately.

title: M1.8 aftershock Tawa northern suburbs, sequence frequency peaking
description: M1.8 aftershock at 10:11 NZST near Tawa. Aftershock sequence frequency peaking at 20-25 M1.5+ events per hour. Magnitude range now primarily M1-M2.

title: M2.6 aftershock, north-east area
description: Magnitude 2.6 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 10:11 NZST, 15km north-northeast, depth 13km. Light shaking felt.

title: Petone industrial area - prefab building connections failing under load
description: Video shows warehouse near Jackson Street with roof connections pulling apart as building settles at differential rates. Prefab bolts shearing audibly with each subsidence pulse.

title: Road closure summary: SH1 and SH2 both severely impacted
description: State highways partially closed; local road damage extensive. Major transport network disruption.

title: Seaview industrial zone: operations fully restored, shipping schedule recovering
description: CentrePort reporting 85% efficiency. Container yards operating normally. Vessel handling back to pre-earthquake scheduling.

title: Structural assessment: Taranaki Street block
description: WCC engineer team conducting comprehensive evaluation of residential/commercial mixed-use building showing signs of seismic damage.

title: Te Aro - structural engineer presents damage assessment report to council management
description: Structural engineer presenting comprehensive damage assessment report to WCC management covering Te Aro district findings. Report documenting 48 inspected buildings with tagging results and cost estimates.

title: Willis Street - multiple subsidence points affecting traffic
description: Multiple liquefaction subsidence points affecting Willis Street between Boulcott and Willeston streets. Alternating traffic; engineering assessment ongoing.

title: Critical suburban feeder rupture
description: Water main rupture on Midland Road. bypass line activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Flood response activated in Hutt
description: Confirmed flooding on Main Street affecting riverside properties. Depth 45cm. water diversion active.

title: Injury: Continuing rescue operations yield additional casualties
description: Ongoing rescue operations at CBD sites yield six additional casualties with crush injuries. Extraction time extending as rubble removal becomes more complex.

title: Lower Hutt - temporary utility bypass infrastructure being constructed
description: Emergency services coordinating temporary gas and water utility bypass around damaged infrastructure on Station Road. Temporary piping routes being established to maintain residential services during main infrastructure repair period.

title: Miramar cutting: Netting installation complete
description: Rockfall containment netting fully installed on Miramar cutting. Geotechnical team clearing final inspection points; preparation for limited reopening underway.

title: Miramar residential street - entire roadway now showing sag depression
description: Beacon Hill Road section through residential area showing 50-metre long depression with 30cm depth. Entire street now slopes toward center point.

title: Multiple vehicle pile-up on SH1 near Civic Centre exit
description: At least 6 vehicles involved; traffic hazards from debris. Injured persons requiring medical attention.

title: NZTA reports State Highway 1 Ngauranga Gorge monitoring status
description: NZTA Waka Kotahi reports M2.0 aftershock at Ngauranga Gorge. SH1 remains passable with normal monitoring in effect. No rockfall or structural damage reported at gorge.

title: Petone main street: numerous business facades collapsed; street impassable
description: NZ Herald reports significant structural damage to commercial properties on Petone Parade. Multiple facades fallen; risk of further collapses during aftershocks.

title: Petone utility repair prioritization plan finalized
description: Wellington Water and Wellington Electricity finalizing utility repair prioritization. Water mains and electrical lines targeted for priority restoration.

title: Public transport: bus fleet returning to scheduled services
description: Wellington bus fleet resuming scheduled routes following power restoration. Traffic signals and charging infrastructure fully operational. Service frequency normalizing.

title: Wellington CBD power infrastructure: all critical systems stable and monitored
description: CBD fully operational on mains power with redundant backup systems verified. Business confidence returning. Evening commute proceeding normally.

title: Airport route via Rongotai: Capacity normalizing
description: Rongotai Airport access route traffic levels dropping below saturation as Miramar cutting opens. Traffic signals phased for optimal flow.

title: Building damage: Newtown Curtis Street
description: Fire and Emergency securing area around residential building with severe structural damage. WCC coordinating urgent engineering assessment.

title: Evacuation: Brooklyn residential area, building stability compromised
description: WREMO issues evacuation for parts of Brooklyn following structural engineer assessment. 180 residents must leave residential buildings on Main Street showing foundation settlement.

title: Hutt Valley Highway - second debris pile discovered
description: Additional debris from hillside identified 200m south of primary slide location. Crews now working multiple clearance sites; traffic severely constrained.

title: KiwiRail Metlink: all rail lines operating at full capacity, normal services restored
description: Northern and Hutt Valley lines fully functional. Evening rush commute proceeding as scheduled. No further power-related disruptions expected.

title: Liquefaction extent still being determined; more areas likely affected
description: Geotechnical survey teams working through all high-risk zones. Damage extent appearing worse than initial reports.

title: Lower Hutt - emergency gas supply rerouting via alternative distribution network
description: Wellington infrastructure operations activating secondary distribution feeders to restore gas supply to Hutt Road business district pending main line repairs. Staged pressure buildup commencing; completion targeted for late afternoon.

title: M2.8 aftershock, Kilbirnie area
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 2.8 aftershock at 10:13 NZST, 10km south near Kilbirnie, depth 11km. Light shaking felt.

title: Rongotai aircraft operations relocated to alternative airport
description: Wellington Airport arranging aircraft operations diversion to alternative airports. Rental car and accommodation services being coordinated.

title: Tawa residents report persistent but manageable aftershock activity
description: Tawa residents report M1.8 aftershock with recognition that sequence has become more manageable. Residents discussing plans to return indoors.

title: Water rationing orders issued to surrounding suburbs
description: Wellington Water issues severe water rationing orders to Karori, Newtown, and peripheral suburbs due to network outage. Residents limited to essential use only.

title: Airport operations: domestic and international flights resuming
description: Wellington Airport now operating all three runways with full power restoration. Domestic and international flights resuming normal schedules. No flight cancellations expected.

title: Building damage: Grey Street
description: Fire and Emergency responding to report of partial interior collapse in heritage residential building. No occupants in structure; damage assessment ongoing.

title: CBD traffic flow: Willis Street capacity gradually increasing
description: Willis Street emergency routing supporting increased traffic volume as adjacent streets cleared. Temporary traffic signals and signage directing flow; congestion easing in core CBD.

title: Liquefaction damage forcing evacuation of Harbor Quay precinct
description: Visible ground subsidence and sand boils. Multiple buildings declared unsafe pending assessment.

title: M1.9 aftershock Johnsonville suburb, tremor quality diminishing
description: GeoNet detects M1.9 aftershock at 10:14 NZST near Johnsonville. Depth 15 km. Aftershock magnitudes now consistently in sub-M2 range. Sequence decay rate accelerating as predicted.

title: Miramar liquefaction recovery project scope definition initiated
description: WCC initiating detailed project scope definition for Miramar recovery including infrastructure replacement and building reconstruction.

title: Newtown - chemical odour reports follow-up investigation ongoing for gas leak
description: Fire and Emergency conducting follow-up investigation of chemical odour reports in Newtown. Portable gas detectors being used to verify absence of gas leaks in affected area.

title: Power restoration stalled: critical infrastructure damage affecting substations
description: Wellington Electricity reports transformer damage at three primary substations. Repair timelines uncertain; expects significant areas without power through evening.

title: Rongotai Airport operational area - entire zone now off-limits to traffic
description: Airport authority declares all vehicle and foot traffic suspended across operational precinct. Ground deformation measured at 50+ cm in multiple zones. Runway repair assessment deferred pending stabilization.

title: Rongotai Airport: all flight operations normal, passenger services at full capacity
description: Runway lighting, terminal facilities, and ground support operating smoothly. No further weather or power delays expected.

title: Aftershock magnitude 3.7 centred beneath Miramar, building movements
description: GeoNet records magnitude 3.7 aftershock at 10:21 NZST. Triggers minor aftershock damage in already compromised buildings. Further collapses of weakened structures reported in CBD.

title: Building damage: Whitmore Street area
description: Fire and Emergency managing structural failure incident in CBD commercial building. Evacuation complete; assessment queued.

title: CBD commercial zone - structural inspection completion summary report issued
description: WCC structural engineering team completing rapid assessment report on CBD commercial zone. Summary document detailing 24 inspected buildings with tagging distribution and preliminary repair cost estimates compiled for city management.

title: Flooding persists near Willis Street underground
description: Willis Street underground carpark remains waterlogged. Pumping continuing but water table remain high due to aquifer impact from ground ruptures.

title: Inundation detected in Zone B (Waterfront)
description: Automated alert from Zone B (Waterfront). Saturation alarm triggered. Flood conditions near Featherston Street.

title: Kelburn village shops area - ground displacement affecting storefronts
description: Ground displacement affecting vehicle and pedestrian access to Kelburn village shops. Approximately 0.2m offset; traffic diverted.

title: M1.7 aftershock Porirua northern region expanding aftershock zone
description: M1.7 aftershock at 10:15 NZST near Porirua. Aftershock zone continues expanding northward. No significant impacts reported in Porirua region.

title: M3.4 aftershock, Ngaio recorded
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 3.4 aftershock at 10:15 NZST, 8km west near Ngaio, depth 12km. Light shaking felt.

title: Main break on Victoria Street
description: Major water loss on Victoria Street. Water spray 2+ metres high. Emergency assistance needed.

title: Power grid restoration expected to proceed through morning
description: Repair crews working systematically through network. Full restoration may take hours.

title: SH2 southbound lanes: Full capacity restored
description: State Highway 2 southbound lanes operating at full capacity after bridge monitoring completion. Northbound capacity reached 90%; congestion easing.

title: Te Aro historic zone - assessment confirms multiple facades unstable
description: Heritage assessment of Vivian Street confirms 6 building facades now rated unstable. Propping required as immediate safety measure before facades collapse.

title: Wellington CBD insurance claims documentation procedures started
description: Procedures established for insured property owners in CBD to document damage and submit claims. Assessment teams available for on-site surveys.

title: Wellington Water: entire network operating at full pressure and quality standards
description: Treatment plants, pumping stations, and distribution mains all operational. Testing protocols passed. Safe drinking water confirmed.

title: Emergency services coordination: all agencies operating at full operational capacity
description: Fire and Emergency NZ, Police, and Ambulance services confirmed fully functional. Emergency centers standing down, operations transitioning to recovery mode.

title: Firefighter injuries reported at structural fire in CBD
description: Gas rupture causing fire in building; firefighters injured while suppressing blaze.

title: Injury: Psychological trauma cases mounting, counseling services activated
description: Wellington Hospital reports increasing number of patients presenting with acute psychological trauma and panic disorders. Mental health crisis team deployed to welfare centres. Counseling services activated region-wide.

title: Johnsonville community reports subtle M1.9 aftershock tremor
description: Johnsonville residents report barely noticeable M1.9 aftershock. Many residents note that magnitude reduction is psychologically helpful. Outdoors gatherings continue as precaution.

title: M2.5 aftershock near city centre
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 2.5 aftershock at 10:16 NZST, 3km south near city centre, depth 11km. Minor shaking detected.

title: Newtown community update - chimney damage cleanup and foundation inspection
description: WCC announcing residential chimney damage mitigation program for Newtown precinct. Licensed surveyors available for free foundation and chimney damage assessment; repair cost estimates to be documented for insurance claims.

title: Rail network: KiwiRail power restoration completed
description: KiwiRail reports full power restoration to all Wellington rail network points. Signalling and platform lighting fully operational. Train services resuming to normal schedule.

title: Third fatality confirmed: elderly resident succumbed to injuries
description: Capital & Coast DHB reports third earthquake-related death. Victim was hospitalized with crush injuries; complications led to mortality.

title: Thorndon harbor embankment evacuation zone widened further
description: Due to continued embankment movement, evacuation zone now expanded to 200m radius. Additional 50+ residents requiring emergency relocation.

title: Building safety assessments accelerating with more teams on ground
description: Red tags being issued for unsafe structures. Residents being relocated from dangerous buildings.

title: CBD - temporary weather protection systems installed on facade damage sites
description: Temporary weather protection tarp systems being installed on facade damage sites across CBD. Weather-related secondary damage prevention being prioritized pending permanent repairs.

title: Main break confirmed: Jackson Street, Petone
description: Water main rupture on Jackson Street. bypass line activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Ngaio Gorge Road - hillside slip ongoing
description: Ongoing hillside slip on Ngaio Gorge Road continues to feed debris onto roadway. Estimated 25 cubic metres additional material overnight.

title: Porirua emergency management continues monitoring northern expansion
description: Porirua City Council reports M1.7 aftershock with continued monitoring of northern aftershock expansion. No new incidents in Porirua.

title: Regional commerce: supermarkets, banks, and services fully operational and restocked
description: Countdown, New World, and independent retailers reporting normal trading. Banking systems operational. Evening shopping proceeding as normal.

title: Repair crews begin containment at worst break site
description: Specialist crews begin containment operations at Civic Square trunk line break. Temporary isolation bypass being constructed to allow partial network restoration.

title: SH2 Gracefield: Industrial access lanes opening
description: Seaview/Gracefield industrial area subsidence repair sufficient for partial lane reopening. Freight access still limited; full capacity restoration pending completion of pavement works.

title: Seaview dock facility - loading equipment now at dangerous angle
description: CentrePort reports major cargo crane on Seaview Road dock now tilted 18 degrees. Boom resting against supporting cable, immediate de-energization required. Equipment destruction risk high.

title: Seaview environmental monitoring for contamination risk initiated
description: Environmental testing team deployed to Seaview to monitor for potential contamination from industrial facilities damaged by liquefaction.

title: Structural assessment: Port facilities at Seaview critically damaged
description: Port operations at CentrePort report critical damage assessment. Wharf infrastructure, cargo handling systems and storage facilities extensively damaged. Port closure indefinite. 400+ port workers and contractors evacuated.

title: Structural assessment: Vivian Street
description: WCC team assessing residential block showing foundation damage and wall separation. Building temporarily cordoned; resident displacement assistance offered.

title: Critical reticulation line rupture
description: Water main rupture on Willis Street. isolation valve activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Customhouse Quay underground carpark - water level now 1 metre high
description: Carpark level 2 now has water accumulation at 1 metre depth and still rising. Pumping systems unable to match inflow rate from main rupture above.

title: Gas rupture: Higher street utility main
description: Underground gas main ruptured affecting multi-block area. Fire and Emergency conducting evacuations and gas company rerouting supply.

title: Kilbirnie building code compliance review framework established
description: WCC establishing building code compliance review framework for reconstruction in Kilbirnie to ensure seismic resilience of replacement structures.

title: M1.8 aftershock Tawa area north suburbs, tempo sustained
description: M1.8 aftershock at 10:18 NZST detected near Tawa in northern suburbs. Depth 13 km. Aftershock activity now in continuous swarm mode with 20+ events per hour in M1.5+ range.

title: M2.7 aftershock, Brooklyn-Newtown
description: Magnitude 2.7 aftershock detected by GeoNet seismographs at 10:18 NZST, 10km south-southwest in Brooklyn area, depth 12km. Minor shaking detected.

title: Mount Victoria area landslide risk alert; residents advised to evacuate
description: Wellington City Council issues precaution: steep terrain showing instability. Residents on slopes requested to move to lower elevations until assessment complete.

title: Newtown recovery complete: all power stable, community services resuming normal operations
description: Schools, libraries, community centers back online. Local residents expressing confidence in recovery. Evening activities proceeding normally.

title: Retail and hospitality: venues reopening citywide
description: Retail stores, restaurants, cafes, and hospitality venues across Wellington reopening following power restoration. Queues forming at popular locations as public returns to normal patterns.

title: Rising water in basement level of ANZ Centre
description: Water inundation in basement level of ANZ Centre. Water spreading rapidly. Help requested.

title: Road network disruption on State Highway 2 northbound
description: Multiple damage points; one lane closed, one-way traffic system in place. Travel times doubled.

title: Ruptured 50mm service connection on Main Street
description: Water main rupture on Main Street. bypass line activated. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Te Aro - Fire and Emergency releases residential area clearance zone status update
description: Fire and Emergency releasing status update on Te Aro residential clearance zones. Courtney Place and Abel Smith Street evacuation zones being maintained; safe re-entry timeline dependent on gas isolation completion.

title: Te Aro internal streets: One-way systems released
description: Te Aro area one-way traffic systems released as pavement assessment completes. Normal bidirectional traffic resumed on most internal streets.

title: Fire and Emergency - structural emergency kit deployments to 12 priority sites
description: Specialized structural emergency response teams deploying pre-fabricated propping systems to 12 priority buildings across CBD and Te Aro. Load-bearing system integrity assessments underway at each site; 24-hour monitoring protocols being established.

title: Flood threshold exceeded in Zone A (CBD)
description: Automated alert from Zone A (CBD). Saturation alarm triggered. Flood conditions near Whitmore Street.

title: Flooding still accumulating on main arterial routes
description: Despite water extraction efforts, flooding depth on Lambton Quay remains 1m. Continuous influx from upstream breaks exceeding pumping capacity.

title: Hataitai subsidence: Temporary repair completed
description: Temporary stabilization of Hataitai main route subsidence completed with steel plates and drainage installation. Two-way traffic restored with speed restrictions; permanent repairs scheduled.

title: Injury management ramping up with field triage centers established
description: Multiple first aid stations set up in central areas. Paramedics managing growing number of patients.

title: Inundation on Whitmore Street
description: Water inundation in basement loading area. Water spreading rapidly. Help requested.

title: M1.6 aftershock Ngaio area, sequence approaching final wind-down phase
description: M1.6 aftershock at 10:19 NZST in Ngaio area. GeoNet analysis confirms sequence approaching final wind-down phase. Expected 50-70 additional M1+ events over next 2-3 hours.

title: Oriental Bay: beachfront fully recovered, restaurants and hospitality services operating
description: Dining establishments reopened, bars fully stocked, entertainment venues operating. Evening crowd gathering for meal service.

title: Petone infrastructure damage database comprehensive compilation
description: WCC compiling comprehensive database of all infrastructure damage in Petone. 200+ sites catalogued for restoration prioritization.

title: SH2 southbound Hutt Valley - traffic backup 5km+
description: Traffic backup exceeds 5km on SH2 southbound due to multiple ground disruptions. Emergency coordination underway with regional transport authorities.

title: Welfare centre: Hataitai Scout Hall activation with 180 initial arrivals
description: Hastily-opened Hataitai Scout Hall now sheltering 180 residents diverted from overflowing Newtown centre. Basic medical support provided by volunteer nurses. Additional supplies being delivered.

title: Building partial collapse on Customhouse Quay endangering waterfront
description: Four-story structure partially failed; debris falling into harbor. Evacuation of surrounding buildings ongoing.

title: Eighth aftershock: magnitude 4.8 rattles nerves at 10:00
description: Continuous stress release evident. GeoNet monitoring shows no let-up in activity. Residents on edge; anxiety levels extremely high across region.

title: Inundation on Jervois Quay
description: Water inundation in basement of Civic Square office complex. Water spreading rapidly. Help requested.

title: Kilbirnie: infrastructure fully stable and monitored, community services normal
description: Isthmus suburb confirming all systems operational. Water pressure normal. Resident confidence high. Emergency support centers closed.

title: Lower Hutt - engineering support hotline established for building owner inquiries
description: WCC establishing dedicated engineering support hotline for building owner inquiries regarding assessment results and repair guidance. Expert engineer available daily for consultation.

title: M3.3 aftershock, CBD waterfront area
description: Magnitude 3.3 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 10:20 NZST, 3km south-southeast near CBD waterfront, depth 11km. Minor seismic activity continues.

title: Ngauranga southbound: Near full capacity operation
description: State Highway 1 Ngauranga southbound traffic approaching full capacity operation. Northbound delayed repairs nearing completion.

title: Outdoor lighting: street lights and public spaces fully illuminated
description: Street lighting and public outdoor lighting systems across Wellington CBD and suburbs now fully operational. Evening conditions managed with normal lighting patterns.

title: Rongotai runway reconstruction engineering specifications drafted
description: Wellington Airport engineering team drafting detailed runway reconstruction specifications accounting for liquefaction-resistant pavement design.

title: Structural assessment: Multiple CBD buildings
description: WCC team conducting systematic assessment of CBD buildings with visible damage. Triage system implemented for most urgent cases.

title: Tawa residents report sustained minor aftershock activity
description: Tawa residents confirm M1.8 aftershock with very minor shaking. Residents report cumulative total of 50+ noticeable earthquakes since mainshock. Emergency sheltering continues in parks.

title: Te Aro historic building - facade tilting away from structure
description: Photos of 1880s building on Vivian Street show exterior brick facade leaning out 10-15cm at roofline. Mortar joints visibly opening on facade corners. Imminent collapse risk identified.

title: Welfare centre: Hataitai Scout Hall at 190 residents, adequate supplies
description: Hataitai Scout Hall now at 190 residents with adequate food and water supplies. Medical volunteer staff managing minor health issues effectively. Facility operating at sustainable occupancy.

title: Cambridge Terrace - pavement heave and cracking
description: Liquefaction-induced heave creating dangerous pavement conditions on Cambridge Terrace. Traffic management slowing throughput; repairs prioritized.

title: Gas leak: Lower Hutt High Street district
description: Multiple gas leaks detected in retail precinct. Fire and Emergency coordinating large-scale evacuation; utilities mobilizing repair crews.

title: Injury update: Total hospitalized reaches 125, three more deaths confirmed
description: Wellington Hospital reports cumulative total now 125 hospitalized. Three additional deaths confirmed from crush syndrome and cerebral hemorrhage. Intensive care facilities at maximum capacity.

title: M2.4 aftershock, southern suburbs
description: Magnitude 2.4 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 10:21 NZST, 11km south-southwest, depth 12km. Minor seismic activity continues.

title: Miramar geotechnical report preliminary findings released
description: Preliminary geotechnical report released identifying liquefaction triggering mechanisms in Miramar and predicting likely subsidence patterns.

title: Miramar peninsula: all power systems stable, military facilities fully operational
description: Naval training campus operating normally. Factory Road and Evans Bay infrastructure confirmed stable. All systems on mains power.

title: Newtown - yellow tag inspection results being documented for building owners
description: WCC releasing detailed inspection reports for five yellow-tagged properties in Newtown. Reports identifying specific structural concerns and recommended remediation measures; owners being contacted for discussion of repair timelines.

title: Ngaio residents optimistic about sequence entering final phase
description: Ngaio residents report M1.6 aftershock and express optimism about approaching end of significant activity. Residents beginning organized return to homes.

title: Petone town center - debris field of broken glass expanding across streets
description: Storefront windows throughout Jackson Street now shattered. Glass debris accumulation creating hazards for foot traffic. Street level unsuitable for public access.

title: Road repair work priority list established by transport authority
description: Critical routes being addressed first; repair sequencing underway. Temporary diversions in place.

title: Secondary network rupture, Hutt feeder main
description: 250mm feeder main serving Lower Hutt suburbs ruptured beneath Hutt Road overpass. Rupture creating water loss affecting 50,000+ residents.

title: Educational facilities: schools resuming afternoon sessions
description: Wellington schools resuming afternoon sessions following morning closures. Libraries and community facilities reopening. Students returning to normal schedules.

title: Evacuation: Signal Hill area extended cordon, additional 400 residents
description: WREMO extends evacuation cordon to Signal Hill area following slope stability assessment. 400 additional residents must evacuate due to landslide risk from saturated soils.

title: Flood reported near High Street
description: Water inundation in riverside properties. Inundation ongoing. Help requested.

title: Hataitai: permanent transformer replacement scheduled for next week, temporary bypass stable
description: Replacement equipment confirmed in courier system. Temporary bypass circuits operating reliably. No further outages anticipated.

title: Karori Road: Full two-way traffic restored
description: Karori Road reopened to full two-way traffic as landslide debris removal completed. Pavement repairs ongoing in background; monitoring for secondary slip activity continues.

title: Kelburn residential area: hillside instability threatening homes
description: Stuff: monitoring underway on steep residential slopes above Kelburn. Geotechnical report identifies potential slope failure risk. Advisories issued.

title: M1.7 aftershock Porirua CBD area, expanding aftershock zone
description: GeoNet records M1.7 aftershock at 10:22 NZST near Porirua CBD. Depth 11 km. Aftershock zone expanding northward along fault. No major incidents reported in Porirua region.

title: Structural damage: Te Aro medical building
description: Health facility with visible structural cracking being evaluated by WCC engineers. Partial operational suspension announced; patients being managed.

title: Te Aro - structural specialist team documents ceiling system failures
description: Architectural structural specialist documenting suspended ceiling collapse patterns across multiple buildings in Te Aro commercial precinct. Photogrammetry and laser scanning capturing damage extent; database being compiled for insurance assessment purposes.

title: Wellington CBD long-term recovery vision planning initiated
description: CBD recovery planning team initiating long-term recovery vision development focusing on resilient rebuilding and economic revitalization.

title: Widespread liquefaction destroying infrastructure on Island Bay coast
description: Coastal erosion and subsidence affecting waterfront properties. Homes at risk; evacuation recommended.

title: Customhouse Quay commercial block - ground floor now below water level
description: Flooding assessment shows combined effect of water main rupture and ground subsidence has submerged ground-floor retail areas at Customhouse Quay. Water depth 40-50cm and rising.

title: Evacuation cordon expansion: Lambton Quay to Civic Square fully sealed
description: WREMO announces full cordon expansion from Beehive to Civic Square, then extending to Lambton Quay heritage district. All access restricted. 2000+ residents and workers in zone must evacuate.

title: Flooding continues unabated through Te Aro
description: Water flowing through Tory Street and Taranaki Street intersections creating continuous flood conditions. Residential evacuation continuing in affected blocks.

title: Liquefaction monitoring stations being installed in high-risk areas
description: Geological team setting up real-time monitoring to track ground movements.

title: M1.5 aftershock Wellington Harbour very shallow 8 km crustal stress
description: M1.5 aftershock at 10:23 NZST in Wellington Harbour at very shallow depth 8 km. Continued shallow crustal stress release. Aftershock sequence pattern normal and decay proceeding.

title: M2.6 aftershock, Wadestown-Karori
description: GeoNet network recorded magnitude 2.6 aftershock at 10:23 NZST, 8km west in Wadestown-Karori area, depth 13km. Light shaking felt.

title: Mount Victoria lookout road - access restricted

title: Oriental Bay promenade: Pedestrian pathways restored
description: Oriental Bay promenade pedestrian pathways restored after emergency barrier removal. Vehicle access remains restricted; recreational area access reopening.

title: Petone: temporary overhead lines performing reliably, permanent cable replacement scheduled
description: Residents report stable power supply. Underground cable damage repair project commencing next month. Temporary solution meeting all load requirements.

title: Structural assessment: Te Aro precinct
description: WCC conducting coordinated assessment of Te Aro buildings showing damage patterns. Multiple structural issues identified.

title: Te Aro - interim occupancy clearance issued for green-tagged office spaces
description: WCC issuing interim occupancy clearance for green-tagged office buildings in Te Aro commercial district. Limited operational resumption permitted; safety protocols and emergency procedures required.

title: Thorndon heritage building documentation for preservation initiated
description: Heritage building documentation team beginning detailed survey of Thorndon heritage buildings showing damage. Documentation will guide future restoration.

title: Building damage: Lambton area
description: Fire and Emergency responding to commercial building with collapsed floor and structural instability. Emergency shoring being arranged.

title: Customhouse Quay: Emergency access finalized
description: Customhouse Quay emergency access routes finalized with temporary barriers protecting damaged sections. Pedestrian movement controlled; vehicle access for essential services restored.

title: Emergency accommodation sought: 1000+ displaced persons requiring shelter
description: WREMO coordination center reports housing crisis emerging. Hotels full; marae and community halls mobilizing. Multiple centers now operating at capacity.

title: Emergency services: full capability restored
description: Fire and Emergency, Police, and Ambulance services now fully operational with normal power. Emergency response times returning to baseline. All emergency facilities powered.

title: Large swath of Seaview port operations halted by liquefaction
description: Wharf structures damaged beyond immediate repair. Shipping diverted; port authority assessing full extent.

title: Lower Hutt - preliminary repair cost assessment initiated for red-tagged buildings
description: Structural engineering consultant beginning damage quantification for six red-tagged buildings on Hutt Road corridor. Cost estimates being prepared for insurance claim and council assessment documentation.

title: Porirua emergency management reports M1.7 aftershock impact

title: Rongotai - ground subsidence spreading toward maintenance facilities
description: Liquefaction and subsidence zone expanding. Maintenance building now showing foundation stress cracks as ground settles nonuniformly beneath it.

title: Seaview utility infrastructure repair staging plan completed
description: Utility repair teams completing staging plan for Seaview infrastructure. Water, sewer, and electrical repairs coordinated in 5-phase schedule.

title: Te Aro final status: all blocks powered, recovery support centers closing
description: Residents returning to homes. Temporary shelters being decommissioned. Community meals ceasing as power restoration complete.

title: Alert: water pressure anomaly in Zone B (Waterfront)

title: CBD building - temporary shear wall installation completed at Willeston Street

title: Kilbirnie resident support services expanding scope

title: Lower Hutt complete recovery: all utilities reporting stable operations, evening normal

title: M3.2 aftershock, Thorndon area

title: Oriental Bay full promenade: Public access expanded

title: Power supply gradual restoration with hourly updates expected

title: SH1 northbound - Terrace Tunnel approach closed

title: Water flooding light industrial buildings
