title: Waterfront area report M1.5 aftershock, stability maintained
description: Wellington Waterfront area residents report M1.5 aftershock with continued structural stability. No new incidents or concerns reported.

title: Building damage: Rintoul Street, Newtown
description: Residential property sustaining roof damage and internal wall collapse. Occupants safely evacuated; immediate structural assessment required.

title: CBD - permanent repair specification development underway for facade panels
description: Architectural engineering firm developing permanent repair specifications for facade panel replacements at Willeston Street building. Material sourcing and contractor bidding processes commencing.

title: Fuel supply concerns: petrol stations rationing due to demand surge
description: Residents attempting to flee creating fuel demand spike. Major stations running out of unleaded. Rationing implemented; queues forming.

title: Injuries from retail building collapse in Kilbirnie mall
description: Shopping center partial structural failure. Multiple casualties; emergency services managing triage.

title: M1.6 aftershock Ngaio suburb detected
description: M1.6 aftershock at 10:26 NZST detected in Ngaio area, depth 12 km. Continued distributed seismic activity across metropolitan region. Aftershock sequence entering maturity phase.

title: M2.3 aftershock, eastern area
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 2.3 aftershock at 10:26 NZST, 9km south-southeast, depth 13km. Light shaking felt.

title: Medical facilities: all hospitals and clinics fully operational
description: Wellington, Victoria, and Hutt Valley hospitals all on mains power. Clinics and medical centers across region fully operational. Elective procedures resuming tomorrow.

title: Miramar residential area - foundation settlement causing door/window misalignment
description: Homeowner reports doors and windows throughout 1970s bungalow on The Esplanade now jammed shut. Walls show fine cracks indicating uneven settling. Structural assessment recommended.

title: Petone community information center established in CBD
description: WCC establishing community information center for Petone residents providing updates, assistance with claims, and rebuilding information.

title: Thorndon Quays: port evening operations in full swing, recovery well underway
description: Waterfront businesses serving evening patrons. Container operations at 85% capacity. Shipping schedule recovering.

title: Building damage documentation underway for insurance and recovery planning
description: Photographic surveys being conducted; damage databases being populated.

title: CBD - facade repair planning initiated with urgent procurement request issued
description: Building owner of Lambton Quay facade-damaged building initiating urgent procurement for replacement panels. Contractor quotes being solicited; temporary weatherproofing maintained pending permanent facade reconstruction.

title: Flooding surrounds emergency service access points
description: Water accumulation near multiple Fire and Emergency stations affecting vehicle dispatch routes. Flood depth 60cm blocking side street access.

title: Kilbirnie intersection - ground rupture risk
description: Multiple ground ruptures visible around Kilbirnie intersections. Hazard cones placed; traffic advised extreme caution through area.

title: Kilbirnie water network - isolation valves now sealing all water distribution
description: Wellington Water isolates entire Kilbirnie network following multiple main ruptures. Suburb now on emergency water supply via tanker trucks.

title: M1.4 aftershock Kilbirnie isthmus, sequence entering final wind-down
description: M1.4 aftershock at 10:27 NZST on Kilbirnie isthmus. GeoNet analysis confirms sequence entering final wind-down phase. Expected completion of significant aftershock phase by 14:00-15:00 NZST.

title: Properties flooded on Willis Street
description: Water inundation in Storm Street subway. Inundation ongoing. Help requested.

title: Rongotai passenger terminal damage risk mitigation plan
description: Engineering team developing risk mitigation plan for Rongotai terminal including temporary supports and progressive demolition if necessary.

title: SCADA: pressure drop in Zone C (Thorndon)
description: Automated alert from Zone C (Thorndon). System anomaly detected. Probable rupture near Jackson Street.

title: SH1 Ngauranga: Delays reducing below 10 minutes
description: SH1 Ngauranga traffic delays dropping below 10 minutes as repair works progress and traffic discipline improves. Southbound fully open; northbound operating at 80% capacity.

title: Welfare centre: Lower Hutt reaches 520 occupants, outdoor overflow planned
description: Lower Hutt welfare centre reaches 520 residents with continued arrivals. WREMO approving rapid deployment of additional large marquee tents. Medical resources fully stretched.

title: Wellington CBD status: power crisis fully resolved, business district operating normally
description: Civic area illuminated and active. Offices closing for evening with full operational success. Tomorrow returning to normal business patterns.

title: Evening update: Wellington region 100% powered, critical services confirmed stable
description: All hospitals, emergency services, water treatment, transport, and communications fully operational. Earthquake power crisis concluded.

title: Government services: resuming normal business operations
description: Wellington government offices and services resuming normal business operations. Council services, passport office, and land information systems all operational.

title: High-priority main break Buller Street
description: Water main rupture on Buller Street. tanker deployed. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Lower Hutt - community report of visible foundation cracks on residential street
description: Residents on Totara Avenue reporting increased foundation cracking and ground settlement overnight. Self-mobilized neighborhood watch has photographed damage patterns; WCC assessment scheduled for late morning.

title: M2.5 aftershock, Ngaio-Thorndon
description: Magnitude 2.5 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 10:28 NZST, 5km west-northwest near Ngaio, depth 12km. Minor seismic activity continues.

title: Miramar building reconstruction feasibility study initiated
description: Feasibility study initiated for Miramar building reconstruction assessing options for seismically-resilient replacement structures.

title: Newtown main route: Traffic normalization continuing
description: Newtown main business route traffic conditions normalizing with pavement repairs nearing completion. Temporary traffic management phased down.

title: Ngaio residents experiencing continuous minor earthquake swarm
description: Ngaio residents report M1.6 aftershock as barely perceptible tremor. Community sentiment turning toward normalcy with recognition that major seismic activity has passed peak.

title: Power out affecting Wellington Water treatment plant operations
description: Main supply interrupted; backup systems activated. Water distribution impacted across suburb.

title: Seaview industrial: second facility reports dangerous chemical spill
description: Emergency services responding to factory release. Hazmat teams deployed; 800m exclusion zone established. Residents warned away from eastern wind paths.

title: Structural assessment: Lower Hutt Queens Drive
description: WCC conducting major structural assessment of retail complex sustaining significant earthquake damage. Building access restricted pending report.

title: Welfare centre: Newtown Community Centre at 400 residents, no further intake
description: Newtown Community Centre now at maximum 400 residents capacity. No additional intake possible. New arrivals diverted to other welfare centres. Facility operating 24-hour support schedule.

title: Aftershock magnitude 3.5 centred Ngaio, minor infrastructure damage
description: GeoNet records magnitude 3.5 aftershock at 10:37 NZST centred near Ngaio. Utilities report minor line damage in western suburbs. No significant additional casualties.

title: Brooklyn Road: Full traffic capacity restored
description: Brooklyn Road fully reopened to normal traffic after final debris removal. Pavement repairs staged over coming weeks; temporary surface adequate for safe passage.

title: Gas leak: Customhouse area
description: Fire and Emergency identifying large gas main rupture in industrial waterfront area. Multi-building evacuation in progress.

title: Ghuznee Street - subsidence affecting multiple blocks
description: Liquefaction subsidence affecting multiple blocks of Ghuznee Street. Traffic manager coordinating flow; structural assessments ongoing.

title: Hospital network coordinating patient management across region
description: Surge protocols activated; overflow arrangements with private facilities arranged.

title: Kilbirnie residents preparing to transition from emergency to recovery mode
description: Kilbirnie residents report M1.4 aftershock and organized community discussion about transition to recovery phase. Damage assessments and welfare checks prioritized.

title: Main break on The Strand
description: Major water loss on The Strand. Surface rupture widening. Emergency assistance needed.

title: NZ Herald: earthquake power crisis response commended for rapid restoration success
description: Analysis of emergency management praising coordination between Wellington Electricity, WREMO, and utilities. Rapid stabilization considered exemplary.

title: Network restoration beginning at isolated sections
description: Wellington Water beginning restoration of isolated tertiary network sections. Temporary bypass installation at Civic Square rupture site progressing ahead of schedule.

title: Newtown - resident coordination meeting organized for impacted property owners
description: Community leaders organizing resident meeting for property owners impacted by structural damage. Expert engineers and insurance representatives invited to discuss assessment results.

title: Petone elevated road section - subsidence creating drop-off hazard
description: Beach Road section near Petone waterfront park shows raised road edge now suspended 20cm above settlement zone. Traffic barrier placement required urgently to prevent vehicle accidents.

title: Wellington CBD business continuity support program launched
description: WCC launching business continuity support program for affected CBD businesses including temporary space assistance and financial recovery support.

title: Financial services: trading systems fully operational
description: NZX and financial trading systems fully restored and operational. Banking institutions proceeding with normal operations. ATMs functioning across region.

title: Gas leak: Lower Hutt High Street
description: Fire and Emergency responding to strong gas odour in retail district. Businesses evacuating; utilities company on scene with detection equipment.

title: Liquefaction threatening industrial facilities in Gracefield area
description: Sand boils and subsidence affecting warehouse district. Structures shifted; contents damaged.

title: M1.5 aftershock Wellington Harbour zone, shallow 9 km
description: GeoNet detects M1.5 aftershock at 10:30 NZST in Wellington Harbour, very shallow depth 9 km. Continued shallow crustal stress release. Aftershock magnitudes now primarily sub-M2.

title: M3.1 aftershock near Te Aro
description: Magnitude 3.1 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 10:30 NZST, 6km south in Te Aro region, depth 12km. Light shaking continues.

title: Petone central district: Traffic management phased down
description: Petone central business district traffic management systems phased down as repairs progress. Near-normal operations resumed.

title: Seaview operations: port continuing evening cargo handling, full productivity resuming
description: CentrePort completing container operations. Vessel schedules recovering. Full normal operations expected by morning.

title: Seaview port - second cargo crane now showing dangerous list
description: Second major crane on Seaview port now tilting 15 degrees from vertical. Boom counterweight no longer effective at preventing topple.

title: Structural assessment: Utility infrastructure damage assessment ongoing
description: WREMO and utility companies conduct joint damage assessment of water, power and gas infrastructure. Critical pipeline damage reported in multiple areas. Temporary restrictions on water consumption issued.

title: Te Aro - portable gas detection sweep completing secondary verification of leak locations
description: Fire and Emergency completing final gas detection sweep of Courtney Place and adjacent streets using portable methane detectors. Verification confirming isolation effectiveness; safe re-entry pathways being established for residents.

title: Telecommunications update: cell network recovering but bandwidth limited
description: Vodafone and Spark report partial restoration of network capacity. Voice calls prioritized over data; video streaming disabled. Prioritization for emergency services.

title: Thorndon embankment remediation options assessment started
description: Engineering consultants beginning assessment of embankment remediation options including possible reinforcement and monitoring improvements.

title: Building damage: Victoria Street area
description: Fire and Emergency securing area around multiple commercial buildings sustaining structural damage. WCC assessment underway.

title: Flooding extends through residential blocks, Thorndon
description: Water from Quay Street ruptures backing up into residential lane ways and courtyards. Residents on ground level vacating properties. Sandbag barriers being erected.

title: Gas detection crew completes chemical smell sweep of Courtney Place district
description: Portable gas sensor teams completing neighborhood sweep of Courtney Place with handheld methane and hydrogen sulfide detectors. Three residential properties flagged for follow-up investigation; leak source isolation proceeding.

title: GeoNet publishes aftershock forecast: 4-6 hours remaining significant activity
description: GeoNet publishes updated aftershock forecast at 10:31 NZST: Aftershock sequence expected to continue 4-6 additional hours with decreasing magnitude and frequency. Final M1+ aftershock expected around 15:00-16:00 NZST.

title: Hutt Valley Highway northbound - continuing clearance
description: Northbound clearance operations continuing on Hutt Valley Highway. Primary debris pile mostly cleared; secondary site requiring 1-2 hour clearance.

title: Injury crisis: Blood bank running critically low, emergency appeal issued
description: Wellington Hospital declares blood supply crisis and issues emergency appeal for blood donations. Type O-negative supplies at 3-hour reserves. Urgent donor drive at multiple welfare centres being organized.

title: M2.2 aftershock, north region
description: Magnitude 2.2 aftershock detected by GeoNet seismographs at 10:31 NZST, 18km north, depth 12km. Minor shaking detected.

title: Rongotai Airport: evening flights operating on schedule, full terminal services available
description: Runway, ground facilities, and passenger services all operational. No power-related delays. Normal airport procedures in effect.

title: Seaview port facility master plan revision underway
description: CentrePort initiating master plan revision for port facility including seismic resilience improvements in replacement infrastructure.

title: Traffic management progress report showing some route improvements
description: Alternate route capacity increasing; emergency response times improving.

title: Water pooling on Customhouse Quay
description: Confirmed flooding on Customhouse Quay affecting Storm Street subway. Depth 20cm. water diversion active.

title: Accident involving emergency vehicle responding to call in Newtown
description: Ambulance struck by falling debris while en route. Driver treated for minor injuries; vehicle damaged.

title: Gas leak alert: Te Aro central
description: Large gas rupture detected in commercial precinct. Fire and Emergency establishing evacuation perimeter; gas company on high alert.

title: Kilbirnie liquefaction risk remediation strategies identified
description: Geotechnical consultants identifying liquefaction risk remediation strategies for Kilbirnie rebuilding including ground treatment options.

title: Kilbirnie parade commercial row - power outages cascading from pole damage
description: Photos show Kilbirnie Parade shopfronts without power following ground-induced electrical tower failures. Secondary outages spreading as demand increases on remaining network sections.

title: Liquefaction extent now mapped: HIGH-risk zones show 30cm avg settlement
description: Geotechnical assessment complete for initial damage swath. Waterfront through Miramar affected. Engineering assessments to guide recovery prioritization.

title: Lower Hutt - emergency infrastructure bypass timeline updated by Wellington City Council
description: WCC updating construction timeline for emergency utility bypass infrastructure on Station Road. Accelerated schedule targeting completion within 48-72 hour window.

title: Media infrastructure: broadcast systems back to full capacity
description: Television and radio broadcast infrastructure fully restored. Media operations centers fully powered. News reporting capability fully restored.

title: Miramar cutting: Single-lane traffic flow established
description: Miramar cutting reopened to single alternating lane traffic after final rockfall assessment clearance. Full bidirectional capacity subject to geotechnical monitoring completion.

title: Waterfront residents note minimal aftershock sensation
description: Wellington Waterfront residents report M1.5 aftershock as barely noticeable tremor. Waterfront zones remain structurally stable with no new incidents.

title: Wellington Water: evening operations fully stable, all supply zones confirmed operational
description: Treatment plants, pumping stations, and network all performing normally. Water testing confirming safety. No ongoing concerns.

title: Evacuation: Porirua CBD edge areas, precautionary extension northward
description: WREMO issues precautionary evacuation for Porirua CBD edge areas following liquefaction risk assessment. 200+ residents advised to leave as prevention measure. Evacuation assembly at Porirua primary school.

title: Kilbirnie connector route: Full service resumed
description: Kilbirnie connector routes fully operational after subsidence stabilization. Traffic flow restored to normal patterns; monitoring continues.

title: KiwiRail Metlink: evening rush commute successful, rail services operating normally
description: Northern and Hutt Valley lines at full capacity. Passenger volume normal. No power-related disruptions.

title: Liquefaction damage assessment nearing completion in first survey areas
description: Initial findings suggesting widespread but manageable damage. Detailed reports being compiled.

title: Lower Hutt - geotechnical survey identifies liquefaction-affected property subset
description: Geotechnical engineer completing soil analysis identifying 12 Hutt Road properties showing liquefaction-related ground settlement. Detailed foundation assessment required for affected properties; investigation timeline established.

title: M2.4 aftershock, CBD waterfront
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 2.4 aftershock at 10:33 NZST, 3km south-southeast near Wellington CBD waterfront, depth 11km. Light shaking felt.

title: Petone recovery timeline estimate revised to 12-month recovery
description: Revised recovery timeline for Petone infrastructure now estimated at 12 months for utilities and 18-24 months for full structural rebuilding.

title: Taranaki Street continued monitoring for movement
description: Continued monitoring of Taranaki Street subsidence showing slight ongoing movement. Measurement points placed every 50m; engineering teams on standby.

title: Thorndon Quay - street-level pooling water now blocking pedestrian routes
description: Water from main rupture combined with subsidence has created 200-metre long water barrier across Thorndon Quay. Water depth 50cm in deepest section.

title: Water line damage assessment shows 12 major breaks

title: GeoNet releases comprehensive M5+ aftershock summary bulletin
description: GeoNet publishes comprehensive aftershock analysis covering M5+ events: M5.1 offshore (08:30), M4.8 CBD (08:38), M4.5 Petone (08:42). All events consistent with Wellington Fault rupture pattern.

title: Newtown residential assessment - yellow tag issued for foundation failure property

title: Ninth measurable aftershock: magnitude 4.6 at 10:14 continues pattern
description: GeoNet confirms continuing stress release. Pattern expected to persist for hours; frequency likely to decrease gradually. Still dangerous.

title: Regional recovery: supermarkets restocked, services normal, consumer confidence returning
description: Evening shopping crowds normal. Stores fully stocked. Banking and commerce operating routinely. Economic recovery beginning.

title: Rongotai interim airport operations plan finalized
description: Wellington Airport finalizing interim operations plan for limited operations at temporary facilities pending runway reconstruction.

title: State Highway 1 arterial route severely congested by closures
description: Multiple exit ramps damaged; traffic diverted to local streets. Gridlock affecting suburbs throughout metro area.

title: Structural assessment: Bridge infrastructure damage assessment prioritized
description: WREMO prioritizes bridge infrastructure damage assessment. Motorway overpasses and pedestrian bridges examined for seismic damage. Temporary traffic restrictions imposed on affected structures.

title: Structural assessment: Whitmore Street
description: WCC team assessing 1960s-era commercial building with multiple visible cracks and differential settlement indicators.

title: Telemetry alert from Zone E (Petone)
description: Automated alert from Zone E (Petone). Flow rate increased 300%. Probable rupture near Colleen Street.

title: Trunk main failure in CBD
description: Water main rupture on Lambton Quay. supply rerouted. Estimated repair: 4-6 hours.

title: Wadestown hillslope: Access fully reopened

title: Welfare coordination: ongoing support for Island Bay residents
description: Welfare centres in Newtown and Thorndon providing ongoing support to displaced Island Bay residents. Evening meal services active. Temporary accommodation being arranged.

title: Building damage: Featherston Street retail
description: Multiple retail units damaged by structural failure. Fire and Emergency managing area and occupant welfare. WCC assessment ongoing.

title: Community centers closing: emergency support operations standing down as crisis resolved
description: Temporary shelters and aid centers ceasing operations. Residents returning to homes. Final welfare checks being completed.

title: M3.0 aftershock, Petone region
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.0 aftershock at 10:35 NZST, 14km northeast in Petone region, depth 13km. Minor seismic activity recorded.

title: Miramar community recovery fund established
description: Community recovery fund established for Miramar residents and businesses affected by liquefaction damage. Initial allocation: 2 million dollars.

title: Mount Victoria routes: Speed restrictions lifted
description: Mount Victoria area routes speed restrictions lifted as pavement stability confirmed. Full normal operations resumed.

title: Petone industrial area - access routes impacted
description: Multiple industrial access routes in Petone affected by liquefaction settlement. Business logistics disrupted; emergency routing established.

title: Power company mobile units being deployed to critical service locations
description: Temporary generator support augmenting permanent repairs. Community centers being powered.

title: SCADA: flow anomaly in Zone B (Waterfront)

title: Street flooding still blocking primary routes, CBD

description: Fire and Emergency completing final gas isolation confirmations and safety verification for Courtney Place evacuation zone. Safe re-entry authorization being prepared for affected residents.

title: Thorndon Quay buildings - cumulative offset now exceeds 40cm
description: Structural survey documents Thorndon Quay street corridor now showing 40-50cm permanent offset. Multiple buildings across zone all settling in consistent direction, suggesting large lateral spreading zone.

title: Tsunami advisory extended: Secondary wave cycle continues through 11:30
description: MetService extends tsunami advisory through 11:30 NZST. Secondary waves continuing with 0.4m amplitudes. Coastal areas remain off-limits. Next advisory update expected at 10:50 NZST.

title: Water flooding commercial properties
description: Water inundation in commercial properties. Inundation ongoing. Help requested.

title: Water pooling on Broadway
description: Confirmed flooding on Broadway affecting commercial properties. Depth 120cm. evacuation initiated.

description: Media reporting ongoing emergency operations in CBD with multiple building damage incidents. Responders overwhelmed with incident volume.

title: Fire and Emergency NZ: all emergency crews released from active response, back to normal patrol

title: Infrastructure damage assessment: permanent repairs scheduled

title: M1.4 aftershock Kilbirnie isthmus, swarm enters final phase
description: M1.4 aftershock at 10:34 NZST detected on Kilbirnie isthmus, depth 10 km. Aftershock swarm now in final decay phase. Expected continuation of 30+ M1+ events per hour declining gradually.

title: M2.1 aftershock, outer zone
description: GeoNet network detected magnitude 2.1 aftershock at 10:36 NZST, 20km north-northeast, depth 11km. Light shaking in outer areas.

description: Fire and Emergency NZ deploying additional search and rescue teams to three collapse sites. Missing persons count at 12. Search operations expected to continue 24+ hours.

description: Follow-up structural engineer assessment confirming temporary monitoring system functionality on yellow-tagged properties. Structural stability confirmed within safe parameters; continued monitoring scheduled for progressive assessment.

description: Older construction showing major cracks and settling. Engineer access denied; structural assessment pending.

title: Te Aro block face - corner building now requires emergency propping
description: Corner building at Vivian Street intersection shows dangerous outward lean of facade. WCC emergency crew installing lateral supports to prevent collapse.

title: Welfare centre: Karori facility at maximum capacity, new arrivals redirected
description: Karori welfare centre reaches maximum 820 capacity. New arrivals being redirected to Kelburn and Newtown facilities. Facility logistics team managing smooth redirection process.

description: CBD reconstruction planning team initiating development of comprehensive structural resilience framework for future reconstruction.

title: Boil water notices issued for affected zones

title: Boundary Road Hill slip - containment ongoing
description: Boundary Road hillside slip material continues to require containment. Approximately 35 cubic metres staged for removal; one lane passable.

title: CBD heritage building - lead paint remediation protocol implemented during repairs
description: Customhouse Quay heritage building repair operations implementing lead-safe work practices during facade remediation. Environmental health consultant overseeing containment protocols; dust suppression and lead-safe demolition procedures being enforced.

title: Defence Force: military facilities fully operational, normal security posture resumed
description: Naval and air force operations at full capacity. Emergency protocols stood down. Miramar and other sites returning to routine.

title: Island Bay access: Coastal route fully operational
description: Island Bay coastal access route fully reopened after engineering assessment confirms limited permanent structural damage. Temporary speed restrictions will remain 48+ hours.

title: Structural assessment: CBD precinct
description: WCC engineers conducting systematic assessment of CBD area. Multiple buildings requiring temporary occupancy restrictions.

title: Structural engineers providing guidance to building owners on damage mitigation
description: Technical advice being offered to prevent further deterioration. Stabilization measures underway.

title: Thorndon heritage preservation strategy finalized
description: Heritage preservation strategy finalized for Thorndon focusing on restoration of significant buildings and preservation of historic character.

title: Welfare centre: Karori total occupancy 820 residents in indoor plus tents
description: Karori Community Hall complex now sheltering 820 total residents - 520 indoors and 300 in marquee tents. Medical bay handling minor injuries and health issues. Catering operation expanded.

title: Building damage: Manners Street retail
description: Multi-unit retail building with shattered shopfronts and interior damage. Fire and Emergency securing area; no initial reports of trapped persons.

title: Evening operations: city returning to normal after power restoration
description: Wellington city gradually returning to evening normal as power now fully restored. Streetlights bright, shops illuminated, restaurants and bars reopening for evening service.

title: Hospital networks: all medical facilities confirmed stable, patient care normal operations
description: Wellington Regional Medical Center and district health services all operational. Evening procedures continuing as scheduled.

title: Kilbirnie residents report series of barely felt aftershocks
description: Kilbirnie residents report M1.4 aftershock as barely perceptible. Residents indicate psychological comfort with magnitude reduction and slower frequency of noticeable events.

title: Liquefaction and ground failure affecting Porirua waterfront buildings
description: Businesses and homes at sea-level affected by subsidence. Residents evacuated; structural damage ongoing.

title: M2.3 aftershock, Te Aro area
description: Magnitude 2.3 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 10:38 NZST, 6km south in Te Aro region, depth 12km. Minor seismic activity detected.

title: Newtown - property damage timeline documentation project nearing completion
description: Community property damage documentation project nearing completion with data compilation underway. Project compiling 134 documented properties with damage photographs and descriptions.

title: Public transport halted: buses, trains, and ferries suspended indefinitely
description: Wellington public transit system offline. Ferry services cancelled due to port damage and aftershock risk. Bus services restricted to essential emergency routes only.

title: Regional routes: Traffic volumes stabilizing
description: Regional traffic volumes stabilizing as major closures reopen. Transit times approaching pre-earthquake norms; emergency protocols winding down.

title: Seaview port recovery funding applications submitted
description: CentrePort submitting recovery funding applications to national disaster relief programs. Seeking 50+ million in support for facility reconstruction.

title: Seaview waterfront storage facility - large tank foundation subsiding
description: Field inspection confirms 60cm localized subsidence beneath industrial storage tank on Gracefield Road. Tank tilting 5 degrees, structural integrity of tank walls at risk.

title: CBD - heritage conservation consultant engaged for facade restoration planning
description: Customhouse Quay heritage building owner engaging specialist conservation architect for facade repair planning. Heritage-sensitive repair methodology being documented; restoration specifications aligning with historic preservation standards.

title: Flooding receding from Petone retail district
description: Water level on Jackson Street beginning to recede. Drainage systems now able to cope with remaining flow. Retail precinct damage assessment beginning.

title: Kilbirnie residential recovery program detailed framework released
description: WCC releasing detailed framework for Kilbirnie residential recovery program including housing assistance and community rebuilding initiatives.

title: Medical system managing injury surge effectively with coordinated response
description: Patient flow optimized; waiting times manageable. No critical shortages reported yet.

title: Regional final status: Wellington power grid fully recovered, resilience monitoring ongoing
description: All 100% of region powered. Secondary infrastructure damage assessment continuing. Permanent repairs scheduled for coming weeks.

title: SH2 Hutt: Bridge reopened to full capacity
description: State Highway 2 Hutt bridge cleared for full vehicle capacity after structural assessment completion. Monitoring will continue for settling; speed restrictions lifted.

title: Stout Street - liquefaction and ground cracks
description: Multiple ground cracks visible on Stout Street between Lambton Quay and Featherston Street. One lane blocked; traffic management maintaining flow.

title: Structural assessment ongoing: 54 buildings now requiring urgent evaluation
description: WREMO structural team assessment continues with 54 buildings now under urgent evaluation. Priority given to multi-storey residential and commercial buildings. Turnaround time 4-6 hours per building.

title: Structural assessment: Hunter Street
description: WCC engineers assessing multiple buildings in central district with cracking and alignment issues. Staged occupancy restrictions being implemented.

title: Water level alert: Zone C (Thorndon)
description: Automated alert from Zone C (Thorndon). Saturation alarm triggered. Flood conditions near Hutt Road.

title: Critical power infrastructure failure in Lower Hutt impacting hospitals
description: Main distribution lines down; medical facilities relying entirely on emergency generators.

title: Earthquake sequence now producing mainly sub-magnitude 2 events
description: GeoNet analysis at 10:38 NZST confirms aftershock sequence has transitioned to predominantly sub-M2 activity. Aftershock frequency now approximately 25-35 M1+ events per hour.

title: Evening summary: earthquake response transition from emergency to recovery and resilience
description: Crisis power management concluded successfully. Focus shifting to damage repairs, infrastructure reinforcement, and community recovery.

title: Final status update: power emergency concluded
description: Wellington Electricity issues final status update: 99.7% of region has power restoration complete. Island Bay on temporary generation; all other infrastructure stable and normal.

title: Inner-city parking: Restrictions being lifted
description: Inner-city parking area restrictions being lifted as emergency zones contract. Phased return to normal parking operations underway.

title: M2.9 aftershock recorded, Lower Hutt
description: Magnitude 2.9 aftershock detected by GeoNet seismographs at 10:40 NZST, 19km northeast in Lower Hutt CBD area, depth 12km. Minor shaking felt.

title: Petone rebuild vision document development underway
description: Petone community and council developing shared rebuild vision document emphasizing community resilience and economic recovery.

title: Te Aro - roofing membrane damage survey results tabulated for insurance

title: Tenth aftershock recorded: magnitude 4.5 as stress release continues

title: CBD Jervois Quay - office building entrance now 25cm below adjacent street

title: Injury: Cumulative 135 hospitalized with 55 in critical care, five deaths
description: Wellington Hospital reports 135 hospitalized with 55 in critical care units. Five fatalities confirmed. Multiple patients deteriorating from crush syndrome complications. Ventilator availability critical issue.

title: RNZ final briefing: earthquake power crisis successfully managed, critical services protected
description: National broadcast summarizing emergency response success. Praise for utility coordination and emergency management. Recovery phase details shared.

title: Rongotai airport recovery partnership agreements signed
description: Wellington Airport signing recovery partnership agreements with national authorities for financial and technical support in reconstruction.

title: Rongotai near airport terminal approach - damage assessment
description: Terminal approach road undergoing structural damage assessment. Cracks and subsidence documented; temporary traffic management in place.

title: Temporary bypass completing at critical rupture site

title: Transport network recovery gaining momentum with incremental route reopenings
description: Several secondary roads reopened; major routes still restricted. Commuter conditions gradually improving.

title: Aftershock sequence summary: 5+ hours of continuous activity
description: Wellington Regional Emergency Management updates: M6.5 mainshock followed by 60+ earthquakes M2 and above. Largest aftershocks M5.1, M4.8, M4.5. Sequence magnitude distribution normal. No additional major aftershocks expected but continued activity likely through afternoon.

title: Building damage confirmed at Customhouse Quay
description: Multiple window breakage, structural cracks visible Customhouse Quay.

title: CBD - structural emergency response summary report being prepared for council
description: Fire and Emergency preparing comprehensive structural emergency response summary documenting all emergency operations and assessments conducted. Report covering 114 inspections and 52 priority interventions.

title: Close of escalation phase: Wellington power restoration complete, system stable by 10:42
description: End of peak escalation period. All major infrastructure restored. Evening operations proceeding normally across entire region.

title: Gas leak: Ghuznee Street warehouse district
description: Large commercial warehouse with detected gas leak. Fire and Emergency cordoning multi-block area; evacuation of industrial precinct underway.

title: M2.0 aftershock recorded, final peak signal
description: Magnitude 2.0 aftershock recorded by GeoNet seismographs at 10:42 NZST, 22km north in Porirua area, depth 12km. Minor seismic activity continues diminishing.

title: Miramar long-term recovery implementation plan adopted
description: WCC adopting comprehensive long-term recovery implementation plan for Miramar focusing on sustainable rebuilding and community cohesion.

title: Post-emergency phase: recovery operations transition to normal schedule
description: Wellington power emergency officially transitioned to recovery phase. Grid operating normally. Recovery of infrastructure damage and displaced residents now primary focus.

title: Power outage: Wellington CBD, 12,000+ customers without electricity
description: Wellington Electricity reports major outage affecting Lambton Quay and surrounding CBD. Transformers offline due to liquefaction damage at Waterfront substation.

title: Six-hour checkpoint: emergency response ramping up; recovery planning begins
description: WREMO coordinates regional response. Military mobilized. Central government declares state of emergency. Local and national coordination centers activated.

title: Summary of widespread injuries across earthquake zone at 2.5-hour mark
description: Estimates suggest 40+ injured across metropolitan area; rescue operations ongoing at multiple sites.

title: WREMO coordinated emergency conference: Regional response activation
description: WREMO convenes emergency conference with Wellington City Council, Hutt Valley, Police, Fire and Emergency NZ, and Health authorities. Regional mutual aid agreement activated. 72-hour response plan finalized.

title: Wellington Station footbridge collapse alert; platforms closed for assessment
description: Immediate closure of pedestrian access after structural failure; emergency egress via main concourse only. Engineering team on-site for safety review.

title: Wellington transport network: Recovery phase underway
description: Major road network restoration reaches near-normal operations with only localized restrictions remaining. Recovery assessment continues on secondary infrastructure; emergency access protocols lifting.

title: Aftershock M5.6 detected Wellington Fault region
description: GeoNet seismograph network detected magnitude 5.6 aftershock approximately 8km northeast of the initial epicentre at Thorndon.

title: Building damage confirmed at Featherston Street
description: Facade partially collapsed, debris on street Featherston Street.

title: CentrePort wharf subsidence measurements; Shed 1 entry restricted
description: Ground survey identifies up to 25 cm of settlement at main cargo handling area. Structural safety barrier erected; equipment temporarily shut down.

title: Ground collapse and sand eruption on Oriental Bay beachfront
description: Large fissures opening up along the waterfront with liquefaction sand and water spurting 2-3 metres high. Multiple beachfront properties subsiding.

title: Liquefaction subsidence reported on Cuba Street CBD
description: Community photo showing ground settlement 15-20cm on Cuba Street between Willis and Customhouse Quay. Building foundation alignment visibly affected.

title: RNZ live updates: Widespread power loss across Wellington CBD and Waterfront
description: RNZ reports that approximately 85% of the CBD is without power following the 6.5 earthquake. Wellington Electricity crews are mobilizing to assess damage to substations near Quay Street and the Civic Centre. Power restoration may take several hours.

title: Taranaki Street CBD: minor subsidence causing slight pavement irregularity
description: Settlement of approximately 5cm across Taranaki Street. Traffic flow unimpeded but flagging crew monitoring.

title: Tsunami advisory issued for Tasman Sea coast
description: MetService issues tsunami advisory for Wellington coastal areas following magnitude 6.5 earthquake. Residents advised to move to higher ground; beaches and waterfront areas to be evacuated within 30 minutes.

title: Building Damage: Multiple storey collapse, Customhouse Quay
description: Fire and Emergency response at Customhouse Quay reports partial collapse of 1960s office building. Structural integrity compromised; immediate evacuation ongoing. Estimated 40-50 occupants may remain in building.

title: First water main break reports emerging from ground surveys
description: Liquefaction damage assessment teams identifying underground infrastructure damage. Preliminary count suggests 10+ breaks in reclaimed land areas.

title: Hutt Valley Line inspection underway, platform 1-2 closed
description: KiwiRail engineering team assessing track damage north of Wellington Station. Inspection of rail bed subsidence expected to take 2-4 hours.

title: Liquefaction sand ejecta Petone Beach frontage
description: Community report of sand boils and water spurts along Petone esplanade near Woburn Reserve. Surface cracking visible in parking area.

title: Major water main rupture confirmed Lambton Quay
description: Wellington Water teams report 450mm transmission main burst beneath Lambton Quay causing surface flooding and street subsidence. Liquefaction-induced shear stress identified as cause.

title: Multiple sand boils visible in Waterfront carpark near Lagoon
description: Resident reports circular sand eruptions across 40m area in the Railway Station carpark. Water and fine sediment fountaining from ground.

title: Petone warehouse collapse affecting port access
description: Two large industrial buildings near SH2 have partially collapsed. Access to the port area is severely restricted. Rescue teams unable to enter the zone.

title: Power restored to Oriental Bay; 3,200 customers back online
description: Wellington Electricity switchboard manual bypass enabled power restoration to Oriental Bay residential area. Restoration crew working on parallel feeder lines.

title: SH1 Ngauranga Gorge northbound closed due to rockfall debris
description: NZTA crews report 30-40cm rock fragments blocking both lanes near the 4km marker. Diversions via SH2 in effect.

title: SH1 Ngauranga Gorge northbound: debris blocking lanes near tunnel approach
description: NZTA traffic cameras detect rockfall and scattered concrete debris across 2 northbound lanes approaching the northern tunnel entrance. Lane 1 passable at reduced speed.

title: Water pressure drop detected CBD network, GeoNet SCADA monitoring
description: Wellington Water SCADA system showing pressure cascade failure across central business district distribution network. Multiple simultaneous breaks suspected in post-quake liquefaction zones.

title: Wellington CBD power restoration begins, multiple substations offline
description: Wellington Electricity crews assess damage at Lambton substation after significant seismic damage. Initial repair estimates suggest 4-6 hours for partial restoration to the CBD and Thorndon areas.

title: Building damage confirmed at Cable Street
description: Facade partially collapsed, debris on street Cable Street.

title: Evacuation order issued for Petone waterfront precinct
description: WREMO orders immediate evacuation of low-lying Petone waterfront between Queens Street and the harbour following liquefaction damage and tsunami risk. Residents moving to higher ground via Jackson Street.

title: Extensive liquefaction damage reported Te Aro precinct
description: Wellington City Council emergency response team confirms widespread ground settlement and sand boils along Cuba Street and surrounding lanes. Approximately 47 buildings showing structural tilting.

title: M5.3 aftershock, Lambton Harbour
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 5.3 aftershock at 10:45 NZST, depth 8km. Strong renewed shaking across Wellington CBD caused falling debris and triggered additional damage.

title: Power still out across Thorndon and Quays
description: No electricity in the Thorndon and Quays area. Multiple transformers down. Traffic lights all dark, making intersections extremely dangerous.

title: SH1 Ngauranga Gorge loose rock; geotechnical engineer requested urgently
description: NZTA traffic camera captures fresh rock fall on northbound lane. Emergency response mobilised; rockfall protection mesh assessment prioritised.

title: Stuff: Evacuation order issued for central Thorndon, 2,000+ residents affected
description: Wellington City Council has ordered evacuation of Thorndon as a precautionary measure due to liquefaction risk and unstable building facades. Emergency services are directing residents toward the Civic Sq welfare centre on foot.

title: Water pressure anomaly detected Quays distribution node cascade failure
description: Wellington Water SCADA detecting unusual pressure fluctuation patterns at Quays distribution node. Cascade failure mode suspected across interconnected trunk main sections. Engineering team mobilizing for field investigation.

title: Wellington Water SCADA alert: pressure drop Oriental Bay feeder main
description: Automated sensor detection of rapid pressure loss in water pipeline serving Oriental Bay. Estimated flow loss 15,000 litres/hour.

title: CentrePort operations suspended; container yard assessment begins
description: All cargo movement halted pending structural assessment of wharf cranes and berths. Initial drone survey shows subsidence of 15-20 cm near Shed 3.

title: Lower Hutt CBD: 8,500 customers in prolonged blackout
description: Wellington Electricity confirms Gear Merc substation damaged by ground subsidence. No ETR available for Lower Hutt CBD restoration; isolation required for safety.

title: SH1 southbound Ngauranga: emergency lane closure, debris removal crew dispatched
description: Multiple sections of southbound carriageway report cracked asphalt and fallen road furniture. NZTA dispatching heavy recovery to Ngauranga entrance; southbound traffic diverted via local roads.

title: Severe ground deformation in Kilbirnie industrial zone
description: Massive subsidence creating trenches across the isthmus. Multiple vehicles stuck in cracked road surfaces. Residents evacuating nearby properties.

title: Strong aftershock M5.1, Lambton
description: GeoNet confirmed magnitude 5.1 aftershock at 10:46 NZST, depth 7km, epicentre Lambton Harbour area. Triggered structural alarm systems across CBD.

title: Tawa area: small landslip on residential access road to Tawa School
description: Embankment failure on school access lane creating hazard. Parents advised to use alternate pickup routes.

title: Water main rupture Wellington CBD - Lambton Quay intersection
description: WCC Infrastructure team detected major water main failure at Lambton Quay and Customhouse Quay. Water geyser 2m high, flooding ground floor retail and creating surface pond.

title: Brooklyn Hill slip blocks Talavera Terrace at Weir Street junction
description: Resident reports loose soil and vegetation from hillside covering the road surface. WCC Civil Defence assessing stability.

title: Building damage confirmed at Willis Street
description: Multiple window breakage, structural cracks visible Willis Street.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 5.1 aftershock strikes 9 km east of Wellington CBD
description: GeoNet seismographs detected a significant 5.1 magnitude aftershock at 10:54 NZST, centred near Rongotai. No additional tsunami warnings, but further damage to compromised structures is likely.

title: Hutt Line track bed subsidence; engineering party despatched to Waterloo
description: GeoNet seismic data correlates with track geometry. Inspection crew arriving 10:20; initial assessment expected by midday.

title: Major water main rupture Lambton Quay, contractor access blocked
description: Six-metre length of 300mm trunk main burst beneath Lambton Quay. Repair crew unable to access site due to subsided pavement and ongoing aftershocks. Estimated 80,000 litres/hour loss.

title: Petone foreshore: significant ground deformation and pooled water
description: Photos show 30cm differential settlement along railway corridor. Stormwater culvert exposed and misaligned. Standing water forming in depression zone.

title: Petone industrial area without power; port operations halted
description: CentrePort and surrounding businesses remain disconnected from power grid. Two transmission lines serving the area confirmed damaged; crews dispatching to Gracefield switching station.

title: Power restoration halted—cable damage at waterfront
description: Wellington Electricity reports underground HV cable severed between Quay and Customhouse Quay due to liquefaction-induced ground movement. Estimated 12,000 customers without power; repairs may take 6+ hours.

title: Rongotai Airport terminal building shows major structural damage
description: Large cracks visible through the terminal facade. Concrete panels have fallen. Operations curtailed indefinitely. Airport access roads severely damaged.

title: Seaview industrial area evacuation underway
description: Fire and Emergency NZ coordinating evacuation of Seaview and Gracefield industrial precinct. Liquefaction and potential gas risks detected; workers and residents from 50+ businesses being moved to assembly points at Kelburn Park.

title: Structural Assessment: Lambton Quay commercial precinct
description: WCC structural engineers beginning rapid assessment of 8-storey commercial building. Facade cracking visible on multiple levels; interior walls showing significant distress. Building flagged as potentially unsafe for re-entry.

title: Water fountaining from Courtney Place rupture
description: Eyewitness reports water shooting 5+ metres high from burst main. Area quickly filling with debris-laden water. Utilities crews notified.

title: Airport runway 16/34 closed for crack assessment
description: Rongotai aerodrome runway inspection identifies longitudinal cracking near midpoint. Scheduled for immediate repair assessment; diversions to Auckland advised.

title: Building facade collapse Thorndon warehouse complex
description: Warehouse on Hutt Road showing severe structural failure with partial brick collapse onto street. No immediate casualties reported. Area cordoned by emergency services.

title: Flooding reported Quay Street commercial precinct
description: Water cascading from multiple broken mains pooling in sunken loading bays. Businesses evacuated. Sump pumps overwhelmed, estimated 500,000 litres per hour flowing.

title: Gas Leak: Apartment foyer detection, Willis Street
description: Apartment building foyer showing gas odour; detection instruments confirm leak presence. Building residents evacuated from lower levels; upper floors monitoring ongoing.

title: Multiple injuries reported at Wellington Hospital from collapsed homes
description: Hospital staff treating crush injuries and fractures from building collapses. Emergency department overwhelmed with incoming patients.

title: Petone outage worsens: 5,600 residents without power
description: Resident reports widespread darkness from Jackson Street through to Hutt Road. Gas smell detected near Petone primary school; power cut precautionary.

title: Seaview industrial zone: building settlement cracks in warehouse row
description: Photos from factory worker showing 50-80mm vertical cracks in three adjacent warehouse walls along SH2. Concrete floors visibly tilted.

title: State Highway 2 Hutt Valley: subsidence crack at Seaview industrial overpass
description: SH2 southbound lane 1 reported impassable due to 1.2m horizontal offset of bridge abutment near Seaview/Gracefield junction. Heavy vehicle restrictions in place immediately.

title: Taita switching station damage assessment; control circuits compromised
description: Wellington Electricity maintenance team identifies seismic damage to control wiring in Taita switching station. Requires 45-minute repairs before load transfer possible.

title: Airport runway 16/34 crack documentation; containment measure activated
description: Wellington Airport Authority photo records longitudinal fracture near centreline. Runway cones deployed; emergency closure protocols initialised.

title: Damage reported at The Terrace
description: Broken glass and stone cladding failure The Terrace.

title: Herald: Multiple building facades collapse on Lambton Quay, no immediate deaths
description: NZ Herald reporters on scene at Lambton Quay confirm that two heritage buildings on the eastern side have suffered significant facade collapse. Fire and Emergency staff are searching the area; streets below are cordoned off.

title: Kilbirnie shops subsidence: storefronts tilted, door frames jammed
description: Multiple businesses report 15-20cm settlement along Evans Bay Parade. Shop windows cracked. Staff unable to open fire exits and roller doors.

title: Massive sinkhole opens Kilbirnie industrial estate
description: Community report: a sinkhole approximately 6 metres wide and 4 metres deep has opened at the intersection of Main Road and Mahina Crescent, swallowing part of a delivery truck. Driver extracted safely.

title: Oriental Bay cordon established - tsunami risk
description: Wellington City Council establishes perimeter cordon around Oriental Bay beach and promenade. Road closures on Freyberg Street; residents in 100m buffer zone to vacate immediately due to tsunami inundation risk.

title: State Highway 2 Hutt Valley: slip debris partially blocking southbound lane
description: Small landslide near State Highway 2 between Taita and Woburn impacting a single lane. Traffic controller deployed.

title: Strong aftershock M5.2 near Te Aro
description: Magnitude 5.2 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 10:49 NZST, epicentre near Te Aro, depth 7km. Secondary ground motion caused building alarm systems to activate across the city.

title: Te Aro neighborhood power slowly returning in patches
description: Resident reports Lambton Quay and surrounding streets showing lights on in some buildings. Local transformer reconfiguration underway by Wellington Electricity to balance load.

title: Water pooling at Civic Square, CBD pedestrian area evacuated
description: Rapid flooding of Civic Square civic plaza from subsurface water main break. Water depth 30cm and rising. Civil Defence evacuating area and establishing perimeter. Public advised to avoid Wakefield Street vicinity.

title: Gas Leak: Residential building, Willis Street
description: Strong gas odour reported by residents at Willis Street apartment block. Fire and Emergency HAZMAT team dispatched to assess. Ventilation system damage suspected from shaking.

title: M5.2 aftershock near Petone
description: Magnitude 5.2 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 10:50 NZST, depth 8km. Petone reclaimed areas experienced renewed liquefaction concerns.

title: Miramar Avenue cutting: major landslide blocks southbound traffic, 40m wide
description: Unstable bank on Miramar cutting (south of Airport Terrace) has slumped, covering Miramar Avenue southbound with approximately 800 cubic metres of soft fill and clay. Local bypass via Rongotai Road advised.

title: Multiple break reports flooding municipal carpark underground level
description: Multiple water main break reports coming in affecting municipal carpark underground levels. Water ingress into Level B2 parking creating hazardous conditions for vehicles. Evacuation of carpark proceeding.

title: Ngaio Hill area: minor rock debris on Ngaio Road doesn't block traffic
description: Small rocks from hillside on verge. No lane closure required but NZTA crew standing by.

title: Quay-side restaurant flooded from pipe fracture
description: 300-seat establishment with basement wine storage completely inundated. Water entering building at 30,000+ litres per hour.

title: SH2 Hutt Motorway northbound, multiple sinkholes between Petone and Seaview
description: NZTA responding to three liquefaction-related subsidence pits on northbound lane. Lane closures in place; all traffic diverted to southbound with care.

title: Seaview industrial area ground subsidence detected
description: NZTA traffic camera and on-site crew confirm sudden ground drop (est. 0.5m) near industrial facilities on Southern Valley Road. Liquefaction and subsidence ongoing.

title: Te Aro liquefaction causing widespread street flooding
description: Water from ruptured mains mixing with liquefaction discharge creating pools across the suburb. Silt covering driveways and gardens.

title: Thorndon/Quays power system collapsed; 6,800 customers offline
description: Wellington Electricity: multiple cable fractures in Thorndon reclaimed area. Cable route via Wellington Waterfront inoperable. Secondary circuits saturated.

title: Aftershock M5.2 centred near Petone—strong shaking reported
description: GeoNet detects magnitude 5.2 aftershock approximately 5km offshore near Petone. Residents report movement of household items and cracking sounds in buildings.

title: Community report: Liquefaction and ground subsidence visible in Te Aro Park
description: Resident, Te Aro reports severe ground displacement and sand boils emerging across Te Aro Park near the playground. Several fence posts have tilted 30 degrees; nearby carpark surface is fractured.

title: Damage reported at Grey Street
description: Interior damage reported, walls cracked Grey Street.

title: Karori welfare centre operational - accepts residents
description: Karori Primary School opened as emergency welfare centre at 10:11 NZST. Capacity 400 persons. First wave of evacuees arriving; catering by Civil Defence volunteers, medical staff on-site. Registration desk active.

title: Lower Hutt CBD remains dark; main distribution feeder offline
description: Wellington Electricity field teams report Hutt Central feeder damaged between Gracefield and Taita. Estimated 2-3 hours for bypass line activation. Approx. 8,500 customers affected.

title: Major power line damage cutting supplies to lower suburbs
description: Overhead lines sagging and broken between Wellington and Hutt Valley. Entire lower suburbs without power. Estimated 18 hours for restoration.

title: Rongotai reclaimed land: subsidence causing surface flooding near depot
description: Photo shows 25-30cm water pooling across runway access road. Liquefaction-induced settlement creating localised low zones. No immediate drainage relief.

title: SCADA alert: Kaiwharawhara treatment plant intake pressure critical
description: Sensor network detects 65% pressure drop on main feed lines. Multiple valve actuators triggered emergency isolation. Water supply to eastern suburbs at risk.

title: SH2 Petone junction: sinkhole opens in northbound lane; immediate closure
description: Traffic camera detects 80 cm diameter subsidence pit. All northbound traffic diverted to southbound with alternating signals.

title: Structural Assessment: Multi-storey building tilting concern
description: WCC engineers identify potential building tilting issue in 5-storey commercial structure. Facade alignment measurements indicate subsidence. Specialist foundation assessment ordered.

title: Building damage confirmed at Molesworth Street
description: Exterior wall bowing, structural concern Molesworth Street.

title: Karori cutting (SH2): earth slip threatens northbound lane stability
description: Hillside instability observed above northbound SH2 near Karori roundabout; fencing damaged and minor cracking detected in bitumen. Monitoring equipment being installed; traffic volume reduced.

title: Kilbirnie isthmus flooding - water overtopping road barriers
description: Community reports indicate water from liquefaction-damaged ground pooling across low-lying sections of Chilton Street. Traffic severely impeded.

title: Metlink bus services suspended; depot damage at Miramar
description: Miramar bus depot sustained roof collapse and fuel line rupture. No buses departing from this hub; services rerouted via CBD depot.

title: Miramar cutting subsidence causing left lane closure on Miramar Avenue
description: Pothole approximately 60cm wide opening on Miramar Avenue near the airport approach. NZTA monitoring for expansion.

title: Pipe burst reported Cuba Street, flooding shops and basement carpark
description: Community reports 150mm water main break under Cuba Street between Manners Street and Courtney Place. Water entering basement level of Kingsgate shopping complex. Retail businesses closing as flood control measures begin.

title: Regional grid outages spike: 34,700 customers affected
description: Grid telemetry shows cascading failures across Greater Wellington distribution network. Automated load-shedding activated to prevent full system collapse.

title: State Highway 2 Seaview section impassable due to subsidence
description: Major road drop of 60cm creating a cliff across northbound lanes. All traffic diverted. Heavy vehicles blocked from route.

title: WCC initial assessment: liquefaction subsidence Jervois Quay precinct
description: Structural engineer team on-site confirms 12-18cm differential settlement affecting building foundations. Recommends immediate evacuation of 4 office buildings.

title: Building Damage: Cafe facade failure, Cuba Street
description: Shop front glass and signage collapsed onto street at Cuba Street cafe. No pedestrians in impact zone at time of failure. Rubble creating navigable hazard.

title: Lower Hutt CBD cordoned - building collapse risk
description: NZTA and Hutt City Council cordon off State Highway 2 through Lower Hutt CBD. Structural engineers assess damaged corner building at Main Street; crane standby for possible material debris removal.

title: M5.1 aftershock in Wellington Fault zone
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 5.1 aftershock at 10:53 NZST, depth 9km, along Wellington Fault. Felt strongly in CBD, Thorndon, and surrounding suburbs.

title: Metlink: Miramar depot fuel system rupture; hazmat team responding
description: Roof collapse damaged underground fuel lines. Containment berm deployed; fuel recovery and environmental assessment underway.

title: Miramar neighbourhood cut off by road fissures
description: Multiple large cracks running across local streets. Residents stranded on both sides of fissures. Walking access difficult and dangerous.

title: Miramar peninsula liquefaction zone expanding: tilted trees and sunken paths
description: Resident reports observation of 20-30 trees permanently tilted at 15-25 degree angles across parkland. Walking tracks subsided 20cm with cracking ground.

title: Newshub: Shelley Bay Road closed due to slippage; Miramar residents isolated
description: Newshub confirms that Shelley Bay Road has been cut by a small landslip, leaving Miramar residents unable to exit toward the CBD. NZTA is working to clear debris; estimated reopening 14:00 NZST.

title: Pipe burst Te Aro, water flooding Willis Street
description: Emergency crew responding to reports of 300mm diameter main fracture at Willis Street/Vivian Street intersection. Fountain-like discharge reaching 4m height.

title: Porirua CBD approach: subsidence on State Highway 1 northbound on-ramp
description: Settlement visible on merge lane to northbound. Single-lane traffic control implemented.

title: Thorndon Quays grid telemetry shows partial substation recovery
description: GeoNet telemetry indicates two of three transformer banks at Quay Street substation coming online. Power restoration to waterfront precinct may start within 30 minutes.
source: GeoNet seismograph network / Wellington Electricity SCADA

title: Tsunami advisory issued—minor sea level rise expected
description: MetService and GeoNet issue precautionary tsunami advisory for Wellington Harbour. Wave height +0.5m to +1.0m possible. Coastal residents advised to move away from shoreline.

title: Brooklyn slips: multiple failures on Beachaven Road hillside, trees blocking traffic
description: Three separate shallow slips reported on steeper sections of Beachaven Road between Brooklyn and Hataitai; fallen native bush blocking eastbound flow. Emergency services clearing debris, road width reduced to single lane.

title: Building Damage: Retail outlet structural instability
description: Large retail outlet showing visible interior cracking and column damage. Stock shelving collapsed; structural support systems compromised. Building immediately vacated.

title: Building damage survey underway - 40+ properties condemned
description: Structural engineers assessing 150+ damaged buildings in CBD. Red stickers appearing on severely damaged structures. Residents being evacuated.

title: Damage reported at Customhouse Quay
description: Interior damage reported, walls cracked Customhouse Quay.

title: Kilbirnie airport district without power; runway lighting offline
description: Resident reports complete blackout from Rongotai to Kilbirnie. Wellington Airport runway lights not visible; unknown if backup systems functioning.

title: Liquefaction sink hole swallowing water from broken main
description: Large depression in ground acting as collection point for water from fractured service line. Estimated 20m x 15m subsidence area.

title: M4.9 aftershock, central Wellington
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 4.9 aftershock at 10:54 NZST, depth 7km. Continued strong shaking in CBD heightened emergency response concerns.

title: Regional transmission grid partially reenergized; secondary outages reported
description: Wellington Electricity reports two secondary transformer stations went offline as main transmission came back online. Voltage surge detected and isolated. Safety protocols activated.

title: Rongotai sand boils damage to Airport Drive access
description: Multiple sand ejecta mounds observed on Airport Drive near Moa Point intersection. Surface cracking and subsidence creating hazard to vehicle traffic.

title: Water supply loss imminent, hand-pump stations deployed Thorndon
description: Wellington Water emergency team deploying portable water tankers and hand-pump distribution points across Thorndon suburb. Mains pressure expected to drop below usable levels within 30 minutes.

title: Wellington Station footbridge partially collapsed; platforms inaccessible
description: Pedestrian bridge over tracks at platform level has failed. Station building structurally sound but access severely restricted pending engineering review.

title: CentrePort crane 5: slew ring bearing catastrophic failure; unit out of service
description: Visual inspection confirms mechanical damage; hoisting operations immediately suspended for this crane. Remaining four cranes operational but fatigued.

title: Injury clusters in Newtown from building collapse zone
description: Local school building partially collapsed. Multiple children and staff transported to hospitals. Current casualty count unknown.

title: Karori road washout: Karori Road above Northland Crescent impassable
description: Liquefaction-induced ground settlement creating 20cm drop across roadway. Local traffic diverted via Victoria Street.

title: Rongotai isthmus - evacuation corridor established
description: Residents from Rongotai and southern airport approach roads being evacuated via designated northbound route on O'Regan Road. Liquefaction and road surface deterioration reported; traffic management by Fire and Emergency.

title: Thorndon Quay closed due to ground deformation
description: NZTA Waka Kotahi seals Thorndon Quay from Kelburn to Civic Square. Surface displacement up to 0.8m and ongoing settlement detected. Detour via Ghuznee Street in effect.

title: Thorndon Quay: multiple cracked facades and safety cordoning underway
description: Photos document 40-60mm vertical cracks in 6 heritage buildings. WCC teams installing safety barriers. Structural engineers called for urgent evaluation.

title: Water system damage assessment report initial findings catastrophic
description: Wellington Water preliminary damage assessment indicating catastrophic extent of distribution network failures. At least 12 major trunk main ruptures identified across metro area. Comprehensive system isolation required.

title: Wellington Electricity: Substation failure in Lower Hutt CBD, partial restoration underway
description: Wellington Electricity reports transformer damage at the Hutt Central substation on Laings Road. Power to Lower Hutt CBD is out; contractors are redirecting supply via overhead lines from Seaview. Partial restoration expected within 2 hours.

title: Commuter reports: Hutt Line track buckling south of Waterloo Station
description: Unconfirmed footage shows curved rail near Waterloo intersection. Area being secured by KiwiRail track crew; ground settlement suspected.

title: Damage reported at Whitmore Street
description: Facade partially collapsed, debris on street Whitmore Street.

title: Lower Hutt eastern approach: small slip on Taita Gorge service road
description: Debris material covering access road to maintenance station. WCC rerouting service vehicles.

title: Miramar beach sand ejecta and slump along shoreline
description: Residents report extensive liquefaction signatures on East By West beach with 0.3m+ subsidence in dune areas. Beach access unsafe.

title: Structural Assessment: Wellington Public Library building
description: Urgent structural inspection underway at Wellington Public Library. Building code compliance concerns raised; parapets showing movement. WCC engineers conducting Level 1 safety assessment.

title: Waiariki Street residential flooding, 12 properties affected Thorndon
description: Large diameter (450mm) trunk main fractured under Waiariki Street causing flooding of 12 residential properties. Water ingress into ground floors and garages. Residents evacuating, emergency services establishing water containment barriers.

title: Wakefield Street southbound: road subsidence, parking buildings evacuated
description: Central Wakefield Street has settled 20cm unevenly; Council immediately closing southern approach to the Civic car park. Surface now impassable for heavy vehicles; light vehicles at walking speed only.

title: Water flooding Kapiti Road shops, Lower Hutt
description: Witnesses say water pouring into retail stores and restaurants. Fire crew pumping stormwater drain to reduce inundation levels.

title: Wellington Hospital on backup power; fuel reserves stable
description: Wellington Regional Hospital confirms diesel generators running. Non-critical services scaled back; surgical suite and ICU prioritized. Fuel consumption estimated at 8 hrs supply.

title: Wellington Waterfront CBD area still experiencing power failures
description: Secondary infrastructure damaged, multiple substations offline. Partial power expected mid-afternoon. Generator stations now active in waterfront precinct.

title: Community alert: Multiple reported injuries at Civic Square evacuation point
description: A resident coordinating at Civic Square reports that several evacuees have sustained crush injuries and minor lacerations during the hasty exodus from central Thorndon. First aid volunteers are on site; ambulances are stretched.

title: Commuter video: Waterloo Station embankment cracking visible from platform
description: Posted footage shows vertical fissures in cutting. Local residents report unconfirmed ground movement during aftershock window.

title: Gas Leak: Basement mechanical room, CBD building
description: Basement mechanical room gas line rupture identified. Strong mercaptan smell detected throughout basement level. Emergency isolation in progress; ventilation systems maximized.

title: Large water main rupture—Newtown supply compromised
description: Wellington Water reports major watermain break on Riddiford Street, Newtown. Significant water loss; customer pressure dropping across suburb. Repairs mobilized; boil water notice issued for 8,400 connections.

title: Liquefaction damage in Kilbirnie making homes uninhabitable
description: Ground beneath 20+ homes on Cambridge Street has shifted 40cm. Foundations cracked. Utility connections severed. Residents evacuating.

title: M5.0 aftershock, Wellington region
description: Magnitude 5.0 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 10:57 NZST, depth 6km. Continued strong shaking in central Wellington prompted residents to relocate to outdoor spaces.

title: Multiple ruptures detected Petone residential streets
description: Jackson, Wilson, and Queen Streets all reporting water main failures. Coordinated emergency response mobilizing.

title: Newtown community hall opens as refuge
description: Newtown Recreation Centre activated at 10:15 NZST with capacity for 250 evacuees. St John Ambulance staff setting up medical triage; first 80 residents arriving from Te Aro and surrounding areas.

title: Seaview port infrastructure still without power; generator deployment underway
description: Temporary diesel generators being staged at Seaview container terminal to maintain critical cooling systems. Main power grid connection via Gracefield remains compromised.

title: Wellington Water SCADA: Petone reticulation main rupture detected
description: Pressure sensor malfunction suggests major pipeline breach near Jackson Street. Water loss estimated 8,000 litres/hour. Crew dispatched.

title: CBD Quays warehouse subsidence - crane tilting hazard
description: Commercial building assessment team observing tilt and subsidence in large warehouse on the Quays. Overhead crane now unstable; building evacuated as precaution.

title: Damage reported at Grey Street
description: Load-bearing wall compromise suspected Grey Street.

title: Grenada North feeder offline; backup circuit being activated
description: Grenada North distribution feeder damaged at span between Kaiwharawhara and Grenada. Wellington Electricity activating backup circuit to serve affected eastern suburbs.

title: Injuries from falling building materials in Oriental Bay
description: Facade panels and roof tiles falling on pedestrians. At least 8 people injured, some with serious lacerations. Ambulances responding.

title: M4.7 aftershock detected Te Aro
description: Magnitude 4.7 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 10:58 NZST, depth 6km. Te Aro area residents moved to safer outdoor locations.

title: Ngauranga Gorge southbound: debris clearance in progress, expect delays
description: NZTA crew using heavy machinery to clear 50+ tonnes of rock. Estimated 2-hour window for southbound traffic.

title: Port crane 5 damaged; hoisting operations restricted
description: CentrePort reports slew mechanism damage on straddle carrier handling crane. Container vessel diversion to alternative berths recommended.

title: Seaview reclaimed land: lateral spread evidence, embankment slump
description: Photos show 30-40cm ground displacement along harbour-facing embankment. Cracks radiating from failure point. Soil liquefaction visible as sand/silt ejection.

title: Taranaki Street queens: section closed due to building facade damage risk
description: Multiple older buildings on Taranaki Street showing cracked facades and loose mortar; Council declaring unsafe and prohibiting traffic/pedestrians on quay section from Jervois to Taranaki intersection.

title: Te Aro residential district: 4,200 customers no power
description: Resident reports all streetlights dark from Courtenay Place through Aro Street. Some buildings damaged; sparks visible from downed power lines on Cuba Street.

title: Wellington Water declare Level 3 water emergency citywide
description: Wellington Water officially declaring Level 3 water system emergency due to cascading main breaks and pressure loss. Public urged to minimise water use. All non-essential service withdrawals suspended. Potable water rationing in effect.

title: Building Damage: Heritage building structural cracking, Featherston Street
description: 1920s heritage building on Featherston Street showing major diagonal cracking through masonry. Roof tiles dislodged. Corner of building sagging visibly.

title: CentrePort vessel diversion: 'Ever Azure' now routing to Port of Tauranga
description: Container ship captain informed of port restrictions. Estimated rerouting adds 18 hours to voyage; cargo tracking updated.

title: Flooding spread Lambton area commercial properties water cascading
description: Flooding from Lambton Quay main break spreading through adjacent commercial properties. Water cascading through building entryways affecting multiple office floors. Utilities shutdown ordered for affected buildings.

title: Greys Avenue CBD: building facade-induced road closure ends
description: Structural assessment complete, building deemed safe. Traffic restrictions lifted.

title: Kilbirnie residential area - staged evacuation begins
description: Liquefaction damage extensive on Kilbirnie Crescent and surrounding streets. Evacuation staged by blocks; residents directed to Aro Street exit. Civil Defence volunteers assisting elderly and mobility-impaired residents.

title: Petone waterfront main break, residential area flooding
description: Underground 350mm main split under Jackson Street. Water surging into residential basements along coastal strip. Wellington Water deploying mobile pump station.

title: RNZ: Central Library damaged, book stacks collapsed; evacuation completed
