description: RNZ reports that the Wellington City Library on Victoria Street has sustained significant internal damage. Collapsed bookshelves have partially blocked the main foyer. All 200+ patrons have been evacuated safely.

title: Road access to Rongotai Airport completely severed
description: Airport Road showing subsidence of 80cm with multiple vehicle-sized potholes. No civilian traffic able to proceed. Official access only.

title: State Highway 2 partially blocked—Hutt Valley section
description: NZTA reports significant cracking in SH2 between Haywards and Whakatikei Bridge. Inspectors on scene; single lane only through affected 2.5km section. Heavy delays northbound.

title: Basement apartment flooding, residents stranded
description: Lower level units filling rapidly with water. Residents unable to exit. Fire and Emergency crews assisting from windows.

title: Building damage confirmed at Farmers Lane
description: Broken glass and stone cladding failure Farmers Lane.

title: Community report: SH1 near MacAlister Park passing: traffic moving but slow
description: Resident passing commute reports vehicles crawling on northbound SH1 through Ngauranga; appears to be debris still in one lane with pilot vehicles managing flow. No visible heavy recovery seen yet.

title: Flooded basement sewage backup, University building Te Aro
description: Broken water main at Victoria University Kelburn Parade campus causing flooded basement. Sewage backing up due to fractured storm drain. University facilities team deploying pump trucks. Building evacuated, cleanup underway.

title: Kilbirnie pool complex: suspected foundation damage, closure ordered
description: Facility manager reports large cracks in floor slab and subsidence. Water systems compromised. All activities suspended pending structural engineering assessment.

title: Kilbirnie retail area faces power delays; essential services prioritized
description: Countdown and New World supermarkets on Rongotai Road remain dark. Wellington Electricity prioritizing power restoration to hospitals and water pumping stations; suburban areas queued for secondary stage.

title: Miramar light restored: 2,100 customers regain power
description: Wellington Electricity deployed mobile transformer to Miramar Peninsula. Partial restoration to residential eastern suburb; secondary streets still without service.

title: Petone CBD shopfronts damaged, looting reports emerging
description: Multiple shop windows broken. One store confirmed looted of groceries. Police presence minimal. Residents concerned about security.

title: Petone Council sports facility damaged by liquefaction settlement
description: Petone Recreation Centre showing structural cracking and uneven floor settlement. Building closed pending engineering assessment.

title: State Highway 1 Ngauranga Gorge: debris removal in progress
description: Fallen roadside material and loose rock cleared from both lanes. Slope stability assessment ongoing; one lane traffic continues with traffic management.

title: Structural Assessment: Parking ramp deterioration
description: Concrete parking ramp showing structural cracking and displacement. Ramp access closed; traffic rerouted to alternative parking. Structural engineers assessing safety for reopening.

title: Building damage confirmed at Willis Street
description: Facade partially collapsed, debris on street Willis Street.

title: CBD Waterfront: substation flood risk due to ground subsidence
description: Photos show pooled stormwater approaching electrical substation near Civic Square. Settlement creating local depression. Electricity risk assessment initiated.

title: Hospitals prioritized; Wellington Electricity rerouting emergency loads
description: Wellington Electricity manually directing available power to Regional Hospital and Hutt Valley Hospital emergency departments. All critical care systems now on backup power feeds.

title: Island Bay tsunami watch - precautionary measures
description: MetService maintains tsunami watch for Island Bay coastline. Residents within 200m of shore advised to leave; estimated wave arrival 10:35 NZST. No evacuation order yet but heightened alert status.

title: M5.2 tremor strikes Petone area
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 5.2 aftershock at 11:01 NZST, depth 8km, with epicentre near Petone. Strong liquefaction effects reported in reclaimed areas of industrial zone.

title: NZTA: Ngauranga Gorge temporary contraflow; alternating traffic management
description: Slope hazard assessment finds loose material requiring removal. One lane access maintained with traffic light control; northbound priority 10-14.

title: Power restoration begins in outer suburbs
description: Karori and Brooklyn lines now back online. Supply limited but increasing. CBD still predominantly dark.

title: Stuff: SH1 Ngauranga Gorge section partially closed; detours in place
description: Stuff reports that the northern approach to Ngauranga Gorge is closed due to fallen rock debris and cracked asphalt. NZTA has posted detour signs toward the Hataitai Rd alternative route.

title: Wellington Hospital declares Code Yellow—triage active
description: Wellington Hospital emergency department activated Code Yellow protocol (multiple casualties). 34 admitted so far; 6 serious/critical. Elective surgery postponed; off-duty staff recalled.

title: Willis Street CBD: two blocks cordoned due to facade crack assessment
description: Pre-1970s building facade showing significant cracking. Wellington City Council restricting traffic as structural team inspects.

title: Extensive liquefaction fissures appearing in Thorndon suburb
description: Long diagonal cracks across multiple properties. Sand fountains still active in some areas. Road verges collapsing into newly formed void spaces.

title: Gas Leak: Medical centre, Taranaki Street
description: Gas supply line damage confirmed at medical facility. Evacuation complete; gas company en route for emergency isolation. Minor burns reported from initial gas release.

title: Hutt Line temporary speed restriction: 15 km/h through subsidence zone
description: KiwiRail establishing temporary restriction between Waterloo and Taita stations. Full line inspection expected to complete by 15:00.

title: M4.6 aftershock, harbour precinct
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 4.6 aftershock at 11:02 NZST, depth 8km. Waterfront area experienced continued strong shaking.

title: Miramar Depot area: minor cracking on industrial access road
description: Hairline cracks in pavement at Port Nicholson Drive. No operational impact reported.

title: SCADA alert: Kaiwharawhara treatment plant inlet pressure collapsed
description: Wellington Water telemetry showing catastrophic pressure loss at Kaiwharawhara treatment plant inlet. Automated shutdown triggered. Treatment capacity reduced to 15% of normal flow. Multiple mains must be isolated for burst location survey.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley northbound: partial closure at Jackson Street on-ramp
description: Lower Hutt CBD: Jackson Street on-ramp to northbound SH2 has separated from deck; Council and NZTA jointly closing on-ramp. Vehicles using Randwick Terrace as alternate access.

title: Seaview industrial precinct: power rationing in effect
description: Wellington Electricity implementing rolling blackouts at Seaview/Gracefield port and manufacturing zone. Critical dockside cranes forced offline; container operations halted.

title: Seaview industrial zone water infrastructure compromise
description: Port-side facilities report inlet flooding from fractured 600mm trunk main. Surrounding industrial area losing water pressure. Portable water trucks being sourced.

title: Wellington Water SCADA alert - Thorndon reservoir level drop
description: Automated sensor network detects rapid water loss from Thorndon supply zone. Suspected main break or lateral line rupture causing pressure anomaly.

title: Aftershock M4.9—eastern suburbs report strong shaking
description: GeoNet records magnitude 4.9 aftershock; epicentre near Rongotai. Residents in Miramar, Kilbirnie report additional property damage (windows, items falling). No new injuries reported.

title: Building Damage: Office interior partition failure
description: Internal office partition walls collapsed across multiple levels. Cubicles destroyed; structural framing exposed. Temporary barriers installed for safety.

title: Building damage confirmed at Whitmore Street
description: Broken glass and stone cladding failure Whitmore Street.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 4.6 aftershock recorded; epicentre near Petone foreshore
description: GeoNet seismographs detected a 4.6 magnitude aftershock at 11:03 NZST near the Petone waterfront. Residents report minor additional shaking; no new damage reports yet.

title: Hutt Valley main network experiencing cascading pressure losses
description: Multiple breaks in primary distribution system. Pressure dropping across entire lower Hutt zone. Secondary breaks likely imminent.

title: KiwiRail: Hutt Line temporary 10 km/h restriction through subsidence zone
description: Severe track geometry measurement confirms ballast displacement. Speed reduced to absolute minimum for safety; full closure assessed as alternative.

title: Miramar peninsula - residential evacuation corridor
description: Fire and Emergency NZ opening evacuation corridor from Miramar via Raiha Crescent to Strathmore Park. Liquefaction-damaged roads; alternative water access routes being opened. 200+ residents staged evacuation underway.

title: Multiple injuries from aftershock hitting evacuated areas
description: Secondary shake causing further building collapses. Additional injuries as people near damaged structures caught off guard.

title: Petone water main rupture confirmation large trunk section destroyed
description: Petone Jackson Street main break confirmed. Large 400mm trunk section completely destroyed. Damage recovery options limited. Complete main replacement required for permanent repair.

title: Rongotai/Airport power status critical; backup systems operational
description: Wellington International Airport running on backup generators. Main feeder line from grid fully severed; restoration crews rerouting power via Miramar link (est. 90 min).

title: Rongotai: subsidence pooling creating 40-50cm water depths over 200m area
description: Aerial view shows significant flooding in depression zone near car rental compound. Water accumulation due to ground settlement. Drainage overwhelmed.

title: Brooklyn-Wadestown: Riddiford Street subsidence creates significant rut
description: Visible 15cm settlement in road pavement between Brooklyn and Wadestown. WCC crew marking hazard.

title: CentrePort: container stacking height reduced to single tier
description: Hazard assessment restricts stacking pending soil bearing capacity testing. Vessel scheduling postponed; estimated 48-hour clearance needed.

title: Island Bay road network severely fractured and impassable
description: Multiple transverse cracks across main access routes. Coastal properties showing significant lateral displacement. Community isolated.

title: Miramar Avenue: earthmoving equipment positioned, debris clearance beginning
description: Two excavators and a dump truck fleet now operating at Miramar cutting landslide site. Estimated 10-12 hours to clear southbound lane; temporary single-lane alternate via Rongotai remains in place.

title: Miramar: residential street damaged - cracked pavement, displaced kerbs
description: Photos from Burnham Street show 15-20mm cracks running along full street length. Kerbs displaced up to 50mm vertically. Parked vehicles resting on uneven ground.

title: Seaview port facility ground failure - stacked containers unstable
description: CentrePort safety team reports liquefaction-induced ground subsidence affecting container stack integrity. Crane operations halted; area evacuated.

title: Water main burst Petone village, residents warned boil water notice
description: 400mm distribution main ruptured beneath Jackson Street, Petone. Estimated 60,000 litres/hour loss. Boil water notice issued to Petone and northern suburbs pending water quality testing. Hutt City Council coordinating portable supply points.

title: Wellington Electricity: 'Restoration priority: water pumping stations'
description: Press briefing states waterborne disease risk high without network restoration. Three water treatment plants running on partial backup; chlorination equipment affected by power loss.

title: CentrePort: Container crane 5 out of commission; limited operations established
description: Four remaining cranes now handling all operations at reduced rate. Vessel scheduling significantly constrained; emergency prioritisation protocols active.

title: Damage reported at Molesworth Street
description: Exterior wall bowing, structural concern Molesworth Street.

title: Herald: Petone waterfront subsidence; industrial buildings at risk of collapse
description: NZ Herald on-scene team reports severe liquefaction on the Petone foreshore. Three industrial warehouses show visible tilting; structural engineers are assessing collapse risk before allowing any re-entry.

title: Lower Hutt welfare centre - Hutt Valley High School
description: Hutt Valley High School gymnasium opened as regional welfare centre at 10:21 NZST. Capacity 600 persons. District Health Board paramedics staffing medical room; 150 evacuees already registered, mostly from Seaview and Wainuiomata.

title: M5.0 aftershock detected lower harbour
description: Magnitude 5.0 aftershock recorded at 11:05 NZST, depth 10km, near harbour entrance. Continued seismic activity causing concern for post-earthquake stability across the region.

title: Massive sinkhole opens Willis Street, pipe failure confirmed
description: Earthquake liquefaction caused underlying soil to collapse. 8m diameter crater with water gushing from broken mains at depth. Cordoning in progress.

title: Petone waterfront environmental concern sediment water quality deterioration
description: Petone waterfront area showing sediment and mud contamination from water main break and liquefaction effects. Water quality monitoring revealing silt contamination. Environmental impact assessment underway.

title: Seaview refinery—minor product loss reported
description: Refinery operator reports small hydrocarbon leak from a pressure relief vent due to seismic shaking. No major environmental impact; containment in progress. Air quality monitoring active.

title: Seaview wharfside: subsidence on vehicle access lane stabilised
description: Port authority reports settlement spreading has halted. Inspection schedule continues.

title: Structural Assessment: Office tower, The Terrace
description: Major structural concerns at 12-storey office tower. Ground floor column spalling significant; foundation movement suspected. Building cordoned; comprehensive engineering assessment ordered.

title: Te Aro CBD power lines still down, causing additional hazards
description: Live wires sagging across streets. Makeshift cordons set up by residents. Risk of electrocution if touched.

title: Water pumping stations power restoration critical; reservoir levels dropping
description: Wellington Water reporting critical pressure drops citywide. Wellington Electricity expediting power to southern and eastern pump stations to maintain gravity flow to suburbs.

title: Building collapses in Hutt Valley creating rescue challenges
description: Two residential buildings collapsed in Lower Hutt. Rescue teams unable to access due to road damage. Survivors calling for help.

title: Flooding Petone waterfront reserve area, Park Road submerged
description: Park Road reserve near Petone waterfront submerged under 50cm of water from combined main burst and liquefaction-induced ground subsidence. Storm drain system overwhelmed. Department of Conservation assessing environmental contamination risk.

title: Gas Leak: Industrial boiler room, Petone
description: Industrial boiler room showing gas line damage. Pilot lights extinguished; equipment shutdown initiated. Utility isolation complete; repair contractors mobilizing.

title: Kilbirnie retail park subsidence cracking main pedestrian passage
description: Community report of significant ground cracking in parking lot and building foundation cracks appearing. Multiple retailers evacuating staff.

title: M4.4 aftershock, Thorndon
description: Magnitude 4.4 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 11:06 NZST, depth 7km. Thorndon area reported continued strong aftershock activity.

title: Miramar experiences intermittent power flickering; substation under stress
description: Residents report lights dimming and brightening repeatedly. Wellington Electricity teams working to stabilize Miramar substation voltage. Localized control circuit damage detected.

title: Newtown: 3,100 residents without heating or light
description: Widespread reports of homes without power or heating. Temperature dropping; welfare centre at Newtown School activated for vulnerable residents.

title: Oriental Bay foreshore: road surface buckling, parking area cordoned
description: The seaward edge of the Oriental Bay foreshore loop has shifted; significant uplift and cracking observed. Parking area north of main beach now fenced off; one-way system imposed for western carriageway.

title: Retail stores closing due to water influx, emergency pumping
description: Stores on Lambton Quay evacuated. Emergency pumps deployed to move water away from entrances.

title: Thorndon office complex: visible internal wall separation and floor warping
description: Building occupants report feeling movement inside. Photos show 20-30mm gaps forming between internal walls and exterior facade. Elevator shut down.

title: Wairarapa Line south of Waterloo: embankment slip, single track operation
description: Minor embankment failure near Taita cutting. One track clear; bidirectional signalling implemented. Full repairs estimated at 72 hours.

title: CBD waterfront - mandatory evacuation zone 1
description: WCC declares Zone 1 cordon (Queens Wharf to Taranaki Street waterfront). Evacuation mandatory; residents and workers from Commercial Street, Featherston Street buildings being escorted to assembly points. 300+ persons evacuated so far.

title: Community report: Gas smell detected near Courtney Place restaurant precinct
description: Multiple residents in the Te Aro area report a strong gas odour near Courtney Place. A potential gas main rupture is suspected; residents are evacuating the area.

title: Damage reported at Lambton Quay
description: Multiple window breakage, structural cracks visible Lambton Quay.

title: Island Bay evacuation order—cliff stability concerns
description: Fire and Emergency NZ issues mandatory evacuation order for Island Bay beachfront due to risk of additional rockfall from cliff faces. 340 residents affected; civil defence buses deployed.

title: Petone wharf section: subsidence creating 25-35cm elevation mismatch
description: Port worker photos show differential settlement between wharf deck and adjacent industrial platform. Crane support structure showing stress cracks.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley: rockfall hazard forces temporary closure near Taita Gorge
description: Boulders from hillside creating danger zone. NZTA performing precautionary closure pending slope assessment.

title: Serious injuries reported in Miramar after structure failure
description: Local community centre collapsed partially. Multiple people trapped in debris. Rescue efforts underway but hampered by poor access.

title: Wairarapa Line embankment stability concern; ballast replacement urgent
description: KiwiRail identifies additional subsidence near Taita. Temporary single-track operation approved pending full repairs; closure not needed.

title: Water tank failure at Ceres Restaurant rooftop
description: Structural damage from shaking caused rooftop storage tank to fracture. Water flooding through multiple floors down to ground level and street.

title: Building Damage: Apartment block, Newtown
description: Multi-storey residential building showing widespread concrete spalling. Cladding panels dislodged from upper floors. Residents evacuated; structural stability uncertain.

title: Central Police Station on backup; comms tower fully functional
description: Wellington Police confirm all emergency comms operational via mobile generators. Dispatch centre at full capacity; officers conducting traffic management manually.

title: Community report: Brooklyn residential slips worsening, Creek Road impassable
description: Resident reports secondary slips now occurring on Creek Road hillside above; two houses above road showing warning cracks. Moving family out as precaution. Road blocked by fallen trees; not safe for normal traffic.

title: Gracefield substation overload risk; load shedding protocols standing by
description: Wellington Electricity SCADA showing Gracefield substation approaching maximum capacity. Secondary circuits being staged for activation if needed to prevent equipment damage.

title: Hataitai residential zone experiencing widespread liquefaction
description: Sand boils affecting 30+ properties. Water and silt spreading across multiple streets. Homeowners starting cleanup efforts.

title: Jackson Street main repair commenced Petone, temporary bypass piping
description: Wellington Water emergency contractor beginning repairs to Jackson Street main. Temporary above-ground bypass pumping system being installed. Work hampered by unstable ground and power supply issues. Estimated 4-6 hour repair timeline.

title: Rongotai Crescent near airport: minor settling in taxiway approach road
description: Small settlement on airport vehicle access. Airport Authority monitoring with geotechnical support.

title: Rongotai residential street buckling from liquefaction heave
description: Road surface on Tasman Street near Hospital showing 15cm+ heave and buckling. Residents evacuated from adjacent homes as subsidence continues.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley: temporary one-way traffic, alternate day arrangement
description: NZTA implementing tidal flow system between Petone and Seaview. Northbound traffic 10:00-14:00, southbound 14:00-20:00. Delays expected.

title: Building damage confirmed at Victoria Street
description: Facade partially collapsed, debris on street Victoria Street.

title: Kelburn cable car suspended—structural damage found
description: Wellington Cable Car Ltd closes service after engineering assessment reveals stress fractures in supporting cable anchors. Repair timeline unknown; pedestrian bypass in place.

title: M5.1 aftershock, Seaview industrial zone
description: GeoNet confirmed magnitude 5.1 aftershock at 11:09 NZST, depth 8km, affecting Seaview and Gracefield. Port operations further compromised by renewed ground motion.

title: Newshub: Kilbirnie high school designated secondary welfare centre; 500 registrations
description: Newshub reports that Kilbirnie High School has opened as a secondary evacuation/welfare centre. By 11:07 NZST, approximately 500 residents have registered; supplies are being arranged.

title: Newtown residential area partially restored; secondary feeder activated
description: Taranaki Street and surrounding suburbs getting power back after Wellington Electricity reroutes load through backup transformer. Approximately 60% of area now energized.

title: SH2 Hutt: northbound closure extended; estimated reopening 17:00
description: NZTA work crews establishing temporary haul road alongside subsidence corridor. Traffic management company mobilising additional signage and personnel.

title: Seaview industrial area inaccessible due to combined damage
description: Road subsidence, liquefaction, and building damage making the area impassable. Port operations suspended. Heavy equipment cannot enter.

title: Seaview warehouse flooded, cargo at risk
description: Industrial property affected by water from broken supply main. Estimated $2M+ in stored goods threatened.

title: Structural Assessment: Beam-column connection failure
description: Major structural beam-column connection failure identified in commercial building. Concrete spalling extensive; structural capacity significantly compromised. Emergency shoring mandatory.

title: Thorndon heritage building damage assessment
description: Structural engineers assessing cracking in Victorian-era building at Tinakori Road. Building temporarily cordoned; residents from adjacent flats evacuated as precaution. Engineers expect assessment complete by 11:00 NZST.

title: Wellington Water report: major water loss Seaview distribution zone
description: SCADA telemetry and field inspection reveal 12,000 litres/hour unaccounted loss in network. Multiple small ruptures and seeps identified due to ground movement.

title: Damage reported at The Terrace
description: Multiple window breakage, structural cracks visible The Terrace.

title: Island Bay outage: 1,800 customers affected; restoration underway
description: Restoration crew deployed to Island Bay. Downed power line on The Esplanade blocking street access; reinstatement ETA 90 minutes.

title: Kilbirnie retail district: storefronts subsiding, inventory damage reported
description: Multiple shops report floors settling 10-15cm with stock tumbling off shelves. Merchandise loss estimated. Structural integrity concerns raised by occupants.

title: M4.3 aftershock near airport
description: GeoNet seismograph confirmed magnitude 4.3 aftershock at 11:10 NZST, depth 9km. Rongotai and airport vicinity affected by shaking.

title: Miramar Circle approach: traffic camera detecting multiple settlement cracks
description: Distributed cracking visible on approach to Miramar Circle roundabout. Structural integrity monitoring active.

title: Miramar coastal property slumping - instability hazard
description: Engineer site visit confirms land slumping on coastal residential block near Miramar Avenue. Homes at risk of further ground failure.

title: Ngauranga Gorge northbound: one lane reopened for piloted traffic flow
description: NZTA confirms northbound lane 2 now passable with traffic management and pilot vehicles. Northbound lane 1 remains blocked; full two-way flow estimated 4-5 hours away pending further cleanup.

title: Power outages extending into afternoon in CBD zones
description: Downtown areas still completely dark at 11 AM. Business district offline. Residents struggling without refrigeration.

title: Report: Petone low-lying areas experience waterlogging deep flooding
description: Community reports multiple flooding reports across Petone low-lying residential areas. Water table surge combined with broken mains creating 60cm+ water levels in some streets. Residents on higher floors requested evacuate to upper levels.

title: SCADA: Secondary trunk main compromised, Miramar sector
description: Automated monitoring detects 80% flowrate reduction on Miramar distribution line. Three pressure relief valves opened. Residential supply at 40% capacity.

title: Wellington Airport: temporary closure extended to 14:30
description: Runway repair requires grouting of fissures and compaction testing. Charter flights rerouted; essential passenger flights cleared for priority resumption.

title: Civic Centre deemed unsafe—building closure ordered
description: WCC building inspector condemns parts of Civic Centre. Significant internal cracking and suspected foundation damage. Council offices relocated to temporary premises; essential services only.

title: Gas Leak: Residential complex, St Jude Street
description: Multiple residents reporting gas odour at apartment complex. Pilot lights and odour detection suggesting pipe fracture. Precautionary evacuation initiated.

title: Liquefaction in Kilbirnie airport road affecting operations
description: Ground displacement along airport approach making runway access treacherous. Heavy emergency vehicles navigating carefully through soft areas.

title: Load balancing operations underway; staged power restoration activating
description: Wellington Electricity dispatching crews to multiple distribution transformers to begin staged load activation. Automated protections preventing simultaneous energization to avoid secondary faults.

title: Newtown southern connector: minor slippage on Constable Street
description: Small hillside debris on one section of street. Not blocking traffic. WCC assessing slope.

title: Petone low zones report worsening water accumulation rates rising
description: Petone low-lying areas showing worsening water accumulation patterns. Water levels rising faster than pumping can remove. Ground saturation increasing subsidence risk.

title: Tsunami impact - Oriental Bay wave inundation
description: First tsunami wave reaches Oriental Bay at 10:27 NZST; water inundates to Freyberg Street car park. Small-boat damage reported; no life-threatening impact but cordon reinforced. Second wave expected 10:45 NZST.

title: Wellington City Council: Central Quays cordoned; full evacuation advisory issued
description: WCC has expanded the evacuation zone to include all of Quays and the immediately surrounding CBD. Official advisory recommends all residents north of Civic Sq evacuate. Emergency shelters at Town Hall and Civic are open.

title: Wellington Station: platform-level access fully suspended; alternative route required
description: Footbridge damage prevents step-free access; temporary stairs and assistance arrangements communicated to passengers.

title: Building Damage: Residential deck separation
description: Multiple residential properties showing deck separation from main structures. Decks tilted at dangerous angles; residents prevented from accessing outdoor areas.

title: Building damage confirmed at Parliament Street
description: Load-bearing wall compromise suspected Parliament Street.

title: CBD liquefaction damage to heritage waterfront precinct
description: Multiple heritage buildings showing foundation cracking and subsidence. Structural engineers mobilizing for stabilization assessment.

title: Cable Street water main fracture, traffic detoured
description: 400mm main burst causing street to flood and become impassable. Traffic signals offline. Detour signs being installed.

title: Commuter alert: Wellington Station bus interchange open; rail access restricted
description: Metlink buses now serving displaced rail passengers from Bus Interchange on Lambton Quay. Capacity limits in effect; requests to avoid non-essential travel.

title: Hataitai residential outage: 2,400 customers in darkness
description: Resident reports no power since mainshock on Barton Street. Transformer station appears damaged; green electrical fire smell reported earlier.

title: Island Bay households report power restoration wave completing
description: Residents across Island Bay suburb confirming lights and appliances returning to normal. Final sections of southern circuit being re-energized by Wellington Electricity after damage clearance.

title: Lower Hutt CBD network pressure critically low, Stage 2 rationing
description: Wellington Water SCADA monitoring showing Lower Hutt distribution network now at critical pressure threshold (15 psi). Stage 2 water rationing implemented immediately. Only essential uses (hospitals, fire suppression) prioritised.

title: M4.9 aftershock central Wellington
description: Magnitude 4.9 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 11:12 NZST, depth 7km. WREMO issued advisory to remain alert for further aftershocks in the 4.5-5.0 magnitude range.

title: Newtown school building emergency demolition warnings issued
description: Structural engineers condemning school building. Urgent evacuation of nearby homes ordered. Demolition trucks staged.

title: Rongotai runway access: water pooling blocking vehicle circulation
description: Airport operations report 30-40cm of standing water blocking access road. Ground settlement creating sump zone. Pumping equipment being staged.

title: Traffic camera: Seaview SH2 overpass—heavy recovery beginning, lanes cordoned
description: NZTA traffic sensor shows closure barrier in place at SH2/Seaview junction; recovery vehicles visible. Southbound traffic diverted toward State Highway 58 Hutt bypass.

title: Basement flooding widespread Thorndon residential streets
description: Multiple properties reporting water pouring into basements from broken lateral connections. Pump trucks from Wellington Water diverted to high-priority sites.

title: CentrePort freight operations suspended indefinitely
description: CentrePort announces suspension of all container and break-bulk operations due to liquefaction damage to wharf structures and crane rail systems. Emergency repairs underway.

title: Hospital admission surge - 85 patients received
description: Wellington Regional Hospital Emergency Department reporting 85 admissions since mainshock. Mix of crush injuries, lacerations, and shock-related presentations. ICU capacity at 60%; additional nursing staff called in from Hutt Valley DHB.

title: Hutt Road closure extending emergency services access complicated
description: Hutt Road water main break flooding now forcing extended road closure. Emergency services requiring 15-minute detours for access. NZTA establishing emergency access protocols.

title: Injury count rises as rescue teams access more damage zones
description: Over 30 injuries now documented across all damaged areas. Hospitals setting up overflow triage zones in parking lots.

title: Metlink: Bus service rerouting plan activated; main CBD routes restored
description: Buses now serving stranded rail passengers from temporary transit hub. Capacity management implemented; standing passengers at 70% threshold.

title: Miramar residential area: multiple properties reporting foundation damage
description: WCC inspectors document 30-40mm cracks in concrete foundations of 8 homes. Settlement-related issues visible. Occupants notified of evacuation procedures.

title: Quay Street waterfront: sectional subsidence creating flood-prone depression
description: Low section of Quay Street now collecting standing water from the harbour. WCC evaluating drainage impact.

title: Residential feeders being energized sequentially; expect 15-min intervals
description: Wellington Electricity implementing sequential feeder energization to manage load surge. Each feeder activated with 15-minute delays to allow protection systems to stabilize.

title: Stuff: Structural damage confirmed at Central Library; interior unsafe
description: Stuff's structural engineer correspondent confirms that internal load-bearing walls at Wellington City Library have cracked. Public access remains prohibited pending detailed engineering assessment.

title: Damage reported at Cable Street
description: Broken glass and stone cladding failure Cable Street.

title: Hutt Road intersection flooded, main break near rail viaduct
description: 500mm trunk main broken beneath Hutt Road near rail viaduct. Flooding at road surface creating impassable intersection of Hutt Road and Taita Gorge Road. Water spraying 3m high from road. NZTA working to establish bypass route.

title: Kilbirnie roundabout eastern approach: minor cracking doesn't block access
description: Hairline cracking visible on approach lanes. Traffic flowing normally. Monitoring ongoing.

title: M4.2 aftershock, Lower Hutt
description: Magnitude 4.2 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 11:14 NZST, depth 8km. Lower Hutt CBD experienced moderate renewed shaking.

title: Newtown main drag (Constitu Avenue): uneven subsidence, parking building unsafe
description: Two businesses on Constitutio Avenue reporting shifted floors and cracks; parking structure deemed unstable. One-way traffic east-west to allow inspection; temporary closure likely within 2 hours.

title: Northern suburbs power outage: 5,900 customers offline
description: Johnsonville and Tawa areas without electricity. Wellington Electricity crew unable to access damage site due to road debris.

title: Oriental Bay beachfront properties showing foundation failure
description: At least 8 houses experiencing foundation subsidence and tilting. Some abandoned due to instability. Residents relocating.

title: Structural Assessment: Lower Hutt shopping centre
description: Major retail complex assessment shows significant damage to load-bearing walls. Roof truss separation noted in sections. Engineering team flagging potential progressive failure risk.

title: Stuff reports: liquefaction damage 'severe' along Waterfront Drive
description: Ground fissures and sand boils documented from Thorndon Quays to Oriental Bay. Street-level subsidence measured at 20-30 cm in multiple locations.

title: Thorndon ground water table rise - flooded basements
description: Wellington Water confirms groundwater rebound in liquefied zone near Customhouse Quay. Multiple basement flooding reports in office buildings.

title: CBD Waterfront: Jervois Quay facing major subsidence zone
description: Engineers confirm 18-24cm settlement affecting 500m stretch of street. Multiple building entries now misaligned. Urgent stabilisation measures requested.

title: Facebook: Petone resident missing—last seen near library
description: Community alert: 73-year-old resident missing since mainshock near Petone public library. Last contact 08:15. Family appeals for sightings; concerned about injuries. Police search underway.

title: Gas Leak: Residential furnace malfunction, Lower Hutt
description: Multiple residential furnace systems showing gas supply disruption. Gas detector activation in several homes. Residents venting naturally; supply lines assessed.

title: M4.8 aftershock, Kilbirnie isthmus
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 4.8 aftershock at 11:15 NZST, depth 9km, with focus near Kilbirnie. Noticeable ground motion in airport area affecting recovery operations.

title: Oriental Bay power grid stabilizing; transformer damage assessment ongoing
description: Wellington Electricity reports stabilizing voltage across Oriental Bay-Bayswater circuit. Seismic damage to primary transformer suspected; engineers conducting detailed inspection before full load application.

title: Parking building basement completely flooded
description: 400-space facility with fractured water main nearby. All levels below street level now underwater. Vehicles abandoned.

title: Petone waterfront completely inaccessible to civilian traffic
description: All road access to waterfront blocked by debris and fissures. Port staff unable to assess damage to infrastructure and vessels.

title: RNZ: Hospital emergency departments overwhelmed; patient transfers underway
description: RNZ quotes Wellington Hospital officials reporting a surge of over 200 earthquake-related injuries in the ED. Minor injuries are being redirected to Hutt Valley Hospital and Kenepuru Station to free capacity for serious cases.

title: RNZ: Waterfront Drive liquefaction event; street-level disruption documented
description: National broadcaster reports extensive ground fissuring visible from aerial drone footage. Sand boils and settlement mapped along entire Quay corridor.

title: Te Aro commercial precinct - partial evacuation
description: Evacuation order for buildings south of Vivian Street between Taranaki and Courtenay Place. Liquefaction-related subsidence in car parks; structural engineers assessing pedestrian bridge over Taranaki Gully. Approximately 400 workers evacuating.

title: Building damage confirmed at Featherston Street
description: Facade partially collapsed, debris on street Featherston Street.

title: Karori Hill slip: debris continues to migrate onto road surface
description: Small rocks and soil still sliding from steep residential section above Kaiwharawhara Stream. Engineers restricting access.

title: Karori roundabout (SH2): stability concerns prompting speed restrictions
description: NZTA monitoring geotechnical engineer recommends 40 km/h speed zone around Karori roundabout. Cracking in slope above road widening slightly; temporary closure unlikely but traffic advisory issued.

title: Kilbirnie reserve water main fractured under sports field
description: Underground 200mm service line split beneath playground area. Water pooling in large depression. Sports facilities closed pending repairs.

title: KiwiRail crew finds 60m section of Hutt Line requires full track replacement
description: Detailed inspection between Taita and Pomare reveals severe ballast displacement and warped sleepers. Material supply chain mobilisation underway.

title: Maintenance crews report additional cable damage at Pipitea substation
description: Wellington Electricity field inspection teams discovering secondary damage to underground cables near Pipitea. Bypass routing under activation.

title: Myrtle Street Hutt residential area, widespread basement inundation
description: Multiple 300mm secondary mains fractured under Myrtle Street residential zone causing synchronized flooding of basement areas across 8 properties. Water levels 40cm deep in some foundations. Emergency pumping teams deployed to prevent structural damage.

title: Petone beach foreshore erosion and bank collapse
description: Coastal erosion accelerated by liquefaction subsidence. Bank collapse observed near Petone settlement boundary threatening access road.

title: Porirua CBD: 3,700 customers without power; repair delayed
description: Wellington Electricity: damage assessment ongoing. Secondary substation at Porirua CBD shows oil leakage; environmental hazard protocols activated.

title: Power grid stabilisation efforts showing some progress
description: Outer northern suburbs including Tawa now getting limited electricity. Central areas still waiting for restoration. Estimated 6-8 more hours.

title: Thorndon: historic building evacuation due to foundation subsidence
description: Photo evidence shows 50mm+ vertical cracks in 130-year-old commercial building. Structural engineer red-tags property. Urgent evacuation of 60+ occupants.

title: Aftershock M4.7—centred Rongotai, airport operations halted
description: Magnitude 4.7 aftershock detected near Wellington Airport. Ground motion triggers automatic runway closure for safety inspection. No aircraft emergency declared; 4 flights diverted to Christchurch.

title: Building Damage: School gymnasium roof section, Te Aro
description: Large section of gymnasium roof at primary school partially collapsed. Fortunately school is currently closed. Structural debris scattered across playground area.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 4.2 aftershock; epicentre shifted to western suburbs
description: GeoNet seismographs recorded a 4.2 magnitude aftershock at 11:15 NZST centred near Karori. Residents report brief but noticeable shaking; no new damage reported in the low-risk hill suburbs.

title: Hataitai Oval Drive: minor subsidence on car park perimeter road
description: Small settling creating slight bump at Hataitai Park access point. No closure needed.

title: Hataitai residential zone - precautionary advisories
description: Wellington City Council issues precautionary advisory for Hataitai residents near Beach Road due to potential secondary liquefaction. No evacuation order yet; residents advised to prepare for possible relocation.

title: Hutt foundational flooding risk increasing subsidence concerns escalating
description: Structural engineers expressing serious concerns about foundation subsidence risk from prolonged ground water saturation. Multiple properties at risk of differential settling. Geotechnical emergency assessment required.

title: KiwiRail engineering report: 80+ metre track section requires full replacement
description: Detailed ballast and sleeper inspection reveals compound failures. Material staging area established; parts sourcing prioritised with suppliers.

title: Thorndon suburb faces multiple utility failures simultaneously
description: Power, water, and gas all disrupted. Residents unable to shower or cook. Boil water notices issued for entire suburb.

title: Community report: Beachaven Road nearly impassable, residents isolated
description: Resident reports Beachaven Road extremely congested, emergency crews still clearing largest slip debris. Side streets toward Hataitai being used as escape route. Main road may be 1-way for another 2 hours.

title: Damage reported at Bowen Street
description: Exterior wall bowing, structural concern Bowen Street.

title: Hataitai distribution circuit undergoing emergency repairs; 45-min delay expected
description: Conductor damage on Hataitai feeder near Maltby Street identified. Replacement sections being spliced by Wellington Electricity crews. Affected customers advised of continued outage window.

title: Karori water reservoir cistern integrity compromised, precautionary drain
description: Wellington Water engineers conducting emergency inspection of Karori reservoir discover structural cracks in concrete cistern following ground motion stress. Water draining as precaution to prevent catastrophic failure. Backup supply from CBD reservoir initiated.

title: M4.1 aftershock, Miramar
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.1 aftershock at 11:18 NZST, depth 7km. Miramar Peninsula residents felt moderate shaking.

title: Miramar households experiencing no water pressure whatsoever
description: Multiple residents reporting complete water loss. Taps running dry. Network isolation affecting entire peninsula community.

title: Petone foreshore: sand boil eruption creating 2m diameter sand cone
description: Spectacular sand fountain subsiding to reveal 2m diameter, 30cm high sand mound. Fine grey silt visible. Photo documentation for geological survey.

title: Port vessel 'Ever Able' diverted to Auckland; estimated 7-day delay
description: Scheduled container ship arrival at CentrePort postponed due to berth unavailability and crane restrictions. 2,400 TEU scheduled for alternative routing.

title: Seaview refinery area liquefaction hazard - tank support settlement
description: Industrial facility reporting ground settlement beneath storage tank farm. Structural integrity of tank supports compromised by liquefaction.

title: Structural Assessment: Bridge pillar crack assessment
description: Major crack identified in bridge pillar foundation. Structural engineers measuring crack propagation rate. Traffic restrictions implemented pending assessment completion.

title: Te Aro liquefaction damage affecting entire suburb viability
description: Ground instability so severe that many houses likely unsalvageable. Urban planning crisis emerging. Long-term relocation may be necessary.

title: Traffic chaos: CBD traffic lights offline, manual control initiated
description: NZTA and Police deploying officers to major intersections. Lambton Quay and Featherston Street junctions now manually directed; congestion expected.

title: Aro Valley circuit showing voltage recovery; residents report lights dimmer than normal
description: Residents confirm power returning to Aro Valley but at reduced voltage levels. Wellington Electricity working to boost transmission feed to restore normal 240V levels.

title: Building damage confirmed at Hunter Street
description: Load-bearing wall compromise suspected Hunter Street.

title: Building damage survey moving into residential zones
description: Structural assessment teams now in suburbs. Many houses showing cracks requiring repair. Families temporarily displaced.

title: CentrePort: vessel arrival 'Ever Able' redirected to Tauranga and Auckland
description: Scheduled 2,400 TEU container ship no longer economically viable for CentrePort. Manifest reorganised for northern ports; 4-day delay absorbed.

title: Herald: Cuban Embassy on Hill Street evacuated; minor structural damage
description: NZ Herald reports the Cuban Embassy on Hill Street has been evacuated due to cracking around door frames and window cracks. Diplomatic staff are safe; building remains closed pending inspection.

title: Karori reservoir damage extensive cistern wall cracking noted
description: Karori reservoir structural inspection revealing extensive cracking pattern throughout concrete cistern walls. Multiple cracks 50-100mm wide. Complete structural failure possible. Emergency reinforcement measures recommended.

title: M4.7 aftershock near Miramar
description: Magnitude 4.7 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 11:19 NZST, depth 8km. Miramar Peninsula residents reported continued strong shaking.

title: Medical centre staffing surge - Newtown clinic
description: Newtown Medical Centre opening extended hours with on-call doctors and nurses. Minor injury triage station established; 35 walk-in patients treated for lacerations and sprains in first hour of operation.

title: Ngauranga Gorge northbound: one lane now passable but with 50kph speed restriction
description: Right lane opened with cones and flagging. Left lane remains closed for ongoing debris assessment.

title: Orient Bay beachfront subsided—vehicle safety warning
description: Community eyewitness reports ground subsidence on Oriental Bay foreshore near parking area. Beach level has dropped approximately 0.4m. Vehicle access to beach lot closed pending survey.

title: Petone High Street knee-deep in water, businesses closing
description: Main commercial street flooded following three separate main ruptures in surrounding streets. Shop owners pumping water from premises. Civil Defence coordinating retail area closure.

title: Seaview industrial area: major structural cracks in port facility building
description: CentrePort inspection team documents 60-80mm cracks in cargo warehouse. Concrete spalling visible. Roof structure showing misalignment. Facility use suspended.

title: Gas Leak: Commercial kitchen, Cuba Street
description: Commercial kitchen at restaurant showing gas accumulation. Sprinkler system activated due to proximity concerns. Gas company currently isolating supply line.

title: Karori residential area low pressure supply disrupted residents
description: Karori suburb supplied from Karori reservoir now experiencing total supply loss due to precautionary drain. 2,400 residents without reticulated water. Portable water containers being distributed to properties. Boil water notice issued.

title: Kilbirnie stormwater system back-up from liquefaction damage
description: Council infrastructure team identifies stormwater main rupture on George Street. Backup water flooding residential sections; pump trucks deployed.

title: NZ Herald: Airport runway closure costing $1.2M per day in diversions
description: Analysis of rerouted flights and passenger rebooking costs reveals significant economic impact. Runway estimated to reopen by 14:00 pending final inspection.

title: Petone beach road: minor settlement on esplanade parking access
description: Small subsidence on Petone Esplanade parking area. Flagging restricts use pending assessment.

title: Petone shops closing early; no power for payment systems
description: Business owner reports inability to operate without electricity. Supermarket on Jackson Street manually closing tills; customers unable to use EFTPOS.

title: Rongotai runway surface cracked and closed indefinitely
description: Multiple transverse cracks running across runway. Aircraft cannot land. All flights diverted to Auckland.

title: Wakefield Street CBD: building facade continues deteriorating, full closure extended
description: Further pieces of stone cladding falling from 1920s office block on Wakefield. Council extending closure zone to entire block face; structural engineer called to assess imminent risk.

title: Airport closure expected to extend past 15:00; runway repair more extensive
description: Wellington Airport Authority announces repair scope exceeds initial assessment. Contractors mobilising additional equipment; civilian flight ban extended.

title: Building Damage: Concrete pre-cast panel failure
description: Exterior pre-cast concrete panels dislodging from building facade. Panels falling creating street-level hazard. Perimeter cordoned; debris removal teams deployed.

title: Building damage confirmed at Victoria Street
description: Exterior wall bowing, structural concern Victoria Street.

title: Injuries include at least two fatalities now reported
description: First confirmed deaths from earthquake. One person died in building collapse, another from secondary injuries. Toll expected to rise.

title: Karori hillside area restored; cable link re-energized after checks
description: Underground cable route to Karori confirmed structurally intact. Wellington Electricity re-energized circuit after fault clearance testing. Power now available to Karori Park and surrounding homes.

title: Karori water crisis residents reporting inadequate portable supply rationing
description: Karori residents reporting frustration with rationed portable water supply quantities. Limited availability of 10-litre containers per household per day. Vulnerable populations requiring additional support.

title: Karori welfare centre - 280 residents sheltered
description: Karori Primary School welfare centre now housing 280 evacuees. Second medical officer arrived; catering expanded to prepare warm meals. Centre operating at 70% capacity; additional supplies being delivered from WCC depot.

title: Kilbirnie water main break, playground closed indefinitely
description: Children's playground beside rupture site cordoned. Reclaimed land subsidence opening fissures around service line.

title: Kilbirnie: residential street subsidence creating 20cm vertical offset
description: Complete section of Mahina Street tilted. Adjacent house showing chimney leaning 15 degrees. Resident photos document ongoing ground deformation.

title: KiwiRail—MainLine North suspended at Ngauranga
description: KiwiRail reports mainline damage near Ngauranga Gorge; railway embankment showing settlement and lateral movement. No freight/passenger services until inspection complete (est. 18+ hours).

title: Newshub: Seaview port facility partially damaged; container stacks shifted
description: Newshub reports that shipping containers at Seaview/Gracefield have been displaced by liquefaction. CentrePort is restricting access; estimated container yard reopening is 48+ hours.

title: CBD power restoration begins: 4,000 customers back online
description: Wellington Electricity: Lambton substation partial recovery achieved. Two of four distribution circuits now carrying load. Estimated 2-3 hrs to full restoration.

title: Civic Centre power estimated 2 hours away; essential building functions minimal
description: Wellington City Council Civic Centre remains on backup power. Full restoration contingent on Lambton substation reaching full operational status.

title: Johnsonville-Tawa connector: small landslip blocks half of Civic Centre Road
description: Embankment failure on Civic Centre Road near Johnsonville shops. One lane open with stop/go control.

title: Karori Drive flooding near Sanctuary Botanic Gardens basement carpark
description: Secondary main break under Karori Drive near Sanctuary Botanic Gardens causing flooding of adjacent basement car parking area. Estimated 35m water outflow velocity creating washout effect. Several parked vehicles partially submerged.

title: Karori water supply critically reduced, pressure issues
description: Liquefaction damage in lower-lying areas affecting trunk lines to elevated Karori suburbs. Pressure gauges showing 15psi. Residents advised to conserve water.

title: M4.0 aftershock, Seaview zone
description: Magnitude 4.0 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 11:22 NZST, depth 8km. Seaview industrial area felt continued moderate shaking.

title: Media: Earthquake damage to Wellington road network remains critical
description: RNZ reports State Highways 1 and 2 both partially closed; CBD arterials severely impacted by liquefaction damage and building collapses. Authorities prioritising clearance of Ngauranga Gorge to restore inter-regional transport.

title: Miramar residential area severe subsidence affecting all properties
description: Differential subsidence creating 30-50cm height differences across streets. All homes in the area require evaluation.

title: RNZ Live Blog: First buses resume to Wellington Station area at 10:45
description: Metlink announces phased service restart with reduced capacity. Routes 1, 2, 3 operational; express services suspended until rail clarity.

title: Rongotai apartment building partial collapse
description: Multi-storey residential complex experiencing progressive foundation failure. Residents evacuated; building deemed unsafe. Rescue teams on standby.

title: Rongotai reclaimed land: flooding spreading across 5-hectare area
description: Ground subsidence creating connected low zones with pooled water. Stormwater system overwhelmed. Surface water reaching 50cm depth in depression zones.

title: Community alert: Water main rupture on Vivian Street; street flooding
description: Residents report that Vivian Street near the Civic Centre has a major water main rupture. Street flooding is impeding evacuation routes; Wellington Water is mobilizing to isolate the main.

title: Damage reported at Lambton Quay
description: Multiple window breakage, structural cracks visible Lambton Quay.

title: Gas smell reported across city centre—evacuation alert
description: Multiple residents and businesses report gas odour in CBD. First responders investigating possible gas main rupture. Portable gas detection units deployed; precautionary evacuation of 2 blocks on Cuba Street.

title: Island Bay village: minor cracking on Owhiro Bay Road
description: Small pavement cracks visible near bay access. No traffic restrictions applied.

title: M4.6 aftershock, Lower Hutt CBD
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 4.6 aftershock at 11:23 NZST, depth 7km. Lower Hutt CBD experienced strong secondary shaking affecting ongoing rescue operations.

title: Newshub: First regional bus services resume; Metlink prioritising main corridors
description: Outer suburbs now receiving limited service; inner city routes fully operational. Capacity restrictions in place; express services curtailed.

title: Road access to Island Bay remains severely compromised
description: Multiple fissures preventing safe vehicle passage. Residents walking out to get supplies. Medical evacuation limited.

title: State Highway 2 main arterial - partial closure
description: NZTA closes two lanes southbound State Highway 2 through Lower Hutt. Liquefaction surface buckling detected near Hutt Valley High School. Traffic diverted via local roads; expected reopening 13:00 NZST pending inspection.

title: Structural Assessment: Historic courthouse facade
description: 1890s courthouse facade showing major cracking and mortar loss. Stone blocks visibly displaced. Temporary shoring structures being erected by emergency contractors.

title: Boil water notice extended to entire metro region, supply uncertainty
description: Wellington Water extending boil water notice to entire metropolitan region citing water quality concerns from main breaks and contamination risk. Chlorine levels uncertain. Residents urged to use bottled or boiled water for consumption and cooking.

title: Brooklyn power partially back on; some streets remain dark
description: Resident confirms lights in eastern Brooklyn homes, but western section near Aro Street still without power. Wellington Electricity continuing work on secondary distribution lines.

title: Gas Leak: Underground line fracture, CBD area
description: Gas utility detecting underground distribution line fracture in CBD area. Gas odour perceptible across several blocks. Area shutdown underway; residents notified.

title: Miramar residential area liquefaction-induced ground failure
description: Extensive sand boils and lateral spreading observed affecting multiple properties. Residents advised to evacuate as ground continues to settle.

title: Miramar: community asset damage assessment underway
description: WCC teams documenting damage to Council facilities. Scout hall showing 30-40mm foundation cracks. Playing fields showing multiple subsidence zones and cracking.

title: Power infrastructure severely damaged in Lower Hutt Valley
description: Multiple transformer stations offline. Recovery may take days. Hospitals on backup generators. Fuel concerns emerging.

title: Power substation flooding affects water distribution pumps
description: Electrical facility near water treatment station flooded. Backup generators activated. Water circulation rates reduced 50%.

title: Regional power still critical: 28,400 customers without electricity
description: Grid telemetry: systemic outages across distribution network. Demand-side management protocols active; non-essential load-shedding ongoing.

title: Resident video: SH2 Hutt sinkhole near Seaview industrial intersection
description: Posted footage shows 1.5-metre diameter subsidence pit in northbound lane. Traffic management in place; road teams working to stabilise base.

title: SH1 Ngauranga northbound lane 1: recovery crane arrived, rocks being extracted
description: Large mobile crane now positioned near tunnel entrance; extraction of individual boulders (up to 2 tonnes) beginning. Estimated 3 hours to clear lane 1 fully.

title: Aftershock M4.5 felt across central Wellington
description: GeoNet records magnitude 4.5 aftershock. Widespread shaking reported; residents feeling tremor for approximately 8 seconds. Minor additional window breakage and items falling reported.

title: Building damage confirmed at Willis Street
description: Interior damage reported, walls cracked Willis Street.

title: CBD street lighting partial; risk management preparing emergency protocols
description: Only 40% of Wellington CBD street lighting restored. WREMO coordinating with council on temporary lighting deployment for evening hours.

title: Kilbirnie industrial operations suspended indefinitely
description: Major employers in the area closed due to building damage and liquefaction. Safety concerns for resumption.

title: Metropolitan water crisis declaration made formal emergency status upgraded
description: Government formally declaring metropolitan-wide water crisis with Level 4 emergency declaration. State of Emergency protocols activated. Military assistance requested for emergency response coordination.

title: Petone waterfront - evacuation complete, zone secured
description: WREMO reports evacuation of Petone waterfront precinct complete at 10:41 NZST. All residents accounted for and relocated to higher ground. Zone now sealed; structural engineers beginning building-by-building damage survey.

title: Rongotai main failure, airport perimeter flooding
description: 450mm diameter line rupture near airport boundary. Liquefied soil unable to support infrastructure. Portable pumping station deployed to prevent runway incursion.

title: Social media: SH2 near Seaview 'looks like a war zone' per commuter account
description: Unverified social media claim of major road damage surface; actual conditions show subsidence pits being managed. NZTA clarifies severity.

title: Stuff: Oriental Bay waterfront subsidence; beach access closed indefinitely
description: Stuff reports that Oriental Bay has subsided approximately 20 cm along the beach. The waterfront path is buckled; coastal engineers have closed all access pending assessment.

title: Wellesley Street CBD: moderate subsidence beneath street level
description: Underground infrastructure settlement creating 10cm depression. Water authority investigating utility line integrity.

title: Wellington CBD: Civic Square area subsidence spreading toward adjacent blocks
description: Progressive ground settlement being monitored. Currently affecting 6-8 building foundations. Each hour showing additional 2-3cm of settlement in core zone.

title: Building Damage: Warehouse structural failure, Petone
description: Industrial warehouse collapse of one complete section. Steel frame twisted; internal equipment crushed. Site now cordoned; structural engineers assessing adjacent sections.

title: CBD hotel building floor collapse reported
description: Ground-level parking structure failure in hotel complex on Lambton Quay. Structural engineers assessing safety; guests relocated to alternative accommodation.

title: CentrePort: emergency berth preparation, Shed 2 secured for inspection
description: Temporary shoring installed at Shed 2 corner. Assessment identifies non-structural cracks; facility cleared for essential supply vessel operation.

title: False report: Karori power restored (UNCONFIRMED)
description: Social media claim that Karori has electricity. Wellington Electricity denies; no crews deployed to hill suburbs yet. Request residents verify before sharing.

title: Kilbirnie-Lyall Bay link road: liquefaction subsidence creating drainage issues
description: The south arm of Lyall Bay Road showing 15cm drop relative to north carriageway. Stormwater backing up; temporary culvert bypass installed. Traffic one-way pending levelling work.

title: M3.9 aftershock, Kilbirnie
description: GeoNet seismograph recorded magnitude 3.9 aftershock at 11:26 NZST, depth 6km. Kilbirnie area reported moderate tremor.

title: Porirua industrial connector: subsidence on approach to Porirua Park Plaza
description: Minor settling affecting one approach lane. Traffic control managing flow.

title: Seaview damage assessment reveals extensive port facility damage
description: Cranes tilted, shipping containers displaced, dock infrastructure compromised. Estimated weeks for recovery.

title: Urgent: Third major main burst detected Willis Street CBD flooding
description: 250mm water main burst under Willis Street near Civic Square causing street-level flooding and water fountain effect. Third major central city main break in 40 minutes. Public evacuated from adjacent businesses. Repair access difficult due to subsidence.

title: Building damage confirmed at Marion Street
description: Facade partially collapsed, debris on street Marion Street.

title: CentrePort: emergency structural shoring installed at Shed 2 corner
description: Non-structural cracking identified; temporary bracing prevents further movement. Shed remains offline for cargo operations; equipment relocation beginning.

title: Civic Square fountain pool drained—structural concerns
description: The fountain in Civic Square has cracked and is draining rapidly. Engineering assessment underway; area cordoned off. Suspected differential foundation settlement.

title: Hataitai residents beginning cleanup after liquefaction
description: Silt and sand covering properties being excavated and removed. Heavy machinery brought in to assist. Insurance assessors arriving.

title: M4.5 aftershock Thorndon area
description: Magnitude 4.5 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 11:27 NZST, depth 6km. Continued tremors in Thorndon prompted additional building evacuations.

title: Newtown main break flooding residential section
description: 250mm service line fractured under street. Water accumulating in neighborhood. Residents warned of potential sewage contamination.

title: RNZ: Newtown Primary School sustains roof damage; no injuries reported
description: RNZ reports that Newtown Primary School's main building roof has been partially lifted by the earthquake, exposing the interior. Classes are cancelled; the building is cordoned off pending structural review.

title: Structural Assessment: Slab-on-ground subsidence
description: Building floor slab showing uneven settlement. Ground subsidence measurements indicate liquefaction beneath structure. Geotechnical analysis of liquefaction extent ordered.

title: Structural assessment - Lambton Quay commercial building
description: Structural engineers inspecting 12-storey office building at Lambton Quay. Initial visual inspection reveals minor façade cracking; building remains structurally sound pending detailed assessment. Occupants remain evacuated as precaution.

title: Thorndon: multiple utility misalignment incidents, service disruptions
description: Water pipes pulling apart. Gas lines shifting. Electrical conduits cracking. City services teams responding to 15+ reported service failures from settlement.

title: Wadestown suburb experiences stable power; grid rebalancing continues
description: Hills area (Wadestown and surrounding high-elevation suburbs) showing stable power supply. Wellington Electricity using reduced-load strategy to avoid overloading restored feeders.

title: Willis Street damage extent significant nearby structures affected
description: Willis Street main break damage assessment showing significant extent of destruction to surrounding infrastructure. Adjacent underground services (power, comms) compromised. Area-wide utility outages compounding emergency.

title: Building damage confirmed at Manners Street
description: Broken glass and stone cladding failure Manners Street.

title: Cable corrosion issues identified at Eastbourne substation
description: Wellington Electricity maintenance team discovering corrosion and seismic damage in cable vault at Eastbourne substation. Isolation and repair work required.

title: Flooded underground car parks CBD evacuated hazard structural stability
description: Multiple CBD underground parking structures experiencing severe flooding from interconnected water main breaks. Pumping systems overwhelmed. Structural engineers warning of concrete erosion and foundation instability risk. Emergency evacuation ordered.

title: Inner city streets flooding, sewage system backing up
description: Combined water main breaks and liquefaction subsidence causing sewage to backup into streets. Health hazard warning issued. Sanitation crews activated.

title: Lower Hutt CBD: additional road closures on Riverside Drive due to building hazards
description: Two more older buildings on Riverside Drive (east side) have sustained severe cracking. Riverside Drive closure extended northward; traffic using Cambridge Terrace alternate. Estimated 24-hour review.

title: Miramar Avenue: subsidence pothole expanding, now 80cm diameter
description: Pavement failure accelerating near Lyall Bay approach. Traffic diverted via Evan Street.

title: NZTA traffic update: Ngauranga Gorge SH1 now two-way, 60 km/h
description: Slope monitoring installed; overhead hazard netting in place. Geotechnical team to remain on-site for 48-hour watch period.

title: Petone: lateral spread creating 40cm ground displacement toward harbour
description: Ground moving horizontally toward water. Photos show 40cm measured offset between fixed points. Liquefaction-induced lateral flow evident on reclaimed land.

title: Thorndon commercial precinct foundation cracking widespread
description: Building safety officer reports that at least 8 commercial buildings show serious foundation and structural cracking. Systematic evacuation ongoing.

title: Thorndon street networks impassable in multiple locations
description: Three main road closures due to subsidence. Residents using back streets. Emergency vehicles taking alternate routes.

title: Thorndon waterfront: restoration crew on-site; cable damage assessed
description: Wellington Electricity technicians conducting cable survey under Lambton Harbour. Fracture points identified; splicing required estimated at 4-5 hours.

title: Ambulance callout numbers spike - 140 attended
description: St John Ambulance reports 140 callouts attended in first hour post-mainshock. Bulk of incidents crush injuries and acute stress reactions. Ambulance stations in Petone and Lower Hutt now operating at surge capacity.

title: CBD flooding structural damage risk buildings threatened water ingress
description: CBD structural engineers warning of immediate risk to multiple building foundations from prolonged water exposure. Underground levels flooding creating severe load conditions. Urgent water removal necessary.

title: Gas Leak: Residential street mains, Newtown
description: Major gas odour detected across residential street. Gas distribution pipe under street fractured. Wellington Gas initiating area shutdown; residents evacuated from affected properties.

title: Herald: Wellington Airport operational but capacity reduced; flights diverted
description: NZ Herald confirms that Wellington Airport is operational but with reduced capacity. The terminal has minor cracks and the runway is being inspected for microfractures. Several regional flights have been diverted to Auckland.

title: Johnsonville local roads: minor slip material on Mahara Street quickly cleared
description: Small debris pile removed by WCC crew within 30 minutes. No extended closure.

title: NZTA: Ngauranga Gorge rock debris cleared; one-way traffic restored
description: Rockfall material removed; slope monitoring network activated. Temporary flow control signal sequences established; alternating direction management deployed.

title: Oriental Bay residents report ongoing ground instability
description: Continued subsidence and minor fissures appearing hours after mainshock. Properties settling unevenly. Structural integrity concerns remain.

title: School evacuation—Rangatikei School, Wadestown
description: Rangatikei School evacuates all students and staff (480 people) due to visible cracking in main classroom block. Parents collecting children; temporary relocation site being arranged.

title: Building Damage: Warehouse mezzanine collapse
description: Warehouse internal mezzanine structure collapsed. Equipment and inventory destroyed. Structural framework severely compromised; building stability uncertain.

title: Building damage confirmed at Vivian Lane
description: Load-bearing wall compromise suspected Vivian Lane.

title: M3.8 aftershock, Island Bay
description: Magnitude 3.8 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 11:30 NZST, depth 7km. Island Bay residents felt moderate shaking.

title: Metlink: 15-minute service headways resumed on operational routes
description: Bus frequency returning to near-normal on CBD circuits. Real-time tracking updated; passengers advised of continued crowding.

title: Miramar Avenue clearance: 40% of debris removed, southbound lane partially passable
description: Excavator teams report 320 cubic metres cleared from Miramar cutting slide. Temporary single-lane crossing allowing emergency vehicles and essential traffic via controlled pilot. Remaining 480 cubic metres expected by 11:00 am.

title: Mount Victoria circuit online; southern plateau areas getting power back
description: Wellington Electricity confirms Mount Victoria feeder fully operational. Power restoration spreading to suburbs including Mount Cook area. Estimated completion for southern residential zones within 1 hour.

title: Petone geotechnical survey confirms liquefaction extent 3+ hectares
description: Initial damage assessment by GNS Science team shows liquefaction affecting approximately 3 hectares near Petone esplanade. Detailed mapping in progress.

title: Petone hospital power restored partially, providing critical service
description: Backup generators engaged but main systems now getting grid power. Emergency surgeries ongoing.

title: Petone industrial zone water contamination concern, Port assessment
description: Petone industrial area main break near CentrePort facilities suspected of contaminating water mains with port area contaminants. Environmental team collecting water samples. Potential biosecurity issue flagged. Water isolation protocols implemented.

title: Te Aro power remains offline; downed lines still hazardous
description: Fire and Emergency NZ: electrical hazard on Aro Street. Power lines still live; public kept 50m away. Wellington Electricity dispatch queuing rescue protocols.

title: Truck-mounted emergency water units rushed to Petone
description: Response vehicles arriving with temporary water supply equipment. Installation beginning on main streets.

title: Wellington Water SCADA: Seaview zone pressure cascading failures detected
description: Multiple secondary main ruptures detected as system stress redistributes. Total estimated loss 18,500 litres/hour. Priority isolation underway.

title: Brooklyn-Mornington: Coromandel Street impassable due to large slip material
description: Hillside debris field covering entire 30-metre section. WCC directing all traffic via Riddiford Street.

title: Community report: Hataitai resident injured by falling building debris on street
description: A resident reports that a person on Hataitai Road was struck by falling bricks from a residential property. The injured person has been transported to hospital; police have cordoned off a 50 m perimeter.

title: Distributed generation systems kicking in; wind farm to grid connection active
description: Wellington Electricity bringing online distributed generation resources including local wind farm. Reducing strain on main transmission while waiting for central restoration.

title: Kilbirnie: bus depot showing major structural distress and settling
description: Maintenance team reports floor becoming unlevel. Buses tilted on stands. Building shows 25-30mm cracks through main structure. Evacuation recommended.

title: M4.4 aftershock, Wellington Fault system
description: GeoNet confirmed magnitude 4.4 aftershock at 11:31 NZST, depth 8km, along Wellington Fault. Seismic monitoring indicates sequence likely to continue over next 24 hours.

title: Metlink: Inner CBD routes now running full timetable frequency; passenger loads heavy
description: Service restoration accelerated; normal 8-minute headways between services. Crowding reported but managed within safety guidelines.

title: Pressure loss spreading through CBD network zones
description: Cascading failures in interconnected main system. Zone 2 showing 50% pressure loss. Fire hydrants inoperable in multiple central city blocks.

title: Te Aro building safety closures now affecting businesses
description: Red stickers on 25+ commercial buildings. Businesses relocating. Economic impact cascading across city.

title: Tsunami all-clear - Oriental Bay and Island Bay
description: MetService issues all-clear for Oriental Bay and Island Bay coasts at 10:52 NZST. Wave heights decreased; no further inundation expected. Cordon zones to remain in place pending post-tsunami inspection.

title: Wellington Airport—runway reopened with restrictions
description: Following structural inspection, Wellington Airport reopens runway with strict 20kt crosswind limit and reduced aircraft weight limits. Domestic services resume slowly; international services delayed.

title: Building damage confirmed at Majoribanks Street
description: Parapet failure, bricks scattered across footpath Majoribanks Street.

title: Hutt Valley secondary distribution system now offline, isolation zones
description: Wellington Water decision to isolate entire Hutt Valley secondary distribution system following cascade failures. Area reduced to single main trunk line operation. Multiple isolation valves being manually operated. Service restoration expected 8+ hours.

title: Island Bay parade: seawall integrity compromised, vehicle access suspended
description: Inspection of Island Bay parade shows seawall shifted 30cm landward; surface rupturing at road edge. Council immediately closing parade to vehicle traffic; pedestrian access via inland routes only.

title: KiwiRail: Hutt Line closure extended; no passenger service until 17:00 minimum
