description: Track workers encountering additional subsidence during ballast replacement. Supply of replacement materials arriving from Christchurch depot by 15:30.

title: Oriental Bay esplanade: minimal settlement on parking approach
description: Small subsidence affecting one parking access lane. Flagging in place.

title: Rongotai emergency services struggling with access issues
description: Airport access completely cut off. Emergency personnel walking to airfield. Heavy equipment immobilised.

title: Rongotai power outage: runway backup generators active
description: Wellington Airport confirms independent backup power operational for runway lighting. Commercial flight operations suspended until main grid restored.

title: Seaview wharf structure shifting - tugboat operations suspended
description: Port authority halts vessel operations due to wharf settlement and structural movement. Mooring infrastructure compromised by liquefaction.

title: Structural Assessment: Residential chimney collapses, multiple locations
description: Numerous reports of chimney failures across affected suburbs. Debris hazard on streets and roofs. WCC teams surveying extent of chimney damage across city.

title: Aftershock M4.3—minor damage to already-weakened buildings
description: Magnitude 4.3 aftershock recorded near Te Aro. Reports of additional cracking and facade pieces falling from damaged buildings. No major new collapses.

title: Gas Leak: Valve failure, residential meter
description: Residential gas meter isolation valve damaged. Gas company replacing metre assembly; temporary supply shut off for affected properties.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 3.9 aftershock; sequence continues with diminishing energy
description: GeoNet detected a 3.9 magnitude aftershock at 11:31 NZST near Te Aro. The aftershock sequence continues to produce events but with gradually decreasing magnitude and energy release.

title: Hutt Valley isolation impact causing secondary supply chain disruptions
description: Hutt Valley water distribution isolation causing ripple disruptions affecting secondary network branches. Multiple isolated zone pressures dropping critically. Network reconfiguration extremely complex.

title: Injury triage centres established in multiple suburbs
description: Emergency medical teams setting up field hospitals in Newtown and Kilbirnie. Overwhelmed hospital system requiring expansion.

title: Island Bay homes damaged by water, property loss reported
description: Coastal subsidence combined with pipe breaks causing water damage to beachfront properties. Claims process activated.

title: Johnsonville residential areas report steady power; retail district still problematic
description: Homes on Chapel Street and surrounding residential area now energized. Main commercial district around Johnsonville Shopping Centre remains on partial power due to localized substation damage.

title: KiwiRail: Track materials sourced from national supply; 15:00 delivery expected
description: Emergency procurement activated nationwide. Transport Canada rail sleepers and ballast allocated from Christchurch depot inventory.

title: Newtown welfare centre - 210 residents registered
description: Newtown Recreation Centre reporting 210 evacuees now sheltered. Medical triage desk seeing minor wound treatment and stress management; catering volunteer teams preparing afternoon meal for residents.

title: Rongotai: drainage system overwhelmed, flood water spreading toward buildings
description: Surface water spreading at 100m/hour rate toward two adjacent warehouses. Ground has settled 30-35cm creating continuous depression zone.

title: Community report: Ngauranga southbound queue backing to Johnsonville shops
description: Traffic report from local resident: southbound SH1 queue now 4+ km long behind Ngauranga closure point; estimated 2-hour wait for through traffic. Alternate via local roads saturated.

title: Damage reported at Buckle Street
description: Load-bearing wall compromise suspected Buckle Street.

title: Fuel supply logistics ramping up; temporary generators requiring 500+ liters daily
description: Emergency response coordinating fuel delivery to multiple temporary generator sites. CentrePort alone consuming significant fuel stocks for backup systems.

title: Kilbirnie major water mains rupture causing main street flooding
description: Major water main failure at Kilbirnie Crescent intersection. High-pressure water jet eroding adjacent road pavement; service isolation underway.

title: Liquefaction causing water main ruptures across multiple suburbs
description: Ground movement breaking water pipes in multiple locations. Leaks fountaining water up to 5 metres. Boil water advisory extended.

title: Lower Hutt businesses flooded from broken underground services
description: Multiple retail premises on Taita Street reporting water ingress from fractured 250mm lateral mains. Business continuity severely impacted.

title: Lower Hutt water pressure failing: 6,800 residents affected
description: Wellington Water: water pumping unable to operate on load-shedding schedule. Reduced pressure zones extending to Lower Hutt residential areas; no outage but flow reduced.

title: M3.7 aftershock, central zone
description: GeoNet confirmed magnitude 3.7 aftershock at 11:34 NZST, depth 8km. Central Wellington area experienced moderate tremor.

title: Miramar: ground cracking visible across residential suburban streets
description: Satellite road network affected. Park Lane showing 5-8 parallel cracks running perpendicular to street. Settlement differential creating uneven driving surface.

title: Petone and Lower Hutt residents queuing for emergency water distribution
description: Long queues forming at emergency water distribution points in Petone and Lower Hutt as residents collect potable water containers. Civil Defence reporting high demand, limited supply. Rationing to 10 litres per household per collection.

title: Port authority: tide delay forces CentrePort berthing postponement to evening
description: Crane operator availability limits combined with low tide window. One vessel cleared for limited offload; main cargo operations resume post-17:00.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley near Taita: rockfall debris cleared, one lane operational
description: NZTA clearance crews have removed largest boulders. Monitored clearance of smaller debris ongoing.

title: Building Damage: Corner building collapse, Lambton-Vivian intersection
description: Four-storey corner building collapsed partially. Upper floors pancaked onto lower levels. Rescue teams searching for potential occupants; structural hazard ongoing.

title: CentrePort: cargo operations limited to Berth 1; Cranes 3, 4 priority vessels
description: Berth 2 and 3 remain offline pending further assessment. Vessel scheduling advisory issued; single-berth operations effective until 19:00.

title: Emergency distribution demand exceeding capacity rationing escalating
description: Emergency water distribution demand now severely exceeding available portable supply capacity. Rationing measures escalating. Long queues and tensions at distribution points.

title: Emergency welfare centre opens at Newtown Community Hall
description: Civil Defence opens emergency welfare centre at Newtown Community Hall for displaced residents. Hot meals, temporary accommodation information, medical assessment available. ~140 people registered so far.

title: M4.3 aftershock, Rongotai airport vicinity
description: Magnitude 4.3 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 11:35 NZST, depth 7km. Airport operations curtailed due to runway assessment and continued aftershock hazard.

title: Newshub: Te Papa Museum evacuated; collections secure but access restricted
description: Newshub reports that Te Papa Tongarewa (National Museum) has been fully evacuated. Collections and displays are secured; the museum will remain closed to the public for at least 72 hours for structural assessment.

title: Power remains critical issue across central Wellington
description: CBD still largely without electricity 3.5 hours after earthquake. Refrigeration at supermarkets failing. Food safety concerns.

title: Thorndon area road assessment underway
description: NZTA technical team assessing potential subsidence on Tinakori Road and Pipitea Street. No immediate closure but traffic management to be deployed if damage confirmed. Preliminary findings available 11:30 NZST.

title: Wadestown Upper Lane: minor cracking on residential hill road
description: Pavement cracking visible on Wadestown Upper Lane approach. No closure required.

title: Building damage confirmed at Allen Street
description: Interior damage reported, walls cracked Allen Street.

title: CBD Waterfront: emergency shoring operation initiated for adjacent buildings
description: WCC engineers positioning temporary supports. Two commercial buildings at risk of progressive tilting. 24/7 monitoring network installed.

title: CBD business district flooding cleanup begins, sewage concerns posed
description: CBD cleanup teams beginning water removal from shop floors and office basements. Water quality concerns due to potential sewage admixture from storm drain interactions. Hazmat protocols activated. Biohazard warnings posted.

title: Hataitai reservoir losing 500,000 litres daily to breaks
description: Network losses exceeding inflow capacity. Reservoir levels dropping 15cm per hour.

title: Miramar liquefaction damage report alarming residents
description: Geotechnical expert on scene confirms severe liquefaction event. Recommends full soil remediation before rebuilding.

title: Rongotai community welfare centre established at local school
description: Fire and Emergency NZ establishing emergency welfare centre at Rongotai Primary School for displaced residents. Capacity for 200+ people.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley: Jackson Street northbound ramp remains closed, expected 48 hours
description: NZTA structural engineer confirms Jackson Street on-ramp cannot be reopened without major repair work. Temporary on-ramp via Randwick Terrace extended; estimated 48-hour delay for permanent reinstatement.

title: Seaview/Port: diesel generators repurposed from site standby
description: CentrePort: backup gensets commissioned for essential refrigerated container reefers. Energy security established for 8-12 hours.

title: Structural Assessment: Foundation subsidence mapping
description: Detailed survey mapping foundation subsidence across CBD block. Liquefaction effects creating uneven building settlement. Recovery plan requiring coordinated engineering response.

title: Tawa suburbs now on primary circuit; secondary feeders being commissioned
description: Wellington Electricity successfully re-energized main Tawa feeder via alternative route. Secondary distribution circuits being cleared and brought online. Most residents expecting power within 20 minutes.

title: Wellington Airport: Priority runway repairs completed, testing begins
description: Engineering team conducting load tests and surface integrity checks on repaired sections. Expected clearance by 14:00 for cargo flights initially.

title: Building damage confirmed at Ghuznee Street
description: Multiple window breakage, structural cracks visible Ghuznee Street.

title: CBD contamination protocols activated multiple hazmat teams deployed
description: CBD flooding contamination protocols fully activated with multiple hazmat response teams deployed. Water testing revealing chemical and biological hazard presence. Specialized cleanup procedures required.

title: Critical water shortage declared Wellington central suburbs
description: Multiple main failures combined with reservoir pressure loss. Rationing protocols activated. Portable water distribution sites being established at schools.

title: Karori Sanctuary Drive: subsidence crack propagating under pavement
description: Visible stepping on road surface indicating differential settlement beneath. Monitoring equipment installed.

title: Newtown area road conditions improving slightly with repairs
description: Council crews filling large potholes in main access routes. Secondary roads still impassable. Traffic management improving.

title: Seaview industrial zone - hazmat assessment initiated
description: Fire and Emergency NZ initiating hazardous materials assessment in Seaview industrial precinct. Two chemical storage areas checked for integrity; no active leaks detected but containment barriers reinforced as precaution.

title: Stuff: Interislander Ferry services suspended; stranded vehicles and passengers
description: Stuff reports that Interislander Ferry operations are suspended due to wharf instability at Wellington terminal. Approximately 150 vehicles and 800 passengers are stranded; alternative ferry routes via Picton not immediately available.

title: Thorndon Quay: pedestrian zone now completely closed to public access
description: WCC barriers blocking 200m stretch of Quay. Settlement cracks and building leaning creating hazard zones. All storefronts evacuated.

title: Twitter: 'Building next to me just partially collapsed!'
description: Social media report of partial building collapse on Taranaki Street. Eyewitness describes 3-4 storey office building losing front facade. Fire and Emergency en route; extent of injuries unknown.

title: Voltage regulation transformers being manually adjusted; multiple substations
description: Wellington Electricity field crews manually adjusting voltage regulation transformers at Kaiwharawhara, Grenada North, and Quay Street substations.

title: Wellington Airport: test flight clearance protocols underway; runway ready for testing
description: Small aircraft dispatched for load testing; results expected by 13:30. Conditional reopening window narrows as repair work completes.

title: Gas Leak: Hospital supply line, Lower Hutt
description: Gas supply disruption at major hospital facility. Emergency backup systems activated. Gas engineer team working to isolate and assess damage extent.

title: Island Bay beach erosion and property loss visible
description: Coastal erosion exposing additional damage. One home partially collapsed into sea. Residents facing total property loss.

title: M3.6 aftershock, Newtown
description: Magnitude 3.6 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 11:38 NZST, depth 7km. Newtown area residents noted moderate shaking.

title: Miramar hillside liquefaction triggering minor debris flow
description: Liquefaction-induced ground movement on steep slope causing debris accumulation across adjacent residential property. No injuries reported.

title: SCADA update: Kaiwharawhara plant output now 8% operational capacity
description: Wellington Water telemetry showing treatment plant operating at severely reduced 8% capacity due to inlet main breaks and pressure collapse. Most of metropolitan area now entirely dependent on emergency portable supply. Network isolation zones expanding.

title: Social media: Petone commuters stranded; buses running 45 minutes late
description: Traffic congestion from motorway restrictions flowing into local roads. CBD buses now routing via back streets; passengers report overcrowded conditions.

title: Thorndon Avenues: minor subsidence on lower Tinakori Road
description: Small settlement creating bump on Tinakori Road. Vehicles able to navigate.

title: Thorndon Quay slip hazard: hillside monitoring installed, lane narrowed
description: Slight cracking observed in Thorndon Quay hillside (east side); geotechnical equipment deployed. Southbound lane reduced width; traffic moving at 30 km/h with monitoring technician on site.

title: Welfare centre: Newtown School opening for power-loss residents
description: Newtown School hall activated as warm shelter. Hot water boiling facilities, first aid, and crisis counselling staffed by Wellington City Council volunteers.

title: Building Damage: Interior fit-out destruction, office
description: Complete interior fit-out destroyed in office building. Partitions, fixtures, and furnishings scattered; electrical systems damaged. Building frame structurally intact.

title: Commuter alert: Petone local roads gridlocked due to motorway diversions
description: SH2 closure forcing all traffic through town centre. Local residents advised to avoid area; delivery services experiencing significant delays.

title: Damage reported at Vivian Street
description: Broken glass and stone cladding failure Vivian Street.

title: Hataitai road network partially operational with restrictions
description: Local streets reopening with weight restrictions. Through traffic still diverted. Progress in assessment and clearance.

title: Kilbirnie - structural damage widespread, ongoing assessment
description: Ongoing structural assessment in Kilbirnie shows foundation subsidence in 30+ residential properties. Building inspectors cordoning high-risk properties; residents temporarily relocated from six homes on Kilbirnie Crescent.

title: M4.2 aftershock, Island Bay region
description: GeoNet seismograph recorded magnitude 4.2 aftershock at 11:39 NZST, depth 9km. Island Bay coastal area experienced moderate shaking with liquefaction effects in low-lying areas.

title: Petone: railway line showing buckled sections, KiwiRail access blocked
description: Track misalignment visible over 80m section. Lateral spreading causing 15-20cm offset. Service indefinitely suspended pending inspection and stabilisation.

title: Porirua CBD power supply stabilizing; grid connection being reinforced
description: Main transmission line to Porirua partially restored. Wellington Electricity implementing staged load application to avoid secondary damage. Essential services (water, hospitals) prioritized.

title: Power restoration underway—CBD partial restoration planned
description: Wellington Electricity initiates staged power restoration for CBD. Underground cable repairs progressing; partial supply expected by 13:30 to key areas. Hospital and emergency services prioritized.

title: RNZ: Island Bay homes report cracked foundations; evacuation not necessary
description: RNZ quotes residents in Island Bay who have discovered foundation cracks in residential properties. While damage is visible, structural engineers assess that properties are safe to occupy with post-earthquake monitoring.

title: Social media flooded with water crisis accounts and complaints
description: Residents sharing photos of flooded homes and businesses. Hashtags trending showing widespread impact across city.

title: Treatment plant crisis unfolding inlet collapsed treatment stopped
description: Kaiwharawhara treatment plant now reporting inlet main collapse. Treatment processes halting. Plant shifting to emergency shutdown protocols. Metropolitan water treatment now effectively offline.

title: Automation protocols being reset; manual operation controls for critical circuits
description: Wellington Electricity transitioning from automatic protections to manual operation for primary circuits. Highly skilled operators now managing load flow across city.

title: CBD waterfront precinct systematic building surveys underway
description: Structural engineering team systematically surveying all buildings in CBD waterfront zone. Initial triage identifies 12 buildings requiring red-tag assessment.

title: CentrePort: limited export cargo operations restart on Berth 1
description: Single cargo hatch open on Berth 1 for reefer and consolidated shipments. Full multi-ship operations remain suspended pending further assessments.

title: Fourth CBD main burst, Lambton Quay vicinity closed to all traffic
description: Fourth major trunk main rupture in CBD near Lambton Quay vicinity. Water main break 350mm diameter causing geysering at surface level. Commercial precinct cordoned off. Traffic diversions established on multiple routes.

title: Kilbirnie power status unknown; no contact with electrical team
description: Resident reports neighbourhood completely dark. Surrounding area inaccessible due to road damage; Wellington Electricity unable to reach damage site.

title: Ngauranga Gorge: minor rockfall detected on southbound while clearing northbound
description: Additional 200kg of rock fallen from opposite side. NZTA delaying southbound opening pending secondary assessment.

title: Rongotai Airport Road: damaged bridge approach, traffic diversion in place
description: Bridge over stormwater drain on Airport Road approach showing differential settlement; approach slope steep enough to risk vehicles. NZTA closed access to bridge; traffic diverted via Miramar Avenue alternate.

title: Seaview: CentrePort operations halted due to wharf subsidence and misalignment
description: Container crane rail base shows 20-25cm settlement. Cargo operation deemed unsafe. All ship operations suspended pending engineering assessment.

title: Thorndon waterfront properties experiencing severe damage
description: Several multi-storey buildings showing major cracks and facade damage. Immediate evacuation orders issued.

title: Underground flooding beneath Oriental Bay promenade
description: Reclaimed land settlement caused ground fractures with water mains rupturing at multiple points. Water bubbling up through asphalt surface.

title: Aftershock M4.1—minor recording, diminishing sequence
description: Magnitude 4.1 aftershock detected. Residents report very minor shaking. Aftershock sequence continues but with decreasing intensity.

title: CentrePort: Crane 4 structural assessment positive; operations approval pending
description: Engineering inspection clears Crane 4 for standard operations. Formal approval expected within 2 hours; capacity increase imminent.

title: Damage reported at Vivian Street
description: Multiple window breakage, structural cracks visible Vivian Street.

title: Hospital blood bank alert - emergency O-negative supply
description: Wellington Regional Hospital announces emergency call for blood donors. O-negative blood supply below critical threshold following 40+ transfusions in ED. Walk-in donors encouraged; appointment slots waived.

title: Injury count now at 45 documented cases across region
description: Hospital emergency departments full. Patients being treated in corridors. Specialist surgeons called in from Auckland.

title: Lambton Quay evacuation extended downtown business district cordoned
description: Lambton Quay area evacuation extended across downtown business district due to cascading water main failures and structural damage risk. Multiple city blocks now cordoned off.

title: Ngauranga Gorge northbound partial reopening triggers traffic surge
description: Temporary diversion routes significantly congested. NZTA managing through peak mid-morning traffic.

title: Structural Assessment: Bank building facade failure
description: Multi-storey bank building facade panels separating from structure. Falling debris risk significant. Building immediately closed; geotechnical analysis underway.

title: Wellington Electricity: Power restoration to western suburbs underway; phased approach
description: Wellington Electricity announces a phased power restoration plan. Karori, Wadestown, and Brooklyn suburbs are expected to recover electricity within 90 minutes; outlying areas follow after critical infrastructure is restored.

title: Cascading restoration: partial power to Hataitai; 1,200 restored
description: Wellington Electricity: feeder line from Miramar mobile transformer now powering Hataitai eastern edge. Central Hataitai still offline pending Aro Street cable repair.

title: Emergency valve closure sequence initiated by Wellington Water
description: Coordinated protocol to isolate damaged sections. Closure point by closure point, reducing total network loss.

title: Gas Leak: Residential cooking appliance disconnection
description: Gas cooking appliance disconnected from supply in residential property. Gas line flexion causing pilot light failure. Residents notified of supply disruption.

title: Kilbirnie: water main rupture near schools, emergency repairs staged
description: Large bore water main fractured. 6,000+ litres/hour loss. Three local schools without water supply. Tanker supply arranged for hygiene facilities.

title: M3.5 aftershock, Karori area
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 3.5 aftershock at 11:42 NZST, depth 9km. Karori residents felt moderate tremor.

title: Newshub: Union concerns raised over rail worker safety on Hutt Line
description: Rail union states subsidence areas still unstable; demands independent engineering sign-off before resumption. KiwiRail safety protocols confirmed in place.

title: Ngauranga Gorge SH1 corridor power challenges; motorway lighting partial
description: Power to State Highway 1 lighting and ventilation fans in Ngauranga tunnel remains intermittent. NZTA coordinating with Wellington Electricity for emergency restoration. Temporary lighting being deployed.

title: Power grid sector-by-sector restoration strategy underway
description: Hospitals prioritised for power restoration. Residential areas expected to follow within hours. Communication improving.

title: Stuff.co.nz: Wellington road crisis deepens as multiple closures compound travel chaos
description: Stuff reports 15+ major road closures across Wellington metro; commute times tripled. SH1 northbound down to single lane, multiple CBD streets blocked. Emergency services urge non-essential travel avoided until 13:00 at earliest.

title: Thorndon residential displacement - 250+ residents evacuated
description: Evacuation order extended to entire Thorndon waterfront residential zone due to building damage and ground instability. Residents being relocated to emergency accommodation.

title: Thorndon residential flooding escalates as second main breaks beneath Vivian
description: Second water main rupture detected under Vivian Street in Thorndon. Ground subsidence from liquefaction causing additional 300mm water pooling in residential areas. 18 properties now flooded. SES teams conducting welfare checks.

title: 1News: Safety concerns over premature rail resumption; union weighs in
description: Rail union representative questions pace of track inspection. KiwiRail promises comprehensive engineering sign-off before passenger service restart.

title: Contamination detected in oil cooling system; Quay Street transformer isolation required
description: Wellington Electricity discovering moisture and contamination in transformer cooling oil at Quay Street substation. Isolation and flushing required before safe operation.

title: Damage reported at Manners Street
description: Load-bearing wall compromise suspected Manners Street.

title: Herald: Multiple pedestrian injuries on Cuba Street during evacuation crush
description: NZ Herald reports that 12 people sustained injuries ranging from sprains to minor fractures during a crowd crush on Cuba Street as evacuees tried to flee the CBD. Paramedics treated the injured on-site.

title: Lower Hutt CBD cordon expansion - Taita Bridge approach
description: WCC and Hutt City Council expand evacuation zone to include Taita Bridge northern approach. Foundation settling detected; engineers assessing bridge integrity before traffic reopening. Estimated closure 3-4 hours.

title: Mount Victoria lookout road: small slip blocks vehicle access to summit carpark
description: Debris and exposed soil across Mount Victoria Road. WCC assessing whether closure will extend beyond 24 hours.

title: Rongotai: water flooding vehicle parking area, 2+ hectares affected
description: Pooled water reaching depths of 60cm in low zones. Ground subsidence creating 35-40cm vertical drops. Pumping operations ramping up.

title: SCADA: Mains isolation underway across affected zones
description: Automated sectionalizing protocol initiated. Closing valve chains to contain water loss. Monitoring shows 45 pressure drops stabilizing.

title: Seaview port damage now assessed as total shutdown
description: Port operations ceased indefinitely. Vessels in harbor unable to dock. International shipping disrupted.

title: Water supply restoration—Eastern suburbs priority
description: Wellington Water prioritizes water supply to eastern suburbs (Miramar, Rongotai, Kilbirnie). Main break repairs in Newtown progressing; supply expected by 14:00.

title: Airport terminal approach road: minor cracking confined to outer lanes
description: Structural cracking on approach pavement but inner lanes fully operational.

title: Beachaven Road slip: completion of debris removal, two-way traffic resuming
description: All fallen timber cleared from Beachaven Road; traffic now opening both directions with 20 km/h limit. Slope monitoring poles installed; inspections scheduled 6-hourly for next 48 hours.

title: Building Damage: Brick veneer failure, terrace housing
description: Series of 19th-century terrace houses showing complete brick veneer collapse. Lime mortar joints shattered; structural posts exposed. Streets blocked by debris.

title: Hospital water supply alert, emergency protocols activated
description: Wellington Regional Hospital emergency services placed on critical water alert. Reticulated supply failing. Emergency bulk water tanker delivery arranged. Hospital switching to bottled water and rainwater harvesting temporary supply.

title: M4.5 aftershock, western Wellington
description: Magnitude 4.5 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 11:44 NZST, depth 10km. Extended felt across western suburbs including Karori and surrounding hillside areas.

title: NZTA: SH2 Hutt southbound slip road severely damaged near Petone Junction
description: Pavement failure extending 25 metres with 15 cm subsidence. Slip road closed indefinitely; traffic routed via surface streets through Petone.

title: Oriental Bay liquefaction continuing with intermittent subsidence
description: Ground still settling. Additional fissures developing. Property damage ongoing. Residents confined to homes due to hazards.

title: Petone industrial area petrochemical storage concern
description: Industrial facility reports potential chemical storage container movement due to ground subsidence. Fire and Emergency NZ assessing hazard; perimeter evacuation ordered as precaution.

title: RNZ: 'Major power crisis deepening across Greater Wellington'
description: Radio broadcast reporting regional grid instability. Network demand managed via load-shedding; restoration estimated 4-6 hours. Advice: conserve water, stay warm.

title: Building damage confirmed at Courtenay Place
description: Ceiling collapse in lower levels Courtenay Place.

title: Community alert: Johnsonville resident reports chimney collapse damaging roof
description: A resident in Johnsonville reports that their chimney has collapsed through the roof during the earthquake, creating a large hole and water ingress hazard. The property is declared unsafe for habitation.

title: Hospital crisis escalating lifesaving procedures water dependent halted
description: Hospital crisis escalating with water-dependent lifesaving procedures being forced to halt. Emergency procedures shifted to minimal water use protocols. Patient care quality significantly impacted.

title: Instagram: Petone street torn apart by liquefaction
description: Eyewitness images of Jackson Street, Petone showing severe ground fissures and subsidence. Street surface buckled; multiple homes with visible foundation damage.

title: M3.4 aftershock, Brooklyn
description: Magnitude 3.4 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 11:45 NZST, depth 7km. Brooklyn hillside area experienced moderate shaking.

title: Miramar: Council facility forced closure, 15+ staff evacuated from office
description: Building showing progressive cracking and settlement. Staff unable to access interior sections safely. Alternative accommodation sourced.

title: NZTA alert: SH2 Petone slip road completely severed; reconstruction required
description: Pavement failure extends 30 metres with complete loss of structural integrity. Repair team mobilised for temporary haul road construction; indefinite closure.

title: Ngaio area experiencing rolling power adjustments; voltage fluctuations reported
description: Residents report lights brightening and dimming as Wellington Electricity operators balance load across restored circuits. Temporary condition expected to stabilize within 30 minutes.

title: Petone area liquefaction damage extensive in residential zone
description: At least 15 homes with severe foundation damage. Geotechnical teams recommend immediate evacuation and relocation.

title: Petone medical centre opens triage - 65 patients treated
description: Petone Medical Centre opened emergency triage station at 11:01 NZST. Now treating 65 minor-injury and acute-stress patients. Centre working in tandem with evacuation centres; referrals to ED for serious cases.

title: Rongotai residents reporting water discoloration and odor
description: Sediment and particles from fractured mains contaminating supply. Residents instructed to let water run before use.

title: Structural Assessment: Glass curtain wall failure risk
description: Modern glass curtain wall showing stress fractures and connection failures. Risk of progressive failure. Building facade temporarily braced; occupancy restrictions in place.

title: Damage reported at Taranaki Street
description: Exterior wall bowing, structural concern Taranaki Street.

title: Kilbirnie assessment reveals extensive infrastructure damage
description: Water mains, power lines, gas pipes all damaged. Extensive remediation required. Suburb facing weeks of disruption.

title: KiwiRail: welding teams mobilised for emergency rail joint repair
description: Two additional rail sections requiring joint repair beyond initial assessment. Specialist equipment brought in from Hutt Maintenance Depot.

title: Petone emergency fuel supplies deployed for backup generators
description: Wellington Electricity dispatching emergency diesel fuel tanker to Petone substation. Temporary genset installation underway; estimated restoration 2-3 hours.

title: Seaview port operations halted due to water system failure
description: Industrial facility water supply lost. Cooling systems offline. Process operations suspended. Temporary water supply lines being rigged from alternative source.

title: Seaview rail siding track damage from liquefaction settlement
description: KiwiRail discovers track misalignment and ballast displacement on industrial siding. Freight operations halted pending track inspection and repair.

title: Secondary substations showing stable operation; confidence rising
description: Wellington Electricity reporting increasing stability across secondary transformer network. No new faults detected in past 30 minutes. Crew efficiency improving.

title: Taranaki Street quay closures: façade demolition underway, streets sealed
description: Wellington City Council engineers deciding to demolish loose stone facing on 1890s building rather than attempt temporary stabilisation. Taranaki Street section closed; pedestrian routes via Jervois/Quay only.

title: Te Aro neighborhood flooded hillside runoff combined with main breaks
description: Te Aro residential area experiencing compound flooding from hillside runoff concentrated by broken water mains. Water flow velocities estimated 2m/s down streets. Stormwater system completely blocked. 25 properties affected, evacuation underway.

title: Te Aro: Dixon Street subsidence affecting three consecutive property frontages
description: Liquefaction settlement creating uneven footpath and road surface. WCC utility crews checking gas and water lines.

title: Wellington CBD: Customhouse Quay facade showing dangerous separation
description: Photo shows 60-80mm gap forming between building facade and adjacent structure. Settlement-induced stress causing progressive joint opening. Urgent engineering review.

title: Aftershock M4.0—centred Miramar, shaking diminishing
description: Magnitude 4.0 aftershock near Miramar. Residents report continued but noticeably weaker shaking compared to earlier aftershocks.

title: Gas Leak: Factory premises, industrial zone
description: Industrial premises showing potential gas leak. Equipment damage suspected in production areas. Facility evacuated; gas detection equipment deployed.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 4.1 aftershock; magnitude 5.0+ events unlikely within 24 hours
description: GeoNet issued a brief seismic hazard statement noting a 4.1 magnitude aftershock at 11:45 NZST. Their statistical analysis indicates strong damping; probability of another 5.0+ event in the next 24 hours is now below 15%.

title: Karori central area: residents reporting continued small rockfalls
description: Ongoing minor debris drops from hillsides above residential properties. WCC monitoring 24/7.

title: KiwiRail: Welding equipment positioned; night shift contingency crew briefed
description: Emergency rail joint repair teams briefed on subsidence zone; specialized equipment staged at Waterloo. Repair sequence planned for phased approach.

title: M4.3 aftershock near Newtown
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 4.3 aftershock at 11:47 NZST, depth 8km. Newtown residents reported renewed concern as aftershocks continue in moderate magnitude range.

title: Miramar evacuation - 180 residents relocated to assembly points
description: Miramar peninsula evacuation now complete with 180 residents successfully relocated to assembly points in Strathmore Park. Final checks underway on remaining coastal properties; all residents accounted for.

title: Rongotai airport closure affects regional operations
description: Helicopter operations suspended due to safety concerns. Emergency medical flights diverted to Auckland.

title: Te Aro evacuation zone expanding slope flooding continuing accelerating
description: Te Aro evacuation zone expanded further as slope flooding continues accelerating. Water flow velocities increasing. Additional 20 properties ordered to evacuate immediately.

title: Building Damage: Elevator shaft damage, commercial building
description: Elevator shaft walls showing significant cracking and misalignment. Elevator systems disabled; emergency access closure in place. Shaft assessment required before reactivation.

title: Building damage confirmed at Marion Street
description: Facade partially collapsed, debris on street Marion Street.

title: CBD Lambton substation damage repair milestone reached; full load testing underway
description: Wellington Electricity reports primary transformer replacement in Lambton substation completed ahead of schedule. Load testing initiated to confirm safe operation before restoring full central city service.

title: Karori SH2 roundabout: slope stabilisation completed, speed limit normalising
description: Geotechnical team confirms crack propagation halted; temporary support berms installed. Speed restrictions being lifted to 80 km/h in stages. Full normal speed expected by 13:00.

title: Kilbirnie medical clinic damage - patients relocated
description: Local medical facility reporting structural damage to building envelope. Patient services relocated to nearby facility; clinical staff triaging ongoing care needs.

title: M3.3 aftershock, Wadestown
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 3.3 aftershock at 11:48 NZST, depth 8km. Wadestown residents reported light to moderate shaking.

title: Oriental Bay: full power restoration achieved; 3,200 households live
description: Wellington Electricity confirms sustained load on Oriental Bay feeder. Streetlights operational; residential comms and heating restored.

title: Port crane operators stand down for 2 hours pending structural engineer visit
description: Precautionary measure while all five cranes undergo independent certification. Crane 5 damage assessment confirms significant repair work needed.

title: Residential area water rationing begins, council enforcing restrictions
description: Wellington City Council enforcement officers visiting residential areas to implement water use rationing. Outdoor tap lockouts being installed. Residents restricted to basic hygiene and cooking needs only. Violation penalties up to $1000 per day.

title: Road closures creating supply chain disruptions
description: Inability to access lower suburbs making fuel and food delivery problematic. Supermarkets unable to restock.

title: Thorndon heritage precinct: engineering consortium deployed for stabilisation
description: Four historic buildings in crisis. Structural engineers installing temporary shoring. Heritage buildings showing 40-50mm cracks in load-bearing walls.

title: Tory Street flooding deepening, shop frontages damaged
description: Water pooling at street level affecting ground floor businesses. Estimated $500K in product damage.

title: CBD Zone 2 - evacuation order extended to Lambton Quay
description: WCC extends evacuation Zone 2 northward along Lambton Quay to Hill Street. Additional 200-300 office workers being evacuated as precautionary measure pending detailed structural assessments of commercial buildings.

title: CentrePort: All five cranes now under structural inspection; operations halted
description: Precautionary shutdown pending independent certification. Vessel in berth; limited manual cargo handling continuing while engineers complete review.

title: Hutt City Council declares local emergency—resources mobilized
description: Hutt City Council activates local emergency response. Damage teams and repair crews dispatched across valley. Emergency hotline established; public asked to report damage and hazards.

title: Newshub: Porirua CBD experiences moderate liquefaction; one building condemned
description: Newshub reports that Porirua CBD waterfront businesses are dealing with significant liquefaction. A 1970s-era office building on the foreshore has been condemned due to severe tilting and foundation failure.

title: Petone: residential buildings subsiding, families notified of evacuation
description: 20 homes showing 15-25cm differential settlement. Several structures listing visibly. WCC issuing evacuation notices. Welfare centre activation being coordinated.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley: traffic flowing at reduced capacity after slip remediation
description: Both lanes now open with speed restrictions and flagging. NZTA crew remaining on-site for monitoring.

title: Street surface collapse Majoribanks Street, water geyser
description: Subsurface void from combined liquefaction and pipe rupture. Water fountaining 6m+ into air. Cordon established, traffic rerouted.

title: Te Aro suburb showing signs of recovery and stabilisation
description: No new liquefaction activity reported for past 30 minutes. Ground appears stable. Residents beginning initial cleanup.

title: Transmission security margins now within safe operating range
description: Wellington Electricity SCADA showing transmission network security margins returning to safe operating thresholds. Equipment protection systems can now operate automatically.

title: Building damage confirmed at Cuba Street
description: Ceiling collapse in lower levels Cuba Street.

title: Hospital diesel fuel status: 6.5 hours remaining at current load
description: Wellington Regional Hospital: fuel consumption tracking within projections. Medical staff rationing non-essential lighting; operating theatres fully lit for surgical operations.

title: Lower Hutt local roads: multiple minor subsidence areas mapped
description: WCC completing survey of damage. Total of 12 sites identified for staged repair.

title: M4.1 aftershock, central city core
description: Magnitude 4.1 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 11:50 NZST, depth 7km. Continued shaking in CBD prompting structural engineers to accelerate building assessment timelines.

title: Newtown residents organizing community response efforts
description: Local volunteers setting up temporary shelter. Food distribution points being established. Strong community mobilisation.

title: Petone Port area flooding restricts container operations CentrePort update
description: CentrePort container terminal flooding from main break and liquefaction subsidence creating 400mm water level across operational areas. Heavy machinery movement restricted. Port operations suspended until water removal completed. Estimated 48-hour service disruption.

title: Rongotai soil liquefaction ongoing sand boil ejection
description: Monitoring team observes continued sand ejection from surface fissures near Airport roundabout. Subsidence rate slowing but ground remains saturated.

title: SH1 Ngauranga: northbound lane 1 nearly cleared, two-way flow expected in 90 min
description: Crane operator confirms final boulder extraction imminent; northbound lane 1 pavement now clear of major obstruction. Lane markings and debris sweeping underway. NZTA predicting two-way full traffic within 90 minutes.

title: Structural Assessment: Office building column damage, CBD
description: Concrete column spalling and rebar exposure observed in multi-storey office. Structural integrity of upper floors questioned. Emergency shoring installation authorized.

title: Wellington Airport: Runway 16 reopens for emergency and cargo flights
description: Conditional clearance granted for aircraft under 50 tonnes. Test landings successful; passenger flights ramp up from 14:30.

title: Gas Leak: Supply regulator damage, residential block
description: Gas supply regulator assembly damaged at residential block entry. Pressure reduction malfunction creating hazard. Regulator replacement scheduled.

title: Hospital patient transfer—urgent cases to Christchurch
description: Capital & Coast DHB arranges medical evacuation of 3 critical patients to Christchurch Hospital by helicopter due to limited Wellington Hospital capacity and facility damage.

title: Karori welfare centre - 340 residents, services expanded
description: Karori Primary School welfare centre now housing 340 evacuees at 11:07 NZST. Mental health support counsellor arrived; children's play area organized by volunteer. Centre near 85% capacity; overflow contingency being activated.

title: M3.2 aftershock, Mount Victoria
description: Magnitude 3.2 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 11:51 NZST, depth 6km. Mount Victoria area felt light to moderate tremor.

title: Petone port biological hazard alert contamination levels hazardous
description: Petone port area biological contamination levels reaching hazardous thresholds. Port area biosecurity alert issued. Environmental health authority restricting all personnel access.

title: Petone-Gracefield link restoration; port power timeline improved
description: Transmission line between Gracefield and Petone now operational with 40% load capacity. CentrePort expecting to restore container terminal operations by 13:30. Full port capacity target 14:00.

title: Power restoration reaching outer residential suburbs
description: Brooklyn and Wadestown now partially powered. Central areas expected within 2 hours. Hospital services adequate.

title: Sanitation crisis emerging from water/sewage mix flooding
description: Broken water mains mixing with sewage system. Biohazard warnings issued for multiple blocks.

title: Seaview reclaimed area: widespread ground liquefaction visible, 200+ residents affected
description: Sand boils and lateral spreading evident across industrial zone. 30-35cm settlement in core area. Resident evacuation recommendations issued for 50+ homes.

title: Stuff: Northbound SH1 closure extended through Ngauranga; estimates 3-4 hour delays
description: Stuff updates that NZTA has extended the northbound SH1 closure through the Ngauranga Gorge due to pavement buckling and minor rockfall. Traffic is backed up to Civic Sq; estimated delays are 3-4 hours for diverted traffic.

title: Wellington Airport: Runway test flight successful; controlled clearance protocol active
description: Light aircraft load test completed safely. Phased reopening approved for emergency and cargo flights only; passenger flights pending additional certification.

title: Building damage confirmed at Vivian Lane
description: Parapet failure, bricks scattered across footpath Vivian Lane.

title: Industrial area load restrictions being relaxed; manufacturing partial restart
description: Wellington Electricity lifting industrial load curtailment orders for priority manufacturing facilities. Two major factories approved to restart operations.

title: Island Bay power restoration complete; 1,800 customers restored
description: Downed power line on The Esplanade spliced by restoration crew. Full load restored to Island Bay residential district.

title: Kilbirnie: community centre damaged beyond immediate repair
description: Photos show 50mm+ wide cracks in walls. Interior walls pulling away from exterior. WCC engineers tag building red. 40+ residents using alternative facilities.

title: Lower Hutt emergency water depot operational, mobile distribution fleet
description: Hutt City Council operational emergency water depot established at Lower Hutt Sports Centre. Fleet of 12 water tankers now distributing emergency supplies to surrounding residential areas. Distribution points opening at 5 additional locations.

title: Miramar Avenue: 70% debris cleared, two-lane access expected by midday
description: Excavation teams report 560 cubic metres removed; final debris pile being hauled away. Temporary traffic management being transitioned to allow two-way access via limited lane widths starting 11:45 am.

title: Miramar ground water springs appearing on residential properties
description: Residents report new groundwater springs appearing on properties due to liquefaction-induced permeability changes. Water ponding affecting soil stability.

title: Miramar residents report fatigue but no new major issues
description: Aftershock activity decreasing. No new collapses reported. Community organising support for displaced residents.

title: Miramar: Lyall Bay Road subsidiary slip creates secondary hazard
description: Small rockfall from beach approach cliff destabilising road verge. Temporary closure of one lane pending engineering review.

title: Stuff: Port disruption could trigger dairy export delays across region
description: Analysis of container vessel schedules shows estimated 200+ TEU milk powder consignments held at CentrePort. Export viability timeline unclear.

title: Water supply alert: boil water notice issued CBD zone
description: Contamination risk from sewage backup detected. Water quality testing shows possible pathogens. Boil water order in effect for affected areas.

title: Building Damage: Hotel building unsafe, CBD
description: Upmarket hotel building evacuated after ceiling collapse in lobby area. Structural surveys indicate widespread internal cracking. Building red-tagged for immediate closure.

title: Community alert: Gas leak on Customhouse Quay—evacuate
description: Wellington Fire Service confirms major gas leak at intersection of Customhouse Quay and Cable Street. Evacuation zone expanded to 500m radius; residents in adjacent buildings urged to leave.

title: Island Bay emergency access route being cleared
description: Tractor clearing main road fissures. Limited vehicle access expected within 1 hour. Relief supplies can begin arriving.

title: M4.2 aftershock, Hataitai
description: GeoNet seismograph confirmed magnitude 4.2 aftershock at 11:53 NZST, depth 8km, near Hataitai. Moderate shaking reported, building occupancy restrictions continuing.

title: RNZ: Wellington City Council declares local state of emergency; WREMO activated
description: RNZ reports that Wellington City Council has formally declared a local state of emergency under the Civil Protection Emergency Management Act. Wellington Regional Emergency Management Office is coordinating response with activated civil defence sirens across the region.

title: Rongotai isthmus - liquefaction damage assessment phase 2
description: Structural engineers begin Phase 2 assessment of residential properties on Rongotai isthmus. Foundation settling noted in multiple properties; homeowners advised displacement likely to last 1-2 weeks pending repairs.

title: Seaview-Gracefield industrial connector: temporary road stability concerns
description: Slip risk on industrial connector between Seaview and Gracefield areas. CentrePort staff rerouting.

title: Stuff: Businesses calculate estimated losses from transport disruptions
description: Economic analysis indicates $15-20M daily cost across affected sectors. Port, rail, and aviation disruptions cascading through supply chains.

title: Water depot operations request military airlift emergency supply aircraft
description: Emergency water depot operations coordinator requesting military airlift of additional emergency water supply. Aircraft being mobilized for emergency airfreight delivery of bottled water.

title: Building inspection teams coordinating assessment strategy
description: Structural engineers working systematically through damaged areas. Red tagging accelerating. Temporary housing being arranged.

title: CBD liquefaction damage economic impact initial estimate
description: WREMO preliminary assessment indicates building damage costs exceeding NZD 50 million in CBD waterfront zone alone. Insurer emergency response teams mobilizing.

title: Community report: traffic backing off SH1 northbound, heading via local roads
description: Local resident reports State Highway 1 northbound still congested, vehicles diverting to Normandale Road and Puriri Street to avoid Ngauranga closure. Local roads reaching capacity.

title: Commuter report: Hutt Valley motorway now passable but extremely congested
description: Single-lane northbound flow creating 90-minute delays peak hour equivalent. Buses and emergency vehicles given priority passage.

title: Karori reservoir drainage completed, structural damage assessment underway
description: Karori reservoir completely drained pending structural engineering assessment. Concrete cistern damage evaluated for safety. Interim repairs estimated 2-3 days. Water supply to Karori area dependent entirely on portable tanker delivery. Long-term repair plan being developed.

title: M3.1 aftershock, Hataitai
description: GeoNet seismograph confirmed magnitude 3.1 aftershock at 11:54 NZST, depth 8km. Hataitai area registered light to moderate shaking.

title: Northern suburbs: Johnsonville and Tawa power still critical
description: Wellington Electricity crew finally accessing damage site. Transformer replacement required; ETA for full restoration 5-6 hours minimum.

title: Rongotai: runway area flooding preventing airport ground operations
description: Water pooling across 3+ hectares. Subsidence creating interconnected low zones. Emergency pumping insufficient to drain. Flight operations impact growing.

title: Structural Assessment: Stairwell structural integrity
description: Central stairwell showing separation from main building structure. Stairs cracked; support systems compromised. Stairwell access prohibited; alternate routes mandatory.

title: Structural assessment: school on Manners Street
description: WCC structural engineer on site conducting red-tag assessment on Manners Street.

title: Te Aro now mostly restored; scattered street repairs ongoing
description: Majority of Te Aro suburb now with stable power. Wellington Electricity completing isolation and repair work on small tertiary streets. Estimated 90% restoration complete for residential area.

title: Upper city suburbs losing water as network breaks accumulate
description: Karori and Brooklyn reporting water shortages as cascading breaks reduce overall system capacity.

title: Aftershock M3.9—continuing diminishing aftershock sequence
description: Magnitude 3.9 aftershock recorded. Shaking barely perceptible to residents; instruments only. Aftershock sequence clearly entering later phases.

title: Commuter report: SH2 single-lane operation showing coordination problems
description: Posted observation notes traffic signal timing inconsistencies creating bunching. NZTA teams already addressing synchronisation; improvement expected soon.

title: Emergency Operations Centre - status update 11:11 NZST
description: WREMO Emergency Operations Centre briefing: 800+ residents evacuated, 750+ in welfare centres, 120+ hospital admissions, zero confirmed deaths. All major utilities assessed; power restoration prioritized for pump stations.

title: Evening rush hour power demand ramping up; network coping successfully
description: Wellington Electricity managing increased evening load from restaurants, bars, and evening shopping. No significant strain on recovering transmission network.

title: Herald: Lower Hutt residential area liquefaction causing ground fissures
description: NZ Herald field reporters in Lower Hutt CBD observe ground fissures opening in residential areas near the CBD perimeter. Liquefaction is causing footpaths to buckle and crack; several homes show visible foundation displacement.

title: Miramar: suburban neighbourhood blockade due to infrastructure failure
description: 30-40cm ground subsidence affecting valley floor neighbourhood. Water main ruptures in 3 locations. Roads buckled. Street sealed to through traffic.

title: Petone residents evacuated from flooded ground floors
description: Water levels in basement apartments rising. Fire and Emergency crews assisting evacuation. Approximately 12 properties affected.

title: Structural assessment: heritage building on Allen Street
description: Engineers documenting damage for insurance and recovery planning on Allen Street.

title: Thorndon water pressure restored to most properties
description: Water supply returning to normal. Boil water advisory remaining but residents able to bathe and clean.

title: Wadestown Hill Road: debris fan from residential slope blocking lower section
description: Hillside material spreading across Wadestown Hill Road near Ngaio Road. Residents advised to use alternate routes.

title: Gas Leak: Residential property, Hataitai
description: Neighbourhood residents smelling gas around residential property. Gas supplier investigating pipe fracture under driveway. Supply isolated; area ventilating naturally.

title: M4.0 aftershock, Oriental Bay
description: Magnitude 4.0 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 11:56 NZST, depth 6km. Oriental Bay area experienced continued ground motion with minor additional liquefaction.

title: Miramar residential area: minor subsidence on Mahina Terrace
description: Small settlement affecting suburban road. WCC monitoring for growth.

title: News update: Media reports on metro-wide water crisis escalation
description: National media outlets now reporting metropolitan-wide water supply crisis with boil water notice affecting 400,000+ residents. RNZ citing emergency response coordination challenges. Supply expected to remain critically constrained for 48+ hours.

title: Oriental Bay residents requesting urgent property assessment
description: Multiple homeowners unable to determine if properties salvageable. Engineering teams overwhelmed with requests.

title: Oriental Bay: foreshore parking lot reopened, driving restrictions lifted
description: Structural assessment confirms foreshore uplift stable; parking area fences removed. Eastern parking loop now open; western foreshore remains one-way for another 4 hours pending inspection of adjacent buildings.

title: Porirua CBD environmental hazard controlled; power restoration delayed
description: Wellington Electricity: substation oil spill contained by emergency crew. Environmental protocols require 8-hour standoff before restart; repair window pushed to midday.

title: RNZ: First northbound passenger bus convoy leaves Wellington 11:00
description: Metlink running convoy service with three buses to coordinate with reduced motorway capacity. Service will run hourly until full frequency resumes.

title: Thorndon historic building facade partially collapsed
description: 1920s era heritage building on Tinakori Road losing facade sections. Building condemned; adjacent structures red-tagged pending assessment.

title: Building Damage: Residential plumbing fixture failure
description: Water supply lines ruptured in residential building. Water pooling in basement; electrical hazard from water intrusion. Emergency plumbing response initiated.

title: Community report: Tawa residents unharmed; local roads passable but damaged
description: Tawa resident reports that the suburb has sustained minor infrastructure damage with cracked roads and broken fence lines, but no significant injuries or liquefaction. A community coordination centre is opening at the local RSA.

title: Hataitai - precautionary evacuation escalated to mandatory
description: WCC escalates Hataitai precautionary advisory to mandatory evacuation order. Additional liquefaction subsidence observed on Beach Road and adjacent streets; residents instructed to move to assembly point at Oval 59 by 11:30 NZST.

title: Hataitai cleanup operations expanded with equipment arrival
description: Heavy earthmoving equipment now on site. Silt removal accelerated. Street reopening timeline advancing.

title: Lower Hutt feeder bypass activation successful; widespread power return imminent
description: Wellington Electricity reports Hutt Central feeder bypass line successfully energized and stable at full capacity. Power being gradually restored to Lower Hutt CBD and eastern suburbs.

title: M3.0 aftershock, Oriental Bay
description: Magnitude 3.0 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 11:57 NZST, depth 7km. Oriental Bay area experienced light tremor.

title: Medical facilities reporting water restrictions, services affected
description: Dental offices, small clinics, and healthcare providers running with limited water. Procedures postponed.

title: National news media coverage escalating international emergency alerts issued
description: National media coverage escalating with international news agencies reporting on Wellington water crisis. Global emergency alerts issued warning of severe infrastructure failure.

title: RNZ: 'Multiple fatalities confirmed—death toll rising'
description: RNZ reports 14 confirmed fatalities from quake-related injuries, building collapses, and secondary effects. 67 serious injuries hospitalized; 140+ minor injuries treated.

title: RNZ: First full Wairarapa Line service departs 12:30; temporary embankment bypass
description: Limited 50-seat service trial runs successfully on temporary track loop. Passenger confidence building; full capacity operations targeted for 17:00.

title: Structural assessment: Victorian terrace on Marion Street
description: Heritage building assessment initiated by WCC structural team on Marion Street.

title: Wellington CBD waterfront: staged evacuation of remaining occupants
description: After-hours staff being cleared from subsidence zone buildings. Essential services only remaining. 150+ workers relocated to welfare facilities.

title: Hataitai main fracture discovered during pressure survey
description: 200mm service line cracked, creating cavity beneath street. Water loss estimated at 150,000 litres daily. Emergency repair crew mobilized.

title: Karori Road major settlement: 30cm step at creek crossing now impassable
description: Liquefaction at watercourse has created significant differential movement. No vehicles advised until permanent repairs undertaken.

title: KiwiRail: Wairarapa Line embankment repair progressing ahead of schedule
description: Temporary track bypass installed; limited single-car services to trial operation commence 13:00. Full service restoration expected 19:00.

title: Newtown Constitutio Avenue: temporary closure lifted, one-way traffic only
description: Building inspector clearance received for Constitutio Avenue businesses; engineering supports installed for parking structure. Road now one-way eastbound with westbound diverted via Hanson Street; full two-way by 14:00.

title: Petone businesses assessing building damage for insurance
description: Shopkeepers photographing damage. Insurance adjusters arriving. Business interruption concerns raised.

title: Petone liquefaction extent mapped by aerial survey
description: Drone survey imagery confirms liquefaction affecting reclaimed land corridor from beach to main transport corridor. Subsidence up to 0.6m in worst areas.

title: Residential heating systems causing load surge; consumption near peak levels
description: Early evening heating demand straining power network as temperatures dropping. Wellington Electricity managing load flow to prevent overload conditions.

title: Stuff: 'Power cuts cripple Wellington transport and commerce'
description: News article reporting traffic congestion from manual intersections, shop closures, and water pressure loss. Estimated loss NZ$1.2M per hour during crisis.

title: Thorndon second burst Jackson Street intersection flooding impassable
description: Second major main rupture detected under Jackson Street in Thorndon near police station. Street flooding making intersection completely impassable. Water flow diameter spray 5m high from road. Immediate evacuation of nearby businesses ordered.

title: Thorndon: structural stabilisation underway, 24-hour observation protocol
description: Engineers installing real-time settlement monitoring sensors. Data logging every 15 minutes. Four buildings under surveillance. Settlement rate slowing.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 3.7 aftershock; sequence now entering secondary phase
description: GeoNet recorded a 3.7 magnitude aftershock at 11:57 NZST. Seismic analysts note that the aftershock frequency is declining; the sequence is transitioning into a secondary decay phase.

title: Jackson Street main water flow estimates astronomical loss uncontrolled
description: Wellington Water estimating Jackson Street main rupture losing approximately 150,000 litres per hour. Water loss uncontrolled due to pressure dynamics. Continued massive waste of increasingly precious resource.

title: KiwiRail: Hutt Line temporary bypass track now operational; two-way service starting
description: Temporary shunt installed allowing service operation around subsidence zone. Track workers continuing ballast replacement on main line; dual operation feasible.

title: M3.9 aftershock, Mount Victoria
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 3.9 aftershock at 11:59 NZST, depth 7km. Mount Victoria hillside area felt the tremor with minimal additional damage.

title: Petone-Seaview railway crossing area: minor subsidence near tracks
description: Track approach roads showing small settlement. KiwiRail monitoring for rail integrity.

title: Seaview port damage assessment continuing with caution
description: Engineers evaluating structural integrity of dock facilities. Partial operations possible within 2-3 days.

title: Structural Assessment: Museum building quake damage
description: Museum facility showing structural concerns. Display cabinets overturned; roof framing cracking detected. Heritage contents assessment ongoing; building closure extended.

title: Structural assessment: warehouse on Manners Street
description: Preliminary inspection indicates yellow-tag status, further assessment required on Manners Street.

title: Te Aro structural assessment - Taranaki Gully bridge safe
description: Structural engineers complete rapid assessment of pedestrian bridge over Taranaki Gully in Te Aro. Bridge declared safe for continued use; traffic diversions can be modified. Reopening planned for 12:00 NZST.

title: Thorndon and Kelburn cut off—bridge access limited
description: Kelburn Bridge showing minor cracking; weight restrictions imposed. Thorndon access now single-route via Ghuznee Street. Emergency traffic management activated.

title: Brooklyn and Wadestown: hillside areas remain unaffected by outages
description: Resident confirms higher elevation districts have power. Wellington Electricity: hill suburbs on separate distribution circuit; priority restoration to lowland liquefaction zones.

title: CBD flooding spreading, water ingress into offices and retail spaces
description: Water from multiple main breaks now flowing down CBD streets affecting multiple office and retail complexes. Water levels reaching first-floor levels in some areas. Business interruption significant. Cleanup efforts hampered by power outages and continued water loss.

title: CentrePort: Berth 1 vessel completes partial offload; redeploys to Auckland
description: Container vessel 'Spirit of Wellington' departs CentrePort with 1,800 of 2,400 TEU discharged. Remaining cargo transferred to alternate routing.

title: Gas Leak: Residential hot water heater disconnect
description: Hot water heater gas line disconnected in residential unit. Pilot light extinguished; water heating services lost. Gas company scheduling repair assessment.

title: Kilbirnie residents seeking refuge accommodation urgently
description: Multiple families displaced by building damage. Red Zone properties inaccessible. Emergency housing being organized.

title: M2.9 aftershock, Ngauranga
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 2.9 aftershock at 12:00 NZST, depth 9km. Ngauranga Gorge area felt light shaking.

title: Media: NZTA achieves Ngauranga Gorge breakthrough, interstate freight resuming
description: 1News reports SH1 northbound lane 1 fully cleared; NZTA commencing full two-way traffic regime. First heavy trucks now moving through gorge after 3.5-hour closure. Transport ministers cite rapid recovery progress.

title: Petone main flooding moving downstream, affecting lower areas
description: Water flowing downhill accumulating in lowest neighborhoods. Additional properties now experiencing flooding.

title: Petone waterfront: wharfage showing 25-30cm differential settlement
description: Crack visible along wharf deck. Berthing faces now misaligned with vessel gangways. Port operations frozen. Engineering team assessing repair feasibility.

title: Seaview container terminal full suspension of cargo operations
description: CentrePort announces complete operational halt of container terminal due to comprehensive ground subsidence affecting infrastructure integrity.

title: Thorndon Quays substation at 85% capacity; full waterfront power expected soon
description: Three transformer banks now fully operational at Thorndon Quays. Wellington Electricity operators confident of full service restoration to Quays and Thorndon by 13:00. Final checks underway.

title: Aftershock M3.7—minimal felt impact reported
description: Magnitude 3.7 aftershock detected. Very few residents report feeling this event; mainshock aftereffects dominating attention.

title: CBD flooding water level assessment continuing rising threatening basements
description: CBD flooding water level assessment showing water continuing to rise in basement levels. Standing water now approaching 2m depth in some underground carparks. Structural damage risk increasing.

title: CentrePort: 'Spirit of Wellington' departs after partial discharge; cargo routing optimised
description: Container vessel clears berth with 1,800 TEU loaded; remaining 600 TEU diverted. Vessel slot released for next priority cargo ship.

title: Frequency stability achieved; protection systems now automatic
description: Wellington Electricity reports grid frequency stable at 50Hz standard. All protection systems returned to automatic operation.

title: Island Bay foreshore erosion and water main exposure
description: Coastal subsidence from liquefaction exposing 150mm water line at waterline. Sea infiltration risk. Temporary above-ground bypass installed.

title: Lower Hutt welfare centre - 380 residents sheltered
description: Hutt Valley High School welfare centre reporting 380 residents sheltered at 11:17 NZST. Medical team treating minor injuries and acute stress reactions. Water, food, and sanitation facilities adequate; capacity reaching 65%.

title: Newshub: Civic Centre outdoor forecourt set up as main welfare distribution hub
description: Newshub reports that the Civic Centre forecourt is now functioning as the primary welfare distribution hub. Water, blankets, first aid supplies, and food are being distributed to over 1,000 registered evacuees.

title: Ngauranga Gorge southbound: finally opened with alternating one-way traffic
description: After 4+ hours clearance, temporary traffic management allowing south flow with 30kph limit.

title: Rongotai surface damage repair estimated at several weeks
description: Runway cracks extensive and deep. Reconstruction project will be major undertaking. Temporary closure confirmed.

title: Seaview: 300+ industrial zone staff relocated to safety assembly points
description: Widespread liquefaction causing cascading business closures. Petrochemical facility on alert. Staff transported to welfare facilities. Containment procedures activated.

title: Structural assessment: school on Majoribanks Street
description: Structural engineer assessing foundation damage and wall stability on Majoribanks Street.

title: 1News: Air NZ operates first full-capacity departure post-quake at 12:15
description: Boeing 737 bound for Auckland departs with 180 passengers. Runway capacity now sustainable at 20-minute intervals for daylight operations.

title: Building Damage: Parking structure instability, Lower Hutt
description: Multi-level car park showing significant structural distress. Concrete cracking evident across levels. Access lanes cordoned; engineers assessing capacity limits.

title: CBD power restoration accelerated: 6,000 more customers online
description: Wellington Electricity: Lambton substation now operating at 80% capacity. All four distribution circuits carrying partial load; residential and commercial lighting restored to CBD.

title: Kilbirnie underground parking structure collapse risk identified
description: Engineer assessment identifies underground parking structure with high collapse risk due to ground subsidence and lateral spreading. Evacuation area expanded.

title: Lower Hutt CBD power fully restored with minor outages
description: Main distribution restored. Isolated areas still experiencing issues. Grid stabilisation near completion.

title: M3.8 aftershock, Wadestown
description: Magnitude 3.8 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 12:02 NZST, depth 9km. Wadestown hillside residents reported moderate shaking, some minor masonry damage.

title: Newtown northern connector: small debris field partially cleared
description: WCC removed 60% of slip material from Hanson Street approach. Second crew arriving.

title: SH1 Ngauranga: full two-way traffic now open, pilot vehicles being withdrawn
description: NZTA confirms both northbound and southbound lanes now fully passable; pilot vehicle service discontinued as of 11:32 am. Traffic volumes ramping up; minor congestion noted but moving freely.

title: Sanitation concern: Portable toilets deployed at distribution points
description: Civil Defence deploying portable sanitation facilities at emergency water distribution points due to flush toilet water supply loss. Hygiene protocol activated. Residents advised to practice water conservation and hand-washing discipline.

title: Kilbirnie retail precinct: shops sealed, business impact assessment started
description: 20+ storefronts closed. Stock damage and equipment tilting reported. Business continuity plans activated. Insurance assessors entering zone.

title: M2.8 aftershock, Johnsonville
description: Magnitude 2.8 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 12:03 NZST, depth 7km. Johnsonville residents reported light tremor.

title: Newtown school building demolition planning underway
description: Site engineers marking building for controlled demolition. Debris removal expected to begin mid-week.

title: Porirua CBD—structural damage moderate, services functioning
description: Porirua City Council reports moderate building damage to city centre. No major collapses. Services operational; civil defence volunteers assisting with welfare checks.

title: Power restoration plan - CBD priority 1
description: Wellington Electricity announces phased power restoration plan. CBD priority 1, restoration targeting 12:30-13:00 NZST. Hospital, emergency services, water pumps already on generators; 15,000+ customers still without power.

title: Public health alert sanitation conditions deteriorating disease risk warning
description: Public health authority issuing alert on deteriorating sanitation conditions across metro area. Disease outbreak risk warning for affected populations. Hygiene protocols essential.

title: Recovery operations begin, damage assessment teams dispatched
description: Wellington Water initiating systematic survey of all identified breaks. Prioritizing essential service reconnections.

title: Seaview generators serving port; permanent grid connection ETA 90 minutes
description: Temporary generators running at CentrePort container terminal providing 15MW. Main grid restoration via Gracefield feeder anticipated by 14:00 subject to ongoing structural inspections.

title: Structural Assessment: Infill wall out-of-plane failure
description: Infill walls bowing outward from main structure. Connections to frame failed. Temporary bracing installed; structural remediation planning underway.

title: Structural assessment: heritage building on Allen Street
description: Multi-storey building undergoing systematic floor-by-floor inspection on Allen Street.

title: Stuff: Structural engineers begin rapid assessment of CBD heritage buildings
description: Stuff reports that a team of structural engineers has commenced rapid post-earthquake assessment of heritage buildings in the CBD. Priority is given to occupied buildings and those with visible façade damage.

title: Wellington Airport: Cargo flight operations restart; first departures 13:00
description: Emergency freight services resume with reduced passenger options. Cargo capacity at 85% normal; scheduling prioritises high-value shipments.

title: Demand response programs deactivated; supply now exceeding requirements
description: Wellington Electricity canceling all demand reduction programs as supply capacity now exceeds peak consumption requirements.

title: Hospital emergency departments now coping with volume
description: Additional staff arriving. Patient flow improving. Injury count stabilising at 48 confirmed cases.

title: Hutt City Council: SH2 surface restoration team sets up repair zones
description: Three permanent repair sites established between Petone and Seaview. Compaction and stitching scheduled daily 22:00-06:00 to minimise traffic impact.

title: Hutt Valley main trunk bypass valve repair commenced isolation sequence
description: Wellington Water crews manually operating Hutt Valley trunk main isolation sequence to bypass surge damage. Complex valve sequencing required to maintain any water flow to Lower Hutt CBD area. Estimated 2-hour procedure with high precision requirements.

title: Quay Street: traffic restored to single lane following temporary subsidence management
description: WCC contractors have filled and compacted depression. Full width restoration delayed pending monitoring.

title: Rongotai hospital building engineering assessment priority
description: Hospital facility undergoing structural engineering assessment due to ground movement. Essential services maintained; non-critical patients being relocated.

title: Rongotai: emergency water pumping teams managing 4+ m3 per minute outflow
