description: Portable high-capacity pumps deployed. Setting up discharge to stormwater system. Ground water intermingling with surface pooling. Complex drainage challenge.

title: Structural assessment: heritage building on Dixon Street
description: Structural engineer assessing foundation damage and wall stability on Dixon Street.

title: Te Aro: downed lines cleared; power restoration commencing
description: Fire and Emergency NZ confirms electrical hazard secured. Wellington Electricity beginning cable splicing on Aro Street; ETA 60 minutes to load restoration.

title: Wakefield Street CBD: demolition crane arriving, controlled facade removal initiated
description: Large mobile crane positioning at Wakefield Street to safely dismantle loose cladding from 1920s office tower. Surrounding area cordoned 50m; controlled drops underway. Estimated 3-hour operation.

title: Water main breaks keep multiplying, crews overwhelmed
description: Additional ruptures reported at Courtney Place, Cable Street, and Tory Street. Response crews unable to respond to all calls. Estimated 24-hour repair timeline.

title: Aftershock M3.5—barely noticeable to residents
description: Magnitude 3.5 aftershock detected near Wellington. Seismic activity continuing but at very low perceptibility levels.

title: Gas Leak: Apartment tower base, Thorndon
description: Gas detection systems activated at apartment tower ground level. Smell of gas perceptible in foyer; emergency ventilation running. Supply line isolation in progress.

title: Hutt City Council: Surface repair crews establish permanent work zones on SH2
description: Three dedicated repair sectors marked out between Petone and Seaview. Night-time compaction and stitching scheduled daily; traffic management refined.

title: Hutt Valley repair sequence exceeding design parameters pressure anomalies
description: Wellington Water reporting Hutt Valley manual isolation sequence encountering unexpected pressure anomalies. Valve sequencing producing unpredicted pressure effects. Procedure being modified in real-time.

title: M4.0 aftershock sequence continues
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 4.0 aftershock at 12:05 NZST, depth 8km. Aftershock sequence showing typical decay pattern with intermittent larger events.

title: Newtown welfare centre - 260 residents accommodated
description: Newtown Recreation Centre now accommodating 260 evacuees. Community volunteers preparing lunch service; additional sleeping mats arranged for overnight provision. Centre at 80% capacity; secondary site standby activated.

title: Porirua SH1 northbound: traffic surge from SH2 diversion creates delays
description: Bottleneck forming at Porirua approach due to SH2 Hutt Valley overflow. NZTA coordinating.

title: RNZ: Radio NZ broadcasting emergency information 24/7; FM networks operational
description: RNZ announces that all FM and AM networks are now broadcasting continuous emergency information, weather updates, and welfare advisories. The public is urged to monitor local RNZ Concord frequency for critical announcements.

title: Te Aro building demolition risk assessment complete
description: Five buildings scheduled for demolition. Surrounding areas cordoned off. Controlled demolition timeline released.

title: Building Damage: Roof-wall connection failure
description: Roof showing separation from supporting walls at connection points. Roof sections misaligned; water infiltration risk high. Emergency roof bracing underway.

title: First successful water main repair completed Lambton Quay
description: Temporary bypass installed around initial rupture. Water flow restored to affected area. Permanent repair scheduled.

title: Flooding Myrtle Street Lower Hutt spreading downslope residential zone
description: Flooding from Myrtle Street main break now spreading downslope affecting lower residential areas. Ground saturation creating unstable foundation conditions for homes. Engineers warning of potential subsidence risk. Evacuations recommended for lower properties.

title: Island Bay parade: inspection underway, limited pedestrian access only
description: Structural engineer on-site at Island Bay parade examining seawall; temporary shoring materials being installed. Full closure remains in place; pedestrians only via parallel Reef Street.

title: Kilbirnie retailers back online; supermarket power stable
description: Countdown and New World Kilbirnie reopening with power restored. Food storage systems brought back to operating temperature. Normal trading hours resuming from 13:00.

title: M2.7 aftershock, Porirua
description: GeoNet seismograph recorded magnitude 2.7 aftershock at 12:06 NZST, depth 8km. Porirua area felt light shaking.

title: Miramar residential area liquefaction lateral spreading confirmed
description: GNS Science field team confirms lateral spreading mechanism affecting coastal cliff properties. Homes in Miramar Avenue facing potential ground failure within hours.

title: Miramar road networks reopening to restricted traffic
description: Main arterial routes now passable. Local streets undergoing repair. Traffic management showing improvement.

title: Miramar: residential street repair coordination, community meeting scheduled
description: WCC organizing community information session. 80+ affected residents attending. Repair priorities and timelines being communicated. Interim road restrictions.

title: NZTA: Lane restrictions ease on Ngauranga Gorge; cautious traffic flow
description: Both lanes now open with alternating direction management. Slope instrumentation shows stable readings; geotechnical team stepping monitoring to 4-hourly.

title: Structural assessment: medical centre on Dixon Street
description: Building cleared for restricted access pending further evaluation on Dixon Street.

title: Traffic situation improving: CBD intersections resuming normal flow
description: NZTA reports manual traffic control effective. Lambton Quay and Featherston Street now flowing at capacity; officers able to stand down from most intersections by 1200 hrs.

title: Community report: Mount Victoria walking track damaged; access closed
description: A resident reports that the Mount Victoria track has sustained significant damage with trail surface disruption and loose rock hazards. DoC and local volunteers are assessing; public access is prohibited indefinitely.

title: Myrtle Street subsidence worsening foundation cracking spreading rapidly
description: Myrtle Street area subsidence worsening with foundation cracking spreading rapidly among residential properties. Geotechnical team warning of potential catastrophic failure.

title: NZTA: Ngauranga Gorge slope instrumentation shows stable conditions; monitoring continues
description: Geotechnical array confirms no additional movement. Speed restriction adjustment to 90 km/h planned for 14:00; normal speeds by 17:00 if stable.

title: Petone container operations now at 50% capacity; full operations by 14:00
description: CentrePort resuming container terminal operations with half-fleet equipment. Full operations expected as permanent grid power fully stabilized.

title: SCADA network showing stabilization in eastern zones
description: Valve isolation procedures effective. Pressure readings rising slowly in Miramar, Rongotai sectors. Full system recovery estimated 8+ hours.

title: Seaview industrial - all hazmat assessments complete, safe
description: Fire and Emergency NZ completes hazmat assessment of Seaview industrial zone. All chemical and fuel storage facilities declared structurally sound; no active leaks detected. Zone remains evacuated pending building-by-building inspection.

title: Stuff: 'Emergency services stretched—mutual aid requested'
description: Stuff reports Fire and Emergency NZ has formally requested mutual aid from Hutt Valley and Porirua services. Auckland and Christchurch fire brigades also mobilizing assistance.

title: Thorndon Quay small slip: debris partially blocking one lane near Frank Kitts Park
description: Material from hillside above Quay Street blocking outbound traffic. Single lane open with flagging crew.

title: Thorndon waterfront businesses assessing structural damage
description: Shop and office owners documenting losses. Insurance claim process beginning. Business continuity planning underway.

title: Wellington CBD: structural engineering phase commencing, blue tarps installed
description: Temporary roof coverings deployed on buildings with facade damage. Engineering consultation contracts issued. 60-day assessment timeline established.

title: CBD building settlement monitoring network deployed
description: Real-time settlement monitoring sensors installed on 15 high-risk buildings. Data transmission to WREMO emergency centre for hazard tracking.

title: Island Bay coastal erosion response being coordinated
description: Coastal engineers onsite. Erosion containment measures being deployed. Long-term protection plan required.

title: M3.7 aftershock, Brooklyn area
description: Magnitude 3.7 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 12:08 NZST, depth 7km. Brooklyn hillside residents felt continued tremors amid ongoing emergency response.

title: Metlink: Miramar bus depot damage assessed; alternative depot online
description: Buses relocated to Seatoun depot; services to/from Miramar now operate via Berhampore loop. Estimated 8-day repair timeline for main facility.

title: Miramar mobile transformer extended: partial Hataitai restoration
description: Wellington Electricity: extended feeder to Hataitai now supplying Aro Street corridor. Estimated 1,900 residential customers restored; central Hataitai to follow.

title: Petone Jackson Street repair progress stalled, water pressure swings
description: Jackson Street main repair work halted due to unexpected water pressure surges from upstream network changes. Repair crew retreating from site temporarily. Pressure monitoring required before repair resumption. Setback extends timeline to estimated 8+ hours.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley Seaview: temporary bridge installed, single-lane bypass active
description: NZTA rapid response team has erected temporary steel Bailey bridge adjacent to damaged Seaview overpass. Single-lane alternating traffic now in operation; estimated 48-hour use pending permanent repair.

title: Structural Assessment: Art gallery structural integrity concerns
description: Modern art gallery building showing facade displacement. Glass exterior showing stress fractures. Internal wall systems showing major cracking; specialist assessment ordered.

title: Structural assessment: school on Vivian Street
description: Building being evaluated for occupancy, engineers installing monitoring equipment on Vivian Street.

title: Te Aro eastern boundary: liquefaction affecting wider corridor than expected
description: Settlement spreading to Victoria Street area. WCC expanding monitoring perimeter.

title: False report: 'Tsunami 3m high hitting beach NOW!'
description: Social media panic report of incoming large tsunami. Verified false—MetService tsunami advisory calls for <1m wave height. No large tsunami occurring.

title: Gas Leak: Industrial pipe vibration damage
description: Industrial gas piping showing vibration-induced damage. Micro-fractures detected at welded joints. Inspection of entire piping system ordered.

title: Herald: Wellington Hospital continues surge operations; surge staff activated
description: NZ Herald confirms that Wellington Hospital has fully activated its mass casualty surge plan. Additional clinical and support staff have been called in; current patient count exceeds 280 earthquake-related injuries.

title: Hospital emergency - additional ICU beds activated
description: Wellington Regional Hospital activates additional ICU capacity. 15 critical-care beds now operational; additional nursing staff from Hutt Valley DHB and private hospitals mobilized. 120 admissions now, 8 in critical care.

title: M2.6 aftershock, Tawa
description: Magnitude 2.6 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 12:09 NZST, depth 6km. Tawa residents felt light tremor.

title: Metlink: Miramar depot fuel spill containment successful; environmental risk minimal
description: Hazmat assessment clears immediate area for service operations. Fuel recovery equipment mobilised; depot repairs progressing on schedule.

title: Power restoration in CBD now 80 percent complete
description: Downtown areas gradually lighting up. Traffic lights functioning in main intersections. Commerce resuming.

title: Rongotai/Airport grid connection restored; temporary generators being shut down
description: Miramar link now providing full power feed to Wellington Airport. Wellington Electricity confirms stable connection. Airport facilities transitioning off backup generators to permanent power supply.

title: Thorndon: heritage building stabilisation showing positive results
description: Settlement rate declining. Temporary shoring systems performing as designed. Monitoring data confirming ground deformation stabilising. Engineering confidence increasing.

title: Water trucks making first distribution rounds in affected areas
description: Mobile water supply reaching residents in zones with complete outages. Long queues forming.

title: Brooklyn-Newtown connector: Aro Street slip has debris 3 metres onto roadway
description: Significant soil and vegetation mass from hillside. WCC engineers assessing slope for further instability risk.

title: Hataitai residents beginning property repairs with permits
description: Building consents fast-tracked for minor damage repairs. Structural work approval process streamlined.

title: Karori community concern rising, water access critical issue
description: Karori residents expressing significant concern about water access situation with reservoir offline and limited portable supply. Community leaders reporting stress and health concerns among vulnerable populations. Request for priority delivery of water tankers to elderly care facilities.

title: Miramar Avenue: debris removal completed, two-lane traffic being released
description: Final 30 cubic metres hauled away; Miramar Avenue now fully cleared. Traffic management transitioning from pilot vehicle control to two-way unrestricted flow starting 11:52 am.

title: Mobile water tankers arrive at Petone distribution centres
description: Emergency water supplies being trucked in. Residents queuing with containers. 20,000 litres per hour capacity operational.

title: Newtown welfare centre: 240 residents sheltered; supplies secured
description: Newtown School shelter fully operational. Hot water, meals, and cots provided. Wellington City Council: extending shelter operations until power restored.

title: Petone: residential evacuation complete, 60+ household relocations processed
description: Families transported to welfare facilities. Property condition inspections ongoing. Personal belongings retrieval being arranged. Community support services activated.

title: Social media: Petone residents share photos of 'massive' road gaps
description: Multiple posts show 20-30 cm vertical displacement on SH2 slip road. Bollards placed; locals warned to avoid area entirely.

title: Structural assessment: medical centre on Cuba Street
description: Heritage building assessment initiated by WCC structural team on Cuba Street.

title: Supermarket chains restocking; refrigeration systems stabilized
description: Countdown, New World, and PAK'nSAVE locations across Wellington recommencing normal cold food storage protocols.

title: Thorndon liquefaction sand ejecta coating entire blocks
description: Community reports sand blanket covering buildings and streets from sand boils. Layer depth 0.2-0.5m in places. Cleanup crews preparing assessment.

title: Building Damage: Church steeple collapse, Newtown
description: Historic church steeple collapsed during aftershock. Stone masonry scattered across surrounding area. Fortunately no one nearby; structural remains dangerous.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 3.5 aftershock; no change in tsunami advisory
description: GeoNet detected a 3.5 magnitude aftershock at 12:09 NZST. The earlier all-clear tsunami status remains; no coastal warnings are in effect.

title: Hataitai northern access: minor landslip on suburban road now cleared
description: Small debris pile removed by local crew. Single-lane operation established.

title: Karori community crisis escalating vulnerable populations struggling severely
description: Karori community crisis escalating with vulnerable populations (elderly, disabled, young children) struggling severely under water rationing conditions. Community leaders demanding emergency relief measures.

title: Kilbirnie - temporary housing arrangements being organized
description: Civil Defence coordinates temporary housing for 25 Kilbirnie residents displaced by foundation damage. Hotels in unaffected suburbs being arranged; residents expected to be relocated by 14:00 NZST.

title: M3.6 aftershock, Ngauranga Gorge
description: GeoNet confirmed magnitude 3.6 aftershock at 12:11 NZST, depth 8km, near Ngauranga Gorge. SH1 motorway corridor affected by ongoing seismic activity.

title: Petone evacuation centres now housing 200+ displaced residents
description: School and community hall converted to shelters. Food and water being distributed. Welfare services available.

title: Social media: 'Resilience of Wellington transport systems is remarkable' posts
description: Multiple commenter observations note improved traffic flow and restored services. Positive sentiment emerging as normalcy returns; thanks expressed to workers.

title: Welfare centre opening at Te Aro Community Hub
description: Second emergency welfare centre opens at Te Aro Community Hub. Supplies, accommodation information, and counselling services available. First 200 registrations processed.

title: Grid status update: 19,200 customers still without power
description: Grid telemetry: regional outage count declining. Load-shedding protocols still active but demand-response reducing pressure. Restoration trajectory on track.

title: KiwiRail: Hutt Line ballast supply secured; track resumption track 15:00
description: Material train arriving from Christchurch now on final approach. Track workers preparing ballast bed; commissioning tests begin immediately upon delivery.

title: M2.5 aftershock, Ngaio
description: GeoNet confirmed magnitude 2.5 aftershock at 12:12 NZST, depth 7km. Ngaio area registered light tremor from sequence.

title: Miramar substation stabilized; voltage controls optimized
description: Wellington Electricity confirms Miramar substation now stable with automatic voltage control fully operational. Flickering reports ceased. Area now on permanent restoration path.

title: Petone businesses organizing community cleanup efforts
description: Local shop owners coordinating to pump water from shared areas. Cooperative effort reducing isolation.

title: Petone waterfront walkway subsidence - pedestrian access closed
description: Popular waterfront walkway showing 0.4m subsidence across 200m section. Public access closed pending repair assessment and stabilization.

title: Seaview port heavy lifting operations resuming carefully
description: Mobile crane checking its structural integrity. Salvage operations beginning on damaged vessels.

title: Seaview: environmental monitoring activated for liquefaction zone
description: Water quality testing commencing. Groundwater sample collection from sand boil sites. Contamination assessment protocols initiated. Results 48 hours.

title: Structural Assessment: Concrete deterioration accelerated
description: Visible concrete deterioration in foundation areas. Seismic loading accelerating corrosion; structural life expectancy reduced. Engineering consultation required.

title: Structural assessment: school on Daniell Street
description: Structural engineer assessing foundation damage and wall stability on Daniell Street.

title: Te Aro evacuation zone expanded, flooding risk continuing to escalate
description: Te Aro flooding risk zone expanded by Civil Defence to encompass all areas downslope from main break sites. Additional 15 properties now under evacuation order. Flooding water levels still rising. Stormwater system completely incapacitated.

title: Thorndon Quay: slope monitoring stable, lane restrictions being eased
description: Hillside monitoring confirms no further movement on Thorndon Quay slope. Lane width expanding to normal gradually; speed limit reducing from 30 to 60 km/h at 12:00 noon.

title: Aftershock M3.3—part of normal decay sequence
description: Magnitude 3.3 aftershock recorded. Seismic network confirms normal aftershock decay pattern consistent with magnitude 6.5 mainshock.

title: Fire risk elevated due to water system failure citywide
description: Fire hydrant network inoperable in extensive CBD areas. Fire service implementing alternative water supply protocols. Tanker trucks on standby.

title: Kilbirnie liquefaction cleanup now mid-stage
description: Silt removal continuing. Property owners documenting losses for insurance. Remediation specialists on scene.

title: Kilbirnie: water service restoration timeline mapped, 5-10 days expected
description: Repair crew identifying main break sites. Temporary bypass lines being installed. Community advised to boil water until clearance achieved.

title: KiwiRail: Emergency ballast delivery train now unloading; completion by 16:00
description: Supply train from Christchurch discharging rock ballast at Waterloo staging area. Placement operations ramping up; track bed restoration on schedule.

title: Miramar Avenue continuing monitoring: no further expansion in pothole
description: NZTA inspecting edges hourly. Temporary asphalt patch applied. Permanent repair scheduled within 48 hours.

title: Newshub: Kenepuru Station overflow clinic treating minor earthquake injuries
description: Newshub reports that Kenepuru Station has been mobilized as an overflow clinic to treat minor earthquake-related injuries, freeing Wellington Hospital ED capacity for serious cases. Trained first aiders are staffing the facility.

title: Structural assessment: medical centre on Mitchell Street
description: Structural engineer assessing foundation damage and wall stability on Mitchell Street.

title: Te Aro landslide risk flagged from water saturation slope instability
description: Geotechnical team flagging significant landslide risk for Te Aro hillside areas from water saturation and slope instability. Precautionary evacuations being considered for upslope residential zones.

title: Thorndon heritage precinct - 4 buildings requiring repairs
description: Structural engineers identify four heritage buildings in Thorndon requiring repair following earthquake damage. Façade cracking and mortar deterioration noted; three buildings remain accessible, one temporarily cordoned.

title: Traffic signals fully restored; traffic management protocols standing down
description: Wellington City Council confirming all traffic signals throughout region now operational. Manual traffic management crews being stood down.

title: CentrePort: Crane 4 certified safe; limited use operations approved
description: Structural inspection clearance granted for Crane 4. Single-hatch container handling resumes; Cranes 1, 2, 3 still under inspection.

title: Gas Leak: School building, Te Aro
description: Gas odour detected in school building; investigation identifies broken pipe in basement mechanical room. School already closed; no occupants present.

title: Hospital water reserves depleted, rationing critical care services
description: Wellington Regional Hospital reporting water tank reserves dropping critically low. Care services being rationed to essential functions only. Elective surgeries postponed. Emergency department operating under strict water use protocols.

title: Kilbirnie local access road: small slip material blocking access
description: Hillside debris blocking residential connector. WCC crew clearing.

title: M3.5 aftershock, Johnsonville
description: Magnitude 3.5 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 12:14 NZST, depth 9km. Johnsonville area residents reported light to moderate shaking continuing.

title: Petone temporary generator online: 4,100 customers restored
description: Wellington Electricity: emergency genset at Petone substation now feeding distribution network. Partial load restored to Petone and Hutt Valley suburbs.

title: Rongotai Airport Road: temporary ramp installed, access restored
description: Mobile platform bridge now spanning damaged culvert approach on Airport Road. Two-way access restored via temporary structure; permanent repair scheduled for 72-hour window.

title: Rongotai airport staff conducting preliminary assessments
description: Runway inspection teams identifying repair priorities. Temporary fix estimates for partial operations.

title: Seaview underground water main network ruptures mapped
description: Wellington Water SCADA monitoring indicates multiple water main ruptures across industrial precinct. Pressure anomalies in 3 distinct zones.

title: Building Damage: Temporary wall system failure
description: Temporary partition walls in office building failed completely. Modular system connections compromised. All temporary partitions being removed.

title: CentrePort: Crane 3 clearance received; dual-crane cargo operations resuming
description: Structural certification complete for Crane 3. Two-crane handling now active; vessel offload rate accelerating to 40 TEU/hour.

title: Hospital emergency order reducing staff non-critical functions suspended
description: Hospital issuing emergency order reducing staff to essential personnel only. All non-critical functions suspended. Water rationing now affecting even emergency operations.

title: Johnsonville—significant building damage, road debris
description: Community reports indicate considerable building damage in Johnsonville shopping area. Road debris blocking multiple streets; debris removal crews mobilized.

title: M2.4 aftershock, Wellington CBD
description: Magnitude 2.4 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 12:15 NZST, depth 8km. CBD area experienced light tremor.

title: Miramar bypass connection successfully installed and tested
description: Temporary supply line from alternative water source operational. Peninsula residents reporting return of normal flow.

title: Newtown fully restored; power quality checks completing
description: All Newtown feeder circuits now energized and stable. Wellington Electricity conducting power quality verification scans for lingering fault conditions.

title: Rongotai isthmus - evacuation corridors secured, residents safe
description: Fire and Emergency NZ confirms all residents successfully evacuated from Rongotai isthmus at 11:41 NZST. 220 residents relocated; final check-sweep of residential streets complete with no persons remaining.

title: Rongotai: flood water level stabilising, pumping operations success
description: Water depth reducing 2-3cm per hour. Ground saturation remaining high. Drainage channels reopened. Estimates suggest 12-hour complete drainage window.

title: Structural assessment: historic church on Alexander Street
description: Building being evaluated for occupancy, engineers installing monitoring equipment on Alexander Street.

title: Stuff: Wadestown home suffers significant chimney collapse; family displaced
description: Stuff reports that a Wadestown residential property has suffered a total chimney collapse through the roof, creating a large opening. The family is displaced; a local church hall is offering temporary shelter.

title: Te Aro liquefaction impact assessment finalised
description: Formal report released. Three neighbourhoods designated as high-risk for future development. Relocation assistance announced.

title: Community report: Ngauranga traffic now flowing freely, journey times normalising
description: Commuter reports SH1 through Ngauranga returning to normal speeds; queue has cleared. Estimates journey times dropping from 2 hours to 35 minutes for Ngauranga passage.

title: Fifth CBD main break detected near parliament grounds emergency zone
description: Fifth major water main rupture detected near Parliament grounds in CBD. 280mm main broken. Area immediately surrounding Parliament complex flooded. Security perimeter expanded. Government operations relocated to alternative facilities.

title: Hospital operations returning to normal; elective procedures resuming
description: Wellington Regional Hospital resuming limited elective surgery program. Emergency departments now fully operational.

title: Injury support services and counseling now available
description: Mental health teams deployed to evacuation centres. First responder wellbeing programmes activated.

title: Johnsonville: Civic Centre Road now fully passable, flagging removed
description: Embankment stabilised following NZTA crew work. Both lanes restored with normal traffic flow.

title: Kilbirnie housing stock damage survey underway
description: Team of building inspectors conducting rapid damage assessment of 250+ residential properties. Preliminary data indicates 30% with moderate to severe damage.

title: Lower Hutt CBD: temporary feeder line deployed from Petone
description: Wellington Electricity: emergency overhead temporary line established across Hutt River floodplain. 2,800 customers in Lower Hutt CBD now receiving power.

title: Lower Hutt water outages affecting hospital operations
description: Hutt Hospital water supply interrupted by network fractures. Emergency generator cooling systems activated. Temporary supply line being connected.

title: Miramar: infrastructure damage bill estimated at 2-5 million dollars
description: Preliminary scoping identifying water mains, roads, and building damage extent. Insurance adjusters processing claims. Central Government emergency support activated.

title: Wellington Airport: Passenger boarding returns to standard procedures 13:30
description: Security and baggage services fully staffed. Flight schedule now matching pre-quake frequency; minor delays due to aircraft positioning.

title: Gas supply isolation—entire CBD area cut off
description: Wellington Gas announces isolation of gas supply to CBD and surrounding areas as precaution following multiple leak reports. Supply restoration timeline uncertain.

title: Karori welfare centre - 360 residents now sheltered
description: Karori Primary School welfare centre reaching near-capacity at 360 residents at 11:45 NZST. Overflow contingency activated; secondary site Karori Community Centre prepared to open within 60 minutes if needed.

title: M3.4 aftershock, Porirua region
description: GeoNet seismograph recorded magnitude 3.4 aftershock at 12:17 NZST, depth 10km, affecting Porirua CBD. Extended felt across northern suburbs.

title: Newtown community volunteers organizing debris clearance
description: Neighbourhood groups coordinating cleanup efforts. Trucks hauling broken concrete and rubble.

title: Parliament closure extended government services relocation underway
description: Parliament extended closure with government services relocation underway to alternative facilities outside CBD flood zone. Political operations severely disrupted.

title: RNZ: Wellington Electricity reports 95% restoration target for CBD by 13:30
description: Wellington Electricity informs RNZ that power restoration to 95% of the CBD is targeted for 13:30 NZST. Crews have restored major transmission routes and are now working on distribution substations.

title: Structural Assessment: Library stack damage assessment
description: Library building showing extensive internal damage. Bookshelves collapsed throughout levels. Structural engineers concerned about stability of internal supports.

title: Structural assessment: historic pub on Alexander Street
description: Building cleared for restricted access pending further evaluation on Alexander Street.

title: Tawa secondary road: minor settlement creates slight depression
description: Small pavement dip on connecting road. Traffic able to navigate safely.

title: Wellington Airport: Passenger flight ramp-up commencing; full schedule by evening
description: Commercial airline operations now priority. 20-minute spacing between arrivals/departures; ground handling staff returning to full complement.

title: Eastern suburbs water pressure rising, situation improving
description: Kilbirnie, Rongotai, and Hataitai reporting improved water access. Pressure gauges showing positive trend.

title: Gas Leak: Rooftop equipment gas line damage
description: Rooftop heating/cooling equipment gas supply line damaged. Equipment shutdown initiated; rooftop access restricted.

title: Island Bay residents report normal power supply; restoration official
description: Island Bay area now fully energized with stable voltage readings. Wellington Electricity marking area as restoration complete. Focus shifting to quality assurance checks.

title: Kilbirnie-Lyall Bay link: drainage reroute complete, traffic back to two-way
description: Temporary culvert reroute now fully functional; Lyall Bay Road subsidence managed via secondary stormwater bypass. Two-way unrestricted traffic confirmed from 12:10 pm.

title: M2.3 aftershock, Petone
description: GeoNet seismograph detected magnitude 2.3 aftershock at 12:18 NZST, depth 7km. Petone area felt light shaking.

title: Oriental Bay beachfront reconstruction planning beginning
description: Council meeting with affected property owners. Buyback schemes being discussed. Long-term planning urgent.

title: Rongotai airport power: commercial services preparing for restart
description: Wellington Airport: runway lighting systems confirmed functional. Commercial flight services expected to resume by 1300 hrs pending traffic control clearance.

title: Rongotai residential area power supply restoration delayed
description: Liquefaction-damaged electrical infrastructure in residential area requires replacement before power restoration. Estimated repair time 48-72 hours.

title: Stuff: 'Wellington transport network recovering faster than forecast'
description: Transport experts comment that coordinated response efforts have shortened estimated closure windows. Road and rail repairs tracking ahead of schedule.

title: Thorndon evacuation order issued, flooding critical zone expansion
description: Wellington City Council issuing evacuation order for all Thorndon residential areas within 200m of detected water main breaks. Flooding risk assessment shows continuing water accumulation. Emergency shelter being organized at nearby schools.

title: Wellington CBD waterfront: temporary access restrictions in effect
description: Cordoned zones expanded to 400m section. Public diverted via alternate routes. Business impacts significant. Temporary workplace arrangements being negotiated.

title: Aftershock M3.1—aftershock sequence well-established pattern
description: Magnitude 3.1 aftershock near epicentre. Aftershock frequency and magnitude following predicted Gutenberg-Richter distribution.

title: CBD waterfront - Zone 1 structural survey complete
description: WCC structural engineers complete survey of Zone 1 (waterfront) CBD buildings. 12 buildings require detailed inspection; 3 flagged for significant repair. Most damage assessed as cosmetic cracking and minor material damage.

title: Community alert: Small sewage overflow on Officer Street near Town Hall
description: A resident reports that sewage is overflowing from a damaged main on Officer Street near the Town Hall. Wellington Water has been notified; contractors are en route with portable fencing and cleanup equipment.

title: Karori Sanctuary area: closure remains in effect pending full assessment
description: Multiple subsidence points make area hazardous. WCC continuing 24-hour monitoring with geotechnical support.

title: Liquefaction subsidence widening main ruptures continuously
description: Ground still shifting. Cracks in fractured mains expanding. Water loss increasing despite valve closures. Structural integrity of affected lines compromised.

title: Power grid now 90 percent operational across region
description: Most suburbs have electricity restored. Few isolated pockets remaining. Water treatment plants operational.

title: Schools declaring early closure as normal operations restored
description: Wellington schools confirming return to normal operations tomorrow. Early closure today confirmed as precautionary measure.
source: Wellington City Council / Ministry of Education

title: Structural assessment: Victorian terrace on Australia Street
description: Preliminary inspection indicates yellow-tag status, further assessment required on Australia Street.

title: Stuff: Transport minister confirms $250M recovery fund; prioritizes rail restoration
description: Government announces comprehensive funding package for earthquake recovery. Priority allocation addresses rail infrastructure; phased release over 90 days.

title: Thorndon mass evacuation coordinating multiple welfare centers emergency
description: Thorndon mass evacuation coordinating multiple emergency welfare centers. 400+ residents now displaced. Emergency accommodation being arranged at school halls and community facilities.

title: Thorndon: utility service restoration commencing, phased approach
description: Water and gas service inspection teams deployed. Electrical services checked for safety. Phased restoration beginning Thursday evening. Residents updated.

title: Building Damage: Retail storefront, central business district
description: Large retail business storefront completely destroyed. Shop windows shattered; interior displays collapsed. Debris cleanup ongoing; building frame may be salvageable.

title: Commuter update: Bus capacity remains at 70%; standing room only
description: Metlink continuing staggered loading to maintain distancing protocols. Evening peak services scheduled at 10-minute headway beginning 16:00.

title: Lower Hutt Riverside Drive: partial reopening to residents, inspection ongoing
description: Hutt City Council engineers clearing Riverside Drive southbound lane; northbound remains cordoned pending façade reinforcement. Residents permitted access to properties via one-way southbound flow.

title: M3.3 aftershock, Tawa area
description: Magnitude 3.3 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 12:20 NZST, depth 8km. Tawa residents felt continued tremors as aftershock sequence progresses.

title: Miramar eastern approach: traffic congestion from diversion routes easing
description: Permanent repair site for pothole now operational. Alternative route traffic reducing.

title: Miramar groundwater mounding creating subsurface flooding hazard
description: Hydrogeological assessment indicates groundwater level rise of 1.5m in liquefied zones. Basement flooding risk imminent for lower-lying properties.

title: Portable water supply now rationing, demand exceeds available capacity
description: Emergency water distribution reporting demand now exceeding available portable supply capacity by 40%. Rationing being implemented across all distribution points. Wait times now exceeding 2 hours for 20-litre water containers.

title: Seaview/Port reefer containers: backup power sustaining inventory
description: CentrePort: backup generators performing within specifications. Temperature-controlled containers maintaining cold chain for export fresh produce.

title: Thorndon residents beginning temporary repairs program
description: Weatherproofing works continuing. Tarpaulins and plywood protecting damaged structures. Building inspectors approving quick repairs.

title: Commuter observation: Bus crowding easing; standees finding seats during off-peak
description: Posted note suggests peak-hour pressures subsiding. Service frequency supporting normal passenger volumes; comfort returning gradually.

title: Emergency water supply shortage tensions escalating distribution point
description: Emergency water supply shortage creating social tensions at distribution points. Queue management becoming difficult. Security personnel deployed to prevent altercations.

title: Hataitai water supply fully restored with restrictions lifted
description: Boil water notice cancelled. Normal supply protocols resumed. Residents grateful for service restoration.

title: Herald: Brooklyn residents report hairline cracks in residential properties
description: NZ Herald field team interviews Brooklyn residents who report hairline cracks appearing in residential foundations and walls. Structural engineers deem most properties safe with monitoring; a few have been cordoned off.

title: M2.2 aftershock, Te Aro
description: Magnitude 2.2 aftershock confirmed by GeoNet at 12:21 NZST, depth 6km. Te Aro area registered light tremor.

title: Oriental Bay transformer inspection complete; full load authorized
description: Wellington Electricity engineers completed detailed seismic damage inspection of Oriental Bay primary transformer. No structural compromise detected. Full load application now authorized.

title: Petone - phased resident return plan announced
description: WREMO and WCC announce phased return plan for Petone residents. Phase 1 (non-waterfront areas) clearance expected 14:00-15:00 NZST pending final structural assessment. Waterfront zone remains under evacuation order.

title: Petone: community welfare centre now accommodating 150+ displaced persons
description: Primary school gym converted to emergency shelter. Meals, medical support, and counselling services operational. Occupancy stable. Support duration 4-6 weeks estimated.

title: Structural Assessment: Lateral bracing capacity reduction
description: Building lateral bracing system showing stress fractures. Seismic resistance capacity reduced significantly. Structural reinforcement assessment underway.

title: Structural assessment: Victorian terrace on Young Brothers Street
description: Heritage building assessment initiated by WCC structural team on Young Brothers Street.

title: Tawa area—minor to moderate damage, no major issues
description: Tawa residents report light to moderate building damage. No major structural failures. Emergency services conduct welfare checks; all residents accounted for.

title: Te Aro businesses pumping water from premises, operational
description: Commercial occupants running portable pumps continuously. Sump systems managing drainage.

title: CBD waterfront retail precinct flooding from water mains
description: Multiple retail shops experiencing significant flooding from combined water main rupture and liquefaction-induced drainage failure. Estimated stock loss in millions.

title: Hospital fuel status: 5 hours remaining; supply truck en route
description: Wellington Regional Hospital: diesel supply truck ETA 1245 hrs. Medical operations continuing normally; critical care priority maintained.

title: Miramar peninsula now operating on emergency water supply only
description: Connection to main network severed. Portable desalination units being brought online. Tanker truck routes established for essential services.

title: Miramar traffic conditions improving significantly
description: Main arterials fully reopened. Local bypass routes established. Traffic management winding down.

title: NZTA: Road cones and barricading removed from Ngauranga Gorge northbound
description: Formal lane return completed; traffic flow management transitioning to standard incident-level procedures. Speed restrictions maintained at 80 km/h.

title: Oriental Bay: small slip near beach approach road doesn't block main traffic
description: Minor debris fall on vehicle turning area. No closure required but WCC monitoring slope.

title: Petone area water contamination confirmed analysis results returned
description: Laboratory analysis confirming water quality concerns in Petone area main network. Heavy metals detected from port area contamination event. Entire Petone water network now suspect. Boil water notice expanded with contamination advisory.

title: Seaview: industrial sector recovery coordination meeting held
description: Business leaders, council, and agencies meeting re: phased restart protocols. Safety clearance requirements being established. Data damage assessments starting.

title: Structural assessment: residential building on Adelaide Road
description: Preliminary inspection indicates yellow-tag status, further assessment required on Adelaide Road.

title: Traffic camera: Wakefield Street facade demolition progressing, partial closure continues
description: NZTA traffic sensor shows ongoing removal of cladding debris; cordoned area still active. Minor traffic delays noted on parallel Lambton Quay. Estimated completion 13:30.

title: Wastewater treatment plants power restored; environmental risk mitigated
description: Wellington Water confirming power restoration to all wastewater treatment facilities. Overflow risks eliminated.

title: Gas Leak: High-rise building mechanical systems, CBD
description: High-rise building mechanical room showing gas detector activation. HVAC system damage suspected from seismic movement. Building-wide ventilation systems engaged.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 3.3 aftershock; sequence magnitude distribution normalizing
description: GeoNet recorded a 3.3 magnitude aftershock at 12:21 NZST. The aftershock magnitude distribution is now following expected statistical decay patterns; major seismic risk is substantially diminished.

title: Island Bay access now restored allowing supply delivery
description: Emergency provisions reaching isolated residents. Medical support vehicles able to enter suburb.

title: M3.2 aftershock near Ngaio
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 3.2 aftershock at 12:23 NZST, depth 7km, near Ngaio. Minor shaking reported in surrounding suburbs.

title: Mount Victoria area residential: small slip on side access not impacting main route
description: Debris on residential lane only. Primary mountain access still restricted.

title: NZ Herald: 'Earthquake declared national emergency'
description: NZ Herald reports government declares the Wellington earthquake a national emergency. Additional Defence Force resources authorized; Emergency Management Act provisions activated.

title: NZTA: SH1 lanes fully open in both directions; monitoring alert status downgraded
description: Ngauranga Gorge now operating under standard incident protocol. Geotechnical team shifting to 24-hour observation cycle; slope instrumentation logging continues.

title: Newtown area - precautions lifted for hill suburbs
description: WCC lifts precautionary evacuation advisory for Brooklyn and Karori hill areas. Only low liquefaction risk areas affected; residents in hill suburbs may return to normal activities. CBD and waterfront areas remain restricted.

title: Petone port operations suspended indefinite closure contamination protocols
description: CentrePort operations suspended indefinitely due to contamination protocols. Port area declared hazardous zone. Shipping operations rerouted to alternative ports.

title: Building Damage: Masonry infill cracking, widespread
description: Widespread masonry infill cracking observed across multiple building facades. Mortar joints systematically failed. Stabilization measures required.

title: Hataitai feeder repairs finished; circuit re-energized and stable
description: Wellington Electricity completed conductor replacement on Hataitai feeder. Circuit tested and confirmed stable. Full power restoration to Hataitai suburb now complete.

title: Kilbirnie: road reconstruction contractor mobilised for street repairs
description: WCC contracting major works. Preliminary estimates: 3-4 weeks for core road repairs. Asphalt and subgrade replacement required. Traffic management planning.

title: M2.1 aftershock, Lower Hutt
description: GeoNet recorded magnitude 2.1 aftershock at 12:24 NZST, depth 8km. Lower Hutt area felt light shaking.

title: Petone business community beginning recovery planning
description: Chamber of Commerce meeting held. Business interruption insurance discussed. Recovery loan schemes presented.

title: RNZ: Major roads recovering as repair teams make rapid headway
description: Radio New Zealand reports SH1 now clear, Miramar Avenue reopened, temporary solutions deployed for SH2 Seaview and Rongotai. Emergency response transitioning to full recovery mode; non-essential restrictions being lifted.

title: RNZ: Port damage 'will be measured in months, not weeks'
description: Industry analyst suggests CentrePort full capacity restoration likely 90+ days out. Economic projections revised for regional trade volumes.

title: SCADA system shows plant output declining further 5% capacity remaining
description: Wellington Water treatment plant telemetry showing continued output decline to just 5% normal capacity. Multiple inlet main breaks continue to prevent incoming water for treatment. System approaching complete shutdown risk.

title: Structural assessment: residential building on Rintoul Street
description: Structural engineer assessing foundation damage and wall stability on Rintoul Street.

title: Thorndon building red-tag count reaches 18 structures
description: Building safety assessment brings red-tag count to 18 in Thorndon precinct. All occupants evacuated; perimeter security established.

title: Thorndon cable splice: major progress; one circuit restored
description: Wellington Electricity: underwater cable repair 40% complete. First distribution circuit now operational under Lambton Harbour.

title: Thorndon water levels dropping to safe levels
description: Street flooding subsiding. Businesses reporting water levels below ground floor openings.

title: Aftershock M2.9—barely recorded, sequence tail
description: Magnitude 2.9 aftershock detected. Aftershock sequence entering exponentially decaying tail phase.

title: Hospital surge - 135 total admissions, stable trajectory
description: Wellington Regional Hospital reports 135 total admissions at 11:51 NZST. Admission rate slowing; majority of acute phase injuries treated. Discharge of minor-injury patients beginning; ICU occupancy at 65%.

title: Newshub: Pharmacies and medical supply shops reopening with emergency protocols
description: Newshub reports that essential pharmacies and medical supply shops in unaffected suburbs are reopening under emergency protocols. Customers are urged to purchase only essential medications and supplies to ensure adequate stock for all residents.

title: Public transport power systems online; bus and train services resuming
description: Wellington public transport infrastructure power restored. Regional buses and KiwiRail services preparing for full service reinstatement.

title: RNZ: Port recovery timeline 'extended but manageable' per CentrePort spokesperson
description: Public statement indicates 4-6 week restoration of full capacity operations. Crane 2 replacement critical path item; interim alternatives being explored.

title: Rongotai: airport terminal water usage restrictions lifted after clearing
description: On-site testing confirms water main integrity restored. Contamination protocol cleared. Partial terminal operations resuming. Runway operations still halted.

title: Seaview port operations achieving partial capacity
description: One dock operational for cargo handling. Crane operations recommencing. Export schedule being rearranged.

title: Te Aro: Wakefield Street settlement spreading, now two blocks cordoned
description: Liquefaction-induced differential movement affecting pavement over wider area. WCC expanding exclusion zone.

title: Thorndon sewage system overwhelmed by groundwater influx
description: Broken water mains forcing sewage to surface. Raw sewage pooling in streets. Health and safety protocols activated. Biohazard zones marked.

title: Treatment plant shutdown imminent complete failure expected within hours
description: Kaiwharawhara treatment plant approaching complete shutdown with failure expected within hours. No water treatment occurring. Metropolitan area entirely dependent on emergency portable supply.

title: Aftershock sequence update from WREMO
description: WREMO issued update at 12:26 NZST on aftershock sequence. Magnitude 3.2-3.1 events occurring approximately every 2-3 minutes. Public advised to remain on alert.

title: Island Bay parade: temporary shoring complete, inspection scheduled 14:00
description: Structural bracing now installed supporting seawall; parade pedestrian access still limited but vehicle restrictions remaining for final geotechnical assessment. Inspector appointment confirmed 14:00.

title: Kilbirnie liquefaction recovery accelerating with contractors
description: Heavy equipment fleet now extensive. Silt removal near completion. Road reconstruction beginning.

title: KiwiRail Track 2 cleared; spot repairs begin on Track 1 subsidence
description: Second track on Hutt Line now open for limited southbound service. Workers excavating ballast bed on Track 1 for compaction and stabilisation.

title: Lower Hutt residential isolation worsening, secondary roads flooded out
description: Lower Hutt secondary residential roads now impassable due to water main flooding. Multiple residential streets completely submerged. Residents unable to access evacuation routes. SES establishing alternative routes and boat access points.

title: Ngaio local roads: minor subsidence on secondary connector
description: Small settlement area closed to traffic. Primary routes unaffected.

title: Petone emergency response HQ established at municipal building
description: Petone emergency operations centre activated with representatives from Fire, Police, and Council. Coordination centre for area response operations.

title: Structural Assessment: Educational building brickwork
description: University building showing significant brickwork cracking throughout facade. Mortar joints failing systematically. Temporary closure ordered; comprehensive structural testing scheduled.

title: Structural assessment: Victorian terrace on Riddiford Street
description: Multi-storey building undergoing systematic floor-by-floor inspection on Riddiford Street.

title: Te Aro power restored: 3,100 customers back online by 1045 hrs
description: Wellington Electricity: Aro Street cable fully spliced. Te Aro residential district receiving full load; heating and comms systems operational.

title: Brooklyn resident: 'Neighbours helping clear debris'
description: Community report from Brooklyn: local residents organizing to clear fallen branches and debris from streets. Neighbors checking on elderly residents.

title: Cable Street bypass connecting successfully to network
description: Temporary main section installed around major fracture. Pressure building. Secondary breaks not anticipated.

title: Gas Leak: Kitchen exhaust ductwork damage
description: Commercial kitchen exhaust ductwork showing gas line contact damage. Gas supply line fractured; kitchen operations ceased. Ductwork and gas line replacement required.

title: Karori continues stable operation; background monitoring ongoing
description: Karori area maintaining stable power supply with no reported fluctuations. Wellington Electricity continuing SCADA telemetry monitoring to detect any secondary damage patterns.

title: KiwiRail: Hutt Line main track ballast replacement 80% complete; resumption near
description: Night crew continuing work; final section compaction underway. Full commissioning testing scheduled for 15:00-16:00; service restoration expected 17:00.

title: Lower Hutt road isolation worsening secondary routes now impassable also
description: Lower Hutt road isolation worsening with secondary routes now impassable. Emergency vehicle access severely restricted. Helicopter evacuation being considered for critical cases.

title: M2.0 aftershock, Miramar
description: Magnitude 2.0 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 12:27 NZST, depth 7km. Miramar area registered light tremor.

title: Miramar: initial building repair scope defined, engineering reports ready
description: Council-commissioned structural engineers completed damage assessments. Four buildings requiring major repairs, eight moderate. Demolition recommendations pending.

title: Oriental Bay - post-tsunami inspection reveals minor damage
description: MetService and WCC post-tsunami inspection of Oriental Bay area. Minor damage to small boats and promenade fencing; no structural damage to buildings. Cordon to remain in place for 2-3 hours pending wave forecast clearance.

title: Rongotai runway repairs beginning with priority scheduling
description: Repair crews working 24-hour shifts. Partial reopening estimated for 10-14 days.

title: Stuff: CentrePort coordinating urgent container offload; port operations scaled back
description: Stuff reports that CentrePort is initiating a scaled-down offload operation for containerised cargo. Due to liquefaction damage, only manual unloading from stable dockside positions is permitted; throughput is at 10% of normal capacity.

title: Broadband and telecommunications backbone restored; internet traffic normal
description: Major internet exchange points and telecommunications infrastructure power fully restored. Internet network performance returning to baseline.

title: CentrePort vessel scheduling now accepting bookings for 16 July onwards
description: Booking window reopens as berth availability improves. Initially at 60% capacity; full capacity expected restoration 20 July pending crane inspections.

title: Media alert: Nationwide water crisis now headline story, interstate aid
description: National news outlets now leading with Wellington water crisis story. Stuff and Newshub reporting on catastrophic infrastructure failure and emergency response challenges. Interstate disaster aid offers being received from Auckland and Christchurch councils.

title: SCADA alerts: Multiple secondary main failures detected
description: Four additional ruptures identified through automated pressure monitoring. Locations: Newtown line, Kilbirnie lateral, Seaview lateral, Karori connection.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley: full normal traffic resumed after slip remediation
description: Both lanes fully open, speed restrictions lifted. NZTA presence reduced to periodic inspections.

title: Seaview industrial fire risk elevated due to infrastructure damage
description: Fire and Emergency NZ identifies elevated fire risk across industrial area due to damaged utilities and chemical storage concerns. Patrols increased.

title: Structural assessment: retail complex on Rintoul Street
description: Heritage building assessment initiated by WCC structural team on Rintoul Street.

title: Taranaki Street quay: facade demolition halted, temporary stabilisation in place
description: Demolition work on Taranaki Street historic building suspended; structural engineers deployed temporary tie-backs and props instead. Street remains closed but salvage option now being considered.

title: Te Aro neighbourhood stabilising after emergency response
description: Aftershock activity diminished. Residents settling into evacuation centres. Long-term planning commencing.

title: Water pumping stations resuming normal pressure: 22,000+ residents affected
description: Wellington Water: reduced pressure zones being cleared as power restored to pump facilities. Water supply returning to normal across CBD and eastern suburbs.

title: Wellington CBD: damage documentation complete, 45 buildings requiring inspection
description: Preliminary engineering survey finalized. Civil survey teams mapping settlement zones. Results being compiled into damage register. Public information booklet prepared.

title: Aftershock M2.7—minor seismic activity continues
description: Magnitude 2.7 aftershock recorded. Seismic network detecting multiple aftershocks per hour in tail phase.

title: Building Damage: Roof failure at residential complex
description: Multi-unit residential complex roof structure partially failed. Rain exposure risk; emergency tarping being arranged. Temporary accommodation services activated.

title: CentrePort: Vessel scheduling window opens for Saturday arrivals; 75% capacity
description: Booking system accepting cargo nominations for weekend operations. Three cranes operational; fourth pending final certification expected 20 July.

title: Hospital system now managing patient load effectively
description: Emergency operations winding down. Patient transfers to regional facilities occurring. Casualty count final at 48.

title: Lower Hutt welfare centre - 420 residents, stable operations
description: Hutt Valley High School welfare centre now accommodating 420 evacuees at 11:55 NZST. All services operational; medical team managing minor ailments and stress. Overnight accommodation being prepared for expected 200+ overnight stays.

title: M3.1 aftershock, central Wellington
description: Magnitude 3.1 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 12:29 NZST, depth 6km. Felt throughout CBD and surrounding suburbs.

title: News report: International aid mobilization earthquake water crisis response
description: International news reporting active aid mobilization for Wellington earthquake water crisis response. United Nations coordinating international support for humanitarian relief.

title: RNZ: Mental health and welfare hotlines now active; counsellors available 24/7
description: RNZ announces that mental health support services are now active and operational. Free counselling hotlines are available 24/7 for earthquake survivors; calls are being triaged to appropriate support services.

title: SH2 Hutt Valley aftereffects: traffic normalized after 5-hour disruption
description: All speed restrictions lifted. Traffic flow returning to near-normal despite occasional delays.

title: Brooklyn power fully stabilized; western sector re-energized
description: Final section of Brooklyn (Aro Street corridor) now energized after Wellington Electricity completed repairs to secondary transformer. Entire suburb now on stable power supply.

title: Emergency water shipment arranged, naval supply vessel departing Auckland
description: Emergency naval water supply vessel departing Auckland with 500,000 litre capacity emergency water supply. Estimated arrival Wellington 0600 hours tomorrow. Government declaring infrastructure emergency to expedite resource deployment.

title: Kilbirnie school buildings structural damage - education disrupted
description: Primary and secondary schools reporting structural damage precluding immediate classroom use. Distance learning protocols activated; welfare check on students underway.

title: Kilbirnie status confirmed: extensive outage; isolated substation
description: Wellington Electricity confirms Kilbirnie distribution station offline. Repair crew deployed but access routes still impassable; ETA 3-4 hours for full repair.

title: M1.9 aftershock, Seaview
description: GeoNet seismograph confirmed magnitude 1.9 aftershock at 12:30 NZST, depth 8km. Seaview area registered minor tremor.

title: Newtown residents reporting water return to normal pressure
description: Supply disruption resolved. Residents no longer under usage restrictions.

title: Newtown school building demolition proceeding as planned
description: Heavy equipment clearing debris. Site preparation underway for reconstruction. Timeline discussed with community.

title: SH2 Hutt: Jackson Street southbound on-ramp assessment complete, 72-hour repair plan
description: NZTA engineers confirm abutment separation repairable via controlled jacking and bolting. Southbound on-ramp restoration beginning 13:00; expected completion within 72 hours if no further aftershocks.

title: Structural Assessment: Reinforcement bond loss potential
description: Concrete reinforcement showing potential bond failure areas. Rebar embedment integrity questioned. Core sampling ordered for assessment.

title: Structural assessment: warehouse on Riddiford Street
description: Building being evaluated for occupancy, engineers installing monitoring equipment on Riddiford Street.

title: Thorndon: partial business reopening approved, safety protocols confirmed
description: First group of buildings cleared for limited access. Essential services only. Monitoring systems remaining active. 24-hour on-call structural team.

title: Wellington Airport: International flight schedule nearly normalised
description: Only Auckland diversions now minimal; Christchurch and Dunedin flights back on primary routing. Ground handling times returning to baseline.

title: Community report: Ngaio residents report minor ground cracks but no liquefaction
description: A Ngaio resident reports that the low-risk hill suburb has sustained surface ground cracking but no evidence of liquefaction. Local stores are preparing to reopen with limited hours.

title: Data center facilities power secured; business continuity protocols winding down
description: Cloud hosting and data center facilities power fully restored and stable. Critical backup systems being powered down.

title: Island Bay evacuation expanded—additional 150 residents
description: Fire and Emergency NZ expands Island Bay evacuation zone due to additional rockfall risk identified. Additional 150 residents issued evacuation orders; civil defence transport mobilized.

title: Karori schools closed, water supply insufficient for hygiene
description: Three schools lacking water for basic sanitation. Alternative water deliveries being arranged. Classes suspended pending restoration.

title: Miramar - preliminary structural assessments completed
description: Structural engineers complete preliminary assessment of Miramar residential properties. Foundation settling in 8 properties; minor repairs required, residents displaced for 3-5 days. No unsafe buildings identified.

title: Ngauranga Gorge: traffic flow improving, alternating control still in place
description: Queue times reducing as crews continue debris monitoring. Estimated full restoration within next 2 hours.

title: Oriental Bay property buyback programme moving forward
description: Government announcing compensation package. Voluntary relocation offers accepted by first families.

title: Petone main repair timeline extended indefinite hold equipment staging
description: Jackson Street Petone main repair timeline extended indefinitely. Equipment staging and contractor reassignment underway. Competing emergency priorities limiting repair resources.

title: Petone: housing recovery plan initiated, temporary accommodation options issued
description: Council-coordinated outreach distributing temporary housing lists. Private rental and flat-share opportunities published. Support services hotline activated.

title: Structural assessment: commercial building on Mitchell Street
description: Structural engineer assessing foundation damage and wall stability on Mitchell Street.

title: Wellington Airport: Arrivals and departures now 'virtually normal' per authority
description: Operations centre reports smooth flow; queuing minimal. Passenger satisfaction improving with capacity restoration; international service fully active.

title: Gas Leak: Underground supply fracture, residential area
description: Gas utility locating underground supply fracture affecting multiple properties. Residents evacuated from immediate area. Gas company initiating repair work.

title: M3.0 aftershock, Petone harbour
description: GeoNet seismograph confirmed magnitude 3.0 aftershock at 12:32 NZST, depth 7km. Petone area residents reported light shaking.

title: Newshub: First southbound Hutt Line service arrives 13:52 at Wellington
description: Limited service trial runs successful; public timetables not yet updated. Full schedule resumption postponed pending Track 1 completion.

title: Newtown Constitutio Avenue: temporary support shoring removed, full two-way traffic
description: Parking structure permanent repairs completed ahead of schedule; temporary supports being removed starting 12:35 pm. Full two-way traffic on Constitutio Avenue confirmed from 13:00.

title: Newtown School welfare centre: extended hours announced through 1400 hrs
description: Wellington City Council: shelter expanding capacity. Additional generators deployed for comfort; evening meals arranged for prolonged stay residents.

title: Oriental Bay area: minor settlement on scenic drive doesn't block access
description: Small subsidence on Bay Road. Traffic flow maintained.

title: Petone Jackson Street repair delayed further, contractor safety concerns
description: Wellington Water contractor expressing serious safety concerns about continuing repair work due to ground instability and ongoing aftershocks. Work suspended pending structural engineering assessment. Repair timeline extended to estimated 12+ hours.

title: Power restoration now 95 percent complete across region
description: All major suburbs have electricity. Restoration crews addressing final isolated pockets.

title: Rongotai land subsidence causing utilities network stress
description: Multiple utility networks experiencing tension and misalignment due to ground subsidence. Gas line isolation in progress as precaution.

title: Aftershock M2.5—tail phase seismic decay
description: Magnitude 2.5 aftershock detected. Aftershocks now in decaying tail phase with minimal impact.

title: Building Damage: Exterior trim separation cascade
description: Exterior trim work separating systematically from building facade. Metal trim panels dislodging. Debris hazard in progress; removal underway.

title: Herald: Island Bay residents assess property damage; most homes remain habitable
description: NZ Herald reports that Island Bay residents are conducting post-earthquake assessments. Most homes show only minor damage such as fallen pictures and cracked plaster; residential areas remain largely habitable.

title: Karori reservoir permanently damaged future operational status uncertain
description: Karori reservoir structural damage assessment completed. Permanent operational status now uncertain. Long-term replacement or major reconstruction required.

title: Lower Hutt water distribution now stable, pressure normal
description: Valley-wide supply restored to operating capacity. Hospital and critical facilities fully functional.

title: M1.8 aftershock, Kilbirnie
description: Magnitude 1.8 aftershock recorded by GeoNet at 12:33 NZST, depth 6km. Kilbirnie area detected minor tremor.

title: Newshub: Rush hour traffic on SH1 'back to normal chaos' per traffic reporter
description: Light-hearted observation of restored congestion suggesting normalcy returning. Minor delays typical of standard peak periods; no emergency-related blockages.

title: Seaview: environmental test results negative, water quality acceptable
description: Sand boil samples showing no elevated contamination. Groundwater testing complete. Full operational clearance issued for non-structural businesses.

title: State Highway 2 - Taita Bridge cleared for reopening
description: NZTA announces Taita Bridge has been cleared for phased reopening at 12:30 NZST. Detailed structural assessment found no significant damage; two-lane traffic pattern to resume with temporary speed restrictions.

title: Structural assessment: retail complex on Mitchell Street
description: Engineers documenting damage for insurance and recovery planning on Mitchell Street.

title: Thorndon reconstruction efforts moving into full swing
description: Building contractors arriving. Repair schedules announced. Residents given estimated return dates.

title: Wadestown continuing normal power operation; no further issues reported
description: Wadestown area stable and normal. No voltage fluctuations or power quality concerns reported. Wellington Electricity considers area fully restored.

title: Airport water system partially restored, operations resuming
description: Temporary bypass around damaged section operational. Water pressure at 60% of normal. Controlled flight operations resuming with restricted services.

title: Brooklyn Coromandel Street: slip material partially cleared, one lane open
description: WCC contractor removing debris with excavator. Second lane expected open within 90 minutes.

title: CBD traffic: lights resuming automated control; manual standdown
description: NZTA: traffic signal power restored to CBD intersections. Officers withdrawing from manual control; system returning to normal operation by 1100 hrs.

title: Community report: Brooklyn Beachaven Road conditions improving, normal speeds safe
description: Resident reports Beachaven Road now open to normal traffic; no further debris falling. Slope monitoring poles visible; feels safe to drive through at regular speed.

title: Government facilities returning to full operations; DPMC declaring all clear
description: Beehive and parliamentary services buildings power fully restored. All-clear declared for government operations resumption.

title: Hataitai community recovery committee established
description: Residents forming volunteer group to coordinate reconstruction. Resource sharing and mutual aid programs launched.

title: Hutt City Council: Petone slip road closure upgraded to 'permanent until repair'
description: Geotechnical assessment shows base failure; requires full reconstruction vs. patch repair. Timeline estimates 14-21 days for completion work.

title: Karori area main break isolated, permanent bypass piping considered
description: Wellington Water considering permanent bypass piping for damaged Karori area distribution main due to extended repair timeline. Above-ground temporary bypass system being designed to restore service within 6 hours.

title: Kilbirnie: water infrastructure phased restoration completed, supply normal
description: All temporary bypass systems removed. Main repair sites sealed. Pressure testing passed. Water quality verified. Boil notice lifted.

title: Miramar property damage documentation project launched
description: Insurance industry and council launching systematic property damage documentation in liquefaction zone. Aerial and ground photography teams mobilized.

title: GeoNet: Magnitude 3.1 aftershock; seismic energy release now minimal
description: GeoNet detected a 3.1 magnitude aftershock at 12:33 NZST. Seismic energy release in the aftershock sequence has diminished substantially; future aftershocks are expected to be increasingly minor.

title: Hataitai evacuation - 140 residents relocated
description: Hataitai evacuation completed at 12:03 NZST with 140 residents relocated to assembly points and evacuation centres. Properties assessed for liquefaction damage; majority declared safe for daytime return after inspection.

title: Hutt City Council area coordination: 18 identified road damage sites prioritised
description: Council meeting with contractors to schedule repairs. Public notices issued for all affected areas.

title: Hutt City Council: Petone slip road demolition and rebuild timeline announced
description: Engineering assessment confirms complete reconstruction required. 14-day timeline for removal and 21-day rebuild cycle; surface reopening estimated 8 August.

title: Karori resident: 'Helping coordinate neighborhood support'
description: Karori resident (low-damage area) organizing community meals and check-ins. Offers accommodation to 2 families displaced from CBD.

title: M2.9 aftershock, Te Aro precinct
description: Magnitude 2.9 aftershock detected by GeoNet at 12:35 NZST, depth 8km. Minor shaking in Te Aro district noted by monitoring systems.

title: Miramar full road restoration near completion
description: Last potholes being filled. Road markings reapplied. Traffic management operations ceased.

title: Structural Assessment: High school building damage survey
description: Secondary school building structural survey identifies major issues. Roof trusses displaced; foundation settling suspected. Building closure extended pending specialist review.

title: Structural assessment: retail complex on Constable Street
description: Multi-storey building undergoing systematic floor-by-floor inspection on Constable Street.

title: Thorndon major rescue operation ongoing residents trapped upper levels
description: Thorndon area major rescue operation ongoing with residents trapped in upper building levels due to surrounding floodwaters. Helicopter assisted evacuations proceeding.

title: CBD liquefaction subsidence creating building tilt hazard
description: Real-time monitoring shows progressive tilt in 4-storey commercial building on Quays. Structural engineer standby team on alert for emergency bracing.

title: Gas Leak: Meter assembly vibration loosening
description: Multiple gas meter assemblies showing vibration-induced loosening. Connections weakened; minor leakage detected. Meters being secured and assessed.

title: Island Bay beach protection works ahead of schedule
description: Erosion control structures installed. Coastal stabilisation complete. Property values beginning stabilisation discussion.

title: Karori SH2 roundabout: all monitoring equipment and speed restrictions removed
description: NZTA confirms Karori roundabout slope fully stabilized; monitoring sensors and temporary berms cleared. Normal 100 km/h speed limit restored as of 12:45 pm.

title: M1.7 aftershock, Island Bay
description: GeoNet detected magnitude 1.7 aftershock at 12:36 NZST, depth 7km. Island Bay area registered minor tremor.

title: Mount Victoria circuit fully operational; telemetry showing green
description: Mount Victoria feeder circuit all healthy indicators confirmed by Wellington Electricity SCADA system. No secondary damage or supply issues detected.

title: Ngauranga Gorge SH1: power partially restored; variable message signs active
description: NZTA: SH1 corridor lighting and emergency comms operational. Normal traffic flow resuming; minor delays expected through 1200 hrs.

title: Rongotai: runway inspection complete, limited aircraft operations approved
description: Airfield survey identified no critical structural damage. Taxiway minor patching required. Partial flights resuming Friday. Full operations Monday target.

title: Seaview industrial water supply fully functional again
description: Port operations water systems online. Industrial facility operations resuming.

title: Social media: Wairarapa Line passengers report 'shaky ride' on trial service
description: Posts describe gentle swaying and speed variation during first temporary bypass traversal. KiwiRail states this is normal for emergency track conditions.

title: Thorndon residential flooding water level stabilizing slightly pumping
description: Thorndon flooding water levels beginning to stabilize as emergency pumping systems continue operation. Water level receding approximately 2cm per hour. However, continuing risk of additional main breaks as repair work proceeds.

title: CBD water pressure improving, fire hydrant system functional
description: Main shutoff valves successfully isolated damaged sections. Pressure building in protected zones. Fire department reports improved response capability.

title: Hataitai: minor subsidence on Mahina Crescent doesn't block vehicle access
description: Cracking visible on pavement but passable. WCC monitoring for expansion.

title: Hospitality sector resuming full night operations; restaurants opening
description: Wellington restaurants, bars, and entertainment venues declaring full operations resumption for evening service.
